definition.en: Your physical experience and environment is the materialization of your beliefs.
definition.zh: 你的肉体经验与环境就是你信念的具体化。

definition.en: The first important step is to realize that your beliefs about reality are just that -- beliefs about reality and not necessarily attributes of reality.
definition.zh: 第一个重要的步骤，就是要明白你对实相的看法就是——对实相的看法，而未必是实相的特性。

definition.en: What you believe to be true in your experience is true.
definition.zh: 在你的经验中，你相信为真的事就是真的。

definition.en: To change the physical effect you must change the original belief -- while being quite aware that for a time physical materializations of the old beliefs may still hold.
definition.zh: 要改变肉体的效果，你必须改变原来的信念——同时要很明白，旧信念的肉体具体化可能还会持续一段时间。

definition.en: Because beliefs form reality -- the structure of experience -- any change in beliefs altering that structure initiates change to some extent, of course.
definition.zh: 因为信念形成实相——经验的结构——任何信念的改变都会改变那个结构，当然也就在某种程度上引发了改变。

explanation: 信念是你对现实的个人看法，而非现实本身的固有属性。你的物理体验和环境，正是信念的物质化呈现。信念分为显性信念和隐形信念：显性信念是你能直接写下的各种态度，常自相矛盾；隐形信念则是你明知却不愿面对的冲突领域。信念具有自我实现的力量——你相信什么，就会在身体、情绪和经历中创造什么。信念之间相互吸引，你对他人的态度会引发对方相应的反应。要改变生活，必须首先区分“你”与“你的信念”，然后识别并抛弃那些带来不良效果的信念。信念会自我喂养、生长壮大，因此未加检视的恐惧信念会逐渐累积，最终可能以“外来入侵”的形式突然爆发。相反，坚持积极信念（如相信善而不信恶）能带来更健康、更自由的生活状态。

quotes.text: There are two ways to get at your own conscious beliefs. The most direct is to have a series of talks with yourself. Write down your beliefs in a variety of areas, and you will find that you believe different things at different times. Often there will be contradictions readily apparent. These represent opposing beliefs that regulate your emotions, your bodily condition and your physical experience. Examine the conflicts. Invisible beliefs will appear that unite those seemingly diverse attitudes. Invisible beliefs are simply those of which you are fully aware but prefer to ignore, because they represent areas of strife which you have not been willing to handle thus far. They are quite available once you are determined to examine the complete contents of your conscious mind.
quotes.translation: 要觉察自己有意识的信念有两种方法。最直接的方式是与自己进行一系列对话。写下你在各个领域的信念，你会发现自己在不同时期相信不同的事物。通常会有显而易见的矛盾。这些矛盾代表着调节你情绪、身体状况和物质经验的对立信念。审视这些冲突。那些看似分歧的态度背后，会浮现出隐形的信念。隐形信念不过是你完全清楚却宁愿忽略的那些信念，因为它们代表着你至今不愿处理的冲突领域。一旦你决心检视意识心的全部内容，这些信念便唾手可得。

quotes.text: Realize that your physical experience and environment is the materialization of your beliefs. If you find great exuberance, health, effective work, abundance, smiles on the faces of those you meet, then take it for granted that your beliefs are beneficial. If you see a world that is good, people that like you, take it for granted, again, that your beliefs are beneficial. But if you find poor health, a lack of meaningful work, a lack of abundance, a world of sorrow and evil, then assume that your beliefs are faulty and begin examining them.
quotes.translation: 如要明白你的物质经验与环境正是你信念的具体化。若你发现自身充满活力、健康、工作高效、生活富足、所遇之人面带微笑，那么理所当然你的信念是有益的。若你看到的世界是美好的、人们是喜爱你的，同样可以认定你的信念是有益的。但若你发现健康不佳、缺乏有意义的工作、生活匮乏、世界充满悲伤与邪恶，那么就要假设你的信念存在偏差，并开始审视它们。

quotes.text: the first important step is to realize that your beliefs about reality are just that -- beliefs about reality and not necessarily attributes of reality. You must make a clear distinction between you and your beliefs. You must then realize that your beliefs are physically materialized. What you believe to be true in your experience is true. To change the physical effect you must change the original belief -- while being quite aware that for a time physical materializations of the old beliefs may still hold.
quotes.translation: 首要步骤是认清你对实相的看法只是对实相的看法，而非实相的本质属性。你必须清楚区分你与你的信念。接着要明白你的信念会具体物质化。你经验中认定真实的事物就会成真。要改变物质效应，就必须改变原始信念——同时清楚意识到旧信念的物质显化可能暂时仍会持续。

quotes.text: It may outrage your intellect, and the evidence of your physical senses may shout that it is untrue, yet a belief in good without a belief in evil is actually highly realistic, since in physical life it will keep your body healthier, keep you psychologically free of many fears and mental difficulties, and bring you a feeling of ease and spontaneity in which the development of your abilities can be better fulfilled. After death it will release you from the belief in demons and hell, and enforced punishment. You will be better prepared to understand the nature of reality as it is. I understand that the concept does indeed offend your intellect, and that your senses seem to deny it. Yet you should already realize that your senses tell you many things, which are not true; and I tell you that your physical senses perceive a reality that is a result of your beliefs.
quotes.translation: 这或许会冒犯你的理智，你肉体的感官证据也可能大声宣称这是不真实的，然而，相信善而不相信恶实际上是非常现实的，因为在物质生活中，它能让你的身体更健康，让你在心理上免于许多恐惧和精神困扰，并带给你一种轻松和自发的感受，在这种感受中，你能力的发展能更好地实现。死后，它将使你摆脱对魔鬼和地狱的信仰，以及强制性的惩罚。你将更好地准备好理解实相的本质。我明白这个概念确实冒犯了你的理智，你的感官似乎也否认它。然而，你应该已经意识到，你的感官告诉你许多不真实的事情；我告诉你，你的肉体感官所感知的实相，正是你信念的结果。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Once more, if you think of daily life as an ever-moving three-dimensional painting with you as the artist, then you will realize that as your beliefs change so will your experience. You must accept the idea completely, however, that your beliefs form your experience. Discard those beliefs that are not bringing you those effects you want. In the meantime you will often be in the position of telling yourself that something is true in the face of physical data that seems completely contradictory. You may say, "I live amid abundance and am free from want," while your eyes tell you that the desk is piled with bills. You must realize that you are the one who produced that "physical evidence" that still faces you, and you did so through your beliefs.
quotes.translation: 再说一次，如果你把日常生活看作一幅不断移动的三维画作，而你是那位画家，那么你就会意识到，随着你的信念改变，你的经验也会随之改变。然而，你必须完全接受这个观念：你的信念塑造了你的经验。抛弃那些没有带给你想要效果的信念。与此同时，你常常会处于这样一种境地：面对似乎完全矛盾的物理数据，你告诉自己某件事是真的。你可能会说：“我生活在丰裕之中，没有匮乏”，而你的眼睛却告诉你，桌上堆满了账单。你必须意识到，正是你通过信念，产生了那些仍然面对你的“物理证据”。

quotes.text: The same kind of development can occur in almost any physical category. Usually more than one belief is involved. Parallel with the belief that vision will fail, you may have the before-mentioned belief that hearing will dim, and these two ideas may be reinforced by a belief that age automatically makes you less a person, turning you into an individual who can no longer relate in the daily pattern of environment. The belief, you see, would work to insure the materialization of that state. (Pause.) On the other hand you may believe that wisdom grows with age, that self-understanding brings a peace of mind not earlier known, that the keen mind is actually far better able to assess the environment, and that the physical senses are much more appreciative of all stimuli. And so those conditions will be physically met in your experience. The physical apparatus itself, following your beliefs, will continue in health.
quotes.translation: 类似的发展几乎可以发生在任何物理类别中。通常涉及不止一个信念。与视力会衰退的信念并行，你可能还有之前提到的听力会变差的信念，而这两个想法可能被一个信念强化：年龄自动让你变得不那么完整，把你变成一个无法再在日常环境模式中互动的人。你看，这个信念会确保那种状态的具体化。（停顿。）另一方面，你可能相信智慧随年龄增长，自我理解带来先前未知的心灵平静，敏锐的心智实际上能更好地评估环境，肉体感官对所有刺激也更为欣赏。那么，这些条件将在你的经验中物理地实现。肉体装置本身，遵循你的信念，将继续保持健康。

quotes.text: As time went on the number of unexamined, frightening beliefs began to accumulate. Ideas and beliefs do feed upon themselves. There is within them a built-in impetus toward growth, development and fulfillment. Over the years two opposing systems of beliefs built up strongly, vying for Augustus's attention. He believed that he was utterly powerless as an individual, that despite all his efforts he would come to nothing, go unnoticed. He felt completely unloved. He did not feel worthy of love. At the same time he let his conscious mind wander, and to compensate saw himself as all-powerful, contemptuous of his fellow human beings, and able to work greater vengeance upon them for their misunderstanding of him. In this line of beliefs he was able to do anything -- cure mankind's ills if he chose, or withhold such knowledge from the world to punish it. Period.
quotes.translation: 随着时间流逝，未经检视的恐惧信念开始累积。观念与信念确实会自我滋养。它们内在具有朝向成长、发展与圆满的固有动力。多年来两套对立的信念体系逐渐壮大，争夺着奥古斯都的注意力。他相信自己作为个体完全无能为力，无论多么努力终将一事无成、无人关注。他感到全然不被爱，也不配得到爱。同时他放任意识心游荡，为了补偿而将自己视为全能的，蔑视同胞，并因他人对他的误解而能施以更残酷的报复。在这套信念中他无所不能——若他愿意就能治愈人类疾苦，或为惩罚世人而扣留这些知识。句号。

quotes.text: Now such invasion is usually the sudden appearance of previously unacceptable beliefs, quite conscious but invisible, tucked away. Then they suddenly appear as alien. In most instances the possession concept makes it all the more upsetting. Easier to face, often, is the idea that the responsibility for such ideas must belong to another entity or being. In all cases of this nature involving Augustus-type episodes, the problem is one of unassimilated beliefs. Instead of such comparatively drastic behavior, however, such beliefs can be expressed through various parts of the body. Unfortunately, a system of medicine that largely deals with symptoms only encourages a patient to project such beliefs on new organs, for instance, after already sacrificing others in operations.
quotes.translation: 这种入侵通常是先前不被接受的信念突然浮现，它们相当有意识却隐而不见，被藏匿起来。然后它们突然以异己的面貌出现。多数情况下附身概念会加剧困扰。更容易面对的说法往往是：这些想法的责任必定属于另一个存有或存在。所有涉及奥古斯都这类案例的本质中，问题都在于未被整合的信念。然而这些信念不必通过如此激烈的行为表现，它们也能经由身体各部位表达。不幸的是，主要处理症状的医疗体系只会鼓励病人将这类信念投射到新器官上——例如在手术切除其他器官之后。

quotes.text: (9:50.) This subject leads to what I will call bridge beliefs, and again Ruburt received some information on this topic ahead of time for his own benefit. (See the notes prefacing the last session.) As you examine your ideas you will discover that even some apparently contradictory ones have similarities, and these resemblances may be used to bridge the gaps between beliefs -- even those that seem to be the most diverse. Because you are the individual who holds the beliefs you will stamp them, so to speak, with certain characteristics that you will recognize. These aspects will themselves emerge as bridge beliefs. They contain great motion and energy. When you discover what they are, you will find a point of unity within yourself from which you can with some detachment, view your other systems of belief.
quotes.translation: （9:50）这个主题引向我将称之为"桥梁信念"的概念，鲁柏也提前收到关于此主题的资讯以利己用（见上节前言）。当你检视自己的观念时，会发现即使某些明显矛盾的信念也有相似之处，这些相似性可用来架接信念间的鸿沟——甚至包括那些看似最南辕北辙的信念。因为你是持有这些信念的个体，可以说你会为它们烙上自己可辨识的特定印记。这些特质本身会显现为桥梁信念。它们蕴含巨大的动力与能量。当你发现它们是什么时，就会在自身找到统一点，由此你能以某种超然态度审视其他信念体系。

quotes.text: Think of the present as a pool of experience drawn from many sources, fed, in your terms, by tributaries from both the past and the future. There are an infinite number of such tributaries (probabilities), and through your beliefs you choose from these, adjusting their currents. For example: If you constantly focus on the belief that your early background was damaging and negative, then only such experiences will flow into your present life from the past. It does no good to say, "But my life was traumatic," therefore reinforcing the belief. You must in one way or another modify that conviction, or preferably change it entirely -- or you will never escape from its effects. This does not mean "lying" to yourself; but if it seems to you that your background held no joys, accomplishments or pleasures, then you are lying to yourself now. You have concentrated upon the negative to such a degree that anything else seems invisible. (See the 644th session in Chapter Eleven.) From the present you have hypnotized yourself, viewing the past not as it was to your experience, but as it appears now in the light of your current beliefs.
quotes.translation: 将当下视为一个由多重源头汇聚而成的经验池，在你看来，它同时接受来自过去与未来的支流灌注。这类支流（可能性）无穷无尽，而你的信念决定了你从中选取哪些，并调整其流向。举例来说：若你持续聚焦于"早年经历充满创伤与负面"的信念，那么唯有此类经验会从过去流入你的当下。不断强调"我的人生充满创伤"只会强化这个信念，毫无助益。你必须以某种方式修正这个执念，最好彻底改变它——否则你将永远无法摆脱其影响。这并非要你"自欺欺人"；但若你认定自己的过往毫无喜悦、成就与欢愉，那才是真正的自欺。你已过度聚焦负面，以致其他一切视而不见。（参见第十一章第644节）你从当下催眠了自己，审视过去时并非依据真实体验，而是透过当前信念的滤镜。

quotes.text: (10:02.) Give us a moment ... So-called future developments of your species are now dependent upon your ideas and beliefs. This applies genetically in personal terms. For instance, if you believe that you can live to a healthy and happy old age, well into your nineties, then even in Western civilization you will do so. Your emotional intent and your belief will direct the functioning of your cells and (emphatically) bring out in them those properties and inherent abilities that will ensure such a condition. There are groups of people in isolated places who hold such beliefs, and in all such cases the body responds. The same applies to the race -- or the species, to be more exact. There is an inexhaustible creativity within the cells themselves, that you are not using as a species because your beliefs lag so far behind your innate biological spirituality and wisdom. Your ideas are beginning to change. But unless you alter your framework you will continue to emphasize medical and technological manipulation. Period. In isolated cases this will show you some of the results possible on a physical basis alone. However, such techniques will not work in mass terms, or allow you, say, to prolong effective, productive life unless you change your beliefs in other areas also, and learn the inner dynamics of the psyche.
quotes.translation: （10:02）稍待片刻……你们物种所谓的未来发展，此刻正取决于你们的思想与信念。这适用于个人层面的基因运作。举例而言，若你相信自己能健康快乐地活到九十高龄，即便身处西方文明，你也能达成此愿。你的情感意图与信念将引导细胞运作，并（强调地）激发其内在特性与天赋能力，确保此状态实现。在偏远地区确有秉持此类信念的族群，他们的身体皆能响应。此理同样适用于种族——更精确地说，是物种。细胞本身蕴含着取之不尽的创造力，但你们作为物种尚未善用，因为你们的信念远落后于与生俱来的生物灵性与智慧。你们的思想正在转变。但若不改变认知框架，你们仍会持续强调医疗与科技操控。句号。个别案例会展现纯物质层面可能达成的某些成果。然而，除非你们同时改变其他领域的信念，并学习心灵的内在动力，否则这些技术无法大规模奏效，也无法延长真正有效且富有创造力的生命。

quotes.text: (9:29.) All of these qualities and attributes are given you by natural law. You are a cooperative species, and you are a loving one. Your misunderstandings, your crimes, and your atrocities, real as they are, are seldom committed out of any intent to be evil, but because of severe misinterpretations about the nature of good, and the means that can be taken toward its actualization. Most individual people know that in some inner portion of themselves. Your societies, governments, educational systems, are all built around a firm belief in the unreliability of human nature. "You cannot change human nature." Such a statement takes it for granted that man's nature is to be greedy, a predator, a murderer at heart. You act in accordance with your own beliefs. You become the selves that you think you are. Your individual beliefs become the beliefs of your society, but that is always a give-and-take.
quotes.translation: （9:29）所有这些特质与禀赋皆由自然法则赋予。你们是合作的物种，是充满爱的物种。你们的误解、罪行与暴行，尽管真实存在，却极少出于作恶意图，而是源于对善的本质及其实现方式的严重曲解。多数人在内在深处知晓此理。你们的社会、政府、教育体系，皆建立在"人性不可信赖"的坚定信念之上。"人性无法改变"——此类陈述理所当然地认定人性本贪婪、掠夺成性、骨子里是谋杀者。你们依信念行事，成为自认为的那个自我。个人信念汇聚成社会信念，这始终是双向的互动。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: "Now Ruburt had only one parent available most of the time, and he did not feel secure in that relationship--a situation chosen ahead of time, now. There is great leeway in the nature of such bondings.... With some people, they are so secure that they provide an overall, fairly permanent inner and outer framework. Ruburt's relationship with his mother left much to be desired. The bonding did not secure him that vital sense of safety, and he felt threatened by abandonment. His bonding to the cultural beliefs of religion was very strong to make up for that initial lack. The sinful-self material represents those ideas that were a strong element in his original belief structures. The 'troublesome' material remained relatively inactive until his curiosity and ability led him to actively challenge those ideas while [he was] also in a situation where the natural fear of abandonment might be suggested. At certain points, the assimilation of new information is so qualifiedly different from the original belief structure that in order to assimilate it the personality is left for a time between belief systems.
quotes.translation: "如今鲁柏大部分时间只有单亲陪伴，在这段关系中他缺乏安全感——这是预先选择的情境。此类情感纽带的本质存在极大弹性……对某些人而言，这种纽带如此稳固，足以提供全面且相对持久的内外框架。鲁柏与母亲的关系却未尽人意，这份纽带未能赋予他至关重要的安全感，他始终被遗弃的恐惧所威胁。为弥补最初的情感缺失，他对宗教文化信念的依附异常强烈。有罪自我的材料代表了他原始信念结构中的核心要素。这些'麻烦的'材料长期处于相对休眠状态，直到他的好奇心与能力驱使他主动挑战这些观念——恰逢可能触发自然遗弃恐惧的情境。在某些节点，新信息的同化与原始信念结构产生本质差异，为完成同化，人格会暂时悬置于不同信念系统之间。"
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: You may desire health, but believe implicitly in your state of poor health. You may desire spiritual understanding, while thinking that you are spiritually unworthy and opaque. (Pause.) When you set your longing against a present belief there is always conflict. Your belief will generate the proper feelings and imaginative endeavors characteristic of it. If you want to be healthy and continually contrast what you want with the present conviction in your poor health, then the belief itself, set up against the desire, will cause added difficulties. In such a case you seem to want the impossible. Desire and belief are not united but apart.
quotes.translation: 你可能渴望健康，却深深相信自己处于不健康的状态。你可能渴望灵性上的领悟，同时却认为自己灵性上不配且愚钝。（停顿。）当你将渴望与当前的信念对立时，冲突便始终存在。你的信念会引发与之相应的情感和想象努力。如果你想要健康，却不断将渴望与当前对不健康的信念对比，那么信念本身与渴望对立，反而会带来更多困难。在这种情况下，你似乎是在追求不可能之事。渴望与信念并未统一，而是分离的。

quotes.text: (10:05.) The inner mechanics of emotions and beliefs are complicated, but these are individuals who feel that physical life has failed them. They are powerless in society. They think in black and white, and conflicts between their emotions, and their beliefs about their emotions, lead them to seek some kind of shelter in a rigid belief system that will give them rules to go by. Such systems lead to the formation of cults, and the potential members seek out a leader who will serve their purposes as surely as they seem to serve his -- through an inner mechanics of which each member is at least somewhat aware.
quotes.translation: （10:05分。）情感与信念的内在机制是复杂的，但有些人觉得物质生活辜负了他们。他们在社会中感到无力。他们以非黑即白的方式思考，情感与对情感的信念之间的冲突，促使他们在僵化的信念体系中寻求庇护，以获得可遵循的规则。这类体系会导致教派的形成，潜在成员会寻找一位领袖，这位领袖既服务于他们的目的，正如他们似乎服务于他的目的一样——通过每个成员至少有所察觉的内在机制。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: It is impossible to tell you of the emotional reality of such an experience. You will have to discover it for yourself. Such bridge beliefs often allow you to perceive the "invisible" beliefs mentioned this evening, and these can then appear to you as a revelation. On second thought, however, you will realize that another belief blocked that one from your view, but that you were always aware of it; and that in a strange way it was also invisible because you took it for granted. You did not consider it a belief about reality but as reality itself, and never questioned it.
quotes.translation: 我无法向你描述这种体验的情感实相，你必须亲自去发现。这类桥梁信念常能让你瞥见今晚提到的"隐形"信念，它们会以启示的形式显现。但细想之下，你会意识到是另一个信念遮蔽了它，而你始终知晓它的存在；奇怪的是，它之所以隐形，恰因你视其为理所当然。你未曾将它视为关于实相的信念，而是直接当作实相本身，从未质疑。

quotes.text: (11:12.) I gave him helpful information, but this could only be used by him as he felt it for himself and traveled through his own system of beliefs. When you understand the nature of reality and your part in forming it, then you can no longer look to others to solve your problems for you, and you realize that your own beliefs are the rich creative elements that you yourself must mix and match. If you think that certain foods will help you, then they will be effective in that system -- because of your beliefs. If you believe in doctors, then they will help you.
quotes.translation: （11:12）我给予他有益的信息，但这些信息唯有当他亲身感受并穿越自身信念体系时才能发挥作用。当你理解实相的本质以及你在创造实相中的角色，便不再指望他人替你解决问题，你会明白自己的信念正是需要亲手调配的丰盛创造元素。若你相信某些食物有益，它们便会在那个体系里生效——因你的信念使然。若你信赖医生，他们自会助你。

quotes.text: In your daily physical life you are usually concerned simply with changing your beliefs about yourself, and then changing the beliefs others hold about you. You will find conflicting beliefs within yourself and you must become aware of these. As an example, you may believe that you want to understand the nature of your inner self -- you may tell yourself you want to remember your dreams, but at the same time still hold a belief in the basic unworthiness of the self, and be quite frightened of remembering your dreams because of what you might find there.
quotes.translation: 在日常物质生活中，你通常只需改变关于自身的信念，继而改变他人对你的看法。你会发现内在存在矛盾的信念，必须觉察这些冲突。举例而言，你或许渴望理解内在自我的本质——你告诉自己希望记住梦境，但同时又固守着"自我本质不配"的信念，因恐惧梦中可能发现的内容而对记梦望而却步。

quotes.text: (4:24.) Now: The dream of the car represents beliefs that you had when you had the car. In a manner of speaking, those beliefs "took you for a joyride" -- therefore the joys represented portions of yourself. In the dream you are quite angry simply because those beliefs did, in a fashion, take the vehicle of your life out of your own hands, since you did not recognize those beliefs as your own in the past. At the end, the car or the vehicle is (underlined) returned to you, and the dream shows that you understand, now, the process that the dream outlined.
quotes.translation: （4点24分）现在：汽车的梦境代表了你拥有那辆车时所持有的信念。可以说，那些信念"带你兜风"——因此，那些喜悦代表了你自身的各个部分。在梦中你相当愤怒，仅仅是因为那些信念在某种程度上将你生命的载体从你手中夺走，因为你过去并未认识到那些信念是你自己的。最后，汽车或载体（强调）归还给了你，这个梦境表明你现在理解了梦中所勾勒的过程。

quotes.text: The shifting of belief may then open him to question his other beliefs, and he realizes that in the area of wealth, for example, he did very well because of his beliefs; but in those others, perhaps deeper experiences opened by his illness, he learns that human experience includes dimensions of reality that had earlier been closed to him, and that these are also easily within his reach -- and without the illness that originally brought them forth. A new conglomeration of beliefs might emerge. In the meantime there was stress, but it was creative.
quotes.translation: 信念的转变可能进而让他质疑自己的其他信念，他意识到在财富领域，例如，他因自己的信念而做得很好；但在其他方面，或许通过疾病开启的更深刻体验中，他了解到人类经验包含了先前对他封闭的现实维度，而这些维度同样易于触及——无需最初引发它们的疾病。一种新的信念组合可能浮现。在此期间虽有压力，但这是创造性的。

quotes.text: For some of you the city will have a theater. For some of you it will not. For those of you who like theater, it will be like none you have ever seen. In it the actors and actresses will take the parts of beliefs -- of fleshed beliefs -- and the morality play, so to speak, will deal with the nature of beliefs and how they are enacted through the centuries as well as through the hours. That theater then will serve many purposes, even as each of you are exquisite performers, and have chosen the roles and beliefs that you have taken....
quotes.translation: 对你们有些人来说，这座城市将有一座剧院。对有些人则没有。对那些喜爱戏剧的人而言，它将是你前所未见的。在剧中，演员们将扮演信念的角色——有血有肉的信念——而道德剧，可以说，将探讨信念的本质，以及它们如何在数百年乃至数小时中被演绎。那座剧院将服务于多种目的，正如你们每个人都是精湛的表演者，并选择了自己所扮演的角色和所持的信念。

quotes.text: Luckily Andrea is working with her own system of beliefs. Presently, however, while she tells herself that age does not matter, she still believes that her desirability as a woman decreases with the passage of each day. So she feels and acts less attractive -- when that belief holds sway. She is fortunate enough to be able to check her physical experience against her beliefs, and astute enough to see areas in which she has made great advances. But let us look at some of those beliefs and apply them to others generally.
quotes.translation: 幸运的是，安德莉亚正在运用她自己的信念系统。然而目前，当她告诉自己年龄并不重要时，她仍然相信自己的女性魅力会随着每一天的流逝而减退。所以当那个信念占据主导时，她感觉自己不那么有吸引力，行为也显得不那么有魅力。她很幸运能够将自己的身体经验与信念进行对照，并且足够敏锐地看到自己在哪些领域取得了巨大进步。但让我们来看看其中一些信念，并将它们普遍应用于他人。

quotes.text: "The 'proper' question to ask is not: 'Can I enter that land?' The land is here, where you are, and it always has been. The methods, the ways, the beliefs, the modes of travel to a destination create the destination itself. (A one-minute pause.) It is impossible for you to operate without beliefs in your present mode of existence (another minute), 'for beyond' those glittering packages of beliefs, however, there exists the vast reservoir of sensation itself, the land that does indeed exist 'beyond beliefs.'
quotes.translation: "该问的'恰当'问题不是：'我能进入那片土地吗？'那片土地就在这里，在你所在之处，它一直都在。通往目的地的方法、途径、信念和旅行模式，本身就创造了那个目的地。（停顿一分钟。）在你目前的存在模式中，你不可能在没有信念的情况下运作（又停顿一分钟），'然而在'那些闪闪发光的信念包裹'之外'，存在着感觉本身的巨大宝库，那片确实存在于'信念之外'的土地。"
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: Reality, then, is viewed through this system of beliefs. If you hold them you will feel that those characteristics are given by God. According to the fervor with which you cherish these ideas you will find that they enclose you, for in a very limited manner they will define your concept of good. People entertaining such beliefs are often very religious in conventional terms. Countries emphasizing like beliefs send missionaries to "convert" those who are pagan, and therefore inferior.
quotes.translation: 因此，实相是通过这个信念系统被看待的。如果你持有这些信念，你会觉得那些特质是神所赋予的。根据你珍视这些想法的热忱程度，你会发现它们将你包围，因为以非常有限的方式，它们会定义你对善的概念。抱持这类信念的人，在传统意义上通常非常虔诚。强调类似信念的国家会派遣传教士去"转化"那些异教徒，因而被视为低等的人。

quotes.text: New paragraph: Because beliefs form reality -- the structure of experience -- any change in beliefs altering that structure initiates change to some extent, of course. The status quo which served a certain purpose is gone, new elements are introduced, another creative process begins. Because your private beliefs are shared with others, because there is interaction, then any determined change of direction on your part is felt by others, and they will react in their own fashion.
quotes.translation: 新段落：因为信念形成实相——经验的结构——信念的任何改变都会在一定程度上改变那个结构，从而引发变化。曾服务于特定目的的现状消失了，新的元素被引入，另一个创造过程开始了。由于你的私人信念与他人共享，由于存在互动，那么你任何坚定的方向改变都会被他人感知，而他们会以自己的方式做出反应。

quotes.text: (10:45.) A certain kind of affirmation of self allows the brain to tune into these more spacious methods of perception that are the natural characteristics of the mind. There are very good reasons why this type of assertion must first occur. The brain (and the entire physical system) is meant to insure your bodily survival and to follow your conscious beliefs about reality. There is always a harmonious unifying connection between your beliefs and activities. Some people feel utterly confident in certain areas and are timorous in others. Some aspects of life may be ignored or even refuted for a time while others are focused upon. The individual will very cleverly and shrewdly go ahead in those areas in which he or she feels safe, often when in the process of altering beliefs. You will not use your spacious mind until you affirm its reality within yourself, and until you are ready to handle the additional data which will then become consciously available to one extent or another. But the spacious mind operates through your creaturehood; in your terms it represents latent abilities of consciousness that can be more or less normal functions.
quotes.translation: （10:45分）某种程度的自我肯定能让大脑调频至这些更广阔的感知方法——这些方法本是心灵的天然特质。这种断言必须首先发生有其充分理由。大脑（以及整个生理系统）旨在确保你身体的存活，并遵循你对实相的有意识信念。你的信念与活动之间始终存在和谐统一的联结。有些人在某些领域充满自信，在其他领域却畏首畏尾。生命的某些面向可能被暂时忽视甚至否定，而其他面向则被聚焦。个体在改变信念的过程中，往往会非常机敏地在自己感到安全的领域前行。除非你在内在确认了广阔心灵的真实性，并且准备好处理那些将不同程度地进入意识层面的额外数据，否则你不会运用你的广阔心灵。但广阔心灵通过你的生物性运作；用你的话说，它代表了意识中那些可以成为或多或少正常功能的潜在能力。

quotes.text: What you think of as (underlined) male ego-oriented characteristics are simply those human attributes that the species encouraged, brought into the foreground, and stressed. Using those actually as guidelines, you have so far viewed your world and formed your cultures. There are some exceptions of note, but here I am speaking historically of the Western world with its Roman and Greek heritage. Your gods became masculine then; competitive. You saw the species pitted against nature, and man pitted against man. You consider the Greek tragedies great because they echo so firmly your own beliefs. Man is seen in opposition in the most immediate fashion with his own father. Family relationships become a mirror of those beliefs, which are then of course taken as statements of fact concerning the human condition. You thus have a very polarized male-female concept.
quotes.translation: 你们所谓的（强调）男性自我导向特质，不过是人类物种所鼓励、凸显并强调的那些人性特征。你们以这些特质为准则，至今仍在观察世界并塑造文化。虽有些值得注意的例外，但此处我特指具有罗马与希腊传统的西方历史。你们的神祇自此转为男性化、竞争性。你们视物种与自然为敌，人与人相争。你们认为希腊悲剧伟大，正因其如此坚定地呼应了你们自身的信念。人类被描绘成与生父直接对抗的形象。家庭关系成为这些信念的镜像，而这些信念自然被当作关于人类处境的真实陈述。由此便形成了极度两极化的男女观念。

quotes.text: The flu season intersects with the Christmas season, of course, when Christians are told to be merry and [wish] their fellows a happy return to the natural wonders of childhood, in thought at least. [They are also told] to pay homage to God. Christianity has become, however, a tangled sorry tale, its cohesiveness largely vanished. Such a religion becomes isolated from daily life. Many individuals cannot unify the various areas of their belief and feeling, and at Christmas they partially recognize the vast gulf that exists between their scientific beliefs and their religious beliefs. They find themselves unable to cope with such a mental and spiritual dilemma. A psychic depression often results, one that is deepened by the Christmas music and the commercial displays, by the religious reminders that the species is made in God's image, and by the other reminders that the body so given is seemingly incapable of caring for itself and is a natural prey to disease and disaster.
quotes.translation: 流感季节自然与圣诞季重叠——此时基督徒被要求欢庆，并（在思想层面至少）祝愿同伴重返童年自然奇迹。他们还被要求向神致敬。然而基督教已沦为支离破碎的悲哀故事，其凝聚力几乎消失殆尽。这样的宗教与日常生活渐行渐远。许多人无法统合自身信念与感受的各个领域，在圣诞时节，他们部分意识到科学信念与宗教信仰间存在的巨大鸿沟。他们发现自己无力应对这种心智与灵性的困境。由此常引发心理抑郁——圣诞音乐、商业展示、提醒人类是按神形象所造的宗教训诫，以及暗示这具身体无法自我照料、天生易遭疾病与灾难的其他警示，都加深了这种抑郁。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Your private beliefs merge with those of others, and form your cultural reality. The distorted ideas of the medical profession or the scientists, or of any other group, are not thrust upon you, therefore. They are the result of your mass beliefs -- isolated in the form of separate disciplines. Medical men, for example, are often extremely unhealthy because they are so saddled with those specific health beliefs that their attention is concentrated in that area more than others not so involved. The idea of prevention is always based upon fear -- for you do not want to prevent something that is joyful. Often, therefore, preventative medicine causes what it hopes to avoid. Not only does the idea [of prevention] continually promote the entire system of fear, but specific steps taken to prevent a disease in a body not already stricken, again, often set up reactions that bring about side effects that would occur if the disease had in fact been suffered.
quotes.translation: 你们的个人信念与他人信念相融合，共同构成文化实相。因此，医学界、科学界或其他任何群体的扭曲观念并非强加于你们，而是你们集体信念的产物——以不同学科的形式被隔离呈现。例如，医生往往极度不健康，正因他们背负着特定的健康信念，其注意力比未涉足此领域者更集中于此。预防观念始终基于恐惧——因为你们不会去预防令人愉悦的事物。因此，预防医学常引发其本欲避免的结果。不仅（预防）观念持续强化整个恐惧体系，而且为未患病身体采取的特定预防措施，也常引发类似实际患病时才会出现的副作用。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: (For example, she spent Monday and Tuesday reading poetry she'd written before the sessions began [in 1963], wondering why she didn't have the impulse to work on Heroics instead. Finally, last night she made her intuitive connection: She had been working on the book the entire time. Heroics isn't to be on how to reach some unattainable superself, but on the barriers that stand in the way of practical self-realization. That old poetry dealt with such impediments. "You can't find your heroic self unless you trust the self you have," she told me. "Seth's been telling us to be alert for negative Freudian and Darwinian beliefs -- and suddenly I'm surrounded by my own. And all of those beliefs stand in the way of trusting my impulses. I finally see where the book is headed. I'm going to work out those beliefs for myself and for our readers.")
quotes.translation: （例如，她周一和周二都在阅读自己在课程开始前（1963年）写的诗歌，纳闷自己为何没有创作《英雄主义》的冲动。最后，昨晚她凭直觉找到了关联：她其实一直在写那本书。《英雄主义》并非关于如何达到某个无法企及的超级自我，而是关于阻碍实际自我实现的障碍。那些旧诗正是处理这些障碍的。“除非你信任自己现有的自我，否则你无法找到英雄般的自我，”她告诉我，“赛斯一直提醒我们要警惕负面的弗洛伊德式和达尔文式信念——突然间我发现自己被这些信念包围。所有这些信念都阻碍了我信任自己的冲动。我终于看清了这本书的方向。我要为自己和读者厘清这些信念。”）
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: The entire orientation is strange or alien only to your conscious belief systems. In one way or another, most people are aware of a desire for death before they die -- a desire they usually do not consciously acknowledge. To a large measure, the sensations of pain are also the results of your beliefs, so that even diseases that are indeed accompanied, now, by great pain, need not be. Obviously, I am saying that "deadly" viruses do not "think of themselves" as killers, any more than a cat does when it devours a mouse. The mouse may die, and a cell might die as a result of the virus, but the connotations applied to such events are also the results of beliefs. In the greater sphere of spiritual and biological activity, the viruses are protecting life at their level, and in the capacity given them.
quotes.translation: 整个取向对你而言之所以陌生或异类，仅仅是因为它不符合你意识层面的信念体系。无论如何，大多数人在死亡前都隐约意识到对死亡的渴望——这种渴望通常不被他们清醒地承认。在很大程度上，疼痛的感觉也是你信念的结果，以至于即便是那些目前确实伴随剧痛的疾病，也未必需要如此。显然，我是在说“致命”病毒并不“自视为”杀手，就像猫吞食老鼠时并不自视为杀手一样。老鼠可能会死，细胞也可能因病毒而亡，但赋予这类事件的含义同样是信念的结果。在灵性与生物活动的更大领域中，病毒在其层面上保护着生命，以其被赋予的能力行事。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: This entire belief system was detrimental enough, when you were devoted to writing and painting as these are generally understood. When Ruburt's psychic abilities began to show themselves, however, those same beliefs made both of you even more cautious than before, and more worried about reprisal from others -- and as far as Ruburt was concerned, more worried about criticism or scorn. All of those beliefs existed along with many unfortunate ones that were sexually oriented -- those that dictated, for example, the traditional roles of man and wife, or man and woman. Ruburt felt some guilt in expressing psychic abilities in such a marked fashion, when it seemed that the male of the relationship should be the most highly talented, and by far the most successful financially (intently). So your roles in that manner upset both of you at times.
quotes.translation: 当你们全心投入写作与绘画这些普遍认知中的活动时，这套信念体系就已足够有害。然而当鲁柏的通灵能力开始显现时，这些信念反而使你们比以往更加谨慎，更担心他人的报复——就鲁柏而言，更害怕招致批评或轻蔑。所有这些信念都与许多不幸的性取向信念并存——例如那些规定夫妻或男女传统角色的教条。当这段关系中男性本应最具天赋且经济上远更成功时（专注地），鲁柏对如此显著地展现通灵能力感到些许愧疚。因此你们在这方面的角色定位时常令双方感到不安。

quotes.text: Now if you are honest with your lists, you will finally come to what I call core beliefs, strong ideas about your own existence. Many other subsidiary beliefs, that earlier seemed separate from each other, should now appear quite clearly as being offshoots of core beliefs. They seem logical only in their relationship to a core idea. Once the core belief is understood to be a false one, the others will fall away.
quotes.translation: 现在，如果你诚实地面对你的清单，你最终会触及我所谓的核心信念——关于你自身存在的强烈观念。许多其他附属信念，最初看似彼此无关，如今应清晰显现为核心信念的分支。它们只有在与核心观念的关系中才显得合乎逻辑。一旦核心信念被理解为虚假的，其他信念便会随之瓦解。

quotes.text: I would like to add a note concerning the importance and dangers of belief. An open mind is by far one of the best advantages that you can possess. Belief is a two way door, although it does not have to be. One belief, for example, may seem to negate another belief. Close examination will often prove this is not the case. Simply for example, a belief in the color red does not negate belief in the color green.
quotes.translation: 我想补充一点关于信念的重要性和危险性的说明。开放的心态是你们所能拥有的最佳优势之一。信念是一扇双向门，尽管它不必如此。例如，一个信念可能看似否定另一个信念。但仔细审视往往会证明事实并非如此。举个简单的例子，对红色的信念并不否定对绿色的信念。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: Now: Any intrusion of other beliefs here will be considered threatening. Both racial problems and religious dissension will be rationalized from the standpoint of these beliefs. Some of my readers may consider themselves quite enlightened, believing, for example, in reincarnation as a series of consecutive lives. However, they may then use that concept to justify their belief in the inferiority of other races. They may say that since an individual chose his or her problems in this life -- deciding for instance to be born black, or poor, or both -- that karma is being worked out; therefore such issues should not be adjusted through a change of law or custom. Period.
quotes.translation: 现在：任何其他信念的介入在此都会被视作威胁。种族问题与宗教纷争都会从这些信念的立场出发被合理化。我的一些读者可能自认为相当开明，例如相信转世是一系列连续的生命。然而，他们随后可能用这个观念来合理化自己对其他种族低劣的信念。他们可能会说，既然个体在此生选择了自己的问题——例如决定生为黑人、穷人，或两者兼具——那么业力正在被化解；因此这类问题不应通过改变法律或习俗来调整。句号。

quotes.text: We will then resume dictation. (Pause.) Your beliefs generate emotion. It is somewhat fashionable to place feelings above conscious thoughts, the idea being that emotions are more basic and natural than conscious reasoning is. The two actually go together but your conscious thinking largely determines your emotions, and not the other way around. Your beliefs generate the appropriate emotion that is implied. A long period of inner depression does not just come upon you. Your emotions do not betray you. Instead, over a period of time you have been consciously entertaining negative beliefs that then generated the strong feelings of despondency.
quotes.translation: 我们将继续口述。（停顿。）你的信念产生情绪。将情感置于有意识思想之上在某种程度上已成风尚，这种观念认为情绪比有意识推理更基本、更自然。两者实际上相辅相成，但你的有意识思考在很大程度上决定了你的情绪，而非相反。你的信念会产生与之相应的恰当情绪。长期的内在抑郁并非凭空降临。你的情绪不会背叛你。相反，在一段时间内，你一直在有意识地持有负面信念，这些信念随后产生了强烈的沮丧感。

quotes.text: The core belief, because of its intensity and because of your habits, will often tend to attract to itself others of a like nature. They will hang on. If you are not accustomed to examining your own mind, then you can allow separate growths of this kind to form about a belief until you cannot distinguish one from the other. This can develop to such an extent that all of your experience is seen only in relationship to this idea-growth. (Seth called for the hyphen.) Data that seems unrelated to this core belief is then not assimilated but thrown into the corners of your mind, unused, and you are denied the value of the information.
quotes.translation: 核心信念因其强度与习惯性，往往会吸引同类性质的信念依附其上。它们会紧紧附着。若你不习惯检视自己的心智，便可能任由这类信念各自滋长，最终难以分辨彼此。这种状况可能发展到如此程度：你所有的经验都只能透过这个"观念丛"（赛斯要求加连字符）来解读。与核心信念看似无关的信息会被弃置在心灵角落，无法被吸收利用，使你错失这些信息的价值。

quotes.text: So as you alter the belief, the physical evidence will gradually begin to "prove" your new belief as faithfully as it did your old one. You must work with your own ideas. While there are general categories of beliefs, and general reasons for them, you must become personally aware of your own, for no one person is completely like any other. The old beliefs served a purpose and fulfilled a need.
quotes.translation: 因此当你改变信念时，物质证据会逐渐开始像证明旧信念那样忠实地"证明"你的新信念。你必须亲自处理自己的观念。虽然信念存在普遍类别与成因，但你必须觉察自身独有的信念，因为没有任何人与他人完全相同。旧信念曾有其目的，也满足了某种需求。

quotes.text: (9:50.) Usually when you do examine your conscious mind you do so looking through, or with, your own structured beliefs. The knowledge that your beliefs are not necessarily reality will allow you to be aware of all the data that is consciously available to you. I am not telling you to examine your thoughts so frequently and with such vigor that you get in your own way, but you are not fully conscious unless you are aware of the contents of your conscious mind. I am also emphasizing the fact that the conscious mind is equipped to receive information from the inner self as well as the exterior universe.
quotes.translation: （九点五十分。）通常当你检视自己的意识心时，你总是透过或带着自己结构化的信念去看。知道你的信念未必是实相，会让你觉察到所有意识上可用的资料。我不是要你如此频繁且用力地检视你的思想，以至于妨碍了自己，但除非你觉察到意识心的内容，否则你并非完全有意识。我也在强调一个事实：意识心被设计来接收来自内在自我和外在宇宙的信息。

quotes.text: If there is a gully in your backyard, it will always collect the rain that falls. Your beliefs are like receptive areas -- open basins -- that you use to collect information. Intrusive data will often fall into such basins, taking on their contours, of course. Beliefs are ways of structuring reality. If you overstructure reality, however, then you will end up with a formal mental garden -- whose precise display may be so rigidly structured that the natural aspect of the plants and the flowers is completely obscured. Even your dream information, then, will flow into structured patterns.
quotes.translation: 如果你家后院有条沟渠，它总会积聚落下的雨水。你的信念就像接收区域——敞开的容器——你用它来收集信息。侵入性的数据常常会落入这些容器中，当然也会呈现其轮廓。信念是构建现实的方式。然而，如果你过度构建现实，最终你会得到一个形式化的心灵花园——其精确的展示可能如此僵化，以至于植物和花朵的自然面貌完全被遮蔽。甚至你的梦境信息，也会流入结构化的模式中。

quotes.text: No negative beliefs were thrust upon you, therefore, despite your will. Period. None can be inflicted upon you that you do not consciously accept. In formal hypnosis, the hypnotist and the subject play a game. If the hypnotist orders the subject to forget what happened, that individual will pretend to do so. In that context both hold the belief in the resulting forgetfulness, and it is the power of belief that is being demonstrated. But instead this is taken as an indication that the conscious mind is helpless under such conditions, generally speaking, and this is not the case.
quotes.translation: 因此，没有任何负面信念是违背你的意愿强加给你的。句号。没有一种信念能在你不自觉接受的情况下强加于你。在正式催眠中，催眠师和受试者玩一个游戏。如果催眠师命令受试者忘记所发生的事，那个人会假装这样做。在这种情境下，双方都相信随之而来的遗忘，这正是信念力量的展现。但这通常被误解为意识在催眠状态下无能为力，而事实并非如此。

quotes.text: Your effective power of action follows the lines of your beliefs. To believe in your own weakness is to deny yourself the power of action. To accept uncritically all beliefs that come to you is to open yourself to a barrage of conflicting data at best, in which the clear lines of action and power become blurred. Contradictory demands and assessments are then sent in to the inner self, which by various methods will try to tell you that something is wrong. Beliefs of like nature attract each other, for you are bound to look for consistencies in your behavior and experience.
quotes.translation: 你有效的行动力量遵循你信念的轨迹。相信自己的软弱就是否定自己的行动力量。不加批判地接受所有来到你面前的信念，充其量是让自己暴露在相互冲突的数据轰炸中，其中清晰的行动和力量界限变得模糊。矛盾的要求和评估随后被发送给内在自我，它会通过各种方式试图告诉你出了问题。性质相似的信念会相互吸引，因为你必然会在自己的行为和经验中寻找一致性。

quotes.text: In physical life the soul is clothed in chemicals, and you will use the ingredients you take into your body to form an image that is in line with your beliefs. Some of these ideas will undoubtedly be accepted by you from your culture. Others will be your own private interpretation of yourself in flesh. Your beliefs about any chemical will affect what it does to you. Under LSD therapy you expect a drastic reaction and are told to prepare yourself. Your experience will follow your beliefs and your therapist's, communicated verbally and telepathically.
quotes.translation: 在物质生命中，灵魂披上了化学物质的外衣，你们会利用摄入体内的成分，形成与信念相符的形象。其中一些观念无疑会从你们的文化中接受而来。另一些则是你们对自己肉身存在的私人诠释。你们对任何化学物质的信念都会影响它对你们的作用。在LSD疗法下，你们预期会有剧烈反应，并被要求做好准备。你们的体验将遵循你们自己的信念以及治疗师通过语言和心电感应传达的信念。

quotes.text: Since all is simultaneous, your present beliefs can alter your past ones, whether from this life or a "previous" one. Existences are open-ended. Now with your ideas of progressive time and the resulting beliefs in cause and effect, I realize this is difficult for you to understand. Yet within the abilities of your creaturehood, your current beliefs can change your experience; you can restructure your "reincarnational past" in the same way that you can restructure the past in this present life (as explained in sessions 657-58 in Chapter Fifteen.
quotes.translation: 既然一切同时存在，你当下的信念便能改变过去的信念——无论来自今生或"前世"。存在是开放性的。以你们对线性时间的观念及由此产生的因果信念，我明白这对你们而言难以理解。然而在你们作为生物的能力范围内，当下的信念确实能改变你的体验；你可以重构"转世过去"，正如你能重构今生今世的过去（如第十五章第657-58节所述）。

quotes.text: So as your beliefs change there will be alterations in your experience and behavior, and points of stress, creative stress, while you are learning. Our rich man just mentioned may suddenly realize that his belief is limiting, in that he concentrated upon it exclusively so that money and health became his sole aims. The shattered belief may leave him open to illness, which would seem like a negative experience. Yet through the illness he may be led to areas of perception he had earlier denied, and [he may] be enriched in that particular manner.
quotes.translation: 因此当你的信念改变时，你的体验与行为也会随之改变，在学习过程中会出现压力点——创造性的压力。我们刚提到的富翁可能突然意识到他的信念具有局限性，因为他全神贯注于此，以致金钱与健康成了唯一目标。破碎的信念可能使他暴露于疾病之中，这看似负面体验。然而通过疾病，他或许会被引向先前否认的感知领域，并以那种特殊方式获得丰盈。

quotes.text: Give us a moment, then ... Imagination also plays an important part in your subjective life, as it gives mobility to your beliefs. It is one of the motivating agencies that helps transform your beliefs into physical experience. It is vital therefore that you understand the interrelationship between ideas and imagination. In order to dislodge unsuitable beliefs and establish new ones, you must learn to use your imagination to move concepts in and out of your mind. The proper use of imagination can then propel ideas in the directions you desire.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……想象力在你的主观生活中也扮演着重要角色，因为它赋予你的信念流动性。它是帮助将信念转化为物质体验的激励媒介之一。因此，理解观念与想象力之间的相互关系至关重要。为了摆脱不合适的信念并建立新的信念，你必须学会运用想象力将概念在心灵中移入移出。正确运用想象力便能将观念推向你所期望的方向。

quotes.text: (9:23.) The natural person is to be found, now, not in the past or in the present, but beneath layers and layers of official beliefs, so you are dealing with an archeology of beliefs to find the person who creates beliefs to begin with. As I have said often, evidence of clairvoyance, telepathy, or whatever, are not eccentric, isolated instances occurring in man's experience, but are representative of natural patterns of everyday behavior that become invisible in your world because of the official picture of behavior and reality.
quotes.translation: （9:23。）自然人格现在既不在过去也不在当下，而是存在于层层叠叠的官方信念之下——因此你正在处理一场信念考古学，以寻找最初创造信念的那个人。正如我常说的，千里眼、心灵感应等现象并非人类经验中古怪孤立的特例，而是日常行为自然模式的体现——这些模式因官方对行为与现实的界定而在你们的世界中变得不可见。

quotes.text: Conscious beliefs focus your attention, channel it and direct your energy so that you can swiftly bring the ideas into your physical experience. They also act as blinders, throwing aside data that cannot be assimilated while preserving the integrity of the beliefs. So our Andrea did not see, or ignored, the smiles that came her way, or the encouragement; and in some cases she even perceived some potentially beneficial events as "negative" -- these then were used to further reinforce the belief in her own inferiority.
quotes.translation: 有意识的信念会聚焦你的注意力，引导并指引你的能量，使你能够迅速将想法带入物质体验中。它们也像眼罩一样，将无法同化的数据抛在一旁，同时维护信念的完整性。因此我们的安德莉亚没有看见或忽略了那些朝她而来的微笑或鼓励；在某些情况下，她甚至将一些潜在有益的事件视为"负面"——这些事件随后被用来进一步强化她自卑的信念。

quotes.text: The early acquiescence to beliefs has a biological importance, therefore, but as the conscious mind attains its maturity it is also natural for it to question those beliefs, and to assess them in relation to its own environment. Many of my readers may have certain ideas about good and evil that are very hampering. These may be old beliefs in new clothing. You may think that you are quite free, only to discover that you hold old ideas but have simply put new terms to them, or concentrated upon other aspects.
quotes.translation: 因此，早期对信念的默认具有生物学上的重要性，但随着有意识心智的成熟，质疑这些信念并根据自身环境评估它们也是自然的。我的许多读者可能对善恶持有某些非常束缚人的观念。这些可能是披着新外衣的旧信念。你可能认为自己相当自由，结果却发现你只是给旧观念换上了新术语，或专注于其他方面而已。

quotes.text: Left alone, the self acts spontaneously as a unit, but as an ever-changing one. Listening to voices both within and without, the conscious mind is able to form beliefs that are in league with the self's knowledge as received from material and nonmaterial sources. Then examination of beliefs takes its place along with other activities -- naturally, easily, without effort. Once the conscious mind has accepted a collection of conflicting beliefs, however, a definite attempt is necessary to sort these out.
quotes.translation: 当自我独处时，它自发地作为一个整体行动，但却是不断变化的整体。意识心聆听内外声音，能够形成与自我从物质和非物质来源获得的知识相一致的信念。随后，信念的检视与其他活动自然、轻松、毫不费力地同步进行。然而，一旦意识心接受了相互矛盾的信念集合，就必须刻意努力来厘清这些信念。

quotes.text: I repeat: Your ideas and beliefs form the structure of your experience. Your beliefs and the reasons for them can be found in your conscious mind. If you accept the idea that the reasons for your behavior are forever buried in the past of this life, or any other, then you will not be able to alter your experience until you change that belief. I am speaking now of more or less normal experience. Later we will discuss more particular areas, such as circumstances in which illnesses date from birth.
quotes.translation: 我重申：你的观念和信念构成了你经验的结构。你的信念及其成因可以在你的意识心中找到。如果你接受这样的观点——即你行为的原因永远埋藏在此生或任何其他生命的过去中——那么除非你改变这个信念，否则你将无法改变你的经验。我现在谈论的是大致正常的经验。稍后我们将讨论更具体的领域，例如疾病自出生起就存在的状况。

quotes.text: The people alive within the world come into it with their own problems and challenges, and this will have much to do with the kind of national and worldwide beliefs that are generated and that dominate. The beliefs, of course, are frameworks in which various kinds of experience are tested. This also applies to religions, and political and social situations as well. There is always a give and take between the individual and the mass system of beliefs in which he has chosen his environment.
quotes.translation: 活在世间的人们带着各自的问题和挑战进入这个世界，这与所产生并主导的国家及全球性信念有很大关系。当然，这些信念是检验各种经验的框架。这也适用于宗教、政治和社会情境。个体与他所选择环境中的大众信念体系之间始终存在着互动与平衡。

quotes.text: Now let me give you a brief example of a core belief. It is a blanket belief: human nature is inherently evil. This is a core belief. About it will spring events that only serve to reinforce it. Experiences -- both personal and global -- will come into the perception of a person who holds this belief, that will only serve to deepen it further.
quotes.translation: 现在让我给你们一个核心信念的简短例子。这是一个笼统的信念：人性本恶。这是一个核心信念。围绕它会产生只强化该信念的事件。持有此信念的人——无论个人还是全球层面的经历——都会进入其感知，而这些经历只会进一步加深这个信念。

quotes.text: The organization of your feelings, beliefs, and intents directs the focus about which your physical reality is built. This follows with impeccable spontaneity and order. If you believe in the sinfulness of the world, for instance, then you will search out from normal sense data those facts that confirm your belief. But beyond that, at other levels you also organize your mental world in such a way that you attract to yourself events that -- again -- will confirm your beliefs.
quotes.translation: 你情感、信念与意图的组织方式，决定了构建你物质实相的核心焦点。这以无懈可击的自发性与秩序运作。例如，若你相信世界充满罪孽，便会从寻常感官数据中筛选出证实此信念的事实。但不仅如此，在其他层面上，你也会以某种方式组织你的心灵世界，从而吸引那些——再次——能印证你信念的事件。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Remember, even false beliefs will seem to be justified in terms of physical data, since your experience in the outside world is the materialization of those beliefs. So you must work with the raw material of your ideas, even while your sense data may tell you that a given belief is obviously a truth. To change your experience or any portion of it, then, you must change your ideas. Since you have been forming your own reality all along, the results will follow naturally.
quotes.translation: 请记住，即使是虚假的信念，也会因外在世界的经验而被视为合理，因为你的外在经验正是这些信念的具体化。因此，即使你的感官数据告诉你某个信念显然是真理，你也必须从你思想的原始素材入手。要改变你的经验或其中任何部分，你就必须改变你的观念。既然你一直在构建自己的实相，结果自然会随之而来。

quotes.text: Another more personal core belief: "My life is worthless. What I do is meaningless." Now a person who holds such an idea will ordinarily not recognize it as an invisible belief. Instead he or she may emotionally feel that life has no meaning, that individual action is meaningless, that death is annihilation; and connected to this will be a conglomeration of subsidiary beliefs that deeply affect the family involved, and all those with whom such a person comes in contact.
quotes.translation: 另一个更个人化的核心信念："我的生命毫无价值。我所做的一切毫无意义。"持有这种想法的人通常不会将其视为一个无形的信念。相反，他或她可能在情感上觉得生命没有意义，个人行动毫无意义，死亡就是湮灭；与此相连的是一系列附属信念，这些信念深刻影响着所涉及的家庭，以及所有与这样的人接触的人。

quotes.text: Structured beliefs collect and hold your experience, packaging it, so to speak; and so when you look at a given experience that seems like another, you put it into the same structured package, often without examination. Such beliefs can hold surprises; when you lift up the cover of one you may find that it has served to hide valuable information that did not belong there. An artificial grouping of ideas, like paper flowers, can be collected about a standard core belief.
quotes.translation: 结构化的信念会收集并固着你的经验，可以说是将其打包；因此，当你看到一个看似与以往相似的经验时，你会不加审视地将其放入同一个结构化的包裹中。这样的信念可能暗藏惊喜：当你掀开它的盖子时，可能会发现它掩盖了本不属于那里的宝贵信息。就像纸花一样，围绕一个标准的核心信念，可以聚集起人为的观念组合。

quotes.text: This was an entirely different kind of art than you have now. It was an attempt to objectify inner reality as it was perceived through a certain belief system. Whether the artist disagreed with certain issues or not, the belief system was there as an invisible framework. That intense focus that united belief systems, that tension between a sensed subjective world and the physical one, and the rarity of images to be found elsewhere, brought art into that great flowering.
quotes.translation: 这是一种与你现今所拥有的截然不同的艺术形式。它试图通过某种信念体系所感知的内在实相加以具象化。无论艺术家是否认同某些议题，这个信念体系始终作为无形的框架存在。那种凝聚信念体系的强烈专注力、感知的主观世界与物质世界之间的张力，以及在其他地方难得一见的意象的稀有性，共同将艺术推向了那个伟大的繁盛期。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: (10:35.) There will be no mystery. You know what your own beliefs are. You will see the groupings, but it is up to you to look inside your own mind and to use the images in your own way. Throw out ideas that do not suit you. If you read this, find such an idea in yourself and then say, "l cannot throw this idea away," then you must realize that your inner remark is in itself a belief. You can indeed throw the idea away, the second one, as easily as the first.
quotes.translation: （10:35）将不再有任何神秘。你清楚自己拥有哪些信念。你会看到这些信念的归类，但关键在于你要向内审视自己的心灵，并以自己的方式运用这些意象。舍弃那些不适合你的想法。如果你读到此处，在自己心中发现某个想法，然后说："我无法舍弃这个想法"，那么你必须明白，你内心的这句评语本身就是一个信念。你确实可以像舍弃第一个想法那样轻易地舍弃第二个想法。

quotes.text: If such information is given to you by another, by a psychic, for example, that individual is also very apt to pick up those "lives" that make sense to you now, and -- unconsciously of course -- to structure them precisely along the lines of your beliefs. This may not seem obvious. (Emphatically:) If an individual believes that he is basically unworthy he will recall, or be given, those lives that justify that idea. If he thinks he must pay for his sins now, then that belief will attract memory of those lives that will reinforce it; this will be highly organized recall, leaving out everything that does not apply.
quotes.translation: 如果这类信息是由他人——例如通灵者——提供给你的，那么这个人也很容易捕捉到那些对你当下有意义的"前世"，并且——当然是无意识地——会严格按照你信念的脉络来构建它们。这或许并不明显。（强调地：）如果一个人相信自己本质上毫无价值，那么他将会回忆起（或被赋予）那些能佐证这个想法的前世。如果他认为自己必须为现在的罪孽付出代价，那么这个信念就会吸引那些能强化此念的前世记忆；这将是一种高度组织化的回忆，会排除所有不相关的内容。

quotes.text: At the same time all beliefs are communicated to others, not only through quite unconscious bodily mechanisms, but telepathically. You will always try to correlate your ideas with exterior experience. (Pause.) All of the abilities of the inner self will be brought to bear to materialize the image of your beliefs, regardless of what they ought to be. The "proper" emotions will be generated, bringing about those body states that exist in your conscious mind.
quotes.translation: 同时，所有信念不仅通过完全无意识的身体机制，还通过心电感应传达给他人。你总会试图将自己的想法与外在经验关联起来。（停顿。）内在自我的所有能力都将被调动起来，以具现你信念的影像——无论这些信念"应该"是什么。相应的"恰当"情绪将被激发，从而引发你意识心智中存在的那些身体状态。

quotes.text: (10:59.) Love is a biological as well as a spiritual characteristic. Basically, love and creativity are synonymous. Love exists without an object. It is the impetus by which all being becomes manifest. Desire, love, intent, belief and purpose -- these form the experience of your body and all the events it perceives. You cannot change one belief but it alters your body experience. The great give-and-take between biological and psychological integrity occurs constantly. Your thoughts are as active as your cells, and as important in maintaining your physical being.
quotes.translation: （10:59。）爱既是生物性特质，也是灵性特质。从根本上说，爱与创造力是同义词。爱无需对象而存在，它是万有得以显化的原动力。渴望、爱、意图、信念与目的——这些构成了你身体的体验及其所感知的一切事件。你无法改变一个信念而不改变你的身体体验。生物完整性与心理完整性之间持续进行着伟大的互动。你的思想与你的细胞同样活跃，在维持你的物质存在方面同等重要。

quotes.text: In that previous book I discussed the ways in which you form your private experience through your beliefs. You have certain pet ideas, therefore, and you use them to structure your own world view of the reality you know. It is important that you understand what your own beliefs are. Many of them might work quite well "at home," but when you begin to journey away from that home station you may find that those same ideas impede your progress.
quotes.translation: 在上一本书中，我讨论了你们如何通过信念塑造个人经验。因此你们持有某些偏爱的想法，并用它们构建自己所知实相的世界观。理解自己的信念至关重要。其中许多信念在"家里"可能运作良好，但当你开始远离那个家园驿站时，可能会发现这些同样的想法阻碍了你的进展。

quotes.text: The doctor is like a biological mechanic, who knows your body far better than you. Now these medical beliefs are intertwined with your economic and cultural structures, so you cannot lay the blame upon medical men or their profession alone. Your economic well-being is also a part of your personal reality. Many dedicated doctors use medical technology with spiritual understanding, and they are themselves the victims of the beliefs they hold.
quotes.translation: 医生就像生物机械师，对你的身体了解远胜于你。这些医学信念与你的经济文化结构交织在一起，因此你不能将责任完全归咎于医生或医疗行业本身。你的经济福祉也是你个人实相的一部分。许多有奉献精神的医生以灵性理解运用医疗技术，但他们自身也是所持信念的受害者。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: If your conscious beliefs are causing you great distress, countering beneficial beliefs may be received from this source. Your being, the greater consciousness that is yourself, intersects with space and time; it is born in flesh simultaneously at many [moment] "points." (See the 668th session in Chapter Nineteen.) You would call each of these immersions into three-dimensional existence a life, with its own self. And you are one of these.
quotes.translation: 若你的有意识信念正造成极大痛苦，便可能从此源头接收到有益的信念。你的本体——那个更大的意识即你自己——与时空相交；它同时在许多"瞬间"点以肉身诞生（见第十九章第668节）。你会将每次沉浸于三次元存在称为"一生"，各有其自体。而你是其中一员。

quotes.text: The episode also mirrored his beliefs, for to his way of thinking he would have to relinquish certain freedoms, and this he was not ready to do. The events basically exist at once, though at your level you have to perceive them in time. As your intimate daily reality can be involved with and colored by probabilities, brought into your experience by your own desires and beliefs, so is your mass culture, world history, and species orientation colored by probable events that do not fit into your officially recognized idea of physical reality.
quotes.translation: 这个事件也映照出他的信念，因为按他的思维方式，他必须放弃某些自由，而他尚未准备好这么做。这些事件本质上同时存在，尽管在你们的层面必须依时间顺序感知。正如你亲密的日常实相可能被或然事件渗透与着色，并由你自身的欲望与信念带入经验，你们的群众文化、世界历史与物种取向同样被那些不符合你们官方认可的物理实相概念的或然事件所着色。

quotes.text: (10:19.) Without this experience of following such a belief in the church so fervently, however, he would not understand the need of people for such beliefs, or be able to reach them as well as he does. His questioning mind was exercised originally as he began to examine religious beliefs. He was afraid that psychic experience, when he encountered it much later, might lead to a new dogma, and was determined not to use it in such a way.
quotes.translation: （10点19分）然而，若非如此热切地追随教堂中的这一信念，他便无法理解人们对这类信念的需求，也无法像现在这样有效地触及他们。他那善于质疑的心智最初正是在审视宗教信仰时得到锻炼的。后来当他遇到通灵体验时，他担心这可能会导向新的教条，并决心不以这种方式使用它。

quotes.text: Other personal beliefs held by both of you to some extent or another, with the usual cultural connotations, stressed a false humility over the rightful natural pride of ability. Such beliefs are often given to children, appearing in such forms as, "don't be a showoff," "don't be an exhibitionist," followed by, again, the dire warning that your fellow creatures suspect any neighbor who is different or who shows any superior abilities.
quotes.translation: 你们两人在不同程度上持有的其他个人信念，带着通常的文化内涵，强调了一种虚假的谦卑，压制了能力应有的自然自豪感。这类信念常被灌输给孩子，以诸如“别出风头”、“别炫耀”等形式出现，接着又是可怕的警告：你的同胞们会怀疑任何与众不同或展现出卓越能力的邻居。

quotes.text: Your own value system then is built up of your beliefs about reality, and those beliefs form your experience. Suppose you believe that to be "good" you must try to be perfect. You may have been told, or read, that the spirit is perfect, and hence thought that your duty was to reproduce that perfect spirit in flesh as best you could. To this end you attempt to deny all imperfect thoughts and emotions. Your own "negative" thoughts appall you. You may believe also what I have told you -- that your thoughts create your reality -- so you become all the more frightened at mental or actual expressions of an aggressive nature. You may be so concerned about hurting someone else that you hardly dare move. Trying to be perfect all the time can be far more than a nuisance: It can be disastrous because of your misunderstanding.
quotes.translation: 你的价值体系是由你对实相的各种信念所构建的，而这些信念又塑造了你的经历。假设你相信要成为"好人"就必须力求完美。你可能被告知或读到过，灵性是完美的，因此认为自己的职责就是尽可能在肉身中重现那完美的灵性。为此你试图否认所有不完美的思想和情绪。你自己的"负面"想法令你惊骇。你可能也相信我曾告诉你的——你的思想创造你的实相——于是你更加害怕任何攻击性本质的心理或实际表现。你或许如此担心伤害他人，以至于几乎不敢轻举妄动。试图时时刻刻保持完美远非麻烦那么简单：由于你的误解，这可能是灾难性的。

quotes.text: Each condition is as real or unreal as the other. Which you? Which world? You have your choice, broadly, within certain frameworks that you have chosen as a part of your creaturehood. The past as you think of it, and the subconscious, again as you think of it, have little to do with your present experience outside of your beliefs about them. The past contains for each of you some moments of joy, strength, creativity and splendor, as well as episodes of unhappiness, despair perhaps, turmoil and cruelty. Your present convictions will act like a magnet, activating all such past issues, happy or sad. You will choose from your previous experience all of those events that reinforce your conscious beliefs, and so ignore those that do not; the latter may even seem to be nonexistent.
quotes.translation: 每一种状况都与其他状况同样真实或不真实。你是哪一个？世界是哪一个？在你作为生物体所选择的特定框架内，你大体上拥有选择权。你所认为的过去，以及你所认为的潜意识，除了你对它们的信念之外，与你当下的经历几乎无关。过去对你们每个人而言，既包含欢乐、力量、创造力与辉煌的时刻，也包含不快乐、或许绝望、动荡与残酷的片段。你当下的信念会像磁铁一样，激活所有那些或喜或悲的过往事件。你会从过往经历中挑选出所有强化你意识信念的事件，而忽略那些不符合的；后者甚至可能显得不存在。

quotes.text: Exterior conditions can always be changed if you understand the principles of which I am speaking. Diseases can be eliminated, even those that seem fatal -- but only if the beliefs behind them are erased or altered enough so that their specific focusing effect upon the body is sufficiently released. The present as you think of it, and in practical working terms, is that point at which you select your physical experience from all those events that could be materialized. Your physical circumstances change automatically as your beliefs do. As your knowledge grows, so your experience becomes more fulfilling. This does not necessarily mean that it evens out in any way, or that there are not peaks and valleys. Each aspiration presupposes the admission of a lack, each challenge presupposes a barrier to be overcome. The more adventurous will often choose greater challenges, and so in their minds the contrasts between what they want to achieve and their present status can seem to be impossible.
quotes.translation: 如果你理解我所说的这些原则，外在状况总是可以改变的。疾病可以被消除，甚至那些看似致命的疾病——但前提是它们背后的信念被抹除或充分改变，以至于它们对身体的具体聚焦效应得到充分释放。你所认为的当下，在实际运作层面，是你从所有可能被物质化的事件中挑选物质体验的那个点。你的物质环境会随着你的信念自动改变。随着你的知识增长，你的体验也会变得更加充实。这并不一定意味着它会以任何方式变得平坦，或者不再有高峰与低谷。每一个渴望都预设了对匮乏的承认，每一个挑战都预设了需要克服的障碍。越具冒险精神的人往往选择越大的挑战，因此在他们的心中，想要达成的目标与当下状态之间的反差可能看似无法逾越。

quotes.text: You do not understand the communications between your selves and pets, for example, where in their own way they interpret and react to your beliefs.* They mirror your ideas, then, and so become vulnerable as they would not be in their natural circumstances. In greater terms their relationship with you is natural, of course, but their innate realization that the creature's point of power is in the present is to some degree undermined by their own receptivity and translation of your beliefs. A young kitten is treated differently than an older one. The cat responds to such conditioning. In the same way your own conclusions about age become fact in your experience. In line with them, if you could convince yourself that you were ten years younger, or ten years older, then it would be faithfully reflected in your personal environment.
quotes.translation: 举例来说，你并不了解自己与宠物之间的沟通——它们以自己的方式诠释并回应你的信念。于是它们映照出你的观念，因而变得脆弱，而这在自然环境中本不会发生。从更宏观的角度看，它们与你的关系自然是恰当的，但动物本能认知到"力量之点在于当下"这一事实，却因它们自身接收并转译你的信念而受到某种程度的削弱。人们对幼猫与老猫的态度截然不同，猫也会对这种制约做出反应。同理，你对自己年龄的结论会在经验中成为事实。若你能说服自己年轻十岁或年长十岁，这改变便会忠实地反映在你的个人环境中。

related_concepts.name_en: The point of power is in the present

quotes.text: Unknowingly, the sick often give up their power to act in a healthy manner to the physicians. The doctors accept this mandate since they share the same framework of belief, so the medical profession obviously needs patients as badly as the ill need the hospitals. Society as you know it, not understanding the nature of normal aggression, considers it violent. The prisons and law enforcement agencies need criminals in the same way that criminals need them, for they operate within the same system of belief. Each accepts violence as a method of behavior and survival. (Pause.) If you do not understand that you create your own reality, then you may assign all good results to a personified god, and need the existence of a devil to explain the undesirable reality. So churches as they now exist in Western society need a devil as well as a god.
quotes.translation: 病人常在不自觉中将健康行动的力量让渡给医生。医生接受这份委托，因他们共享相同的信念架构——因此医疗行业显然需要病人，正如病人需要医院。你所认知的社会，因不理解正常攻击性的本质，将其视为暴力。监狱与执法机构需要罪犯，正如罪犯需要它们，因两者运作于同一信念体系内，双方都将暴力视为行为与生存的方式。（停顿）若你不明白自己创造自身实相，便会将所有美好结果归于人格化的神，并需要魔鬼的存在来解释不理想的实相。因此，西方社会现存的教会既需要神，也需要魔鬼。

quotes.text: The use of your private energy brings you into intimate relationship with your own source of power. Healing involves great natural aggressive thrusts of energy, growth, and the focus of vitality. The more powerless you feel, the less able you are to utilize your own healing abilities. You are then forced to project these outward upon a physician, a healer, or any outside agency. If your own belief in the physician "works" and you are cured of symptoms, you are physically relieved, and yet your own belief in yourself may be further infringed upon. If you are making no effective efforts to handle your own problems, then the symptoms will simply reappear in a new fashion, and the same process will be reinitiated. You may lose faith in your doctor while still retaining confidence in doctors as a whole, and run from one to another.
quotes.translation: 运用你个人的能量，会使你与自身的力量之源建立亲密关系。疗愈涉及能量、成长与生命力的巨大自然攻击性迸发。你越感到无力，就越难运用自身的疗愈能力。于是你被迫将这些能力投射到医生、疗愈师或任何外在机构上。若你对医生的信念"奏效"而症状痊愈，身体虽获缓解，但你对自身的信念可能进一步受损。若你未有效处理自身问题，症状便会以新形式重现，相同过程将重新启动。你或许会对某位医生失去信心，却仍整体信任医生群体，于是辗转于不同医生之间。

quotes.text: (Ironically, Charles Darwin's natural selection, "the survival of the fittest," [a phrase that Darwin himself did not originate, by the way], allows for all sorts of pain and suffering in the process -- the same unhappy facts of life, in Darwin's view, that finally turned him into an agnostic, away from a God who could allow such things to exist! As I interpret what I've read, Darwin didn't deny the existence of a god of some kind, but he wanted one that would abolish what he saw as the "upward" struggle for existence. According to the geological/fossil record, this conflict had resulted in the deaths of entire species. Darwin came to believe that he asked the impossible of God. Instead, he assigned the pain and suffering in the world to the impersonal workings of natural selection and chance variation [or genetic mutation]. For Darwin and his followers -- even those of today, then -- nature's effects gave the appearance of design or plan in the universe without necessitating a belief in a designer or a god; although, as I wrote in Note 7, from the scientific standpoint this belief leaves untouched the question of design in nonliving matter, which is vastly more abundant in the "objective" universe than is living matter, and had to precede that living matter.
quotes.translation: （讽刺的是，查尔斯·达尔文的自然选择理论"适者生存"——顺便说一句，这个短语并非达尔文原创——允许过程中存在各种痛苦和磨难；在达尔文看来，正是这些不愉快的生活事实最终使他成为不可知论者，远离了那个允许此类事物存在的上帝！根据我对所读内容的理解，达尔文并未否认某种上帝的存在，但他希望有一个能消除他所见的"向上"生存斗争的上帝。根据地质/化石记录，这种冲突已导致整个物种灭绝。达尔文逐渐相信他向上帝要求了不可能之事。于是，他将世间的痛苦与磨难归因于自然选择和随机变异（或基因突变）的非人格运作。对达尔文及其追随者——甚至包括今天的追随者——而言，自然界的效应呈现出宇宙中有设计或计划的表象，却无需相信存在设计者或上帝；不过，正如我在注释7中所写，从科学角度看，这个信念并未触及非生命物质中的设计问题，而"客观"宇宙中非生命物质远比生命物质丰富得多，且必须先于生命物质存在。

quotes.text: 1. Over the years, my outside reading on evolution has covered many often conflicting viewpoints. Whether their beliefs are rooted in the tenets of conventional biology (Darwinism), for instance, or allied with those of the creationists (who hold that God made the earth and all of its creatures, just as described in the Book of Genesis), the advocates of rival theories have impressed me as having at least one thing in common: No matter how violently they may disagree, their arguments lack all sense of humor. This is serious stuff, world! Whatever happened to the spontaneity and joy in life? For surely, I found myself thinking as I read all of those antagonistic ideas, spontaneity and joy were the very ingredients that Seth would place uppermost in any theory or scheme of life's "beginnings," regardless of its philosophical stance.
quotes.translation: 1. 多年来，我对进化论的外部阅读涵盖了众多经常相互矛盾的观点。无论他们的信念植根于传统生物学（达尔文主义）的教条，还是与神创论者（认为上帝创造了地球及其所有生物，正如《创世记》所述）的观点结盟，这些对立理论的倡导者给我留下的印象是至少有一个共同点：无论他们如何激烈地争执，其论点都缺乏幽默感。这可是严肃的事情，世界啊！生命中的自发性和喜悦都去哪儿了？因为，当我阅读所有这些对立观点时，我发现自己不禁想到，无论哲学立场如何，自发性和喜悦正是赛斯会置于任何关于生命"起源"的理论或方案中最核心的元素。

quotes.text: Puberty comes at a certain time, triggered by deep mechanisms that are related to the state of the natural world, the condition of the species, and those cultural beliefs that in a certain sense you transpose upon the natural world. In other respects, your cultural environment is of course natural. The time that puberty comes varies, then, and afterwards it is possible to parent a child. A time then comes when the period is over. During what is called the sexually active time; the larger dimensions of personhood become strictly narrowed into sexually stereotyped roles -- and all aspects of identity that do not fit are ignored or denied. The fact is that few people fit those roles. They are largely the result of the interpretations of religion as conventionally understood. And the scientists, for all their seeming independence, often simply found new intellectually acceptable reasons for unconsciously held emotional beliefs.
quotes.translation: 青春期在特定时刻到来，由深层机制触发，这些机制与自然世界的状态、物种的状况以及你在某种意义上投射到自然世界上的文化信念相关。在其他方面，你的文化环境当然是自然的。因此，青春期到来的时间会有所不同，之后便可能生育子女。然后，当这段时期结束时，便进入所谓的性活跃期；人格的更大维度被严格压缩成性别刻板角色——所有不符合这些角色的身份面向都被忽视或否认。事实上，很少有人真正符合这些角色。它们很大程度上是传统理解的宗教诠释的结果。而科学家们，尽管看似独立，却常常只是为潜意识中持有的情感信念找到了新的、在智识上可接受的理由。

quotes.text: Any true psychic development of personality, however, is bound to lead to an understanding of the nature of the psyche that is far too large for any such confusion of basic identity with sexuality. The concept of reincarnation itself clearly shows the change of sexual orientation, and the existence of a self that is apart from its sexual orientation, even while it is also expressed through a given sexual stance. To a good extent, sexual beliefs are responsible for the blocking-out of reincarnational awareness. Such "memory" would necessarily acquaint you with experiences most difficult to correlate with your current sexual roles. Those other-sex existences are present to the psyche unconsciously. They are a portion of your personality. In so specifically identifying with your sex, therefore, you also inhibit memories that might limit or destroy that identification.
quotes.translation: 然而，任何真正的人格灵性发展，都必然导向对心灵本质的理解，这种理解远非任何将基本身份与性混淆所能涵盖。轮回概念本身清楚地显示了性取向的变化，以及一个独立于性取向的自我的存在，即使它同时通过特定的性立场来表达。在很大程度上，性信念是阻碍轮回觉知的原因。这种“记忆”必然会让你了解那些与你当前性别角色最难关联的经历。那些异性的存在在潜意识中呈现于心灵。它们是你人格的一部分。因此，当你如此具体地认同你的性别时，你也抑制了可能限制或摧毁这种认同的记忆。

quotes.text: Those who imagine they look upon nature with the most objective of eyes are those whose subjective beliefs blind them most of all, for they cannot see through their own misinterpretations. It has been said that statistics can be made to say two things at once, both contradictory; so the facts of nature can be read in completely different fashions as they are put together with the organizational abilities of the mind operating through the brain's beliefs. The exterior core of dreams is also blemished to that degree, but the inner core of dreams provides a constant new influx of material, feedback, and insight from the psyche, so that the personality is not at the mercy of its exterior experience only -- not confined to environmental feedback only, but ever provided with fresh intuitive data and direction.
quotes.translation: 那些自以为用最客观眼光看待自然的人，正是其主观信念最蒙蔽他们的人，因为他们无法看穿自己的误解。据说统计数据可以同时表达两件矛盾的事情；同样，自然的真相也可以以完全不同的方式被解读，这取决于通过大脑信念运作的心灵组织能力。梦境的外在核心也因此在某种程度上被玷污，但梦境的内在核心不断提供来自心灵的新材料、反馈和洞见，使人格不至于仅受外在经验的摆布——不局限于环境反馈，而是始终获得新鲜的直觉数据和方向。

quotes.text: Many of my readers are familiar with private meditation, when concentration is focused in one particular area. There are many methods and schools of thought here, but a highly suggestive state of mind results, in which spiritual, mental, and physical goals are sought. It is impossible to meditate without a goal, for that intent is itself a purpose. Unfortunately, many of your public health programs, and commercial statements through the various media, provide you with mass meditations of a most deplorable kind. I refer to those in which the specific symptoms of various diseases are given, in which the individual is further told to examine the body with those symptoms in mind. I also refer to those statements that just as unfortunately specify diseases for which the individual may experience no symptoms of an observable kind, but is cautioned that these disastrous physical events may be happening despite his or her feelings of good health. Here the generalized fears fostered by religious, scientific, and cultural beliefs are often given as blueprints of diseases in which a person can find a specific focus -- the individual can say: "Of course, I feel listless, or panicky, or unsafe since I have such-and-such a disease."
quotes.translation: 我的许多读者熟悉私人冥想，即注意力集中于某一特定领域。这里有许多方法和思想流派，但结果会产生一种高度暗示性的心境，在其中寻求灵性、心智和身体的目标。没有目标就无法冥想，因为那个意图本身就是一种目的。不幸的是，你们许多公共卫生项目以及通过各种媒体发布的商业声明，为你们提供了最可悲的集体冥想。我指的是那些列出各种疾病具体症状的冥想，其中进一步告诉个体要带着这些症状检查身体。我还指那些同样不幸的声明，它们指定了可能没有明显症状的疾病，但警告说，尽管个体感觉健康，这些灾难性的身体事件可能正在发生。在这里，由宗教、科学和文化信念培养出的普遍恐惧，常被作为疾病的蓝图，个体可以在其中找到具体的焦点——个体可以说：“当然，我感到无精打采、恐慌或不安，因为我患了某某疾病。”
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: If you do not buy headache potions, your uncle or your neighbor may be out of business and not able to support his family, and therefore lack the means to buy your wares. You cannot disconnect one area of life from another. En masse, your private beliefs form your cultural reality. Your society is not a thing in itself apart from you, but the result of the individual beliefs of each person in it. There is no stratum of society that you do not in one way or another affect. Your religions stress sin. Your medical profession stresses disease. Your orderly sciences stress the chaotic and accidental theories of creation. Your psychologies stress men as victims of their backgrounds. Your most advanced thinkers emphasize man's rape of the planet, or focus upon the future disaster that will overtake the world, or see men once again as victims of the stars.
quotes.translation: 如果你不购买头痛药水，你的叔叔或邻居可能会失业，无法养家糊口，也就没有能力购买你的商品。你不能将生活的某个领域与其他领域割裂开来。整体而言，你个人的信念形成了你的文化实相。你的社会并非独立于你之外的存在，而是其中每个个体信念的结果。没有一个社会阶层是你以某种方式无法影响的。你们的宗教强调罪孽，你们的医学强调疾病，你们有序的科学强调混乱和偶然的创造理论，你们的心理学将人视为其背景的受害者，你们最先进的思想家强调人类对地球的强暴，或聚焦于将降临世界的未来灾难，或再次将人视为星辰的受害者。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: The communication between the inner and outer egos should obviously be as clear and open as possible. As a general rule, the inner ego depends upon your assessment of physical events. Your involvement in the private aspects of your living, and your participation in mass events, has much to do with your estimation of the physical situation, and with your beliefs and desires regarding it. Give us a moment ... A very simple example (colon): If you want to write a letter you do so. There is no conflict between your desires, beliefs, and the execution of the act, so the action itself flows smoothly. If for some reason or another, through a poor assessment of your reality, you believe that such an act is dangerous, then you will hamper the flow between the desire and the execution. The flow of creativity begun by the inner ego will be impeded.
quotes.translation: 内在自我与外在自我之间的沟通显然应尽可能清晰开放。一般而言，内在自我依赖于你对物质事件的评估。你参与生活的私人层面，以及你对大众事件的参与，与你对物质情境的评估，以及你对此的信念和欲望密切相关。稍等一下……一个非常简单的例子（冒号）：如果你想写一封信，你就会去做。你的欲望、信念与行动的执行之间没有冲突，因此行动本身流畅进行。如果出于某种原因，通过对你实相的错误评估，你相信这样的行动是危险的，那么你就会阻碍欲望与执行之间的流动。内在自我启动的创造力之流将受到阻碍。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: The parents have worked to give their children such advantages, and the parents themselves are somewhat confused by their children's attitudes. The money and position, however, have often been attained as a result of the belief in man's competitive nature -- and that belief itself erodes the very prizes it produces: The fruit is bitter in the mouth. Many of the parents believed, quite simply, that the purpose of life was to make more money. Virtue consisted of the best car, or house or swimming pool -- proof that one could survive in a tooth-and-claw world. But the children wondered: What about those other feelings that stirred in their consciousnesses? What about those purposes they sensed? The hearts of some of them were like vacuums, waiting to be filled. They looked for values, but at the same time they felt that they were themselves sons and daughters of a species tainted, at loose ends, with no clear destinations.
quotes.translation: 父母们努力给予子女这些优势，而他们自己却对子女的态度感到困惑。然而，金钱与地位往往源于对人类竞争本性的信念——而这种信念本身侵蚀了它所创造的奖赏：果实入口苦涩。许多父母单纯地相信，生命的目的就是赚更多钱。美德体现在最好的汽车、房屋或游泳池上——证明一个人能在弱肉强食的世界中生存。但子女们疑惑：那些在意识中翻涌的其他感受呢？他们感知到的那些目的呢？其中一些人的心灵如同真空，等待被填满。他们寻找价值，却同时觉得自己是受玷污种族的儿女，漫无目的，没有明确方向。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Women make a grave error when they try to prove their "equality" with men by showing that they can enter the armed forces, or go into combat as well as any man (with more amusement). War always makes you less as a species than you could be. Women have shown uncommon good sense in not going to war, and uncommon bad sense by sending their sons and lovers to war. Again: To kill for the sake of peace only makes you better killers, and nothing will change that. In any war, both sides are fanatical to the extent that they are involved. I am quite aware that often war seems to be your only practical course, because of the set of beliefs that are, relatively speaking, worldwide. Until you change those beliefs, war will seem to have some practical value -- a value which is highly deceptive, and quite false.
quotes.translation: 当女性试图通过证明自己能像任何男性一样参军或参战（甚至更乐在其中）来证明与男性"平等"时，她们犯下了严重错误。战争永远使物种低于其应有水准。女性不参战展现了非凡的明智，而送儿子与爱人上战场则展现了非凡的愚蠢。再说一次：为和平而杀戮只会让你成为更优秀的杀手，这一点永远不会改变。在任何战争中，双方都因卷入程度而变得狂热。我很清楚，由于相对而言全球性的信念体系，战争常常看似你们唯一可行的途径。除非你们改变这些信念，否则战争似乎仍具有某种实用价值——这种价值极具欺骗性，且完全虚假。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: (Louder, when I asked Seth to repeat a phrase:) I believe that man runs the mile much quicker now (by about 12 seconds) than he did, say, thirty years ago. Has the body's effective speed suddenly quickened? Hardly. Instead, mental beliefs about the body's performance have changed, and increased physical speed resulted. The body can indeed run faster than the current record (of 3:49). I merely want to show the effect of beliefs upon physical performance. All people do not want to be expert runners, however. Their creativity and their ideals may lie in quite different fields of endeavor, but individual performance always adds to the knowledge of the species. Good, better, best. Is it bad to be a poor runner? Of course not, unless running is your own particular avocation. And if it is, you improve with practice.
quotes.translation: （当我请赛斯重复一句话时，声音更响亮地：）我相信人类现在跑一英里的速度比三十年前快得多（大约快12秒）。难道身体的有效速度突然加快了吗？几乎不可能。相反，关于身体表现的信念改变了，从而导致了身体速度的提升。身体确实能跑得比当前纪录（3分49秒）更快。我只是想展示信念对身体表现的影响。然而，并非所有人都想成为专业跑者。他们的创造力和理想可能存在于完全不同的努力领域，但个人的表现总是会增进物种的知识。好、更好、最好。跑得差就不好吗？当然不是，除非跑步是你个人的特别爱好。如果是的话，你会通过练习而进步。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
