quotes.text: I have no idea, of course, what physical state I'd be in if the Seth phenomenon hadn't appeared in my life (in late 1963), or if I hadn't had those sessions to rely upon. And even in the most private-type sessions Seth always wound his material into more public areas, so that we have reams of unpublished (and very controversial) material dealing with the connections between one's illness and other members of the family, community relationships, and with the very belief systems that underlie all of human activity. The kinds of beliefs we have about people bring about the kinds of illnesses we encounter. That is certainly one of Seth's clearest messages. The individual is always in a state of change. To name and dignify a group of symptoms only brings them further into prominence, and offers them another framework for permanency.
quotes.translation: 我当然不知道，如果赛斯现象没有出现在我的生命中（1963年底），或者如果我没有那些课程可以依赖，我会处于怎样的身体状态。即使在最私密类型的课程中，赛斯也总是将他的资料编织进更公开的领域，因此我们有大量未出版（且极具争议性）的资料，涉及一个人的疾病与家庭其他成员、社区关系之间的联系，以及与所有人类活动基础的那些信念系统。我们对人们持有的信念种类会导致我们遭遇的疾病种类。这无疑是赛斯最清晰的信息之一。个体始终处于变化状态。给一组症状命名并赋予其重要性，只会使它们更加突出，并为它们提供另一个永久性的框架。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: There are as many kinds of suffering as there are kinds of joy, and there is no one simple answer that can be given. As human creatures you accept the conditions of life. You create from those conditions the experiences of your days. You are born into belief systems as you are born into physical centuries, and part of the entire picture is the freedom of interpreting the experiences of life in multitudinous fashions (all intently). The meaning, nature, dignity or shame of suffering will be interpreted according to your systems of belief. I hope to give you along the way a picture of reality that puts suffering in its proper perspective, but it is a most difficult subject to cover because it touches most deeply upon your hopes for yourselves and for mankind, and your fears for yourselves and for mankind.
quotes.translation: 痛苦与喜悦的种类一样繁多，没有单一的简单答案可以给出。作为人类，你们接受生命的条件。你们从这些条件中创造出你们日复一日的经历。你们诞生于信念体系之中，正如你们诞生于物理世纪之中，而整个图景的一部分，便是以无数种方式诠释生命经历的自由（全神贯注地）。痛苦的意义、本质、尊严或耻辱，将根据你们的信念体系被诠释。我希望在过程中为你们呈现一幅现实的图景，将痛苦置于恰当的视角中，但这是一个极难涵盖的主题，因为它最深刻地触及你们对自身及人类的希望，以及你们对自身及人类的恐惧。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: (10:05.) Give us a moment.... Man always does best, or his best, when he sees himself in heroic terms. While the Roman Catholic Church gave him a powerful, cohesive belief system (pause), for many reasons those beliefs shifted so that the division between man and God became too great. (Pause.) Man the sinner took over from man the child of God. As a result, one you see in art particularly, man became a heroic figure, then a natural one. (Pause.) The curiosity that had been directed toward divinity became directed toward nature. Man's sense of inquiry led him, then, to begin to paint more natural portraits and images. He turned to landscapes also. This was an inevitable process. As it occurred, however, [man] began to make great distinctions between the world of the imagination and the world of nature, until finally he became convinced that the physical world was real and the imaginative world was not. So his paintings became more and more realistic.
quotes.translation: （10:05分。）给我们一点时间……当人类以英雄般的视角看待自己时，他总是做得最好，或尽其所能。虽然罗马天主教会曾给予他一个强大而凝聚的信念体系（停顿），但由于诸多原因，那些信念发生了转变，以至于人与神之间的鸿沟变得过于巨大。（停顿。）作为罪人的人类取代了作为神之子的人类。结果，你们尤其在艺术中看到，人类先成为英雄形象，随后又成为自然形象。（停顿。）原本指向神性的好奇心转向了自然。人类的好奇心引导他开始描绘更自然的肖像与图像。他也转向了风景画。这是一个不可避免的过程。然而，随着这一过程的发生，（人类）开始在想象世界与自然世界之间做出巨大区分，直到最终他确信物理世界是真实的，而想象世界则不然。因此，他的画作变得越来越写实。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: "The dream representing his grandfather symbolically allowed him to go back to the past in this life, to a time of severe shock--his grandfather's death--which occurred when (Ruburt, at age 19) was beginning to substitute scientific belief for religious belief, wondering if his grandfather's consciousness then fell back into a mindless state of being, into chaos, as science would certainly seem to suggest. In the dream his grandfather survives. His grandfather survived in a suit that was too large, which means that there was still room for him to grow. Ruburt had a small experience of hearing a voice speak in his mind (yesterday)--a voice of comfort, all he remembered of quite legitimate assistance he received from other personalities connected with the French life, that came as a result of the French dream.
quotes.translation: 象征祖父的梦境让他得以回溯此生中一段剧烈冲击的过往——祖父的去世——当时（鲁柏19岁）正开始用科学信念取代宗教信念，怀疑祖父的意识是否如科学似乎暗示的那样，坠入无意识的存在状态，陷入混沌。梦中祖父存活了下来。他穿着一件过于宽大的西装存活，这意味着他仍有成长的空间。鲁柏曾有过一次微小体验（昨日），听见脑海中有个声音说话——那是安慰之声，是他从与法国人生相关的其他人格那里获得的合理协助中唯一记得的部分，这源于那个法国梦境。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: The people count upon the government to realistically define the conditions of the world, to have proper intelligence so that the activities in foreign lands are known, to keep up proper communication with other governments, and so forth. Now in some important respects the reasoning mind is like the government in this analogy. If the people in power are paranoid, then they overestimate the dangers of any given world situation. They overreact, or overmobilize, using a disproportionate amount of energy and time for defense, and taking energies away from other projects. The reasoning mind acts in the same fashion when paranoid beliefs are in power. It therefore tells all of the citizens -- or cells of the body -- to mobilize for action, to be on the alert, to pare down all but necessary activities, and so forth.
quotes.translation: 人们依赖政府来现实地定义世界状况，拥有适当情报以了解海外活动，与其他政府保持恰当沟通等等。在若干重要方面，理性心智就像这个比喻中的政府。若当权者偏执多疑，便会高估任何世界局势的危险性。他们反应过度或过度动员，耗费不成比例的能量与时间用于防御，从其他计划中抽走能量。当偏执信念掌权时，理性心智也以同样方式运作。它命令所有公民——或身体细胞——动员行动、保持警戒、削减一切非必要活动等等。

quotes.text: It is far more important that you understand this than that you become overly concerned with labyrinthian "past reasons," for you can get so lost in a negative approach that you forget that these beliefs can be changed in the present. For various reasons, you hold beliefs that you can alter at any time. Many individuals die young, for example, because they believe so strongly that old age represents a degradation of the spirit and an insult to the body. They do not want to live under the conditions as they believe them to be. Some quite frankly prefer to die in what others would consider to be the most dire circumstances -- swept away by the raging waves of an ocean, or crushed in an earthquake, or battered by the winds of a hurricane.
quotes.translation: 理解这一点远比过度纠结于迷宫般的"过往缘由"重要得多，因为你会深陷负面取向而忘记这些信念可以在当下改变。出于各种原因，你持有的信念随时可以改变。例如许多人英年早逝，是因为他们坚信衰老是精神的堕落与身体的侮辱。他们不愿活在自己所认为的那种条件下。有些人坦率地宁愿在他人眼中最可怖的环境中死去——被汹涌海浪卷走，或在地震中碾碎，或被飓风摧残。

quotes.text: Your scientific beliefs tell you that your entire world happened accidentally. Your religions tell you that man is sinful: The body is not to be trusted; the senses can lead you astray. In this maze of beliefs you have largely lost a sense of your own worth and purpose. A generalized fear and suspicion is generated, and life too often becomes stripped of any heroic qualities. The body cannot react to generalized threats. It is, therefore, put under constant strain in such circumstances, and seeks to specify the danger. It is geared to act in your protection. It builds up strong stresses, therefore, so that on many occasions a specific disease or threat situation is "manufactured" to rid the body of a tension grown too strong to bear.
quotes.translation: 你们的科学信念告诉你们，整个世界是偶然发生的。你们的宗教告诉你们，人类是有罪的：身体不可信赖，感官会误导你们。在这信念的迷宫中，你们很大程度上失去了对自身价值与目的的感受。普遍的恐惧与怀疑被制造出来，生命往往被剥夺了任何英雄特质。身体无法对普遍化的威胁做出反应。因此，在这种情形下，它被置于持续的压力之下，并试图具体化危险。它被设计来保护你们。因此，它积聚起强烈的压力，以至于在许多情况下，会"制造"出特定的疾病或威胁情境，以释放身体难以承受的过度紧张。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: (9:35.) Because of the energy he is given by others, he will have a certain consciousness of his own, but such a mock devil has no power or reality to those who do not believe in his existence, and who do not give him energy through their belief. He is, in other words, a superlative hallucination As mentioned earlier, those who believe in a hell and assign themselves to it through their belief can indeed experience one, but certainly in nothing like eternal terms. No soul is forever ignorant.
quotes.translation: （9:35）由于他人给予他的能量，他会拥有某种自我意识，但这样的假魔鬼对那些不相信其存在、也不通过信念给予他能量的人来说，既无力量也无实相。换句话说，他是一个极致的幻觉。如前所述，那些相信地狱并因信念而将自己归入其中的人，确实能体验到地狱，但绝非永恒之类。没有灵魂会永远无知。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: "The idea is that the scientists' system of beliefs is bound to result in some destructive action; that is, the implied attitudes of today's scientists lead them to be less careful of life than they should be, and separate them from nature in a way that leads to some contempt on their parts of individual living things. The leaders of religious cults, like that of Jonestown, overexaggerate grandiose ideals of brotherhood and love, for example (as Seth has mentioned), while often forbidding the natural expression of love on the part of one individual for another -- assaulting family affiliations and so forth. As a result, the idealized love becomes more and more inaccessible, with the growth of more guilt and despair.
quotes.translation: 关键在于科学家的信念体系必然导致某些破坏性行为；也就是说，当今科学家隐含的态度使他们不如应有的那般珍视生命，并以某种方式与自然割裂，导致他们对个体生命产生轻蔑。例如，像琼斯镇那样的宗教教派领袖（如赛斯所提）过度夸大兄弟情谊与爱的崇高理想，却常常禁止个体之间自然表达爱意——破坏家庭纽带等。结果，理想化的爱变得遥不可及，随之而来的是更多的罪恶感与绝望。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: You make your own reality. So, programmed ahead of time, they perceived [data] according to the psychic conventions that had been established. There are tigers in Asia, but you can travel through Asia and if you do not want to you'll never see a tiger. It's according to where you go. In the unknown reality your thoughts are instantly made apparent and real, materialized according to your beliefs. There, if you believe in demons, you will see them -- without ever realizing that they are part of the environment of your psyche, formed by your beliefs, and thrown out as mirages over a very real environment that you do not perceive. You will believe the psychic tour books and go hunting for demons instead of tigers.
quotes.translation: 你创造自己的实相。因此，他们预先设定程序，根据已建立的灵性惯例感知（数据）。亚洲有老虎，但你可以穿越亚洲——若你不想遇见，就永远见不到老虎。这取决于你前往何处。在未知的实相中，你的思想会立即显化为真实，根据你的信念物质化。在那里，若你相信恶魔，就会看见它们——却从未意识到它们是你心灵环境的一部分，由你的信念形成，如同海市蜃楼般投射在你未能感知的真实环境之上。你会相信灵性旅行指南，转而追逐恶魔而非老虎。

quotes.text: If instead there is a physical event that you strongly desire, then use that energy to imagine its actual occurrence as vividly as you can. If you follow these directions and understand the meaning for them as given, you will find the results most startling and effective. Energy may be directed to any portion of the body, and if you do not block its actions by disbeliefs, that portion will be cured. Remember, however: If you hold the belief that you are a sickly person, that can hinder you. [In that case, then, to] change that particular kind of belief is your first concern. (Pause.) One of the purposes of this book is to tell you that no one is born to be a sickly person, so reading it can help you there.
quotes.translation: 如果你强烈渴望某个物质事件的发生，那就运用那股能量尽可能生动地想象它实际发生的场景。若你遵循这些指示并理解其背后的意义，你会发现结果惊人且有效。能量可导向身体的任何部位，只要你不因怀疑而阻碍其运作，该部位便能痊愈。但请记住：若你坚信自己是个体弱多病的人，这便会成为阻碍。因此，改变这种特定信念是你的首要任务。（停顿）本书的目的之一，便是告诉你没有人天生注定体弱多病，阅读此书能助你突破此困局。

quotes.text: Parents and physicians believe, instead, that the child is a victim, ill for no personal reason, but indisposed because of elements attacking him -- either the outside environment, or [something] working against him from within. The child may be told: "You have a cold because you got your feet wet." Or: "You caught the cold from Johnny or Sally." He may be told that he has a virus, so that it seems his body itself was invaded despite his will. He learns that such beliefs are acceptable. It is easier to go along than to be honest, particularly when honesty would often involve a kind of communication his parents might frown upon, or the expression of emotions that are quite unacceptable.
quotes.translation: 父母和医生反而相信，孩子是受害者，生病并非出于个人原因，而是因为受到外界环境或内在因素的攻击——要么是外部环境，要么是体内某些东西在作祟。孩子可能会被告知："你感冒是因为脚弄湿了。"或者："你是从约翰尼或莎莉那里传染了感冒。"他可能被告知感染了病毒，仿佛他的身体在违背其意愿的情况下被入侵了。他学会接受这样的信念。随波逐流比诚实更容易，尤其是当诚实往往涉及父母可能不赞同的沟通方式，或表达完全不被接受的情绪时。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: (9:14.) In the same way, some private-life decisions or events may appear disadvantageous to the intellect for the same reasons, while instead they are also self-corrective measures that you are not able to perceive because of your beliefs. The rational approach, as it is now used, carries a basic assumption that anything that is wrong will get worse. That belief of course is highly detrimental because it runs against the basic principles of life. Were this the case in your terms of history, the world would never have lasted a century. It is interesting to note that even before medical science, there were a goodly number of healthy populations. No disease rubbed out the entire species.
quotes.translation: （9:14。）同样地，某些私人生活中的决定或事件可能因相同原因显得对理智不利，而实际上它们也是你因信念而无法察觉的自我修正措施。目前所用的理性方法隐含一个基本假设：任何错误的事情都会变得更糟。这种信念当然极为有害，因为它违背了生命的基本原则。若按你们的历史尺度来看真是如此，世界根本撑不过一个世纪。有趣的是，即使在医学诞生之前，仍有相当多健康的人口。没有任何疾病能灭绝整个人类物种。

quotes.text: If you think of these as planets, then your other ideas orbit about them. There may be some "invisible beliefs," and there may be one or two invisible core beliefs. These, following the analogy, would be hidden behind the other brighter, more obvious "planets," and yet would show their presence through their effects upon your relationships with all of the other visible core beliefs in your "planetary system."
quotes.translation: 若将这些视为行星，那么你的其他想法便围绕它们运行。可能存在一些"隐形信念"，以及一两个隐形核心信念。按此比喻，它们会隐藏在那些更明亮、更明显的"行星"背后，却通过影响你与"行星系统"中所有其他可见核心信念的关系来显现自身的存在。

quotes.text: Since you feel that sex is the only proper method of love's expression, and yet also believe that sex and love are divided, you are in a quandary. These sexual beliefs are also far more important in national relationships than you realize, for you attempt to take what you think of as a masculine stance as a nation. So, for example, does Russia. India takes a feminine stance -- in terms of your beliefs, now.
quotes.translation: 既然你认为性是爱的唯一恰当表达方式，却又同时相信性与爱是割裂的，你便陷入了两难境地。这些关于性的信念在民族关系中的重要性远超你的认知，因为你们试图以国家姿态采取所谓的男性立场——正如俄罗斯那样。而印度则采取女性立场——当然，这是基于你们当前信念体系而言。

quotes.text: Disconnected from their usual daily attraction to physical events, your emotions will often form their own landscapes, utilizing dreams as their creative medium. I have explained the great correlation that exists between your feelings and beliefs and physical conditions such as weather. (See Chapter Eighteen.) In somewhat the same way, you have a part to play individually in the creation of the dream landscape. It is also the result of your feelings and beliefs on a different level, and while it is not perceivable in physical terms -- laid out with its mountains and continents as your planet is, to be examined by your instruments -- it exists in terms quite as valid.
quotes.translation: 当情感脱离对物质事件惯常的日常关注时，它们常会以梦境为创作媒介，构建自己的景观。我曾解释过你们的情感信念与天气等物质条件之间的重大关联（见第十八章）。类似地，在梦境景观的创造中，你们每个人都扮演着特定角色。这同样是你们在不同层面的情感与信念的产物，虽然它无法以物质形式被感知——不像你们星球那样以山脉大陆的形态铺展，可供仪器探测——但其存在方式同样真实有效。

quotes.text: You may be convinced that human nature is evil, or that no one is safe from another's aggression, or that people are motivated mainly by greed. Such beliefs attract their own reality. If you have anything worth losing, you are then automatically convinced that someone else will take it from you, or try their hardest to do so. In your own way you send out messages to just such a person. On basic levels your convictions will be quite similar, but one will see himself as the victim and one as the aggressor -- that is, each of you will react differently to the same set of beliefs. However the two of you are necessary if a crime of that nature is, or is to be, committed.
quotes.translation: 你可能深信人性本恶，或无人能免于他人的侵犯，或人主要受贪婪驱使。这类信念会吸引其自身的实相。若你拥有任何值得失去之物，便会自动确信他人会将其夺走，或竭力试图如此。你自会以独特方式向这类人发出讯息。在基本层面上，你们的信念将极为相似，但一人视己为受害者，另一人视己为侵犯者——即你们各自对同一套信念的反应不同。然而，若此类性质的罪行正在或将要发生，你们二人便是不可或缺的。

quotes.text: Now: For Ruburt, I want him to remember the idea of effortlessness, because with the best of intentions he has been trying too hard. (Pause.) I want him to remember that relaxation is one of creativity's greatest champions -- not its enemy. He is naturally gifted with the quickness of body and mind. Remind him that it is safe to express his natural (underlined) rhythms, to remember the natural person. Your most vital inspirations are effortlessly yours. I want you to see how many of your beliefs are the result of the old framework, for in that way you will find yourselves releasing yourselves more and more, so that your own strengths come to your support.
quotes.translation: 现在：关于鲁柏，我要他记住"毫不费力"的概念，因为他虽怀最佳意图却过于努力了。（停顿）我要他记住，放松是创造力最伟大的盟友——而非敌人。他天生具备身心敏捷的禀赋。提醒他：表达其自然（强调）节奏是安全的，要记得那个自然的人。你最关键的灵感毫不费力便属于你。我要你们看清，有多少信念是旧框架的产物，因为唯有如此，你们才会发现自己正逐步释放自我，让自身的力量前来支持你们。

quotes.text: A belief in health can help you utilize a "poor" diet to an amazing degree. If you are convinced that a specific food will give you a particular disease, it will indeed do so. It appears that certain vitamins will prevent certain diseases. The belief itself works while you are operating within that framework, of course. A Western doctor may give vitamin shots or pills to a native child in another culture. The child need not know what particular vitamin is being given, or the name for his disease, but if he believes in the physician and Western medicine he will indeed improve, and he will need the vitamins from then on. So will all the other children.
quotes.translation: 对健康的信念能让你以惊人的程度利用"不良"饮食。若你坚信某种特定食物会引发特定疾病，它确实会如此。某些维生素似乎能预防特定疾病。当然，当你在此框架内运作时，信念本身就会发挥作用。一位西方医生可能给异文化土著儿童注射维生素或服用维生素片。孩子无需知道注射的是何种维生素，或自己疾病的名称，但只要他信任这位医生和西方医学，他确实会好转，从此便需要依赖维生素。其他孩子亦复如是。

quotes.text: You think of viruses as evil, spreading perhaps from country to country, to "invade" scores of physical mechanisms. Now thoughts are "contagious." You have a natural immunity against all thoughts that do not fit in with your own purposes and beliefs, and naturally (pause, groping), you are "inoculated" with a wholesome trust and belief in your own thoughts above others. The old ideas of voodooism recognized some of these concepts, but complicated and distorted them with fears of evil, psychic invasion, psychic killing, and so forth. You cannot divide, say, mental and physical health, nor can you divide a person's philosophy from his bodily condition.
quotes.translation: 你们视病毒为邪恶之物，认为它们可能从一国蔓延至另一国，"入侵"无数身体机制。而思想才是真正具有"传染性"的。你们对所有不符合自身目的与信念的思想拥有天然免疫力，并且（停顿，摸索着）你们被"接种"了对自己思想的健康信任与信念，使之凌驾于他人之上。古老的巫毒教观念曾认识到这些概念，却因对邪恶、灵性入侵、灵性杀戮等恐惧而将其复杂化并扭曲。你们无法将心理健康与身体健康割裂，也无法将一个人的哲学观与其身体状况分离。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: In the dream state you allow yourself greater freedom, trying out certain ideas and beliefs in this more plastic framework. You may therefore accept new beliefs initially in the dream state, and the intellectual or emotional realization may only come "later." In dreaming, the conscious mind itself is far more lenient and playful. It can afford this greater permissiveness because it well knows that it need not immediately test out theory in the daily context. It very willingly looks inward toward those areas of the inner self's experience to see what it can find for its own use, quite like an explorer searching for resources in virgin territory.
quotes.translation: 在梦境状态中，你允许自己拥有更大的自由，在这个更具可塑性的架构中尝试某些想法和信念。因此，你最初可能在梦境状态中接受新的信念，而理智或情感上的领悟可能只在"之后"才会到来。在梦中，意识心本身要宽容得多，也更具游戏性。它可以承受这种更大的纵容，因为它很清楚自己不必立即在日常环境中测试理论。它非常乐意向内审视内在自我经验的那些领域，看看能找到什么供自己使用，就像探险家在处女地寻找资源一样。

quotes.text: The imagination has always dealt with creativity, and as man began to settle upon a kind of consciousness that dealt with cause and effect, he no longer physically perceived the products of his imagination directly in the old manner. He realized in those earlier times that illness, for instance, was initially as much the result of the imagination as health was, for he experienced far more directly the brilliant character of his own imagination. The lines between imaginative and physical experience have blurred for you, and of course they have also become tempered by other beliefs and the experiences that those beliefs then engender.
quotes.translation: 想象力一直与创造力打交道，当人类开始安顿于一种处理因果关系的意识时，他不再以旧有的方式直接感知想象力的产物。在那些早期时代，他意识到，例如，疾病最初与健康一样，同样是想象力的结果，因为他更直接地体验到自己想象力的辉煌特质。想象经验与身体经验之间的界限对你们而言已经模糊，当然，它们也被其他信念以及这些信念随后产生的经验所调和。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: (10:10.) Even in primitive societies, witch doctors and other natural therapists have understood that the point of power is in the present, and they have utilized natural hypnosis as a method of helping other individuals to concentrate their own energy. All of the gestures, dances, and other procedures are shock treatments, startling the subject out of habitual reactions so that he or she is forced to focus upon the present moment. The resulting disorientation simply shakes current beliefs and dislodges set frameworks. The hypnotist, or witch doctor, or therapist, then immediately inserts the beliefs he thinks the subject needs.
quotes.translation: （10:10）即使在原始社会，巫医和自然疗法师也明白力量之点就在当下，他们利用自然催眠法帮助他人集中自身能量。所有手势、舞蹈及其他仪式都是冲击疗法，通过震撼使对象脱离惯性反应模式，迫使其聚焦于当下时刻。由此产生的定向失调会动摇现有信念，瓦解既定框架。随后催眠师、巫医或治疗师会立即植入他们认为对象所需的信念。

related_concepts.name_en: The point of power is in the present

quotes.text: (10:49.) The same applies to mental conditions, which have a way, sometimes, of working themselves out better without your professional therapies than with them -- often cures happen in spite of your best-intentioned treatment. One of the latest ideas is that certain mental conditions are caused by chemical imbalances. Supplying these does result in some improvement, but such inequalities do not cause any disease. Your beliefs about the nature of your own reality do. If medication of that sort improves the immediate situation, the inner problem of beliefs must still be worked out. Otherwise other illnesses will be substituted.
quotes.translation: （10:49）同理适用于心理状况——有时这些状况无需专业治疗反而能自行改善，甚至常在你们最善意的治疗下仍发生自愈。最新理论认为某些心理疾病由化学失衡导致，补充这些物质确实能改善症状，但失衡本身并非病因。你们对自身实相本质的信念才是根源。即便药物能暂时缓解状况，信念的内在问题仍需解决，否则会引发其他疾病替代。

quotes.text: There are too many levels here to discuss all at once ... One such level reinforces a trust in himself. The trust is accepted, however, because he is finally ready to work through the issues. As given [at various times over the years, mostly in personal material], they involve cultural training and religious indoctrinations.4 He is challenging, finally, the old beliefs that say that the self's spontaneity is not to be trusted. He is challenging those ideas emotionally and philosophically, uniting physical action and inner mobility. In the past he was still afraid to touch those beliefs with any but the slightest of hands.
quotes.translation: 这里涉及的层次太多，无法一次性讨论……其中一个层次强化了他对自己的信任。然而，这种信任之所以被接受，是因为他终于准备好要处理这些问题。正如多年来（主要是在个人资料中）所提到的，这些问题涉及文化训练和宗教灌输。他终于开始挑战那些认为自我的自发性不可信赖的旧信念。他在情感和哲学层面上挑战这些观念，将身体行动与内在流动性结合起来。过去，他仍然害怕触碰那些信念，哪怕只是用最轻微的手。

quotes.text: The bulk of the material in Personal Reality concerns the nature of beliefs, and the physical and mental environments that are created, both individually and en masse, as a result of those beliefs. It follows, then, that a number of the sessions in that book either deal with health and illness, or with subjects that approach those topics in various ways. Chapters 16 and 17 in particular contain material on what Seth calls natural hypnosis, and on Western medicine, physicians, the suggestions associated with medical insurance and "health" literature, diet, childbirth, hospitals, natural death, good and evil, and so forth.
quotes.translation: 《个人实相》中的大部分内容涉及信念的本质，以及因这些信念而创造的个体和集体的物质与心理环境。因此，该书中的许多章节要么直接讨论健康与疾病，要么以各种方式接近这些主题。特别是第16章和第17章，包含了赛斯所谓的自然催眠、西方医学、医生、与医疗保险和“健康”文献相关的暗示、饮食、分娩、医院、自然死亡、善与恶等内容。

quotes.text: Applied to old age, the color black denotes a returning to those unconscious forces. Now all of this so far is from the standpoint of American and Western belief. It is simply the reality in which many of my readers are involved. In other "underground" systems of belief, however, black is seen as a symbol of great knowledge, power and strength. When this is carried to an extreme you wind up with devil cults, in which the poorly understood powers of creativity and exuberance rush out in distorted form; the undersides of consciousness are then glorified at the expense of the other, white, "conscious and objective" values.
quotes.translation: 应用于老年时，黑色象征着回归那些无意识的力量。到目前为止，这一切都是从美国和西方信念的角度出发的。这只是许多读者所参与的现实。然而，在其他“地下”信念体系中，黑色被视为伟大知识、力量和权力的象征。当这一点被推向极端时，就会产生恶魔崇拜，其中那些未被充分理解的创造力和活力以扭曲的形式爆发出来；意识的阴暗面随后被美化，而牺牲了另一种白色、“有意识和客观”的价值。

quotes.text: The king, for example, may be at one time the symbol of great inner wealth. He may be kingly but poor, signifying the idea that wealth does not necessarily involve physical goods. He might at another time appear as a dictator, cruel and overbearing, where he would represent an entirely different framework of feeling and belief He might show himself as a young monarch, signaling a belief that "youth is king." At various times in history the same image has been used quite differently. When people are fighting dictatorial monarchs then often the king appears in dreams as a despicable character, to be booted and routed out.
quotes.translation: 例如，国王可能一度象征内在的巨大财富。他可能具有王者风范却一贫如洗，暗示财富未必等同于物质拥有。另一些时候，他可能以独裁者形象出现，残暴专横，代表截然不同的感受与信念体系。他可能显现为年轻君主，昭示"青春即王权"的信念。历史上同一意象在不同时期被赋予迥异含义。当人民反抗专制君主时，国王常以卑鄙角色出现在梦境中，注定被驱逐消灭。

quotes.text: He was determined to find the kind of mate that would best suit him and his own unique characteristics. That intent was in his mind. When that challenge was met he used his will and mobilized all of his power to fulfill his abilities, and to bring about conditions in which he hoped Joseph (as Seth calls me) could also fulfill his. The will, again, operates according to the personality's beliefs about reality, so its desires are sometimes tempered as those beliefs change. In his own way Ruburt always concentrated upon one challenge at a time -- boring in, so to speak, and ignoring anything else that might distract him.
quotes.translation: 他决心寻找最能契合自身独特特质的伴侣。这个意图深植于他的意识。当这个挑战被克服后，他运用意志调动全部力量来发挥自身能力，并创造出让约瑟（赛斯对我的称呼）也能发挥潜能的境况。再次强调，意志根据人格对实相的信念运作，因此当信念改变时，其欲望也会随之调整。鲁柏总是以他的方式一次专注于一个挑战——可以说是锲而不舍，完全忽略任何可能分散注意力的其他事物。

quotes.text: There is a steady even flow in which conscious activity through the neurological structure brings about events, and a familiar pattern of reaction is established. When you alter these conscious beliefs through effort, then a period of time is necessary while the structure learns to adjust to the new preferred situation. If beliefs are changed overnight, comparatively less time is required.
quotes.translation: 存在一种稳定的持续流动，其中通过神经结构进行的有意识活动引发事件，并建立起熟悉的反应模式。当你通过努力改变这些有意识的信念时，就需要一段时间让结构学习适应新的偏好情境。如果信念在一夜之间改变，所需的时间相对较少。

quotes.text: If you do this faithfully, within a month you will find the new conditions materializing in your experience. Your neurological structure will respond automatically. The unconscious will be aroused, bringing its great powers to bear, bringing you the new results. Do not try to overdo this, to go through the entire day worrying about beliefs, for example. This can only cause you to contrast what you have with what you want. Forget the exercise when it is completed. You will find yourself with impulses that arrive in line with these newly inserted beliefs, and then it is up to you to act on these and not ignore them.
quotes.translation: 如果你忠实地这样做，一个月内你就会发现新的状况在你的经验中具体化。你的神经结构会自动响应。潜意识会被唤醒，发挥其强大力量，为你带来新的结果。不要试图过度为之，例如整天担忧信念问题。这只会让你不断对比现状与期望。完成练习后就放下它。你会发现与这些新植入的信念相符的冲动自然涌现，然后由你决定是否付诸行动，而非忽视它们。

quotes.text: Dialogues (see the 639th session in Chapter Ten) is now a book, just completed, but it also represented a movement of the self through a question-and-answer format, through which Ruburt recognized and faced many diverse beliefs. Each reader can utilize the same method whether or not artistic achievement is also involved, through objectifying personal beliefs in a dialogue form. This also happens frequently in the dream state, when you allow your natural creativity so much freedom. Often there are dreams in which "you" are two separate people, either strangers or familiar, each asking questions of the other.
quotes.translation: 《对话录》（见第十章第639节）如今已是一本刚完成的书籍，但它也代表了自我通过问答形式进行的运动，鲁柏借此识别并面对了许多不同的信念。每位读者都可以运用同样的方法——无论是否涉及艺术成就——通过对话形式将个人信念客观化。这在你允许自然创造力充分自由时，也常发生在梦境状态中。常有这样的梦境：'你'分裂成两个独立个体，无论是陌生人还是熟人，彼此互相提问。

quotes.text: Some people encounter war directly, however, in terms of hand-to-hand combat, or bombing. Others are only inconvenienced by it. Here the mass shared environment is encountered as physical reality according to individual belief, love, and intent. In the deepest meaning there is no such thing as a victim, either of war, poverty, or disease. This does not mean that those negative qualities should not be combatted, for in the terms of conventional understanding it certainly appears that men and women are victims in many such cases. Therefore they behave like victims, and their beliefs reinforce such experience.
quotes.translation: 有些人直接经历战争，例如肉搏战或轰炸，而另一些人仅受其不便。在此，根据个人信念、爱与意图，大众共享的环境以物质实相的形式呈现。在最深层的意义上，不存在战争、贫穷或疾病的受害者。这并非意味着不应对抗这些负面特质，因为按传统理解，许多情况下男女确实看似受害者。因此他们表现出受害者行为，其信念又强化了此类经验。

quotes.text: In your terms, beliefs are accepted initially from the parents -- this, as mentioned earlier, having to do with mammalian experience. (See the 619th session in Chapter Four.) The hypnotist then acts as a parent substitute. In cases of therapy, an individual is already frightened, and because of the beliefs in your civilization he looks not to himself but to an authority figure for help.
quotes.translation: 按你们的说法，信念最初源自父母——如前所述，这与哺乳类经验有关（见第四章第619节）。催眠师随后扮演父母替代角色。在治疗案例中，个体已然恐惧，因你们文明中的信念，他不向自身求助，而向权威人物寻求帮助。

quotes.text: For a time then you may experience a feeling of loss as you move from one group of beliefs to another. However, others, sharing your new beliefs, will gravitate toward you and you to them. I will say more about this later in the book, but it explains for example why a diet-watcher, suddenly determined to lose weight, may meet with veiled or even open resistance from family or friends; why the person who makes new resolutions may find himself baffled by associates' ridicule; why the alcoholic trying not to drink finds others tempting him quite openly, or teasing him into indulgence by hidden tactics.
quotes.translation: 有一段时间，当你从一组信念转向另一组信念时，可能会经历一种失落感。然而，其他与你共享新信念的人会向你靠拢，你也会向他们靠拢。我将在本书后面进一步讨论这一点，但这可以解释为什么一个突然决心减肥的节食者可能会遭遇家人或朋友隐蔽甚至公开的抗拒；为什么下定决心改变的人会发现自己被同伴的嘲笑所困扰；为什么试图戒酒的酗酒者会发现别人公然诱惑他，或用隐蔽的策略戏弄他，诱使他放纵。

quotes.text: In your country, the free enterprise system originated -- change the word to "immersed" -- is immersed in strange origins. It is based upon the democratic belief in each individual's right to pursue a worthy and equitable life. But that also [became] bound up with Darwinian ideas of the survival of the fittest, and with the belief, then, that each individual must seek his or her own good at the expense of others, and by the quite erroneous conception that all of the members of a given species are in competition with each other, and that each species is in further competition with each other species.
quotes.translation: 在你们的国家，自由企业制度起源于——把"起源于"改为"沉浸于"——沉浸于奇异的起源。它基于民主信念，即每个人都有权追求有价值且公平的生活。但这后来也与达尔文的适者生存观念纠缠在一起，随之而来的信念是，每个人必须牺牲他人来谋求自身利益，并基于一个相当错误的观念：同一物种的所有成员彼此竞争，而每个物种又与其他物种进一步竞争。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: All of your present experience was drawn from probable reality. During your life, any event must come through your creaturehood, with the built-in time recognition that is so largely a part of your neurological structure; so usually there is a lag, a lapse in time, during which your beliefs cause material actualization. When you try to change your convictions in order to change your experience, you also have to first stop the momentum that you have already built up, so to speak. You are changing the messages while the body is used to reacting smoothly, unquestioningly, to a certain set of beliefs.
quotes.translation: 你们所有的当前体验都来自可能性实相。在你们的一生中，任何事件都必须通过你们的生物性来呈现，伴随着内建的时间认知——这在很大程度上是你们神经结构的一部分；因此通常存在一个时间滞后或间隔，在此期间你们的信念导致物质的具体化。当你们试图改变信念以改变体验时，你们还必须首先停止已经积累的动量，可以这么说。当身体习惯于对某一组信念做出顺畅、不加质疑的反应时，你们正在改变信息。

quotes.text: Religious, scientific, medical, and cultural communications stress the existence of danger, minimize the purpose of the species or of any individual member of it, or see mankind as the one erratic, half-insane member of an otherwise orderly realm of nature. Any or all of the above beliefs are held by various systems of thought. All of these, however, strain the individual's biological sense of integrity, reinforce ideas of danger, and shrink the area of psychological safety that is necessary to maintain the quality possible in life. The body's defense systems become confused to varying degrees.
quotes.translation: 宗教、科学、医学及文化传播都强调危险的存在，贬低物种或其中任何个体的目的，或将人类视为原本有序的自然界中唯一反复无常、半疯癫的成员。各种思想体系都持有上述信念中的某一种或全部。然而，所有这些都扭曲了个体生物学上的完整性，强化了危险观念，并压缩了维持生命品质所必需的心理安全领域。身体的防御系统因此陷入不同程度的混乱。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Unfortunately, certain aspects of Christianity were stressed over others, and that dictum was based upon the belief in a wicked self that needed to be disciplined and diverted into constructive activity. The belief in such an unsavory self stops many people from any exploration of the inner self -- and, therefore, from any direct experience that will give them counter-evidence. If you are afraid of yourself, if you are afraid of your own memories, you will block your associative processes, fearing for example that they will bring to light matters best forgotten -- and usually sexual matters.
quotes.translation: 不幸的是，基督教某些方面被过度强调，其教义建立在"邪恶的自我需要被约束并转向建设性活动"的信念之上。这种对令人不快的自我的信念，阻止了许多人探索内在自我——因此也阻止了他们获得能提供反证的直接体验。如果你害怕自己，害怕自己的记忆，就会阻断联想过程，例如担心这些记忆会揭示最好被遗忘的事情——通常是性方面的事情。

quotes.text: In that framework, even the emotions of love and exaltation are seen as no more than the erratic activity of neurons firing (pause), or of chemicals reacting to chemicals. Those beliefs alone bring on suffering. All of science, in your time, has been set up to promote beliefs that run in direct contradiction to the knowledge of man's heart. Science has, you have noted, denied emotional truth. It is not simply that science denies the validity of emotional experience, but that it has believed so firmly that knowledge can only be acquired from the outside, from observing the exterior of nature.
quotes.translation: 在这种框架下，就连爱与狂喜的情感也被视为不过是神经元随机放电（停顿），或化学物质相互反应的产物。仅凭这些信念就会带来痛苦。在你们这个时代，整个科学体系都被建立来推广与人类心灵认知直接矛盾的信念。你已注意到，科学否认了情感的真实性。这不仅是科学否认情感体验的有效性，更是因为它坚信知识只能从外部获得——通过观察自然界的表象。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: As soon as you begin to compare what you are with some idealized concept of yourself, you automatically feel guilty. Until you work with your beliefs, this guilt can be initiated by the most harmless episodes and characteristics. It is a good idea to write down a list of specific acts or incidents that fill you with a sense of guilt. Often you will be able to trace them to early childhood beliefs quite easily -- some instilled by a well-meaning parent to protect you, or out of an adult's ignorance. Brought into the open, however, many of these will dissolve before your comprehension.
quotes.translation: 一旦你开始将真实的自己与某种理想化的自我概念进行比较，你就会自动产生内疚感。除非你着手处理自己的信念，否则这种内疚感可能由最无害的事件和特质引发。建议你列出一份清单，写下那些让你充满内疚感的具体行为或事件。通常你很容易就能追溯到童年早期的信念——有些是出于善意的父母为了保护你而灌输的，或是出于成年人的无知。然而，一旦将这些信念带到明处，许多都会在你的理解中消融。

quotes.text: (Slowly:) The part of Augustus who felt powerful and alien became personified. When Augustus felt threatened then the conscious mind switched over, accepting as operating procedure the system of beliefs in which Augustus saw himself as all-powerful, secure -- but as alien. This part of his beliefs, therefore, and this particular self-image, took over his conscious mind and became what we will here call Augustus Two. When Augustus Two assumed leadership then the physical body itself was not only strong and powerful, but capable of physical feats far surpassing those of Augustus One.
quotes.translation: （缓慢地：）奥古斯都感到强大而疏离的那部分人格化了。当奥古斯都感到受威胁时，意识心就会切换，接受奥古斯都视自己为全能、安全但疏离的信念体系作为运作程序。因此，他信念中的这一部分，以及这个特定的自我形象，接管了他的意识心，成为我们在此所称的"奥古斯都二号"。当奥古斯都二号掌权时，身体不仅变得强壮有力，还能完成远超奥古斯都一号的体能壮举。

quotes.text: So when you think of your beliefs and who you are, you must also think of your species, and how you are told your species came to be. For your private beliefs are also based upon those theories, and the beliefs, culturally, of your times.
quotes.translation: 因此，当你思考自己的信念与身份时，也必须思考你的物种，以及你所被告知的物种起源。因为你的个人信念同样建立在这些理论之上，也建立在所处时代的文化信念之中。

quotes.text: (9:29.) A man believing he has heart trouble will finally, through his own anxiety, affect the functioning of his "involuntary" system until his heart is definitely harmed if the belief goes unchecked. The conscious mind directs the so-called involuntary systems of the body, and not the other way around. No idea slips insidiously past your awareness to affect your involuntary system unless it fits in with your own conscious beliefs. Once more, you will not be sick if you think you are well -- but there may be other ideas that make you believe in the necessity for poor health.
quotes.translation: （9点29分。）一个相信自己有心脏病的人，最终会因自身的焦虑影响其"非自主"系统的功能——若这种信念不受约束，心脏确实会受到损害。意识心主导着所谓的身体非自主系统，而非相反。没有任何想法能暗中绕过你的觉知影响非自主系统，除非它符合你自身的意识信念。再次强调，若你自认健康便不会生病——但可能存在其他观念让你相信健康不佳的必要性。

quotes.text: It could not produce violets, nor could it be a good apple tree while it tried to. The mistaken belief is one that does not fit the basic conditions of your inner being. So if you believe that you are at the mercy of physical events, you entertain a false belief. If you feel that your present experience was set in circumstances beyond your control, you entertain a false belief.
quotes.translation: 它无法产出紫罗兰，若勉强为之也无法成为优质的苹果树。错误的信念是指那些不符合你内在本质基本条件的信念。因此，若你相信自己受制于物质事件，便是持有虚假信念。若你认为当前经历是由无法掌控的环境所决定，便是持有虚假信念。

quotes.text: The theory of evolution,4 for instance, is an imaginative construct, and yet through its lights some generations now have viewed their world. It is not only that you think of yourselves differently, but you actually experience a different kind of self. Your institutions change their aspects accordingly, so that experience fits the beliefs that you have about it. You act in certain ways. You view the entire universe in a fashion that did not exist before, so that imagination and belief intangibly structure your subjective experience and your objective circumstances.
quotes.translation: 例如，进化论是一种想象力的建构，然而几代人现在都透过它的光芒来看待世界。你们不仅以不同的方式看待自己，而且实际上体验到一种不同的自我。你们的机构也随之改变其面貌，使得经验符合你们对它的信念。你们以特定方式行动。你们以一种前所未有的方式看待整个宇宙，因此想象力和信念无形中构建了你们的主观经验和客观环境。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: For a while the new adults often feel themselves to be invincible, beyond the boundaries of creaturehood, even; this belief, again, endows them with the strength and energy they need to begin a life for themselves and to form their own mass world. Yet in material terms they must all realize, sooner or later, not only the challenges but the other peculiar characteristics of creaturehood, in which basically no such generalized beliefs make sense.
quotes.translation: 有一段时间，这些新成年人常常觉得自己所向无敌，甚至超越了生物性的界限；这种信念再次赋予他们开始为自己生活、形成自己大众世界所需的力量和能量。然而，在物质层面上，他们迟早都必须认识到，不仅包括挑战，还有生物性的其他独特特征——在这些特征中，基本上没有这种普遍化的信念是有意义的。

quotes.text: As creatures you are a part of nature. The change of thoughts, feelings and beliefs into physical, objectively perceived phenomena is as natural as water changing into ice, for example, or a caterpillar turning into a butterfly. You not only form the structure of your civilizations and social institutions through the transference of beliefs, thoughts and feelings; but in this natural exchange you also help on quite intimate levels in the "psychic manufacture" of the physical environment itself, with all of its great sweeping variety, and yet seasonal stability.
quotes.translation: 作为生物，你们是自然的一部分。思想、情感和信念转化为物质性的、客观可感知的现象，其自然程度如同水结成冰，或毛虫蜕变为蝴蝶。你们不仅通过信念、思想和情感的转移构建文明与社会制度的结构；在这场自然的交换中，你们还在极为亲密的层面上协助了物质环境本身的"心灵制造"——这个环境既包罗万象、千变万化，又保持着季节性的稳定。

quotes.text: (11:09.) Then, according to your beliefs, you deal with the physical dilemma as it is presented in those terms. You will react individually with your own purposes in mind. Your own unique and highly private beliefs help bring about the overall emotional condition. The pool of emotional energy into which your emotions flow is still composed of unalike charges, but generally speaking, the individual contribution of all those participating will fall into a coherent pattern that gives impetus and direction to the storm, providing the charge and the power behind it.
quotes.translation: （11:09。）接着，根据你们的信念，你们会以这些条件所呈现的方式处理物质困境。你们将带着各自的目的做出个人反应。你们独特而高度私密的信念有助于形成整体的情绪状态。你们情绪所汇入的情绪能量池仍由不同电荷组成，但总体而言，所有参与者的个人贡献将形成连贯的模式，为风暴提供动力和方向，并赋予其背后的电荷与力量。

quotes.text: The physical universe serves then as a threshold for probabilities, and all possible species find their greatest fulfillment within that system, each of them neurologically tuned into their own reality and their own "time." So the body itself, as it presently exists, is innately equipped with other neurological responses that to you would seem to be biologically invisible. Nevertheless, your consciousness and your beliefs are what direct this neurological recognition. At birth, and before structured learning processes begin, you are far freer in that regard.
quotes.translation: 物质宇宙因此充当了可能性的门槛，所有可能的物种都在这个系统中找到最大的圆满，每个物种的神经系统都调谐到自身的实相与自身的"时间"。因此，身体本身——以其当前的存在形式——天生具备其他神经反应能力，这些反应在你们看来似乎是生物学上不可见的。然而，正是你们的意识与信念引导着这种神经认知。在出生时，在有结构的学习过程开始之前，你们在这方面要自由得多。

quotes.text: I have frequently said that beliefs cause reality, and that no symptom will simply fade away unless the "reason" is ascertained -- but such reasons go far beneath your current ideas of cause and effect. They involve intimate philosophical value judgments on the part of each individual. Beneath them, the apparent causes of limitations in personal life, there are other far-reaching beliefs, and each individual will use those elements in his private experience to back these up. This applies to any kind of lack or hindrance severe enough to be a problem.
quotes.translation: 我常言信念创造实相，任何症状都不会凭空消失，除非其"缘由"被查明——但这些缘由远在你们当前因果观念之下。它们涉及每个个体深层的哲学价值判断。在这些限制个人生活的表面原因之下，存在着更深远广大的信念，而每个人都会运用自身经验中的那些元素来强化这些信念。这适用于任何严重到成为问题的匮乏或阻碍。

quotes.text: What did you wear to work three days ago? What did you have for breakfast a week ago? Who sat next to you in kindergarten? What frightened you last? Are you afraid of sleep? Did your parents beat you? What did you do just after lunch yesterday? What color shoes did you wear three days ago? You remember only significant events or details. Your emotions trigger your memories, and they organize your associations. Your emotions are generated through your beliefs. They attach themselves so that certain beliefs and emotions seem almost synonymous.
quotes.translation: 三天前你穿什么去上班？一周前早餐吃了什么？幼儿园时谁坐在你旁边？最近一次让你害怕的是什么？你害怕睡眠吗？你父母打过你吗？昨天午饭后你做了什么？三天前你穿什么颜色的鞋子？你只记得重要的事件或细节。你的情绪触发记忆，并组织你的联想。你的情绪由信念产生。它们彼此依附，以至于某些信念与情绪几乎成为同义词。

quotes.text: Women whose husbands have had vasectomies have themselves often resolved sexual problems that have bothered them. Fear is reduced in that area. (Long pause.) Cervical cancer can involve -- can involve -- distortions of the growth process itself, because of the complicated distortions of belief on the woman's part. In a way the very pain of cancer -- of some cancers -- often acts through its intensity as a reflection of the person's belief that life is painful, tormenting. At the same time, the pain is a reminder of feeling and sensation.
quotes.translation: 丈夫做过输精管结扎手术的女性，往往自己解决了困扰她们的性问题。那个领域的恐惧减少了。（长停顿。）子宫颈癌可能涉及——可能涉及——生长过程本身的扭曲，因为女性自身信念的复杂扭曲。在某种程度上，癌症——某些癌症——的剧痛常常通过其强度反映了一个人的信念：生命是痛苦、折磨的。同时，疼痛是对感觉和知觉的提醒。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: (9:55.) Following such a belief, you will confuse suffering with saintliness, desolation with purity, and the denial of the body as spirituality and a badge of holiness. Under such conditions you can even seek out illness to prove to yourself the strength of your own spirituality -- and to impress it upon others. The same kind of moral value judgment can be placed in almost any area of human activity, and will of course have social repercussions. Those reactions will add to the prevailing beliefs and in turn affect the individual.
quotes.translation: （9:55。）遵循这样的信念，你会将苦难与圣洁混淆，将荒凉与纯净混淆，将否定身体视为灵性和神圣的徽章。在这种条件下，你甚至会寻求疾病，以向自己证明自身灵性的力量——并以此给他人留下深刻印象。几乎任何人类活动领域都可能被赋予同样类型的道德价值判断，这当然会产生社会影响。这些反应会强化主流信念，进而影响个人。

quotes.text: Remember that ideas are as natural as the weather. They follow patterns, then, and obey certain laws even as more strictly physical phenomena do. Unfortunately, no one examines the nature of mental reality from such a viewpoint. You will be born in the midst of certain mass beliefs, and these may vary according to the country of your nativity. As you come into your body with all of its physical surroundings, so at birth do you emerge into a rich natural psychological environment in which beliefs and ideas are every bit as real.
quotes.translation: 记住，意念和天气一样自然。它们遵循模式，并遵守某些法则，甚至比严格意义上的物理现象更甚。不幸的是，没有人从这样的角度检视心灵实相的本质。你出生在特定的大众信念中，这些信念可能因你的出生国家而异。正如你带着所有物质环境进入身体，出生时你也进入一个丰富的自然心理环境，其中信念和意念与物质环境一样真实。

quotes.text: "I feel inferior because my mother hated me," or, "I feel unworthy because I was scrawny and small as a child." You may find as you work with your beliefs that a feeling of inferiority seems to stem from such episodes. It is up to you as an adult to get on top of your beliefs, to realize that a mother who hates her child is already in difficulties, and that such a hate says far more about the mother than it does about her offspring. It is up to you to understand that you are now a grown person, and not a child to be bullied.
quotes.translation: "我觉得自卑，因为我母亲恨我，"或"我觉得无价值，因为我小时候瘦小孱弱。"当你处理信念时，可能会发现自卑感似乎源于这些事件。作为成年人，你有责任掌控自己的信念，意识到恨孩子的母亲本身已陷入困境，这种恨更多地反映了母亲自身而非孩子。你必须明白，你现在是成年人，不再是任人欺凌的孩子。

quotes.text: It becomes a counter suggestion, yet it is all a part of the same hypnotic process, based upon his belief in his original illness. While it gives temporary results, the fact that he needs it reinforces his dependency upon it. If his belief in his poor health continues unchecked, the medicine will no longer serve as an adequate counter measure. It would seem only good sense to refrain from the foods that bring on the condition. Yet each time this is done, the individual acquiesces more and more to the hypnotic suggestion.
quotes.translation: 这成为了一种反向暗示，然而它仍属于同一催眠过程的一部分，基于他对原始疾病的信念。虽然它能带来暂时的效果，但他需要这种药物的事实反而强化了他对药物的依赖。如果他对自身健康状况不佳的信念持续不受约束，药物将不再足以作为有效的对抗措施。避免食用引发病症的食物似乎只是明智之举。然而，每次这样做时，个体就越来越顺从于催眠暗示。

quotes.text: Often, of course, those who try the hardest to be "good" do so because they fear for their basic worth, and those who speak of having youthful minds and bodies do so because they are so terrified of age. In the same way, many who shout about independence are afraid that they are basically helpless. In most instances these opposing beliefs are held quite consciously, but kept apart from each other. Therefore they are not reconciled.
quotes.translation: 当然，那些最努力想要"做好人"的人，往往是因为他们恐惧自身的基本价值；而那些谈论拥有年轻心态与身体的人，则是因为他们如此恐惧衰老。同样地，许多高喊独立的人，其实害怕自己本质上无助。在大多数情况下，这些相互矛盾的信念被有意识地持有，却被彼此隔离。因此，它们从未得到调和。

quotes.text: He felt bewildered in a world of opposites. Conflicting beliefs were uncritically accepted. (Pause.) The conscious mind will always attempt to make sense out of its beliefs, to form them into patterns and sequences. It will usually organize ideas in as rational a way as possible, and dispense with those that seem to contradict the overall system of its beliefs.
quotes.translation: 他在一个充满对立的世界里感到困惑。相互矛盾的信念被不加批判地接受。（停顿）意识心智总会试图理解自己的信念，将它们组织成模式和序列。它通常会尽可能理性地整理观念，并舍弃那些似乎与自身信念整体系统相矛盾的部分。

quotes.text: You must be aware of the contents of your own reasoning mind. Find the ambiguities. Regardless of the nature of your beliefs they are indeed made flesh and material. The miracle of your being cannot escape itself. Your thoughts blossom into events. If you think the world is evil, you will meet with events that seem evil. There are no accidents in cosmic terms, or in terms of the world as you know it. Your beliefs grow as surely in time and space as flowers do. When you realize this you can even feel their growing.
quotes.translation: 你必须觉察自己理性心智的内容。找出其中的模糊之处。无论你的信念性质如何，它们确实会化为血肉与物质。你存在的奇迹无法逃避自身。你的思想绽放为事件。如果你认为世界是邪恶的，你就会遇到看似邪恶的事件。从宇宙层面而言，或从你所知的世界层面而言，没有意外。你的信念在时空中的生长，如同花朵般确凿无疑。当你意识到这一点时，你甚至能感受到它们的生长。

quotes.text: Certain kinds of medications can indeed help, but those given in your hospitals simply drug the consciousness out of its own understanding, and inhibit the body mechanisms that make for an easy transition. In your prisons you do the same thing, of course, isolating groups of people with like beliefs -- denying them all natural stimuli so that a greater contagion of similar beliefs ensues. You separate such people from the normal contact of their loved ones, and all usual conditions for growth or development.
quotes.translation: 某些药物确实能有所帮助，但医院里开出的那些药物只是将意识麻醉，使其脱离自身的理解，并抑制了身体中促进平稳过渡的机制。当然，在你们的监狱里也做着同样的事——将持有相似信念的人群隔离起来，剥夺他们所有的自然刺激，使得同类信念的传染性进一步加剧。你们将这些人与其所爱之人的正常接触隔绝开来，也剥夺了他们成长或发展所需的一切常规条件。

quotes.text: There are gradations and fluctuations within its activity. It is far more flexible than you give it credit for. (Pause.) The ego can use the conscious mind almost entirely as a way of perceiving external or internal realities that coincide with its own beliefs. It is not that certain answers do not lie openly accessible, therefore, but that often you have set yourself on a course of action in which you believe, and you do not want to open yourself to any material that may contradict your current beliefs.
quotes.translation: 在其活动之中存在着层级与波动。它的灵活性远比你认为的要大得多。（停顿。）自我几乎可以完全将意识心用作一种感知外在或内在实相的方式，而这些实相恰好与其自身的信念相符。因此，并非某些答案无法公开获取，而是你常常已将自己置于一条你深信不疑的行动路径上，不愿向任何可能与你当前信念相矛盾的信息敞开自己。

quotes.text: The mind is also equipped to see its own beliefs, reflect upon them and evaluate their results, so using this tool as it was meant to be used would automatically help man in recognizing both his beliefs and their effects. Part of this great permissiveness has to do with the fact that man is to realize that he creates his own reality. Free will is a necessity. The leeway given allows him to materialize his ideas, meet them in physical experience, and evaluate for himself their particular kind of validity.
quotes.translation: 心智同样具备审视自身信念、反思它们并评估其结果的能力，因此，若按此工具的本意来使用它，自然会帮助人类认清自己的信念及其影响。这种极大的宽容性部分与以下事实相关：人类必须认识到自己创造了自己的实相。自由意志是必要的。所给予的余地使他能够将他的理念具体化，在物质经验中与之相遇，并自行评估它们各自的有效性。

quotes.text: Each species is involved in a cooperative venture, upon which ultimately all earthly existence rests. You project your present beliefs backward into history, and you misinterpret many of the conditions that you observe in the natural world. This cooperation that I speak of is based on love, and that love has a biological as well as a spiritual basis. Your beliefs, for example, cause you to deny the existence of emotions in animals, and any instances of love among them are assigned to "blind" instinct.
quotes.translation: 每个物种都参与一项合作事业，所有尘世存在最终都依赖于此。你们将当前的信念投射回历史中，并误解了在自然界观察到的许多状况。我所说的这种合作基于爱，而爱既有生物学基础也有灵性基础。例如，你们的信念导致你们否认动物具有情感，它们之间的任何爱之实例都被归因于"盲目的"本能。

quotes.text: Now: An artist produces a body of work in his lifetime. Each painting is but one materialization, one focused presentation, of an endless variety of probable paintings. The actual work involved in the selection of data is still made according to the beliefs in the artist's conscious mind as to who he is, how good an artist he is, what kind of artist he is, what "school" of artistic beliefs he subscribes to, his ideas of society and his place in it, and esthetic and economic values, to name but a few.
quotes.translation: 现在：一位艺术家在其一生中创作了一系列作品。每幅画都只是无数可能画作中的一种物质化呈现、一个聚焦的表达。选择素材的实际工作仍根据艺术家意识心智中的信念进行——这些信念涉及他是谁、他有多优秀、他属于哪种艺术家、他信奉哪种艺术"流派"、他对社会及其自身位置的看法，以及美学与经济价值等等。

quotes.text: Now the same applies to what is frequently called spiritual healing. If through the concentrated use of psychic energy your body is cured by such a healer, you will also simply trade those symptoms for others unless you change your initial beliefs. Now sometimes a healer or a doctor, with his effectiveness in healing a condition, will show you by inference that the healing energy was always within yourself, and this realization may be enough to allow you to change your beliefs about health entirely.
quotes.translation: 现在，同样的道理也适用于常被称为灵性疗愈的情况。如果你通过灵性能量的集中运用被这样的疗愈者治愈了身体，除非你改变最初的信念，否则你只会用这些症状换取其他症状。有时，一位疗愈者或医生通过有效治愈某种状况，会间接向你展示疗愈能量始终存在于你自身之内，而这一领悟可能足以让你彻底改变对健康的信念。

quotes.text: A young adult gifted in a particular area may hold a belief that this ability makes him or her superior to all others. This may be quite beneficial for the person involved at a given time, to provide the needed impetus for development and the necessary independence in which the ability can grow. The same person, years older, may find that the identical belief has been held too long, so that it denies very important emotional give-and-take with contemporaries, or becomes restrictive in other ways.
quotes.translation: 一位在特定领域天赋异禀的年轻人可能持有这样的信念：这种能力使他或她优于所有人。这在特定时期对当事人可能相当有益，能提供发展所需的动力和让能力成长所必要的独立性。同一个人，多年后可能会发现同样的信念被持有太久，以至于否定了与同龄人之间非常重要的情感交流，或以其他方式变得具有限制性。

quotes.text: Each system, of course, brings forth its own culture, "technology," art, and science. The physical body is basically equipped to maintain itself as a healthy long-living organism far beyond your present understanding, medically speaking. The cellular comprehension8 provides all kinds of inner therapeutics that operate quite naturally. There is a physical give-and-take between the body and environment beyond that which you recognize; an inner dynamics here that escapes you, that unites the health of plants, animals, and men. In the most simple and mundane of examples, if you are living in a fairly well-balanced, healthy environment, your houseplants and your animals will also be well. You form your environment and you are a part of it. You react to it, often forgetting that relationship. Ideally, the body has the capacity to keep itself in excellent health -- but beyond that, to maintain itself at the highest levels of physical achievement. The exploits of your greatest athletes give you a hint of the body's true capacity. In your system of beliefs, however, those athletes must train and focus all of their attention in that direction, often at the expense of other portions of their own experience. But their performances show you what the body is capable of.
quotes.translation: 当然，每个系统都会催生其自身的文化、"技术"、艺术和科学。从医学角度来说，身体的基本装备足以维持自身作为健康长寿的有机体，这远超你们当前的理解。细胞层面的理解提供了各种自然运作的内在疗法。身体与环境之间存在超越你们认知的物质交换；这里有一种你们未能察觉的内在动力，它联结着植物、动物和人类的健康。在最简单平凡的例证中，如果你生活在一个相当平衡健康的环境中，你的室内植物和宠物也会状态良好。你塑造你的环境，而你是它的一部分。你对其做出反应，却常常忘记这种关系。理想情况下，身体有能力保持自身处于极佳的健康状态——但不仅如此，还能将自身维持在最高水平的身体成就上。你们最伟大运动员的成就，暗示了身体的真实能力。然而在你们的信念体系中，那些运动员必须训练并将全部注意力集中在这个方向上，往往以牺牲自身经验的其他部分为代价。但他们的表现向你们展示了身体所能达到的境界。

quotes.text: (4:40.) Ruburt, at one period, even feared that the young psychologist at Oswego was correct -- that his psychic abilities were mere attempts to prove himself superior to you.* These are all beliefs that both of you have wrestled with over the years. You also had many excellent beliefs going with you also, so that you did indeed use your abilities and express your natures. You enjoyed your relationship with each other, the relationship with friends, and you did also enjoy some financial success.
quotes.translation: （4:40。）鲁柏曾一度甚至担心奥斯威戈那位年轻心理学家是对的——他的通灵能力只是试图证明自己比你优越。* 这些都是你们多年来一直与之抗争的信念。你们也同时拥有许多优秀的信念，因此你们确实运用了自己的能力，表达了本性。你们享受彼此的关系、与朋友的关系，也享受了一定的经济成就。

quotes.text: In order for consciousness to develop in your terms, there must be freedom for the exploration of all ideas individually and en masse. Each of you are living entities, growing toward your own development. Each of your beliefs, therefore, has its own unique origin and feeling patterns, so you must for yourself travel back through your beliefs and your own feelings until intellectually and emotionally you realize your rightness, your completely original existence in time and space as you know it.
quotes.translation: 以你们的说法，为了让意识发展，必须有个体与集体探索所有观念的自由。你们每个人都是活生生的实体，朝着自身的发展成长。因此，你们每个信念都有其独特的起源和感受模式，所以你必须亲自回溯自己的信念和感受，直到在理智和情感上都认识到自己的正当性，认识到自己在所知的时空中的完全原创性存在。

quotes.text: Your daily experience will be affected by your race, your beliefs about it, your beliefs about other races, and the climate of opinion in general. On a quite simple basis, if you consider God in human terms you will project him as belonging to your own race. If you belong to a minority or if you are black, then you may be caught in a conflict of beliefs.
quotes.translation: 你的日常经验会受到你的种族、你对它的信念、你对其他种族的信念以及整体舆论氛围的影响。简单来说，如果你用人性的角度看待上帝，你会将他投射为属于你自己的种族。如果你属于少数群体或是黑人，那么你可能会陷入信念的冲突中。

quotes.text: It may be easy for you to see beliefs that are invisible to others in themselves. Reading this book, you may be able to point at friends or acquaintances and see clearly that their ideas are invisible beliefs which limit their experience -- and yet be blind to your own invisible beliefs, which you take so readily as truth or characteristics of reality.
quotes.translation: 你或许能轻易看出他人身上那些他们自己看不见的信念。读这本书时，你或许能指着朋友或熟人，清楚看出他们的想法正是限制其经验的隐形信念——然而对自己那些被轻易当作真理或现实特性的隐形信念，你却视而不见。

quotes.text: Here we have a system of belief in which it is wrong to be white, American, or wealthy, or even at all well-off in financial terms. All of the distortions in Christianity are apparent, where the first group is blind to them, of course. Here, though, wealth and a white skin are not only bad, but obvious symptoms of moral deterioration. If the first system of beliefs sees money and goods as a sign of God's blessing, the second group views material possessions as evidence of spiritual decay.
quotes.translation: 这里存在一套信念体系，认为身为白人、美国人、富人，甚至经济上稍微宽裕都是错误的。基督教的所有扭曲之处在此显而易见——当然，第一群人对此视而不见。然而在此体系中，财富与白皮肤不仅是不好的，更是道德堕落的明显症状。若第一套信念体系视金钱与物质为上帝赐福的象征，第二群人则将物质拥有视为灵性衰败的证据。

quotes.text: Andrea never doubted the "fact" that life was more difficult for a woman than for a man. (See the 643rd session.) When she examined her beliefs this escaped her. The invisible belief, however, affected her behavior and experience. Now she understands it and can deal with it as belief, and not as a condition of reality over which she has no control.
quotes.translation: 安德莉亚从未怀疑过“女性比男性生活更艰难”这一“事实”（见第643节）。当她检视自己的信念时，这一点被她忽略了。这个隐形的信念却影响了她的行为和体验。现在她明白了这一点，并能将其作为信念来对待，而非视为自己无法控制的现实条件。

quotes.text: An analyst might consider Augustus as schizophrenic and label him neatly, but such terms are basically meaningless. If the analyst, over a period of time, should convince Augustus that his condition in the present resulted from some specific inhibited event in the past, and if the analyst was an intuitive and understanding man, then Augustus might change his beliefs enough so that some kind of "cure" was worked. He would then conveniently remember such an event and display the expected emotions as he re-experienced it. Unfortunately in his present state, powerless as it were without Augustus Two, he might also simply call on his "alter ego" to show the good doctor that he was no one to trifle with.
quotes.translation: 分析师可能将奥古斯都诊断为精神分裂症并给他贴上一个整齐的标签，但这类术语基本上毫无意义。如果分析师在一段时间内能让奥古斯都相信，他目前的状况源于过去某个被压抑的具体事件，并且这位分析师是个直觉敏锐且善解人意的人，那么奥古斯都可能改变他的信念到足以产生某种“治愈”效果。他会方便地记起这样的事件，并在重新体验时表现出预期的情绪。不幸的是，在他目前的状态下——仿佛没有奥古斯都二号就无能为力——他可能干脆召唤他的“另一个自我”来向这位好医生证明，他可不是好惹的。

quotes.text: The scientific framework is basically, now, just as senseless, though within it the facts often seem to prove themselves out, also. There are viruses, for example. Your beliefs become self-evident realities. It would be impossible to discuss human suffering without taking that into consideration. Ideas are transmitted from generation to generation--and those ideas are the carriers of all of your reality, its joys and its agonies. Science, however, is all in all (underlined) a poor healer. The church's concepts at least gave suffering a kind of dignity: It did (underlined) come from God--an unwelcome gift, perhaps--but after all it was punishment handed out from a firm father for a child's own good.
quotes.translation: 科学架构基本上与现在同样毫无意义，尽管在其框架内事实也常看似自证其说。例如病毒的存在。你的信念会成为不言自明的实相。若不考虑这一点，便无法讨论人类的苦难。观念代代相传——这些观念承载着你所有实相、喜悦与痛苦。然而科学终究（着重号）是拙劣的疗愈者。教会的概念至少赋予苦难某种尊严：它确实（着重号）来自上帝——或许是不受欢迎的礼物——但终究是严厉父亲为子女好而施予的惩罚。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: We are dealing to make a point with a belief already made physical. But if you continued such concentration the toe either would not heal or would develop into a worse condition. Behind all this, of course, would be the belief that caused the difficulty; but once you have brought about a group of symptoms you must be very careful that you do not begin to view your field of reality from that position. When you do, you add both inner and outer images that reinforce the condition.
quotes.translation: 我们正通过一个已具现化的信念来阐明观点。但若你持续专注于此，脚趾要么无法愈合，要么会恶化成更糟的状况。当然，这一切背后是引发问题的信念；但一旦你引发一组症状，就必须非常谨慎，切勿开始从那个位置看待你的实相领域。当你这样做时，便会增添强化该状况的内在与外在影像。

quotes.text: If our ideas were already accepted in the world, there would be no need for our work. Prentice-Hall is, of course, well-intentioned, and under their belief system it is nearly sacrilegious to be anything more than officially disapproving of medical matters. That is, some disapproval is acceptable. To attack medical corruption, or medical errors, or particular clinics, for example, is within bounds, but to attack the belief system of the entire structure is something else again.
quotes.translation: 如果我们的观念已被世界接受，就无需我们的工作了。普林蒂斯-霍尔出版社自然出于善意，但在他们的信念体系下，对医疗事务稍加正式谴责已近乎亵渎。也就是说，些许谴责尚可接受。例如抨击医疗腐败、医疗过失或特定诊所仍在许可范围内，但攻击整个体系的信念结构则另当别论。

quotes.text: (10:10.) The knowledge of the body's own biological probabilities takes place at those ancient levels, and at those levels there is activity that results in a cellular communication existing between all species. The brain has built-in powers of adaptation to an amazing degree, so that innately one portion can take over for any other portion, and perform its activities as well as its own. Beliefs in what is possible and not possible often dull that facility, however. While the neural connections are specific, and while learned biological behavior dominates basically, the portions of the brain are innately inter-changeable, for they are directed by the mind's action.
quotes.translation: （十点十分）身体自身生物可能性的认知发生在那些古老层面，而在那些层面存在导致所有物种间细胞通讯的活动。大脑具有惊人的内在适应能力，使得某部分天生能替代其他部分运作，并同时执行自身功能。然而，关于"可能"与"不可能"的信念常会钝化这种能力。尽管神经连接具有特异性，且习得的生物行为基本主导一切，但大脑各区域本质上是可互换的——因为它们受心灵行动所指引。

quotes.text: A new belief in the present, however, can cause changes in the past on a neuronal level. You must understand that basically time is simultaneous. Present beliefs can indeed alter the past. In some cases of healing, in the spontaneous disappearance of cancer, for instance, or of any other disease, certain alterations are made that affect cellular memory, genetic codes, or neuronal patterns in the past.
quotes.translation: 然而，当下新的信念能在神经元层面改变过去。你必须明白，时间本质上是同时性的。当下的信念确实能改变过去。在某些疗愈案例中，例如癌症或其他疾病的自发消失，过去会经历某些改变，影响细胞记忆、基因密码或神经元模式。

quotes.text: (9:25.) When the projects were done, particularly with Ruburt, there was still the cultural belief that time should be so used (underlined), that creativity must be directed and disciplined to fall into the proper time slots. In other words, to some extent or another he tried to use an assembly-line kind of time for your creative productivity. This may work when manuscripts are being typed, and so much physical labor is involved, but overall you are using the "wrong" approach to time, particularly for any creative artist. This again applies particularly to Ruburt, though you are not exonerated in that regard (with some humor.
quotes.translation: （9点25分）当那些计划完成时——尤其是涉及鲁柏的部分——仍存在一种文化信念：时间应当被如此利用（强调），创造力必须被引导和规训以落入适当的时间段。换言之，他或多或少试图用流水线式的时间来处理你们的创造性产出。这在打字稿子等涉及大量体力劳动时或许可行，但整体而言，你们在使用"错误"的时间观念，尤其对任何创意艺术家而言。这点再次特别适用于鲁柏，尽管你在此方面也难辞其咎（略带幽默）。

quotes.text: In other words, the magical approach and the so-called rational one are to be combined in a certain fashion for best results. People sometimes write you, telling of their intent to make money -- or rather, to have it. They concentrate upon money, so they say, and wait for it in full faith that it will be attracted to them because of their belief and concentration. They might do the point of power exercise,2 for example. They may also, however, have quit their jobs, ignored impulses to find other work, or to take any rational approaches, and rely upon, say, the magical approach alone. This does not work either, of course.
quotes.translation: 换句话说，魔法途径与所谓的理性途径必须以特定方式结合，才能获得最佳效果。人们有时会写信给你，诉说他们想要赚钱——更确切地说，是拥有财富。他们声称自己专注于金钱，并以全然信念等待它被吸引而来，只因他们相信并专注于此。他们可能会进行力量之点练习，例如。然而，他们也可能辞去工作，忽略寻找其他工作的冲动，或放弃任何理性途径，完全依赖魔法途径。这当然也行不通。

related_concepts.name_en: The point of power is in the present

quotes.text: I would like you to recognize your own beliefs in several areas. You must realize that any idea you accept as truth is a belief that you hold. You must, then, take the next step and say, "It is not necessarily true, even though I believe it." You will, I hope, learn to disregard all beliefs that imply basic limitations.
quotes.translation: 我希望你能在几个领域里认识你自己的信念。你必须明白，任何你接受为真理的想法，都是你所持有的信念。接着，你必须迈出下一步，说："即使我相信它，它也不一定是真的。"我希望你会学会忽略所有暗示基本限制的信念。

quotes.text: In such instances a dream, or a series of them, will often then alter the person's beliefs in a way that could not otherwise occur, by providing new information. The same data might come in a state of inspiration, but it would in any case be the result of an acquisition of knowledge otherwise inaccessible. Love, purpose, belief, and intent -- these shape your physical body and work upon it and with it even as at other levels cellular consciousness forms it.
quotes.translation: 在这种情况下，一个梦或一系列梦，往往会通过提供新信息，以其他方式无法实现的方式改变这个人的信念。同样的数据可能以灵感状态出现，但无论如何，这将是获取原本无法获得的知识的结果。爱、目的、信念和意图——这些塑造了你的物质身体，并在其上、与之共同运作，正如在其他层面上，细胞意识形成了它一样。

quotes.text: As your conscious beliefs determine your bodily condition, and as your body is maintained at an unconscious level (though in line with your beliefs), so natural catastrophies are the result of the beliefs that give rise to emotional states which are then automatically transformed into exterior atmospheric conditions.
quotes.translation: 既然你有意识的信念决定了你的身体状况，而你的身体在无意识层面（虽与你的信念一致）维持运作，那么自然灾害正是信念的产物——信念引发情绪状态，这些情绪状态随后自动转化为外在的大气条件。

quotes.text: You must therefore understand and examine your beliefs, realize that they form your experience, and consciously change those that do not give the effects you want. In such an examination you will be aware of many excellent beliefs that work for you. Trace these through. See how they were followed by your imagination and emotions. If possible, look in your own past for points where recognizable new ideas came to you and beneficially changed your experience.
quotes.translation: 因此你必须理解并检视自己的信念，认识到它们塑造了你的经验，并有意识地改变那些无法带来预期效果的信念。在检视过程中，你会意识到许多对你有利的卓越信念。追溯这些信念的脉络，观察它们如何被你的想象力与情感所追随。若有可能，回顾你过往经历中那些带来可辨识新观念、并有益地改变你经验的转折点。

quotes.text: Belief systems are as necessary and natural as physical organs are. In fact, their purpose is to help you direct the functioning of your biological being. You give no conscious thought to the coming and going of cells within your organs. Left alone, your thoughts will come and go through your belief systems just as naturally; and ideally, they will balance out, maintaining their own health and directing your body so that its innate therapies take place.
quotes.translation: 信念系统与身体器官一样必要且自然。事实上，它们的目的是帮助你引导生物性存在的运作。你无需有意识地思考器官内细胞的生灭。若不加干预，你的念头自会如细胞般自然流经信念系统；理想状态下，它们会自行平衡，维持自身健康，并引导你的身体启动内在疗愈机制。

quotes.text: Your consciousness is not a thing that you possess. Your individuality is not a thing with limits. If you ask, "What is my individuality in all of this?" or, "Which 'I' am I?" then you are automatically thinking of yourself as a psychological entity with definite boundaries that must be protected at all costs. You may say, "I was born in a house on a certain street in a certain town, and no present belief to the contrary will change that fact." If, in the present, one past event can be altered within your neuronal structure, however, then basically no event is safe from such change.
quotes.translation: 你的意识并非你所拥有的物件。你的个体性亦非有界限之物。若你追问"我的个体性在这其中究竟为何？"或"哪个'我'才是我？"，你便自动将自己视为必须不惜代价守护其明确界限的心理实体。你或许会说："我出生于某镇某街某屋，任何与此刻相悖的信念都无法改变这个事实。"然而，若当下某个过往事件能在你的神经元结构中被改变，那么本质上没有任何事件能免于这种改变。

quotes.text: (9:35.) Men's dreams were also different in those times, filled far more with metaphysical images, for example, more alive with saints and demons -- but overall one framework of belief existed, and all experience was judged in its light. Now, you have far more decisions to make, and in a world of conflicting beliefs, brought into your living room through newspapers and television, you must try to find the meaning of your life, or the meaning of life.
quotes.translation: （九点三十五分）彼时人类的梦境亦不相同，充斥着更多形而上的意象——例如圣徒与恶魔更为鲜活的显现——但整体上存在单一的信念框架，所有经验皆以此为准绳。而今你们面临更多抉择，在信念冲突的世界里，当报纸与电视将矛盾观点送入客厅，你们必须竭力寻找自己生命的意义，或是生命本身的意义。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: As I have said before, your thoughts are reality. They directly affect your body. It seems that you are highly civilized people because you put your ill into hospitals where they can be cared for. What you do, of course, is to isolate a group of people who are filled with negative beliefs about illness. The contagion of beliefs spreads. Patients are obviously in hospitals because they are ill. The sick and their doctors both work on that principle.
quotes.translation: 正如我曾说过的，你的思想就是实相。它们直接影响你的身体。你们似乎自认为是高度文明的人类，因为你们把病人送进医院让他们得到照料。但你们实际所做的，不过是隔离一群充满负面疾病信念的人。信念的传染性会扩散。病人显然是因为生病才住院，而病人和医生都遵循这个原则运作。

quotes.text: There may or may not be disorientation on your part, according to your beliefs and development. Now I do not necessarily mean intellectual development. The intellect should go hand in hand with the emotions and intuitions, but if it pulls against these too strongly, difficulties can arise when the newly freed consciousness seizes upon its ideas about reality after death, rather than facing the particular reality in which it finds itself. It can deny feeling, in other words, and even attempt to argue itself out of its present independence from the body.
quotes.translation: 根据你的信念与发展程度，你可能会或不会感到迷失方向。我并非特指智力发展。智力应与情感和直觉并驾齐驱，但如果它过度对抗这两者，当新获自由的意识紧抓住死后实相的观念，而非面对它所处的特定实相时，困难就可能产生。换言之，它可能否认感受，甚至试图通过论证来说服自己摆脱当前脱离肉身的独立状态。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: During the day, Andrea was able to look at both beliefs and see them as opposing ideas that she had held about herself. She believed she was unique and good -- and also that she was inferior and bad. At various times one belief would color her experience nearly to the exclusion of the other. Just before this session Andrea called back. She realized that she had indeed set up the situation by not dealing honestly with her own conscious ideas.
quotes.translation: 白天时，安德莉亚得以审视两种信念，并将它们视为自己曾持有的对立想法。她相信自己既独特又善良——同时也觉得自己卑微又恶劣。在不同时期，其中一种信念几乎完全主导了她的经验，将另一种排除在外。就在这节课前，安德莉亚回电了。她意识到自己确实因未能诚实面对自身有意识的观念而制造了这种局面。

quotes.text: The only way out of it is to become aware of your beliefs, aware of your own conscious thought, and to change your beliefs so that you bring them more in line with the kind of reality you want to experience. Imagination and emotion will then automatically come into play to reinforce the new beliefs.
quotes.translation: 唯一的出路是觉察自己的信念，觉察自己有意识的思想，并改变信念，使其更符合你想要体验的那种实相。届时想象力与情感将自动发挥作用，强化这些新信念。

quotes.text: Such affirmation will lead you to your own inner discoveries, and attract from the deepest portions of your being the particular kind of information, experience, or perception that you need. The loving acceptance of yourself will allow you to ride through beliefs as you would through the changing characteristics of a countryside. The more a belief encourages you to use your abilities and vitality, then the more affirmative it is.
quotes.translation: 这样的肯定将引导你走向自己的内在发现，并从你存在的最深处吸引你所需的特定信息、经验或感知。对自己的慈爱接纳将允许你穿越信念，就像你穿越乡村不断变化的特征一样。一个信念越鼓励你运用你的能力和活力，它就越是肯定的。

quotes.text: Demons of any kind are the result of your beliefs. They are born from a belief in "unnatural" guilt. You may personify them. You may even meet them in your experience, but if so they are still the product of your immeasurable creativity, though formed by your guilt and your belief in it.
quotes.translation: 任何形式的魔鬼都是你信念的结果。它们诞生于对"非自然"罪疚的信念。你可以将它们拟人化，甚至可能在经验中遇见它们，但即便如此，它们仍是你那不可估量的创造力的产物，尽管是由你的罪疚及对其的信念所塑造。

quotes.text: His nature is protective. The basic ideas and beliefs that have been personified into his being, that became his being, were formed to protect Augustus One from the destructive ideas given to him in his childhood, to combat the beliefs in powerlessness and futility. To that degree they were added onto the original ideas, but still at an early age; so it was from the child's concept of a powerful being that Augustus Two sprang.
quotes.translation: 他的本性是保护性的。那些被拟人化进入他存在、成为他存在的基本观念和信念，是为了保护奥古斯都一世免受童年时期灌输的破坏性观念，对抗无力与徒劳的信念。在某种程度上，这些观念叠加在原始观念之上，但仍在早年形成；因此，奥古斯都二世正是源自孩子对强大存在的概念。

quotes.text: The inner self is embarked upon an exciting endeavor, in which it learns how to translate its reality into physical terms. The conscious mind is brilliantly attuned to physical reality, then, and often so dazzled by what it perceives that it is tempted to think physical phenomena is a cause, rather than a result. Deeper portions of the self always serve to remind it that this is not the case. When the conscious mind accepts too many false beliefs, particularly if it sees that inner self as a danger, then it closes out these constant reminders. When this situation arises the conscious mind feels itself assailed by a reality that seems greater than itself, over which it has no control. The deep feeling of security in which it should be anchored is lost.
quotes.translation: 内在自体正投身于一项激动人心的尝试，在其中学习如何将其实相转化为物质形式。意识心因此与物质实相完美协调，常被其所感知的事物如此炫目，以至于它倾向于认为物质现象是原因而非结果。自体的更深部分始终提醒它事实并非如此。当意识心接受了太多错误信念，尤其是当它视内在自体为危险时，它便会关闭这些持续的提醒。当这种情况发生时，意识心会感到被一个看似比它更强大、它无法控制的实相所侵袭。它本应扎根其中的深层安全感便丧失了。

quotes.text: Forget now that in this case such a division occurred, and imagine instead the successive thoughts and feelings that you possess. When you feel weak you are weak. When you feel joyful your body benefits and becomes stronger. Augustus's case simply shows in exaggerated form the effects of your beliefs upon your physical image. If you think, "Aha, then from now on I will only think good thoughts -- and therefore be healthy, and inhibit my 'bad' thoughts, or do anything at all with them but think them," then in your own way you are doing what Augustus did. He began by believing that some of his thoughts were so evil that they must somehow be made nonexistent. So inhibiting what you consider as negative thoughts, or assuming that they are so terrible, is no answer.
quotes.translation: 暂且忘记奥古斯都案例中发生的这种分裂，而是想象你拥有的连续思想和感受。当你感到虚弱时，你确实虚弱；当你感到喜悦时，你的身体受益并变得更强壮。奥古斯都的案例只是以夸张形式展示了你的信念对你身体形象的影响。如果你认为"啊哈，那么从今以后我只想好的念头——这样就能健康，并抑制我的'坏'念头，或者对它们做任何事就是不去想它们"，那么你正以你自己的方式重蹈奥古斯都的覆辙。他最初相信有些念头如此邪恶，以至于必须让它们不存在。因此，抑制你认为负面的念头，或假设它们如此可怕，绝非解决之道。

quotes.text: The individual, when it is time then, begins to see beyond temporal life, to open up dimensions of awareness that in your terms he or she could not afford while involved in the intense physical focus of normal adult life. Unfortunately the personality has no system of beliefs, as a rule, to support such an expansion. The natural therapies, both physical and mental, are denied. Drugs are often used as depressants, clouding the clarity of what seems to be distorted vision. This is one of the most creative, valuable aspects of your lives. Instead the old are made to feel useless in your society. Often of course they share this value judgment, and their experience within your communities has in no way prepared them to face subjective experience.
quotes.translation: 当个体面临生命终点时，便开始超越时间性生命，开启在你们看来正常成人生活因专注物质实相而无法触及的觉知维度。不幸的是，人格通常缺乏支持这种扩展的信念体系。自然疗法——无论是身体层面还是心理层面——都被否定了。药物常被用作抑制剂，遮蔽了看似扭曲的视野本应有的清晰度。这其实是你们生命中最具创造力、最有价值的部分之一。然而在你们社会中，老年人被灌输无用的感受。当然他们往往也认同这种价值判断，而他们在社群中的经历从未让他们准备好面对主观体验。

quotes.text: (10:30.) Use all of your energy and attention. Then forget about it. Do not check to see how well it is working. Simply make sure that in that period your intentions are clear. Then in one way or another, according to your own individual situation, make one physical gesture or act that is in line with your belief or desire. Behave physically, then, at least once a day in a way that shows that you have faith in what you are doing. The act can be a very simple one. If you are lonely and feel unwanted, it can merely involve your smiling at someone else. If you are poor, it can involve such a simple thing as buying an item you want that costs two cents more than the one you would usually buy -- acting on the faith, even that feebly, that the two cents will somehow be given you or come into your experience; but acting as if you had more than you do.
quotes.translation: （10:30。）运用你所有的能量和注意力。然后放下它。不要检查它运作得有多好。只需确保在那段时间里你的意图是清晰的。然后，根据你个人的具体情况，以某种方式做出一个符合你信念或欲望的身体姿态或行动。因此，每天至少一次以身体行为表明你对自己所做的事有信心。这个行动可以非常简单。如果你感到孤独和不被需要，它可以只是对某人微笑。如果你贫穷，它可以涉及一件简单的事，比如买一件你想要的、比平时买的那件贵两分钱的物品——凭着信心行动，即使这信心很微弱，相信那两分钱会以某种方式给予你或进入你的经验；但行动时仿佛你拥有的比实际更多。

quotes.text: In those areas in which you are dissatisfied, you feel that you are powerless, or that your will is paralyzed, or that conditions continue despite what you think of as your intent. Yet if you pay attention to your own quite conscious thoughts, you will find that you are concentrating upon precisely those negative aspects that so appall you. You are hypnotizing yourself quite effectively and so reinforcing the situation. You may say, horrified, "What can I do? I am hypnotizing myself into my overweight condition (or my loneliness, or my poor health)." Yet in other facets of your life you may be hypnotizing yourself into wealth, accomplishment, satisfaction -- and here you do not complain. The same issues are involved. The same principles are operating. In those positive life situations you are certain of your initiative. There is no doubt. Your beliefs become reality.
quotes.translation: 在那些你不满意的领域，你感到自己无能为力，或你的意志瘫痪，或尽管你认为自己有意图，情况却持续不变。然而，如果你注意自己相当清醒的思想，你会发现你恰恰在专注于那些让你如此惊恐的负面方面。你正在非常有效地自我催眠，从而强化了这种状况。你可能会惊恐地说："我能怎么办？我正在把自己催眠进超重的状态（或我的孤独，或我的健康不佳）。"然而，在你生活的其他方面，你可能正在把自己催眠进财富、成就、满足——而在这里你并不抱怨。同样的问题涉及其中。同样的原则在运作。在那些积极的生活情境中，你对自己的主动性确信无疑。没有怀疑。你的信念变成了现实。

quotes.text: This is an excellent example of the way in which natural hypnotism can act to affect your system adversely. In a manner of speaking, repetitious actions intimately involve beliefs at the "magical" level. The behavior usually represents efforts to ward off "evil" that the individual feels is imminent. While it is easy then to understand the nature of exterior actions of repetitive quality, it is far more difficult to see many physical symptoms in the same light -- but here also whole groups of recurring reactions to certain stimuli are involved. Behind them there is often the same kind of compulsion.
