In their own way symptoms frequently operate, actually, as repetitive neurological ritual, meant to protect the sufferer from something else that he fears even more.
quotes.translation: 这是一个绝佳的例子，说明自然催眠如何以不利方式影响你的系统。可以说，重复性行为在“魔法”层面上与信念紧密相连。这种行为通常代表个人试图抵御他们认为即将来临的“邪恶”。虽然理解重复性外在行为的本质很容易，但以同样视角看待许多身体症状却困难得多——但这里同样涉及对特定刺激的整组反复反应。它们背后往往存在同样的强迫性。实际上，症状常常以自身方式运作，如同重复性的神经仪式，旨在保护患者免受他们更恐惧的其他事物伤害。

quotes.text: This recognition can lead them -- and often does -- to seize their own energy and use it in a strong creative manner. A natural catastrophe or a riot are both energy baths, potent and highly positive in their ways despite their obvious connotations. In your terms this in no way absolves those who start riots, for example, for they will be working within a system of conscious beliefs in which violence begets violence. Yet even here individual differences apply. The inciters of riots are often searching for the manifestation of energy which they do not believe they possess on their own. They light and start psychological fires, and are as transfixed by the results as any arsonist. If they understood and could experience power and energy in themselves they would not need such tactics.
quotes.translation: 这种认知可以引导他们——而且常常确实如此——去抓住自己的能量，并以强烈的创造性方式运用它。自然灾害或暴乱都是能量浴，尽管它们带有明显的负面含义，但在其自身方式上却是强大且高度积极的。用你们的说法，这绝不意味着那些挑起暴乱的人可以免责，因为他们将在有意识的信念体系内运作，在这个体系中暴力只会滋生暴力。然而即使在这里，个体差异也存在。暴乱的煽动者常常在寻找他们相信自己并不拥有的能量显现。他们点燃并引发心理之火，并像任何纵火犯一样被结果所迷住。如果他们理解并能在自身中体验力量与能量，他们就不需要这样的策略。

quotes.text: Obviously, many riots are quite consciously instigated. Certainly thousands of individuals, or millions of them, do not consciously decide to bring about a hurricane, or a flood or an earthquake, in the same manner. In the first place, on that level they do not believe such a thing possible. While conscious beliefs have a part to play in such cases, on an individual basis the "inner work" is done just as unconsciously as the body produces physical symptoms. The symptoms often seem to be inflicted upon the body, just as a natural disaster seems to be visited upon the body of the earth. Sudden illnesses are thought of as frightening and unpredictable, with the sufferer a victim, perhaps, of a virus. Sudden tornadoes or earthquakes are seen in the same light, as the result of air currents and temperature, or fault lines instead of viruses. The basic causes of both, however, are the same.
quotes.translation: 显然，许多暴乱是有意识地煽动的。当然，成千上万或数百万的个体并不会以同样的方式有意识地决定引发飓风、洪水或地震。首先，在那个层面上，他们不相信这样的事情是可能的。虽然在这些情况下有意识的信念确实扮演了角色，但在个体基础上，"内在工作"就像身体产生生理症状一样无意识地进行。这些症状常常像是强加于身体的，就像自然灾害似乎降临于地球之体一样。突发疾病被认为是可怕且不可预测的，患者可能是病毒的受害者。突发龙卷风或地震也被以同样的方式看待，被视为气流和温度的结果，或是断层线而非病毒的结果。然而，两者的根本原因是相同的。

quotes.text: The man called Christ was not crucified. In the overall drama however it made little difference what was fact, in your terms, and what was not -- for the greater reality transcends facts and creates them. You have free will. You could interpret the drama as you wished. It was given to you. Its great creative power still exists and you use it in your own way, even changing your own symbolism as your beliefs change. But the main idea is the affirmation that the physical being, the self that you know, is not annihilated with death. This comes through even in the distortions. The whole concept of God the Father, as given by Christ, was indeed a "new testament." The male image of God was used because of the sex orientation of the times, but beyond this the Christ personality said, "... the kingdom of God is within (among) you" (Luke 17:21).
quotes.translation: 被称为基督的人并未被钉死在十字架上。然而在整体戏剧中，事实是什么——以你们的说法——与什么不是事实，其实并无多大差别——因为更大的实相超越事实并创造事实。你们拥有自由意志。你们可以随心所欲地诠释这出戏剧。它被赐予你们。它伟大的创造力依然存在，你们以自己的方式运用它，甚至随着信念的改变而改变自己的象征体系。但核心思想是肯定：你们所知的物质存在与自我，并不会随死亡而湮灭。这一点即使在扭曲的诠释中依然清晰可见。基督所传达的"天父"这一整体概念，确实是一份"新约"。神祇的男性形象之所以被采用，是由于当时社会的性别取向，但基督人格超越于此，说道："神的国就在你们心里（或：在你们中间）"（路加福音17:21）。

quotes.text: Many who write want to develop and use the same abilities, yet it is obvious from their letters that their beliefs prevent them from trusting the inner self enough. You cannot fear your own being and expect to travel through it, to explore its dimensions. First you must take the simple step of affirming your identity. That affirmation will release those attributes that you have and open up new avenues of experience. They will and must be your own. When you ask others to interpret your dreams, for example, you are automatically putting the fulfillment of your own potentials a step away. When you ask another to tell you the direction of your life, then to some extent you keep from yourself the realization that you yourself possess it. Without that awareness no methods will help you.
quotes.translation: 许多来信者渴望发展并运用同样的能力，但从他们的信件中明显看出，其信念阻碍了他们充分信任内在自我。你无法在恐惧自身存在的同时，期望穿越它、探索它的维度。首先你必须采取简单的一步：肯定自己的身份。这份肯定将释放你所拥有的特质，并开启新的经验途径。这些途径将且必须是属于你自己的。例如，当你请他人解读你的梦境时，你便自动将自身潜能的实现推远了一步。当你请他人告诉你人生的方向时，你便在某种程度上阻碍了自己认识到：你本就拥有这种方向。没有这份觉知，任何方法都无济于事。

quotes.text: 2. I think that as a child I often sensed my parents' feelings of strangeness about this reality, although I was quite unable to express myself in those terms. Perhaps I'm reinterpreting old memories in the light of Seth's material here. Consciously, however, I knew nothing then about probable realities or the power of belief; I was just acutely aware of the unending differences of opinion between my mother and father, and of my unformed questions about the reasons for their behavior; at the same time I saw them struggling to live like others I knew. I don't think I even discussed my confused feelings with my brothers as we grew older. On several occasions Seth has given very blunt, very perceptive interpretations of the churning relationship involving my parents. That material is too long and complex to excerpt here, but I'd like to treat it separately sometime.
quotes.translation: 2. 我想，童年时我常能感知到父母对这个世界的不安感，尽管当时我完全无法用这些词汇表达自己。或许我正在用赛斯资料重新诠释旧日记忆。但就意识层面而言，那时我对可能实相或信念的力量一无所知；我只是敏锐察觉到父母之间无休止的分歧，以及我对他们行为缘由那些尚未成形的疑问；同时我看到他们挣扎着要像我所认识的其他人那样生活。随着年岁渐长，我甚至不记得曾与兄弟们讨论过这些困惑。赛斯曾多次对我父母之间翻腾的关系做出非常直率、非常透彻的解读。那段资料过于冗长复杂，无法在此摘录，但改日我愿单独探讨。

quotes.text: Thoughts have their own kind of structure, as cells do, and they seek their own fulfillment. They move toward like thoughts, and you have as a species an inner mass body of thought. Privately your thoughts are expressions of your idealizations; and while expressing those inner patterns they also modify and creatively change them. Each cell in your body is to some extent altered with each thought that you think. Each reaction of the cells alters your environment. The brain then responds to the alteration. There is a constant give-and-take. As the cells respond at certain levels to ever-changing streams of probabilities, so do your thoughts. Your body responds as you think it should, however, and so your conscious beliefs about reality have much to do with those probable experiences that you accept as a part of your intimate living.
quotes.translation: 思想如同细胞般拥有自身的结构，它们寻求自身的圆满。它们会趋向同类思想，而你们作为物种拥有内在的集体思想体。私密而言，你们的思想是你们理想化的表达；在表达这些内在模式的同时，它们也修改并创造性地改变这些模式。你身体的每个细胞都会因你所想的每个念头而有所改变。细胞的每个反应都会改变你的环境。大脑随后会对这种改变做出反应。存在着持续的互动。正如细胞在特定层面回应不断变化的可能性之流，你的思想也是如此。然而，你的身体会按照你相信它应有的方式做出反应，因此你对实相的有意识信念，与你接受为亲密生活一部分的那些可能经验密切相关。

quotes.text: Because you have in the past convinced yourselves that the conscious mind must of necessity be cut off from inner reality, you think that it must be alienated from the dream state. Following such beliefs, you find yourselves thinking of dreaming as chaotic, unreasonable, and as completely divorced from normal conscious direction, purpose, or function. It often seems that sleep is almost a small death, and psychologists have compared dreaming with controlled insanity.5 You have so divorced your waking and dreaming experience that it seems you have separate "lives," and that there is little connection between your waking and dreaming hours. The rich tapestry of probable actions from which you choose your official life becomes just as invisible. This is quite needless.
quotes.translation: 由于过去你们说服自己，认为有意识心智必须与内在实相隔绝，你们便认为它必须与梦境状态分离。遵循这样的信念，你们发现自己将做梦视为混乱、不合理，且完全脱离正常有意识的引导、目的或功能。睡眠常常看似一场小死亡，心理学家甚至将做梦比作可控的疯狂。你们如此割裂清醒与做梦的经验，以至于仿佛拥有分离的"生命"，清醒与做梦的时光之间几乎毫无关联。你们从中选择官方生活的可能行动之丰富织锦，也因此变得不可见。这完全是不必要的。

quotes.text: (With emphasis:) Now in your framework of beliefs the psychiatrists and the doctors are helpful. They know more than you do about the techniques upon which you all agree. While the society accepts these techniques, then you are to some extent dependent upon them, and you had better think twice before you let them go. But in greater, more vital issues, the sick doctor does not know as much about health as an "uneducated, untrained," but healthy person -- and I am speaking in quite practical terms. The person who is healthy understands the dynamics of health. In your framework it seems that his or her understanding can be of little practical value to you if you are, for instance, unhealthy. But a true medical profession would be, literally, a health profession. It would seek out people who were healthy and learn from them how to promote health, and not how to diagram disease.
quotes.translation: （加重语气说：）在你们的信念体系里，精神科医生和医生确实有帮助。他们比你们更了解你们共同认可的那些技巧。只要社会还接受这些技巧，你们就在某种程度上依赖它们，在放弃它们之前最好三思。但在更重大、更关键的问题上，生病的医生对健康的了解，还不如一个"没受过教育、没经过训练"但健康的人——我说的是非常实际的话。健康的人懂得健康的动力。在你们的体系里，如果你不健康，他或她的理解似乎对你没什么实际价值。但真正的医学职业，字面上说，应该是健康职业。它会寻找健康的人，向他们学习如何促进健康，而不是如何绘制疾病图谱。

quotes.text: The inner lands have not been as well explored. To say the least, they lie in virgin territory as far as your conscious mind is concerned. Others have journeyed to some of these interior locales, but since they were indeed explorers they had to learn as they went along. Some, returning, provided guidebooks or travel folders, telling us what could be expected. You make your own reality. If you were from a foreign land and asked one person to give you a description of New York City, you might take his or her description for reality. The person might say "New York City is a frightful place in which crime is rampant, gangs roam the streets, murders and rapes are the norm, and people are not only impolite but ready to attack you at a moment's notice. There are no trees. The air is polluted, and you can expect only violence." If you asked someone else, this individual might say instead: "New York City has the finest of museums, open-air concerts in some of the parks, fine sculpture, theater, and probably the greatest collection of books outside of the Vatican. It has a good overall climate, a great mixture of cultures. In it, millions of people go their way daily in freedom." Period. Both people would be speaking about the same locale. Their descriptions would vary because of their private beliefs, and would be colored by the individual focus from which each of them viewed that city.
quotes.translation: 内在领域尚未被充分探索。至少可以说，就你们意识心智所知，它们处于处女地。其他人曾旅行到某些内在地点，但因为他们确实是探索者，必须边走边学。有些人回来后提供了指南或旅行手册，告诉我们可能会遇到什么。你创造你自己的实相。如果你来自异国，请一个人描述纽约市，你可能会把他或她的描述当作实相。这个人可能会说："纽约市是个可怕的地方，犯罪猖獗，帮派横行街头，谋杀和强奸是常态，人们不仅不礼貌，而且随时准备攻击你。没有树木。空气污染，你只能期待暴力。"如果你问另一个人，这个人可能会说："纽约市有最好的博物馆，公园里的露天音乐会，精美的雕塑，剧院，可能还有梵蒂冈以外最丰富的藏书。它气候总体良好，文化多元。在这里，数百万人每天自由地走自己的路。"句号。两个人都在说同一个地方。他们的描述因个人信念而异，并因每个人看待那座城市的个人焦点而着色。

quotes.text: You may, for example, have read books numbering the "inner realms," and telling you what you can expect to encounter in each. Many of these speak of lords or gods of the realm, or of demons. In a strange way these books do provide a service, for at certain levels you will find your own ideas materialized; and if you believe in demons then in those terms you will encounter them. The authors, however, suppose that the devils have a reality outside of your belief in them, and such is not the case. The demons simply represent a state of your own mind that is seemingly out there, objectified. Therefore, whatever methods the authors used to triumph over these demons is often given as proof not only of the demons' reality, but of each method's effectiveness.
quotes.translation: 例如，你可能读过一些书，列举了"内在领域"，告诉你每个领域会遇到什么。许多书提到领域的主宰或神祇，或恶魔。奇怪的是，这些书确实提供了一种服务，因为在某些层面，你会发现自己的想法具体化；如果你相信恶魔，那么在这些层面上你会遇到它们。然而，作者们假设恶魔在你相信它们之外有实相，但事实并非如此。恶魔只是代表你自己心智的一种状态，看似在外面，被客观化了。因此，无论作者用什么方法战胜这些恶魔，往往不仅被当作恶魔实相的证据，也被当作每种方法有效性的证明。

quotes.text: That kind of structuring also does a disservice, however, for it prevents you from coming in contact with your own original concepts. There is no reason, for example, to encounter any demons or devils in any trance or out-of-body condition.3 (Pause.) In such cases your own hallucinations blind you to the environment within which they are projected. When your consciousness is not directly focused in physical reality, then, the great creativity of the psyche is given fuller play. All of its dimensions are faithfully and instantly produced as experience when you learn to take your "normally alert" conscious mind with you; and when you are free of such limiting ideas, then at those levels you can glimpse the inner powers of your own psyche, and watch the interplay of beliefs and symbols as they are manifested before your eyes. Until you learn to do this you will most certainly have difficulty, for you will not be able to tell the difference between your projections and what is happening in the inner environment.
quotes.translation: 然而，这种结构方式也会造成损害，因为它阻碍你接触自己原创的概念。例如，在任何出神或出体状态中，根本没有理由遭遇任何恶魔或鬼怪。（停顿）在这种情况下，你自己的幻觉会蒙蔽你，使你无法看清它们所投射的环境。当你的意识不直接聚焦于物质实相时，心灵的巨大创造力就会得到更充分的发挥。当你学会带着"正常警觉"的意识同行，并摆脱这些限制性观念时，你就能在那个层面窥见自己心灵的内在力量，目睹信念与象征在你眼前展现的互动。除非你学会这一点，否则你必定会遇到困难，因为你将无法分辨自己的投射与内在环境中实际发生的事。

quotes.text: One of your beliefs, then, was a strong joint one that you had to protect your energy at all costs, and block out worldly distractions. Ruburt with his practical mind interpreted this more literally than you did, and physical restriction was a part of his natural early environment [because of his mother's chronic illness], as it was not in yours. But he is amazingly resilient ... The power of his will is indeed awesome, and he is just now beginning to feel it. With that awareness, it can be used in a new physically oriented directive. The altered directive is all that is necessary. The rest will unconsciously follow.... The point of power is in the present;13 this kind of material and its understanding [by Ruburt and others] is more important than "past" causes.
quotes.translation: 因此，你们的一个强烈共同信念是：必须不惜一切代价保护自己的能量，隔绝世俗的干扰。鲁柏以其实用思维，比你更字面地诠释了这一点，而身体限制是他早期自然生活环境的一部分（因其母亲的慢性病），这在你身上并不存在。但他具有惊人的韧性……他的意志力确实令人敬畏，而他此刻才刚刚开始感受到它。有了这份觉知，它就能被用于新的、以身体为导向的指令。所需的仅仅是改变指令，其余部分将无意识地随之而来……力量就在当下这一刻；13 这类资料及其（被鲁柏等人）的理解，比"过去"的原因更为重要。

related_concepts.name_en: The point of power is in the present

quotes.text: Each person will have his or her own private experience here, but gradually certain kinds of physical data will seem to disappear while others may take prominence. For example, you might mentally hear sounds, while knowing they have no physical origin. You may see nothing in your mind, or you may see images that seem to have no exterior correlation, but you may hear nothing. For a while ordinary physical data may continue to intrude. When it does, recognize it as your home station, and mentally let yourself drift further away from it. What is important is your own sensation as you experience the mobility of your consciousness. If ever you grow concerned simply return to your home station, back to the left or right according to the direction you have chosen. I do not suggest that you use "higher" or "lower" as directions, because of the interpretations that you may have placed upon them through your beliefs.
quotes.translation: 每个人在此都会有各自独特的体验，但逐渐地，某些物质数据会看似消失，而另一些则可能变得突出。例如，你可能会在脑海中听到声音，同时明知它们没有物质来源。你可能在脑海中什么也看不见，也可能看见似乎与外界无关的影像，但什么也听不见。有一阵子，普通的物质数据可能会继续侵入。当它发生时，请认出那是你的"本台"，并在心智上让自己进一步漂离它。重要的是你在体验意识流动性时的自身感受。若你感到担忧，只需返回你的"本台"，根据你选择的方向向左或向右回归。我不建议你使用"上"或"下"作为方向，因为你可能已通过自己的信念为它们赋予了特定的诠释。

quotes.text: Evolutionary thinking is challenged not only by questions of protein synthesis, and energy/entropy (see Note 5), however. Equally insistent are the puzzles posed by the missing intermediate forms in the fossil record: Where are all the remnants of those creatures that linked birds, reptiles, cats, monkeys, and human beings? The hypothetical evolutionary tree of life demands that such in-between forms existed; it seems that by now paleontologists should have unearthed enough signs of them to make at least a modest case for their belief systems; the lack of scientific evidence is embarrassing. Since my mind works that way, I could make minutely detailed drawings of a graduated series of such entities (gradualism being a basic premise in Charles Darwin's theory), but would the creatures shown have been viable? Could they actually have existed for the necessary millennia while evolving into the species whose fossil remains have been discovered, or that live today? As indicated in Note 5, evolutionists are serving goodly portions of speculation along with inadequate theory -- or, really, hypothesis.
quotes.translation: 然而，进化论思想不仅受到蛋白质合成与能量/熵问题的挑战（见注释5）。化石记录中缺失的中间形态同样构成令人费解的谜题：那些连接鸟类、爬行动物、猫、猴子和人类的生物遗骸究竟在哪里？假设的生命进化树要求这类中间形态确实存在；按理说，古生物学家如今应该已发掘出足够证据，至少能为他们的信念体系提供些许支撑；科学证据的缺失令人尴尬。以我的思维方式，我能精确绘制出这类生物渐进系列的细节图（渐进论是查尔斯·达尔文理论的基本前提），但所描绘的生物真能存活吗？它们是否真能在进化成那些已发现化石或现今存活的物种所需的千万年间实际存在？如注释5所示，进化论者正用不充分的理论——确切说是假说——搭配大量臆测。

quotes.text: You are social creatures, as the animals are. Despite many of your cherished, erroneous beliefs, your nations exist as the result of cooperation, not competition, as do all social groupings. To be ostracized is no laughing matter. The comfort of social discourse represents one of the great building blocks of families and civilizations. The set criteria of reality, therefore, operate as organizing, psychic, and physical frameworks. There is still more flexibility within those frameworks, however, than is recognized. You still try to carry your own cultural versions of reality into the dream state, for example, but the natural heritage of both body and mind escapes such repression -- and despite yourselves, in your dreams you come in touch with a greater picture of reality that will not be shunted aside.
quotes.translation: 你们是社会性生物，正如动物一样。尽管你们珍视许多错误的信念，但你们国家的存在是合作的结果，而非竞争，所有社会群体皆如此。被排斥绝非儿戏。社会交流的慰藉是家庭与文明的伟大基石之一。因此，现实的设定标准作为组织性的、心灵的和物质的框架运作。然而，这些框架内的灵活性仍未被充分认识。例如，你们仍试图将自己文化版本的现实带入梦境状态，但身体与心灵的自然遗产会逃脱这种压抑——尽管你们不情愿，在梦中你们会接触到一幅更宏大的现实图景，它不会被轻易搁置一旁。

quotes.text: Dreams deal with associations and with emotional validities that often do not seem to make sense in the usual world. I said before that no one can really give you a definition of the psyche. It must be experienced. Since its activities, wisdom and perception rise largely from another kind of reference, then you must often learn to interpret your encounter with the psyche to your usual self. One of the largest difficulties here is the issue of organization. In regular life, you organize your experience very neatly and push it into accepted patterns or channels, into preconceived ideas and beliefs. You tailor it to fit time sequences. Again: The psyche's organization follows no such learned predisposition. Its products can often appear chaotic simply because they splash over your accepted ideas about what experience is.
quotes.translation: 梦境处理的是联想与情感的真实性，这些在通常世界中往往显得不合逻辑。我之前说过，没有人能真正给你一个关于心灵的定义。它必须被体验。既然它的活动、智慧与感知主要源于另一种参照系，那么你必须常常学会将你与心灵的相遇解读给通常的自我。这里最大的困难之一是组织问题。在常规生活中，你非常整齐地组织你的经验，将其推入公认的模式或渠道，推入先入为主的观念与信念。你将其裁剪以适应时间序列。再次强调：心灵的组织并不遵循这种习得的倾向。它的产物常常显得混乱，仅仅因为它们溢出了你关于经验是什么的既定观念。

quotes.text: Any information or knowledge must have a pattern if you are going to understand it at all. Ruburt's own painting, his knowledge of his psychic abilities, his love of Joseph -- all served to form a pattern which then attracted the Cézanne material. He received this "automatically," writing down the words that came almost too quickly for him to follow. His craft or art of writing brought the material to clear focus. The information itself, however, had nothing to do with words, but with an overall comprehension of the nature of painting, a direct knowing. Ruburt used his own abilities as a container, then. This direct kind of knowledge is available, again, on any subject, to anyone who provides a suitable pattern through desire, love, intent or belief.
quotes.translation: 任何信息或知识若要被理解，必然有其模式。鲁柏的绘画、他对自身通灵能力的认知、对约瑟的爱——这些共同构成了一个模式，进而吸引了塞尚的资料。他"自动"接收这些内容，记下几乎快得让他跟不上的文字。他的写作技艺使这些资料清晰聚焦。然而信息本身与文字无关，而是对绘画本质的整体领悟，一种直接的认知。鲁柏将自己的能力作为容器。这种直接认知同样适用于任何主题，只要有人通过渴望、爱、意图或信念提供合适的模式。

quotes.text: To the male child, the penis is something that belongs to him personally in the same way that an arm or leg does, or that his mouth or anus does. He does not consider it a weapon (humorously). He is not jealous of his father's love for the mother, for he understands quite well that her love for him is just as strong. He does not wish to possess his mother sexually in the way that adults currently suppose. He does not understand those terms. He may at times be jealous of her attention, but this is not a sexual jealousy in conventionally understood terms. Your beliefs blind you to the sexual nature of children. They do enjoy their bodies. They are sexually aroused. The psychological connotations, however, are not those assigned to them by adults.
quotes.translation: 对男童而言，阴茎就像手臂、腿、嘴巴或肛门一样，是属于自己的身体部位。他并不将其视为武器（幽默地说）。他不会嫉妒父亲对母亲的爱，因为他很清楚母亲对他的爱同样强烈。他并不像成年人目前所认为的那样，希望以性方式占有母亲。他根本不理解那些术语。他有时可能会嫉妒母亲对别人的关注，但这并非传统意义上的性嫉妒。你们的信念蒙蔽了你们对儿童性本质的认识。他们确实享受自己的身体，确实会被性唤起。然而，其心理内涵并非成年人所赋予的那些。

quotes.text: Involved in the arguments are the leading cancer investigative organizations in the country. For example: Scientific advisers to the government's National Cancer Institute, which is conducting elaborate studies of many thousands of women of varying ages, have called for a halt to the routine screening of younger women. These scientists are on record as stating that such X-raying may cause more breast cancer than it cures. Many millions of dollars, and much time and effort, have been and are being given to such research programs. It will be difficult to alter those studies because of entrenched belief systems. Even the economic factors become important: Beside the great sums involved in the "official" programs, for instance, many private radiologists have also found mammography screening to be quite profitable.
quotes.translation: 争论中涉及了国内领先的癌症研究机构。例如：政府国家癌症研究所的科学顾问们正在进行针对数千名不同年龄女性的详尽研究，他们呼吁停止对年轻女性进行常规筛查。这些科学家公开表示，这种X光检查可能引发比治愈更多的乳腺癌。数百万美元以及大量时间和精力已经被投入并将继续投入这些研究项目中。由于根深蒂固的信念系统，改变这些研究将非常困难。甚至经济因素也变得重要：除了“官方”项目涉及的巨额资金外，许多私人放射科医生也发现乳房X光筛查相当有利可图。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: I'm well aware of current scientific theories about the supposed separate functions of the two hemispheres of the brain: The left half is said to control logical activities like writing, while the right half is responsible for the intuitive artistic abilities. Perhaps -- but after all, writing can be intuitively based, and art can be logically produced. At least the whole brain (its hemispheres are connected deep within by the corpus callosum) must contain that necessary basic creative ability that may then be apportioned out -- but only to an extent, I think -- between the hemispheres. Barring physical injury/surgery, there must be more communication between its halves (via the corpus callosum) than the brain is given credit for. There's so much we don't know yet about the brain (let alone the mind!). What about telepathy between the hemispheres? I think the divisions charted for the brain so far may be too strict, that beliefs about such separations may get in the way of our perception of the brain's beautifully whole operation.
quotes.translation: 我很清楚当前关于大脑两个半球所谓独立功能的科学理论：左半脑据说控制写作等逻辑活动，而右半脑负责直觉性的艺术能力。也许如此——但毕竟，写作可以基于直觉，艺术也可以逻辑地产生。至少整个大脑（其半球通过深处的胼胝体相连）必须包含那种必要的基本创造能力，然后这种能力可能被分配到半球之间——但我认为只是在一定程度上。除非有身体损伤或手术，否则大脑两半球之间（通过胼胝体）的交流一定比我们所认为的要多。我们对大脑的了解还太少（更不用说心灵了！）。两半球之间是否存在心电感应？我认为目前对大脑的划分可能过于严格，关于这种分离的信念可能会阻碍我们感知大脑那美丽的整体运作。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Such events occur as a result of individual beliefs, desires, and intents. There is no such thing as a chance encounter. No death occurs by chance, nor any birth. In the creative atmosphere of Framework 2, intents are known. In a manner of speaking, no act is private. Your communication systems bring to your living room notices of events that occur throughout the world. Yet that larger inner system of communications is far more powerful in scope, and each mental act is imprinted in the multidimensional screen of Framework 2. That screen is available to all, and in other levels of consciousness, particularly in the sleep and dreaming stages, the events of that inner reality are as ever-present and easily accessible as physical events are when you are awake.
quotes.translation: 这类事件的发生源于个人的信念、欲望和意图。世上没有偶然的相遇，也没有偶然的死亡或出生。在架构二的创造性氛围中，意图是彼此知晓的。可以说，没有哪个行为是私密的。你们的通讯系统将世界各地发生的事件带入客厅，然而那个更宏大的内在通讯系统在范围上要强大得多，每个心智活动都烙印在架构二的多维屏幕上。这个屏幕对所有人都开放，而在其他意识层面，尤其是在睡眠和梦境阶段，那个内在实相的事件就像你们清醒时感知物质事件一样，始终存在且易于获取。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: They cast their light over historical events because they are responsible for those events. They mix and merge the inner, unseen but felt, eternal psychic experience of man with the temporal events of his physical days, and form a combination that structures thoughts and beliefs from civilization to civilization. In Framework 2 the interior power of nature is ever-changing. The dreams, hopes, aspirations and fears of man interact in a constant motion that then forms the events of your world. That interaction includes not only man, of course, but the emotional reality of all earthly consciousnesses as well, from a microbe to a scholar, from a frog to a star. You interpret the phenomena of your world according to the mythic characteristics that you have accepted. You organize physical reality, then, through ideas. You use only those perceptions that serve to give those ideas validity. The physical body itself is quite capable of putting the world together in different fashions than the one that is familiar to you.
quotes.translation: 它们将光芒投射在历史事件上，因为正是它们造就了这些事件。它们将人类内在的、不可见却可感知的永恒心灵经验，与物质岁月的短暂事件混合交融，形成一种组合，从而在文明之间构建起思想和信念。在架构二中，自然的内在力量不断变化。人类的梦想、希望、渴望和恐惧在持续运动中相互作用，进而形成你们世界的事件。当然，这种相互作用不仅包括人类，还包括从微生物到学者、从青蛙到星辰的所有地球意识的情感实相。你们根据所接受的神话特征来诠释世界现象。因此，你们通过观念来组织物质实相，只使用那些能赋予这些观念有效性的感知。物质身体本身完全有能力以不同于你们熟悉的方式组合世界。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: In evolution man's nature is amoral, and anything goes for survival's sake. There is no possibility of any spiritual survival as far as most evolutionists are concerned. The fundamentalists would rather believe in man's inherent sinful nature, for at least their belief system provides for a framework in which he can be saved. Christ's message was that each man is good inherently, and is an individualized portion of the divine -- and yet a civilization based upon that precept has never been attempted. The vast social structures of Christianity were instead based upon man's "sinful" nature -- not the organizations and structures that might allow him to become good, or to obtain the goodness that Christ quite clearly perceived man already possessed.
quotes.translation: 在进化论中，人的本性是非道德的，为了生存可以不择手段。就大多数进化论者而言，灵魂的存续毫无可能。原教旨主义者宁愿相信人类与生俱来的罪性，至少他们的信仰体系提供了得救的框架。基督的讯息是：每个人生而良善，是神性的一部分个体化呈现——然而从未有过基于这一教义的文明尝试。基督教庞大的社会结构反而建立在人的"罪性"之上——而非那些可能让人变得良善，或获得基督清楚看见人类早已拥有的良善的组织架构。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Now: The beliefs of [Charles] Darwin and of [Sigmund] Freud3 alike have formed together to give you a different screen. Experience is accepted and perceived only as it is sieved through that screen. If Christendom saw man as blighted by original sin, Darwinian and Freudian views see him as part of a flawed species in which individual life rests precariously, ever at the beck and call of the species' needs, and with survival as the prime goal -- a survival, however, without meaning. The psyche's grandeur is ignored, the individual's sense of belonging with nature eroded, for it is at nature's expense, it seems, that he must survive. One's greatest dreams and worst fears alike become the result of glandular imbalance, or of neuroses from childhood traumas.
quotes.translation: 现在：达尔文与弗洛伊德的信念共同为你构筑了不同的滤网。经验唯有通过这层滤网筛选才能被接受与感知。若基督教世界视人为原罪所玷污，达尔文与弗洛伊德的观点则视人为有缺陷物种的一部分，个体生命岌岌可危，永远受制于物种需求，以生存为首要目标——然而这种生存毫无意义。心灵的庄严被忽视，个体与自然的归属感被侵蚀，因为似乎必须以牺牲自然为代价才能生存。人类最伟大的梦想与最深的恐惧同样沦为腺体失调或童年创伤引发的神经官能症。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Many people lost their lives recently in the tragedy of [Jonestown] Guyana. People willingly took poison at the command of their leader. No armies stood outside the grounds. No bombs fell. There was no physical virus that spread through the multitude. There was no clothing to decorate the mechanisms of events. Those people succumbed to an epidemic of beliefs, to an environment [that was] closed mentally and physically. The villains consisted of the following ideas: that the world is unsafe, and growing deadly; that the species itself is tainted by a deadly intent; that the individual has no power over his or her reality; that society or social conditions exist as things in themselves, and that their purposes run directly counter to the fulfillment of the individual; and lastly, that the end justifies the means, and that the action of any kind of god is powerless in the world.
quotes.translation: 最近许多人死于圭亚那琼斯镇的悲剧。人们听从领袖的命令自愿服毒。没有军队驻扎在场地外，没有炸弹落下，没有病毒在人群中传播，也没有任何装饰物来粉饰事件机制。那些人屈服于信念的流行病，屈服于一个精神与肉体双重封闭的环境。罪魁祸首包含以下观念：世界不安全且日益致命；人类物种本身被致命意图污染；个体无力掌控自己的实相；社会或社会状况是独立存在的事物，其目的与个体实现直接对立；最后，目的正当化手段，而任何形式的神明力量在世间都无能为力。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Religion and science both loudly proclaim their search for truth, although they are seemingly involved in completely opposing systems. They both treat their beliefs as truths (underlined), with which no one should tamper. They search for beginnings and endings. The scientists have their own vocabulary, which is used to reinforce the exclusive nature of science. Now I am speaking of the body of science in general terms here, for there is in a way a body of science that exists as a result of each individual scientist's participation. A given scientist may act quite differently in his family life and as a scientist. He may love his family dog, for example, while at the same time think nothing of injecting other animals with diseased tissue in his professional capacity.
quotes.translation: 宗教与科学都大声宣称自己追求真理，尽管它们看似处于完全对立的体系中。两者都将自己的信念视为真理（下划线强调），不容他人篡改。它们追寻开端与终结。科学家们拥有自己的专业术语，用以强化科学的排他性。此处我泛论科学体系——某种程度上，科学体系因每位科学家的参与而存在。某位科学家在家庭生活中与作为科学家时可能判若两人：他或许深爱家中的狗，却同时以专业身份毫无顾忌地将病变组织注入其他动物体内。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: (10:20.) I want to make it plain that such ideas are rampant in society, and are at the basis of many personal and national problems. They are behind large issues, involved in the [Three Mile Island] nuclear fiasco, for example, and in the scientist's idea of power and creation. Both of you, highly creative, find your creativity in conflict with your ideas of sexuality, privately and in your stances with the world. Much of this is involved with the unfortunate myths about the creative person, who is not supposed to be able to deal with the world as well as others, whose idiosyncrasies are exaggerated, and whose very creativity, it is sometimes said, leads to suicide or depression. No wonder few numbers of creative people persist in the face of such unfortunate beliefs!
quotes.translation: （10:20）我要明确指出，这类观念在社会中泛滥成灾，成为许多个人问题与国家问题的根源。它们潜藏于重大议题背后——例如三里岛核事故，以及科学家对权力与创造的理解。你们两位极具创造力的人，无论私下还是面对世界时，都发现自己的创造力与性观念产生冲突。这很大程度上与关于创造者的不幸迷思有关：人们认为创造者不如常人善于处世，其怪癖被夸大，甚至有人说创造力本身会导致自杀或抑郁。面对如此不幸的信念，难怪鲜有创造者能坚持到底！
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Through such methods, and through such group hysteria, the responsibility for separate acts is divorced from the individual, and rests instead upon the group, where it becomes generalized and dispersed. The cause, whatever it is, can then cover any number of crimes, and no particular individual need bear the blame alone. Fanatics have tunnel vision, so that any beliefs not fitting their purposes are ignored. Those that challenge their own purposes, however, become instant targets of scorn and attack. (Pause.) Generally speaking in your society, power is considered a male attribute. Cult leaders are more often male than female, and females are more often than not followers, because they have been taught that it is wrong for them to use power, and right for them to follow the powerful.
quotes.translation: 通过这类方法，通过这种群体歇斯底里，个体行为的责任被剥离，转而归于群体，变得泛化而分散。无论原因为何，都能掩盖无数罪行，无需特定个体独自承担罪责。狂热者拥有隧道视野，任何不符合其目的的信念都被无视。然而，那些挑战其目的的信念，会立刻成为轻蔑与攻击的目标。（停顿）一般而言，在你们社会中，权力被视为男性特质。邪教领袖多为男性，女性则多为追随者，因为她们被教导：运用权力对她们而言是错误的，追随强者才是正确的。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Some people are only aware of -- or largely aware of -- impulses toward anger, because they have inhibited those natural impulses toward love that would otherwise temper what seemed to be aggressive desires. When you begin trusting yourselves, you start by taking it for granted that to some extent at least you have not trusted yourself or your impulses in the past: You have thought that impulses were dangerous, disruptive, or even evil. So as you begin to learn self-trust, you acknowledge your impulses. You try them on for size. You see where they lead you by allowing them some freedom. You do not follow urges through that would hurt others physically, or that seem in direct contradiction to your present beliefs -- but you do acknowledge them. You do try to discover their source. Behind them you will almost always find an inhibited impulse -- or many of them -- that motivated you to move in some ideal direction, to seek a love or understanding so idealized in your mind that it seemed impossible to achieve. You are left with the impulse to strike out.
quotes.translation: 有些人只意识到——或主要意识到——愤怒的冲动，因为他们压抑了那些本可调和看似攻击性欲望的自然之爱的冲动。当你们开始信任自己时，首先要理所当然地承认：至少在一定程度上，你们过去并未信任自己或自己的冲动——你们曾认为冲动是危险的、破坏性的，甚至邪恶的。因此，当你们开始学习自我信任时，你们要承认自己的冲动。你们要试穿它们，看看是否合身。你们通过给予它们一些自由，观察它们将你们引向何方。你们不会遵循那些会伤害他人身体、或与你们当前信念直接矛盾的冲动——但你们确实承认它们。你们确实试图发现它们的源头。在它们背后，你们几乎总会发现一个被压抑的冲动——或许多个——它们曾激励你们朝向某个理想方向行动，去寻求一种在你们心中如此理想化、以至于看似不可能实现的爱或理解。你们最终只剩下出击的冲动。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: This sense of division within the self forces you to think that there is a remote, spiritual, wise, intuitive inner self, and a bewildered, put-upon, spiritually ignorant, inferior physical self, which happens to be the one you identify with. Many of you believe, moreover, that the physical self's very nature is evil, that its impulses, left alone, will run in direct opposition to the good of the physical world and society, and fly in the face of the deeper spiritual truths of inner reality. The inner self then becomes so idealized and so remote that by contrast the physical self seems only the more ignorant and flawed. In the face of such beliefs the ideal of psychic development, or astral travel, or spiritual knowledge, or even of sane living, seems so remote as to be impossible. You must, therefore, begin to celebrate your own beings, to look to your own impulses as being the natural connectors between the physical and the nonphysical self. Children trusting their impulses learn to walk, and trusting your impulses, you can find yourselves again.
quotes.translation: 这种自我内部的割裂感迫使你们认为：存在一个遥远、灵性、智慧、直觉的内在自我，以及一个困惑、受欺、灵性无知、低劣的物质自我——而你们恰好认同于后者。此外，你们许多人相信，物质自我的本质就是邪恶的，它的冲动若不加约束，将直接违背物质世界与社会的善，并公然对抗内在实相更深层的灵性真理。于是内在自我变得如此理想化且遥不可及，相比之下，物质自我似乎更加无知与缺陷重重。面对这样的信念，心灵发展、星光体出游、灵性知识、甚至健康生活的理想，都显得如此遥远，以至于不可能实现。因此，你们必须开始庆祝自己的存在，将你们的冲动视为物质自我与非物质自我之间的自然连接器。信任自己冲动的孩子学会了走路，而信任自己的冲动，你们可以重新找到自己。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: We're not against reincarnation, then, only careful about our beliefs concerning it. Within the context of my discussion, reincarnation is Seth's historical version of his counterpart concept, which is that each of us is physically connected with certain other males and females who are living at the same time we are, and who are exploring physical life from a variety of viewpoints in ways that no one physical self could possibly match. This means that each reincarnational self has its own cluster of counterpart selves within its own time period, and that all are interconnected on nonphysical levels, joining together like magical gears meshing in constantly changing patterns across time and reality. And once one understands ideas of reincarnation and counterparts in these terms, it becomes difficult to think of one without the other, so inevitable do they appear to be.
quotes.translation: 因此，我们并非反对轮回转世，只是对我们关于它的信念保持谨慎。在我讨论的语境中，轮回是赛斯对其“对应体”概念的历史性表述——即我们每个人在物理层面都与某些同时代生活的其他男性和女性相连，他们正以任何单一物理自我都无法企及的方式，从多种视角探索物质生命。这意味着每个转世自我在其自身时代都拥有一组对应的自我，所有这些都在非物理层面相互连接，如同魔法齿轮般啮合，在时间与现实中不断变换模式。一旦人们以这种方式理解轮回与对应体的概念，就很难将两者分开思考——它们显得如此密不可分。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: The body learned to maintain its stability, its strength and agility, to achieve a state of balance in complementary response to the weather and elements, to dream computations that the conscious mind alone could not hold. The body learned to heal itself in sleep in its dreams--and at certain levels in that state even now each portion of consciousness contributes to the health and stability of all other portions. Far from the claw-and-dagger universe, you have one whose very foundation is based upon the loving cooperation of all of its parts. That is given--the gift of life brings along with it the actualization of that cooperation, for the body's parts exist as a unit because of inner relationships of a cooperative nature; and those exist at your birth (most emphatically), when you are innocent of any cultural beliefs that may be to the contrary.
quotes.translation: 身体学会了维持稳定、力量与敏捷，通过与天气和元素的互补反应达到平衡状态，完成清醒意识无法单独承载的梦境运算。身体学会了在睡眠的梦境中自我疗愈——在那个状态的某些层面，每个意识部分都贡献于所有其他部分的健康与稳定。这绝非利爪匕首的宇宙，而是一个其根基建立在所有部分充满爱意的合作之上的宇宙。这是天赋——生命的礼物本身就伴随着这种合作的实现，因为身体各部分之所以能作为整体存在，正是源于合作性质的内在关系；而这些关系在你出生时（最强调地说）就已存在，那时你尚未受到任何可能与之相悖的文化信念的沾染。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: I'm sure that Seth would be the first to agree that consciousness obviously contains an unlimited number of viewpoints, regardless of which ones we humans may choose to call "true" at any particular time. Consciousness is just as amenable to having some of its physical manifestations scientifically studied, its parts manipulated through "genetic engineering," as it is to encompassing Seth's material. All of our species' actions represent our keen and creative interests in studying ourselves in the finest details possible. That the scientific approach has limitations is obvious. So do all others in this physical realm. A discipline, of whatever nature and motivation, can erect barriers to "outside" influences--and those barriers are often artifacts growing almost automatically out of the very nature of the belief system in question.
quotes.translation: 我相信赛斯会是第一个同意这一点的人：意识显然包含无限多的观点，无论我们在任何特定时刻选择称哪些为"真实"。意识既可以被科学地研究其某些物质表现，通过"基因工程"操纵其部分，也可以容纳赛斯的资料。我们物种的所有行为都代表着我们以最精细的方式研究自己的敏锐而创造性的兴趣。科学方法有其局限性，这是显而易见的。在这个物质领域中，所有其他方法也是如此。任何学科，无论其性质和动机如何，都可能为"外部"影响设置障碍——而这些障碍往往是几乎自动从所涉信念系统的本质中产生的人造物。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: In a fashion, those stylized figures that stood for the images of God, apostles, saints, and so forth, were like a kind of formalized abstract form, into which the artist painted all of his emotions and all of his beliefs, all of his hopes and dissatisfactions. Let no one make God the Father look like a mere human, for example! He must be seen in heroic dimensions, while Christ could be shown in divine and human attributes also. The point is that the images the artists were trying to portray were initially mental and emotional ones, and the paintings were supposed to represent not only themselves but the great drama of divine and human interrelationship, and the tension between the two. The paintings themselves seemed to make the heavenly horde come alive. If no one had seen Christ, there were pictures of him.
quotes.translation: 在某种程度上，那些代表上帝形象、使徒、圣徒等程式化的人物，就像一种形式化的抽象形态，艺术家将其所有情感、所有信念、所有希望与不满都注入其中。例如，绝不可让天父看起来仅像凡人！他必须以英雄的维度呈现，而基督则可同时展现神性与人性特质。关键在于，艺术家试图描绘的形象最初是心智与情感的，这些画作不仅代表自身，更代表神人互动的伟大戏剧，以及两者间的张力。画作本身似乎让天界众军活了起来。即便无人见过基督，仍有他的画像存在。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: I am making generalizations here, but each individual case should be looked at in its own light. Such people as a rule, however, have an exaggerated version of the self (pause), so idealized (long pause) that its very existence intimidates practical action. They are afraid of making mistakes, terrified of betraying this sensed inner psychological superior. Usually, such an idealized inner self comes from the acceptance of highly distorted beliefs--again, concerning good and evil. You end up with what can amount to two main inner antagonists: a superior self and a debased self. The qualities considered good are attracted to the superior self as if it were a magnet. The qualities that seem bad (underlined) are in the same fashion attracted to the debased self. Both of them, relatively isolated psychological polarities, hold about equal sway. All other psychological evidences that are ambiguous, or not clearly understood by either side, group together under their own psychological banners. This is a kind of circular rather than linear arrangement, however, psychologically speaking.
quotes.translation: 我在此进行概括，但每个个案都应以其自身角度审视。然而，这类人通常拥有一个夸张的自我版本（停顿），如此理想化（长时间停顿）以至于其存在本身就令实际行动望而生畏。他们害怕犯错，恐惧背叛这种感知到的内在心理优越者。通常，这种理想化的内在自我源于对高度扭曲信念的接受——再次涉及善恶问题。最终你会得到两个主要的内在对抗者：一个优越自我与一个卑劣自我。被视为善的品质如同被磁铁吸引般聚向优越自我，而被视为恶（下划线强调）的品质则以同样方式聚向卑劣自我。两者作为相对孤立的两极心理，几乎拥有同等影响力。所有其他模糊不清或未被任何一方明确理解的心理证据，则各自集结于自身的心理旗帜下。然而，从心理学角度而言，这是一种循环而非线性的排列。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: "Life as we know it is excitement; highly organized--excitement at all levels, microscopic, macroscopic, psychic. It is the result of the relationship between balance and imbalance, between organization and 'chaos.' It is excitement ever in a state of flux, forming psychic and material knots. It is explosive yet filled with order; it becomes so filled with itself that it explodes in the same way that a flower bursts; the same principle is acting in a hurricane or a flood or a murder or the creation of a poem, or the formation of a dream; in the birth and death of individuals and nations. We instinctively know that disasters mimic the birth and death of cells within our bodies--we instinctively know that all life survives death, that death is the bursting of life into new forms, hence our fascination with accidents and fires. The psyche itself leapfrogs our beliefs at usual conscious levels, and sees us as a part of all life, excitedly forming all kinds of complexes which then fill themselves to the brim, exploding, escaping the framework only to form another. The emotions themselves can sense this when we let them, and grasping that sense of excitement can show us a glimpse of the even greater freedom of our own psychic existence, which flows into us as individuals and then bursts apart that short-lived form into another, as the excitement of individuation leaps from life to life."
quotes.translation: 我们所知的生命是兴奋；高度组织化的——微观、宏观、心灵各个层面的兴奋。它是平衡与失衡、秩序与"混沌"之间关系的结果。它是永远处于变动状态的兴奋，形成心灵与物质的结。它充满爆发力却又井然有序；它如此充盈自身，以至于像花朵绽放般迸发；同样的原理作用于飓风、洪水、谋杀、诗歌创作或梦境形成；也作用于个体与国家的生灭。我们本能地知道灾难模仿着体内细胞的生灭——我们本能地知道所有生命都超越死亡，死亡是生命迸发为新形态的过程，因此我们对事故与火灾着迷。心灵本身在通常的意识层面跃过我们的信念，将我们视为所有生命的一部分，兴奋地形成各种情结，这些情结随后充盈到极致，迸发而出，挣脱框架只为形成另一个。当我们允许时，情绪本身能感知这一点，抓住那种兴奋感能让我们一瞥自身心灵存在的更大自由——它流入我们个体之中，随后将那个短暂形态迸裂为另一个，正如个体化的兴奋从生命跃向生命。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: "They were these: that the entire world with its organization was kept together by certain stories, like those of the Roman Catholic Church; that it was dangerous beyond all knowing to look through the stories or examine them for the truth, and that all kinds of taboos existed to keep us from doing this, since ... on the other side, so to speak, there was an incomprehensible frightening chaotic dimension, malevolent; powers beyond our imagining; and that to question the stories was to threaten not just personal survival but the fabric of reality as we know it. So excommunication was the punishment, or damnation ... which meant more than mere ostracism, but the complete isolation of a person from those belief systems, with nothing between him or her and those frightening realities ... without a framework in which to even organize meaning. This was what damnation really meant. To seek truth was the most dangerous of well-intentioned behavior, then ... and retribution had to be swift and sure.
quotes.translation: "这些信念是：整个有组织的世界是靠某些故事维系在一起的，比如罗马天主教会的故事；透过这些故事审视或检验其真相是极其危险的；存在着各种禁忌阻止我们这样做，因为……可以说，在另一边存在着一个难以理解、令人恐惧的混乱维度，充满恶意；拥有超乎我们想象的力量；质疑这些故事不仅威胁个人生存，更威胁我们所知的实相结构。因此逐出教会就是惩罚，或是永罚……这不仅仅意味着放逐，而是将一个人完全隔离于那些信念体系之外，让他或她与那些可怕实相之间毫无屏障……甚至没有一个能组织意义的框架。这才是永罚的真正含义。那么，追求真理就成了最危险的善意行为……而报应必须迅速且确定。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: "His poetry acted in some regards as a stimulator. That breakthrough, you might say, with perhaps some exaggeration, was a lifesaver, for without some such expansion Ruburt would have felt unable to continue the particular brand of his existence. It is not possible to say in words what one person or another looks for in life, or what unique features best promote his or her growth and development. Even two plants of the same kind sometimes require completely different treatments. The sessions, then, opened the door to a particular kind of value fulfillment that was natural to Ruburt's being. Now to some extent it was that poor, unhappy sinful self, a psychological structure formed by beliefs and feelings, that was also seeking its own redemption, since even it had outgrown the framework that so defined it.
quotes.translation: "他的诗歌在某些方面起到了刺激作用。你可以说，或许有些夸张，那次突破是救命稻草，因为如果没有某种程度的扩展，鲁柏会觉得自己无法继续他那种独特的存在方式。无法用言语说清一个人在生活中寻求什么，或什么独特特质最能促进他或她的成长与发展。即使是同种类的两株植物，有时也需要完全不同的照料。因此，这些课程为鲁柏的存在本质所自然具有的特定价值完成打开了大门。现在，在某种程度上，正是那个可怜、不快乐、有罪的自我——一个由信念和情感形成的心理结构——也在寻求自身的救赎，因为它已经超越了那个如此定义它的框架。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: "Now remember that Ruburt's mother used phrases like: 'I hereby disown you,' and: 'You are hereby disinherited,' and: 'I consider you no longer my daughter.' Such situations increased Ruburt's sense of not being safe, yet also reinforced feelings of independence, for he did not have to feel as dependent upon Marie as he might otherwise. The time would come, however, when the old bondings had to be encountered, for they simply could not hold the newer larger frameworks of understanding. The ideas of the so-called sinful self represent several layers of activity, then--troublesome aspects of belief structures that are shared by millions in your society, and by certain levels of Ruburt's personality. He is now trying to assimilate a greater framework, to become bonded to a higher sequence of knowledge.
quotes.translation: "现在请记住，鲁柏的母亲常使用这样的措辞：'我在此与你断绝关系'，'你已被剥夺继承权'，以及'我不再视你为女儿'。这类情境加剧了鲁柏的不安全感，却也强化了他的独立意识——他不必像原本那样依赖玛丽。然而，当旧有的情感纽带无法承载更宏大的理解框架时，直面这些羁绊的时刻终将到来。所谓'有罪自我'的概念实则代表着多重活动层面——这些令人困扰的信念结构，既为你们社会中数百万民众所共有，也存在于鲁柏人格的某些层次。此刻他正试图整合更广阔的框架，与更高序列的知识建立联结。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: (9:05.) One person's dreams, therefore, while his or her own, will still fit into an important notch in the dreams of a given family. One person might, because of his or her own interests, seek largely from dreams warnings of difficulty or trouble, and therefore be the family's dream watchguard--the one who has, say, the nightmares for everyone else. That person will also serve a somewhat similar role in the waking state, as a member of a family. The question in such instances is the reason for such a person's overconcern and alarm in the first place--why the intense interest in such possible catastrophes, or in crime or whatever?--and the answer lies in an examination of the person's feelings and beliefs about the nature of existence itself.
quotes.translation: （9:05分）因此，一个人的梦境虽属其私有，却仍会嵌入特定家庭梦境的重要缺口。某人可能因其个人兴趣，主要从梦境中寻求困难或麻烦的预警，从而成为家庭的'梦境守护者'——比如为所有人承担噩梦的角色。此人清醒时在家庭中也扮演着类似角色。这类案例的关键在于，此人最初为何会过度担忧与警觉——为何对潜在灾难、犯罪或其他事物抱有如此强烈的兴趣？答案需通过检视此人关于存在本质的感受与信念来探寻。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: Dreams often serve as the frameworks in which sudden remarkable insights appear that later enable a man or a woman to envision the world in a way that was not earlier predictable. The world's activities always include the insertion of surprising events. This is true at all levels of nature, from microscopic to macroscopic. As I have said before, all systems are open. The theories of both evolutionists and creationists strongly suggest and reinforce beliefs in the consecutive nature of time, and in a universe that begins in such-and-such a fashion, continuing on to such-and-such an end--but there are horizontal events that appear in the true activity of nature, and there are horizontal entry points and exit points in all experience. These allow for the insertion of unofficial new energy, the introduction of surprising events. Period.
quotes.translation: 梦境常作为框架，让突如其来的非凡洞见得以显现，这些洞见后来使人们能够以先前无法预见的方式看待世界。世界的活动始终包含意外事件的介入。这在自然界的各个层面都是如此，从微观到宏观。正如我先前所说，所有系统都是开放的。进化论者和创造论者的理论都强烈暗示并强化了关于时间连续性的信念，以及宇宙以某种方式开始、持续到某种终结的信念——但在自然的真实活动中存在着横向事件，在所有经验中都存在着横向的入口点和出口点。这些允许非官方的崭新能量介入，允许意外事件的引入。句号。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: People want, then, to throw aside old structures of belief. They yearn, often without recognizing it, for the remembered knowledge of early childhood, when it seems that they experienced for a time a dimension of experience in which the unexpected was taken for granted, when "magical events" occurred quite naturally. They begin to look at the structure of their lives in a different fashion that attempts to evoke from nature, and from their own natures, some graceful effortlessness, some freedom nearly forgotten. They begin to turn toward a more natural and a more magical approach to their own lives. At such times the conserving elements in nature and in society itself do not seem as strong as they did before. Surprising events that were earlier covered up or ignored seem to appear with greater frequency, and everywhere a new sense of quickness and acceleration gradually alters the expectations of people in regard to the events of their own lives, and to the behavior they expect from others. You are in such times now.
quotes.translation: 于是人们想要抛弃旧的信念结构。他们渴望——往往自己并未意识到——童年早期被记忆的知识，那时他们似乎曾短暂体验过一个维度，在其中意外被视为理所当然，"神奇事件"自然而然地发生。他们开始以不同的方式审视自己的生活结构，试图从自然及自身本性中唤起某种优雅的从容，某种几乎被遗忘的自由。他们开始转向更自然、更神奇的生活方式。在这样的时刻，自然与社会中的保守元素似乎不再像从前那样强大。先前被掩盖或忽视的意外事件似乎更频繁地出现，一种全新的敏捷与加速感逐渐改变了人们对自己生活事件以及对他人的行为期望。你们现在就处于这样的时代。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: Ironically, as individuals and nations we talk about casting off old beliefs while cherishing them as long as possible. Why have large segments of consciousness chosen to operate in such a fashion? I think we're creating a probable reality in which consciousness has the absolute freedom to explore all facets of itself--every one we can think of, and therefore create. Within our national orientations, within our religious and secular, scientific and artistic structures, we are choosing to go to the extremes of "good" and "bad," and to deal with the consequences, all stewing together in what seems like an impossible mix of reason and emotion, learning and joy, pain and violence, and life and death. Naturally, many of us don't like certain facets of our creations, yet we must deal with all of them if we are to make any sense out of our reality. Otherwise, our growing will be too limited; we'll remain slaves to our animosities.
quotes.translation: 讽刺的是，作为个体和国家，我们一边谈论抛弃旧信念，一边却尽可能长久地珍视它们。为何大部分意识会选择以这种方式运作？我认为我们正在创造一种可能实相——在其中意识拥有绝对自由去探索自身的每个面向，包括我们能想到并因此创造的一切。在我们国家的取向中，在宗教与世俗、科学与艺术的结构里，我们选择走向"善"与"恶"的极端，并处理其后果——所有这些都在理性与情感、学习与喜悦、痛苦与暴力、生与死看似不可能混合的熔炉中沸腾。自然，我们许多人并不喜欢自己创造的某些面向，但若想理解自身实相，就必须面对所有面向。否则，我们的成长将过于受限，沦为敌意的奴隶。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: (9:44.) It is certainly too simple to say what I am going to say, yet it is almost as if you would be better off turning the entire rational approach upside down, taking it for granted that all of its assumptions were false, for they are indeed more false than true (intently). Again, you see, the divisions are arbitrary on your part. The intellect is, again, the result of highly spontaneous processes of which it itself knows nothing, and the intuitions that are considered so undisciplined and unreasonable are based upon calculations far more spectacular than those of which the conscious mind can conceive. The intellect could not follow them, so the distinctions are not basic: They are the result of beliefs and habitual usage. Therefore, of course, I speak of them separately, as you think of them.
quotes.translation: (9:44分) 我要说的话当然过于简单，但几乎可以说：你们最好将整个理性方法彻底颠倒，理所当然地认为其所有假设都是错误的——因为它们确实错误多于正确（专注地）。你看，这些区分在你们看来是武断的。理性本身正是高度自发过程的结果，而它对此一无所知；那些被认为毫无纪律、不合逻辑的直觉，其依据的计算远比显意识所能想象的更为精妙。理性无法追随这些计算，因此这些区分并非根本性的：它们是信念和习惯性用法的结果。因此，我当然会像你们所想的那样分开谈论它们。

quotes.text: Your species shares with the other species a feeling of kinship for its kind. There is a great give-and-take of ideas. You end up, then, with a consensus, generally speaking, as to what a reasonable picture of agreed-upon reality is. Your system has frowned upon many experiences, considering them eccentric behavior in an adverse fashion, since your belief systems have so regimented behavior, and so narrowly defined sanity. (Long pause.) The intellect, I want to stress, is socially oriented. It is peculiarly suited, of course, to react to cultural information. (Pause.) It wants to see the world as it is seen by the minds of others. Through that kind of action it helps form your cultural environment, the civilization of which you are justly proud.
quotes.translation: 你们物种与其他物种一样，对其同类怀有亲缘感。思想之间存在着大量的交流与互动。因此，总体而言，你们最终会就公认现实的合理图景达成共识。你们的体系曾对许多经验皱起眉头，视其为反常行为并加以负面评判，因为你们的信念体系如此严格地规范行为，又将理智的定义框定得如此狭隘。（长停顿。）我想强调，理智是社会导向的。它自然特别适合对文化信息做出反应。（停顿。）它希望看到世界如同他人心智所见那般。通过这种作用，它帮助塑造了你们的文化环境，以及你们引以为傲的文明。

quotes.text: (11:21.) The individuals alive at such a time will also have a hand in such decisions, however. Once more, because you are self-conscious beings your beliefs regulate your reality. An animal knows unconsciously that it is unique and has a place in the scheme of being. Its sense of grace is built-in. Your free will allows for the freedom of any belief, including one that says you are unworthy, with no right to your existence.
quotes.translation: （11:21。）然而，生活在这样的时代中的个体也将参与这些决定。再次强调，因为你们是自觉的存在，你们的信念调节着你们的实相。动物无意识地知道自己是独一无二的，并在存在架构中拥有其位置。它们与生俱来便具有优雅感。而你们的自由意志允许任何信念的自由，包括那种声称你们毫无价值、无权存在的信念。

quotes.text: You will accept from your dreams that information that largely agrees with your waking conscious beliefs. There is interaction, as mentioned previously, in which new beliefs are tried out, so to speak. In that regard, you are not at the mercy of your dreams in any meaning of the word.
quotes.translation: 你们会从梦境中接受那些大体上与你们清醒时的意识信念相符的信息。如前所述，其中存在互动，可以说新信念会在此过程中被尝试。就此而言，你们并非以任何字面意义受制于你们的梦境。

quotes.text: New paragraph (and more rapidly): As egotistical consciousness expands to include hereto largely neglected data, then it will experience, practically speaking, a new kind of identity; knowing itself differently. Its concepts of godhood will significantly alter, as will the dimensions of emotion. Your heritage includes vastly richer veins of love, yet your concepts of self and godhood have severely limited these. You often seem to hate those with different beliefs than your own, for example, and you have perpetrated cruelties upon others in the name of religion and in the name of science, because your limited ideas about the nature of the self led you to fear your emotions. Often you are afraid that love will overwhelm you, for instance.
quotes.translation: 新段落（且更快速）：当自我意识扩展以包含迄今被忽略的大量数据时，实际上它将体验到一种新的身份认同；以不同的方式认识自己。它对神性的概念将显著改变，情感维度亦然。你的传承包含远为丰沛的爱之矿脉，但你对自我与神性的概念却严重限制了这些。例如，你常憎恨那些与你信念相异者，并以宗教与科学之名对他人施以暴行，因为你对自我本质的狭隘认知使你恐惧自身情感。譬如，你常害怕爱会淹没你。

quotes.text: In your terms, the person at birth is affected by multidimensional conditions, and the collective position of the planets is but one very minute indication of the other realities involved. Ruburt is correct: Even in conventional terms a true horoscope would have to involve the time of death in your temporal reality, as well [as that of birth]. Your focus of attention forms boundaries that predispose you to believe in a point at which your consciousness emerges, as you understand it, and a point when it is no longer effective, or dies. Your beliefs in such concepts limit your perception, for by altering the focus of your attention you can to some extent become aware of perception before and after the recognized points of birth and death.
quotes.translation: 以你们的说法，人出生时受多维条件影响，行星的集体位置仅是所涉其他实相中极微小的指标。鲁柏说得对：即便按传统说法，真正的星盘必须包含你们时间实相中的死亡时刻（如同出生时刻）。你的注意力焦点形成界限，使你倾向于相信存在一个你理解的意识浮现的起点，以及它不再有效或死亡的终点。你对这类概念的信念限制了你的感知，因为通过调整注意力焦点，你可以在某种程度上觉察到公认的出生与死亡节点之前及之后的感知。

quotes.text: He requires undue amounts of praise and attention from others, since he obviously will get little from himself. In a fashion, to an extent he will refuse to be accountable for his actions--therefore taking them out of the frame of judgment within which other people must operate. He then can avoid putting his "talents and superior abilities" to the test, where he feels he would certainly fail. He half realizes that the superior self and the debased self are both of psychological manufacture. His abilities are not really that grand. His failures are not nearly that disastrous. The belief in these highly contrasting elements of personality keep him in a state of turmoil, however, so that he feels powerless to act in any concerted fashion.
quotes.translation: 他需要他人过度的赞美与关注，因为他显然无法从自身获得这些。在某种程度上，他会拒绝为自己的行为负责——从而将这些行为置于他人必须遵循的评判框架之外。这样他便能避免将"天赋与卓越能力"付诸考验，因为他深知自己必将失败。他隐约意识到那个优越自我与卑劣自我皆是心理构造。他的能力其实没那么出众，他的失败也远非那般灾难性。然而，对人格中这些高度对立元素的信念使他持续处于混乱状态，以致他感到无力以任何协调一致的方式行动。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: In the first place you must understand that in your own uniqueness it is futile to compare yourself to others, for in so doing you try to emulate qualities that are theirs, and to that extent deny your own miraculous being and vision. Once you begin comparing yourself to others there is no end to it. You will always find someone more talented than you are in some way, and so will continue to be dissatisfied. Instead, through working with your own beliefs, take it for granted that your life is important; begin with it and where you are. Do not deride yourself because you have not reached some great ideal, but start to use those talents that you have to the best of your ability, knowing that in them lies your own individual fulfillment.
quotes.translation: 首先你必须明白，在你独特的本质中，与他人比较是徒劳的，因为这样做时你试图模仿属于他们的特质，并在某种程度上否定了自己神奇的存在与视野。一旦开始与他人比较，便永无止境。你总会发现某人在某方面比你更有才华，因而持续感到不满。相反，通过处理自己的信念，理所当然地认为你的生命是重要的；从当下和你的所在之处开始。不要因未达到某个伟大理想而贬低自己，而是开始尽己所能运用你拥有的天赋，明白其中蕴含着你个人的圆满。

quotes.text: (9:45.) When your body and mind are working together then the relationship between the two goes smoothly, and their natural therapeutic systems place you in a state of health and grace. I told you earlier (in the 614th session in Chapter Two, for instance) that your feelings follow the flow of your beliefs, and if this does not seem true to you it is because you are not aware of the contents of your conscious mind. You can close your physical eyes. You can close the eyes of your conscious mind also, and pretend not to see what is there. It is because you do not trust your own basic therapeutic nature, or really understand the conscious or unconscious mind, that you run to so many therapies that originate from without the self.
quotes.translation: （9:45）当你的身体和心智协同运作时，两者之间的关系便顺畅无碍，它们自然的疗愈系统将你置于健康与恩宠的状态。我之前告诉过你（例如在第二章第614节中），你的感受遵循信念的流动，如果这对你而言似乎不真实，那是因为你尚未觉察意识心智的内容。你可以闭上肉体的眼睛。你也可以闭上意识心智的眼睛，假装看不见那里有什么。正因你不信任自己基本的疗愈本性，或未能真正理解意识与无意识心智，你才会求助于如此多源自自我之外的疗法。

quotes.text: Some of this, again, is difficult to explain (pause), but in a fashion the intellect is a cultural (underlined) phenomenon. Period. It is amazingly resilient, in that according to the belief structures of any given historical period, it can orient itself along the lines of those beliefs, using all of its reasoning abilities to bring such a world picture into focus, collecting data that agree, and rejecting what does not.
quotes.translation: 其中有些内容再次难以解释（停顿），但某种程度上，智力是一种文化（强调）现象。句号。它具有惊人的适应性，因为根据任何特定历史时期的信念结构，它能沿着这些信念的脉络调整自身，运用所有推理能力将这样的世界图景聚焦，收集相符的数据，并排斥不相符的内容。

quotes.text: Beliefs about the infant's sexual nature are of course a part of its advance programming. We are not speaking here of forced growth patterns, or of psychic or biological directions, impressed upon it so that any later divergence from them causes inevitable stress or pain. The fact remains that the child receives patterns of behavior, gently nudging it to grow in certain directions. In normal learning, of course, both parents urge the child to behave in certain fashions. Beside this, however, certain general, learned patterns are biologically transmitted to the child through the genes. Certain kinds of knowledge are transmitted through the genes besides that generally known, having to do with cellular formations and so forth.
quotes.translation: 关于婴儿性本质的信念当然是其先天编程的一部分。我们在此并非谈论强加于婴儿的强迫性成长模式，或心理与生物方向——仿佛日后任何偏离都会造成必然的压力或痛苦。事实是，婴儿确实接收行为模式，被温柔地引导向特定方向发展。在正常学习过程中，父母自然会以特定方式督促孩子。然而除此之外，某些普遍习得的模式会通过基因生物性地传递给婴儿。除了已知的细胞结构等知识外，某些特定知识也通过基因传递。

quotes.text: In such a case you will realize that your previous ill health was caused by your belief. If you have any physical problems, concentrate instead upon the healthy portions of your body and the unimpeded functions that you have. In the healthy areas, your beliefs are working for you.
quotes.translation: 在这种情况下，你会意识到先前的病痛是由你的信念造成的。若你有任何身体问题，请转而专注于你身体健康的部位以及畅通无阻的功能。在健康的领域里，你的信念正在为你效力。

quotes.text: Each character, however, or portion of a scene, would represent in fragmented form another quite valid program [or reality, in brackets]. In the dream state, then, you are sometimes aware of too many stations. When you try to make them fit into your recognized picture of reality, they may seem chaotic. There are ways to bring the picture into focus: There are ways to tune into those other quite-natural frequencies, so that they present you with a more expansive view both of the world as you define it, and of its greater aspects. Period. The psyche is not encased, in your case (amused), within a frame too fragile to express it. Only your beliefs about the psyche and about the body limit your experience to its present degree.
quotes.translation: 然而，每个角色或场景片段都会以碎片化形式代表另一个同样有效的程序[或实相，括号内]。因此在梦境状态中，你有时会接收到太多频道。当你试图将它们纳入你认知的现实图景时，它们可能显得混乱。有方法能让画面聚焦：有方法能调频至那些同样自然的频率，使它们为你呈现更广阔的视野——既包括你定义的世界，也包括其更宏大的面向。句号。就你而言（忍俊不禁），心灵并非被禁锢在过于脆弱的框架中。唯有你对心灵与身体的信念，将你的体验限制在现有程度。

quotes.text: The belief in personal worth draws about it the belief in the personal value of others, for they show their best faces to our fortunate friend. His life constantly reinforces this concept, and while he is peripherally aware that some people are "nicer" than others, his main intimate experience allows him to see the best in others and in himself. This becomes one of the strong frameworks through which he views existence.
quotes.translation: 对个人价值的信念会吸引对他人个人价值的信念，因为他人会向我们幸运的朋友展现他们最好的一面。他的生活不断强化这一观念，虽然他能隐约意识到有些人比其他人"更友善"，但他主要的亲密体验让他能够看到他人和自己最好的一面。这成为他看待存在的重要框架之一。

quotes.text: Even with so-called mental disorders, however, orientation with the body is very important, as are the individual's beliefs about his own form and its relationship with others and with time and space. (Pause.) There will often be chemical imbalances in such a situation, unconsciously produced by the individual, sometimes in order to allow him to work out a series of hallucinatory events. Such sustained "objectified dreaming" necessitates a change, chemically, from the normal state of waking consciousness. It is important to note that regardless of the mental or physical illness adopted, it is chosen for a reason, and is a natural method that the individual himself knows he is physically and mentally equipped to handle.
quotes.translation: 然而，即使面对所谓的精神障碍，与身体的协调仍然非常重要，个人对自己形态的信念、以及这种形态与他人、时间和空间的关系也同样重要。（停顿。）在这种情况下，往往会出现化学失衡，这是个体无意识产生的，有时是为了让他能够经历一系列幻觉事件。这种持续的"客体化梦境"需要在化学层面改变正常的清醒意识状态。值得注意的是，无论采用何种精神或身体疾病，都是出于某种原因而被选择的，这是一种自然方法，个体自己知道自己在身心上具备处理它的能力。

quotes.text: Man, on the other hand, has more to contend with. He must deal with beliefs and feelings often so ambiguous that no clear line of action seems possible. The body often does not know how to react. If you believe that the body is sinful, for example, you cannot expect to be happy, and health will most likely elude you, for your dark beliefs will blemish the psychological and biological integrity with which you were born.
quotes.translation: 另一方面，人类需要应对更多。他必须处理那些常常模糊不清的信念和感受，以至于似乎没有明确的行动路线可行。身体往往不知道如何反应。例如，如果你相信身体是有罪的，你就无法期待快乐，健康也极有可能与你无缘，因为你阴暗的信念会玷污你与生俱来的心理和生理完整性。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: So your physical life is the result of a spectacular spontaneous order--the order of the body spontaneously formed by the units of consciousness. Your experience of the world is largely determined by your imaginations and your reasoning abilities. These did not develop through time, as per usual evolutionary beliefs. Both imagination and reason belonged to the species from the beginning, but the species has used these qualities in different ways throughout what you think of as historic time. There is great leeway in that direction, so that the two can be combined in many many alternate fashions, each particular combination giving you its own unique picture of reality, and determining your experience in the world.
quotes.translation: 因此，你们的物质生活是壮观自发秩序的结果——由意识单位自发形成的身体秩序。你们对世界的体验很大程度上由想象力和推理能力决定。这些能力并非如通常的进化信念所言随时间发展而来。想象力和理性从一开始就属于这个物种，但该物种在你们所谓的历史时期中以不同方式运用这些特质。这个方向有极大余地，因此两者可以以许多许多交替方式结合，每种特定组合都赋予你们独特的现实图景，并决定你们在世界的体验。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: Now the physical organism as such is capable of that kind of reality system. It is not better or worse than your own. It is simply alternate behavior, biologically and spiritually possible. No complicated physical transportation systems were set up. In the physical state, in what you would call the waking state, these individuals slept. To you, comparatively speaking, their waking activities would seem dreamlike, and yet they behaved with great natural physical grace, allowing the body to function to capacity. They did not saddle it with negative beliefs of disease or limitation. Such bodies did not age to the extent now, that yours do, and enjoyed the greatest ease and sense of belonging with the environment.
quotes.translation: 物质有机体本身确实能够构建那种实相系统。它既不优于也不劣于你们自己的系统。这只是另一种行为模式，在生物学和灵性上都是可能的。没有建立复杂的物理运输系统。在你们所谓的清醒状态中，这些个体处于睡眠状态。对你们而言，相对而言，他们的清醒活动会显得如梦似幻，然而他们却以极其自然的身体优雅行事，让身体发挥到极致。他们没有用疾病或限制的负面信念来束缚身体。这样的身体不会像你们现在这样衰老，并且与环境享有最大的轻松与归属感。

quotes.text: Ruburt and Joseph know that Star Trek is not "real." Planets can explode on the television screen, and Ruburt will not spill one drop of coffee. The cozy living room is quite safe from the imaginary catastrophes that are occurring just a few feet from the couch. Yet in a way the program reflects certain beliefs of your society in general, and so it is like a specialized mass waking dream -- real but not real. For a moment, though, let us change the program to your favorite cops-and-robbers show. A woman is shot down in the street. Now this drama becomes "more real," more immediately probable, less comfortable. So watching such a program, you may feel slightly threatened yourself, yet still largely unconcerned.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏和约瑟知道《星际迷航》不是"真实的"。电视屏幕上行星可以爆炸，鲁柏手中的咖啡却不会洒出一滴。舒适的客厅与沙发几英尺外发生的虚构灾难完全隔绝。然而在某种程度上，这个节目反映了你们社会普遍持有的某些信念，因此它就像一场专门化的集体清醒梦——既真实又不真实。不过，让我们暂且换个节目，换成你们最喜欢的警匪片。一个女人在街上被枪杀。现在这个戏剧变得"更真实"，更直接地具有可能性，更令人不安。因此观看这样的节目时，你可能会感到自己略微受到威胁，但大体上仍不以为意。

quotes.text: Since the later 1960s, when my own troubles began, I stubbornly resisted medical assistance. If I had broken a leg I would have gone to a doctor to get it set. I felt that I could handle my particular kind of difficulty alone. (Long pause.) The symptoms were obvious enough: stiffness, slowing down of motion, and general lack of mobility. I could keep track easily enough, I thought, of my own progress as I worked directly with my body, without drugs to confuse the issue, and with no one else between me and the reality I had so cunningly created. How else could I really learn anything? The more middlemen that I entertained between my physical condition and my personal beliefs, the more confused I thought I'd be.
quotes.translation: 自1960年代末我的问题开始以来，我一直固执地拒绝医疗协助。如果我摔断了腿，我会去找医生接骨。但我认为我能够独自处理自己特有的困难。（长停顿。）症状相当明显：僵硬、动作迟缓、整体行动不便。我以为自己可以轻易追踪进展，直接与身体合作，不用药物混淆问题，也没有任何中介横亘在我与那个我巧妙创造的实相之间。否则我怎么能真正学到东西？我在身体状况与个人信念之间引入的中介越多，我就越会感到困惑。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: As Seth suggests, through even a five-minute exercise, in which we sit quietly and look about, we can become aware that the present is the point of power. In his exercise, we gently remind ourselves that we aren't at the mercy of our past beliefs unless we think we are. We have the full freedom to insert new creative goals in our point-of-power exercises. Next, we relax, to give our fresh suggestions time to begin working within us. Next, physically we make a simple gesture or act, no matter how modest, that is in line with our desires for the future. Periodically we repeat the exercise -- but easily, without pressure, confident that we're doing well. Action is thought in physical motion, Seth tells us. ...
quotes.translation: 正如赛斯所建议，即便通过五分钟的练习——安静坐下环顾四周——我们也能意识到当下即是力量之点。在他的练习中，我们温和地提醒自己：除非我们自认为受制于过去的信念，否则我们并不受其摆布。我们拥有完全的自由，在力量之点练习中注入新的创造性目标。接着放松，让新鲜的建议有时间在我们内在开始运作。随后在身体层面做出一个简单的手势或动作——无论多么微小——只要符合我们对未来的渴望。定期重复这个练习，但要轻松不施压，确信自己做得很好。赛斯告诉我们：行动是思想在物质层面的运动……

related_concepts.name_en: The point of power is in the present

quotes.text: (10:27.) No matter how open it may seem that you are, you will nevertheless accept certain emotions that you think of as safe, and ignore others, or stop them at particular points, because you are afraid of following them further. (Pause.) This behavior will follow your beliefs, of course. (Long pause.) If you are over forty, for instance, you may tell yourself that age is meaningless, that you enjoy much younger people, that you think young thoughts. You will accept only those emotions that appear to be in keeping with your ideas of youth. You become concerned with the problems of the young. You accept what you think of as optimistic health-giving thoughts. You consider yourself quite emotional, perhaps.
quotes.translation: （10:27分）无论你自认为多么开放，你仍会接受某些自认为安全的情绪，而忽略其他情绪，或在特定节点阻止它们，因为你害怕继续深入探索。（停顿）这种行为当然会遵循你的信念。（长停顿）例如，若你年过四十，你可能会告诉自己年龄毫无意义，你享受与年轻人相处，思想年轻。你只会接受那些看似符合你年轻观念的情绪。你开始关注年轻人的问题，接受自认为乐观健康的想法。或许你还自认相当感性。

quotes.text: The well-meaning announcements pertaining to heroin, marijuana, and acid (LSD) can also be damaging, in that they structure in advance any experience that people who take drugs might have. On the one hand, you have a culture that publicly points out as common the often exaggerated dangers that can occur with drugs, and on the other holds out drugs as a method of therapy. Here the dangers become something like initiation rites, in which loss of life must be faced before full acceptance into the community can be established. But those involved with native initiation rituals knew far more what they were doing, and understood a framework of beliefs in which the outcome -- success -- was fairly well assured.
quotes.translation: 关于海洛因、大麻和LSD（麦角酸二乙酰胺）的善意警告同样可能造成伤害，因为它们预先构建了吸毒者可能产生的任何体验。一方面，文化公开强调吸毒可能引发的常见且常被夸大的危险；另一方面，又将毒品视为治疗手段。此时危险变得类似某种启蒙仪式——必须直面死亡威胁，才能获得群体完全接纳。但参与原始启蒙仪式的人更清楚自己在做什么，他们理解那个确保成功结局的信念框架。

quotes.text: You are not at the mercy of past beliefs, therefore. On the other hand, the sooner you begin to act upon new ones the better. Otherwise you are not trusting them in the present. If you are poor and want to have more money, and try to maintain a belief in abundance -- while still faced with the fact of present poverty -- you must in your reality make some symbolic move that shows you are willing to accept a change.
quotes.translation: 因此，你并非受制于过去的信念。另一方面，越早开始践行新信念越好，否则你便未在当下信任它们。若你贫穷且渴望更多财富，试图维持丰盛的信念——却仍面对当下贫困的事实——就必须在现实中做出某种象征性举动，表明你愿意接纳改变。

quotes.text: The division between the two aspects of experience begins to take on the characteristics of completely diverse behavior. The unconscious becomes more and more unfamiliar to consciousness. Those beliefs build up about it, and the symbolisms involved are exaggerated. The unknown seems to be threatening and degenerate. The color black assumes stronger tendencies in its connection with evil -- something to be avoided. Self-annihilation seems to be a threat ever-present in the dream or sleep state. At the same time all of those flamboyant, creative, spontaneous, emotional surges that emerge normally from the unconscious become feared and projected outward, then, upon enemies, other races and creeds.
quotes.translation: 经验两个层面之间的分野开始呈现出截然不同行为的特征。无意识对意识而言变得越来越陌生。关于它的那些信念逐渐累积，所涉及的象征意义也被夸大。未知似乎充满威胁且堕落。黑色与邪恶的关联愈发强烈——成为需要避讳之物。自我湮灭仿佛始终潜伏在梦境或睡眠状态中的威胁。与此同时，所有那些从无意识中自然涌现的华丽、创造、自发、情感澎湃的浪潮，都变得令人恐惧，并被投射向外，投射到敌人、其他种族和教义之上。

quotes.text: For an exercise, then, imagine for a while that the subjective world of your thoughts, feelings, inner images and fantasies represent the "rockbed reality" from which individual physical events emerge. Look at the world for a change from the inside out, so to speak. Imagine that physical experience is somehow the materialization of your own subjective reality. Forget what you have learned about reactions and stimuli. Ignore for a time everything you have believed and see your thoughts as the real events. Try to view normal physical occurrences as the concrete physical reactions in space and time to your own feelings and beliefs. For indeed your subjective world causes your physical experience.
quotes.translation: 那么，作为一项练习，请暂时想象：你思想、感受、内在影像与幻想的主观世界，代表着个体物质事件从中涌现的"基岩实相"。不妨换个角度，从内向外看待这个世界。想象物质经验在某种程度上是你自身主观实相的具体化。忘掉你所学过的关于反应与刺激的一切。暂时忽略你曾相信的一切，将你的思想视为真实事件。试着将日常物质现象视为你的感受与信念在时空中的具体物质反应。因为你的主观世界确实在引发你的物质经验。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: (10:29.) If you believe that you come down with a cold every time you are in a draft, you are using natural hypnosis. If you think that you must come and go at everyone else's beck and call, then you are like Dineen, who believes that she must do what this "hypnotist" tells her to do. In her case Dineen gave up the responsibility for action and initiative, yet because one must act the reasons were assigned to another. Ruburt also pointed this out. Dineen asked for advice from me and again Ruburt said, quite correctly, "You must learn to stop depending upon others, to use your own common sense. You must stop trying to use one symbol against another, and look at your own life and your beliefs."
quotes.translation: （十点二十九分。）如果你相信每次吹到穿堂风就会感冒，你就是在运用自然催眠。如果你认为自己必须随叫随到、听命于他人，那么你就如同迪宁，她相信自己必须听从这位"催眠师"的指令。就迪宁而言，她放弃了行动与主动性的责任，但由于人必须有所行动，她便把理由归咎于他人。鲁柏也指出了这一点。迪宁向我寻求建议，鲁柏再次非常正确地指出："你必须学会不再依赖他人，运用自己的常识。你必须停止试图用一个象征对抗另一个象征，转而审视你自己的生命与信念。"

quotes.text: You may find some of your most cherished conceptions to be misconceptions in the light of your new experiences. Since explorations are highly personal, you will most likely begin them from the framework of your current beliefs. Symbols may be utilized, and these may change their meaning for you as you progress. The symbols may evolve, therefore. In the beginning of this work I "warned" the reader that here in these sessions we would go beyond ideas of one god and one self.3 I stated that your ideas of personhood would be expanded. As "Unknown" Reality is being produced Ruburt and Joseph are having their own experiences, and uncovering the nature of the unknown reality as it applies to them.
quotes.translation: 你可能会发现，你最珍视的一些观念，在你新经验的观照下，其实是误解。由于探索极具个人性，你很可能从当前信念的框架开始。可能会运用象征，而这些象征的意义会随着你的进展而改变。因此，象征本身也会演化。在这项工作的开端，我曾“警告”读者，在这些课程中，我们将超越单一神祇和单一自我的概念。我声明过，你们对人格的观念将会被扩展。随着《未知的实相》的创作，鲁柏和约瑟正在经历他们自己的体验，并揭示未知实相的本质，正如它适用于他们自身。

quotes.text: Granting that, however, cults interact, and so there is quite a relationship between the state of religion, when it operates as a cult, and the state of science when it operates as a cult. Right now your cultish religions exist in response to the cultish behavior of science. Science insists it does not deal with values, but leaves those to philosophers. In stating that the universe is an accidental creation, however, a meaningless chance conglomeration formed by an unfeeling cosmos, it states quite clearly its belief that the universe and man's existence has no value. All that remains is what pleasure or accomplishment can somehow be wrested from man's individual biological processes.
quotes.translation: 然而，即便如此，教派之间仍会互动，因此当宗教以教派形式运作时，与科学以教派形式运作时，两者之间存在着相当的关系。目前，你们那些教派式的宗教，正是对科学教派式行为的回应。科学坚持它不处理价值问题，而将这些问题留给哲学家。然而，当科学宣称宇宙是偶然的创造，是一个由无情宇宙形成的无意义随机聚合体时，它便清楚地表明了它的信念：宇宙和人类的存在毫无价值。剩下的，只有从个人生物过程中勉强攫取的快乐或成就。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Through this belief he viewed all of his experience, correlating it; he encouraged those impulses that furthered it, and impeded those that did not. Now: Because of this particular temperament he put all of his eggs in one basket, so to speak. Those of you who do the same thing will see yourselves in one particular way, whatever it is. You will primarily organize your experience along definite lines. It may be your sex role or your professional role. You may see yourself as a mother or a father first of all, as a teacher, an editor, or as a "man's man." You will, however, emphasize one certain quality above all others -- your athletic nature, your spiritual bent, whatever it may be.
quotes.translation: 透过这个信念，他检视自己所有的经验并加以整合；他鼓励那些强化该信念的冲动，而抑制那些不符的冲动。现在：由于这种特殊的气质，可以说他把所有鸡蛋都放在同一个篮子里。那些做同样事情的人，无论是什么情况，都会以某种特定方式看待自己。你们会主要沿着明确的路线组织自己的经验。可能是你的性别角色或职业角色。你可能首先将自己视为母亲或父亲、教师、编辑，或"男子汉"。然而，你会特别强调某一种特质——你的运动天性、灵性倾向，或其他任何特质。

quotes.text: -- and we will resume dictation ... Your beliefs about age, like everything else, will form your experience, and your mass beliefs will affect your civilization. With the current concepts held by your society, men and women fear old age from the time of youth. If young adulthood is considered the epitome of life, blessedness, and success, then old age is viewed as the opposite -- a time of failure and decay.
quotes.translation: ——我们将继续口述……你对年龄的信念，就像其他一切一样，会塑造你的经验，而你的群体信念会影响你的文明。以你们社会当前持有的观念，男女从年轻时就开始恐惧老年。如果年轻成年期被视为生命、福佑与成功的缩影，那么老年则被视为其反面——一个失败与衰败的时期。

quotes.text: "Witches" were not considered insane, for example, or deranged, for their psychological beliefs fit in only too well with those of the general populace. They were considered evil instead. (Pause.) The vast range of psychological expression, however, had some kind of framework to contain it. (Pause.) The saint and the sinner (pause) each had access to great depths of possible heroism or despair.
