Psychological reality, for all of the religious (pause) dangers placed upon it, was anything but a flat-surfaced experience. It was in fact because the church so believed in the great range of psychological activity possible that it was so dogmatic and tireless in trying to maintain order.
quotes.translation: 举例来说，"女巫"并不被视为精神错乱或疯狂，因为她们的心理信念与一般大众的信念完全吻合。相反，她们被视为邪恶。（停顿）然而，心理表达的广阔范围仍有某种框架来容纳它。（停顿）圣人与罪人（停顿）各自都能触及英雄主义或绝望的深层可能。心理实相，尽管被赋予宗教（停顿）上的危险，绝非扁平化的经验。事实上，正因为教会如此相信心理活动可能达到的广阔范围，它才会如此教条且不遗余力地试图维持秩序。

quotes.text: In your terms other universes, with all of their own space and time structures, were created simultaneously, and exist simultaneously. The effect of looking outward into space, and therefore backward into time, is a kind of built-in convention that appears within your own space-time picture. You must remember, then, when you think in terms of origins, that the very word, "origin," is dependent upon time conventions, and a belief in beginnings and endings. Beginnings and endings are themselves effects that seem to be facts to your perceptions. In a fashion they simply represent beginnings and endings, the boundaries, the reaches and the limitations of your own span of attention.
quotes.translation: 用你们的话来说，其他宇宙——带着它们各自完整的时空结构——是同时被创造出来，并且同时存在的。向外望向太空，因此也就是向后望向时间的效果，是一种内建于你们自身时空画面中的惯例。那么，当你们思考起源问题时，必须记住"起源"这个词本身就依赖于时间惯例，以及对于开始与结束的信念。开始与结束本身是看似事实的效果，呈现在你们的感知中。从某种方式来说，它们仅仅代表着开始与结束——你们注意力范围的边界、触及范围与局限。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: I have used Andrea because so many typical Western beliefs coincide in her reality -- the idea that aging is disastrous; that women are relatively powerless without a man beside them; that life is, practically speaking, highly difficult while it should be ideally simple. All of these ideas obtain their charge from a basic belief in the powerlessness of the conscious self to form and regulate its experience.
quotes.translation: 我之所以用安德莉亚为例，是因为在她现实中汇聚了如此多典型的西方信念——衰老是灾难性的；女性没有男人相伴就相对无力；生活实际上极其艰难，而它本应理想地简单。所有这些想法都源于一个基本信念：有意识的自我无力形成并调节自身的经验。

quotes.text: (12:15.) These are all beliefs held by many people. Those who have them will meet them in experience. Physical data will always seem to reinforce the beliefs, therefore, but the beliefs formed the reality. We are going to attempt to knock down such limiting concepts.
quotes.translation: (12:15) 这些都是许多人持有的信念。持有这些信念的人会在经验中遇见它们。物质资料似乎总会强化这些信念，但其实是信念形成了实相。我们将尝试击垮这类限制性观念。

quotes.text: Under certain conditions, therefore, the body can maintain itself while the "main consciousness" is away from it. The body consciousness is quite able, then, to provide the overall equilibrium. At certain levels of the sleep state this does in fact happen. In sleepwalking the body is active, but the main consciousness is not "awake." It is not manipulating the body. The main consciousness is elsewhere. Under such conditions the body can perform tasks and often maneuver with an amazing sense of balance. This finesse, again, hints at physical abilities not ordinarily used. The main consciousness, because of its beliefs, often hampers such manipulability in normal waking life.
quotes.translation: 因此在特定条件下，当"主要意识"离开身体时，身体仍能维持自身。身体意识完全有能力提供整体平衡。在睡眠状态的某些层次上，这确实会发生。梦游时身体是活跃的，但主要意识并未"清醒"。它并未操控身体。主要意识在别处。在此条件下，身体能执行任务，并常以惊人的平衡感灵活行动。这种精妙再次暗示了通常未被使用的身体能力。由于主要意识的信念，它在正常清醒生活中常常阻碍这种可操控性。

quotes.text: Many of your beliefs are of course cultural, but you have still accepted those that served your own purposes. As a rule, men in your society believe themselves logical while women are considered intuitive. Women, now trying to assert their rights, often fall into the same trap, but backwards -- trying to deny what they think of as inferior intuitive elements for what they think of as superior logical ones.
quotes.translation: 你们许多信念当然具有文化性，但你们仍接受了那些符合自身目的的信念。通常而言，你们社会中的男性自认为理性，而女性则被视为直觉性。如今试图争取权利的女性，往往也落入同样的陷阱，只是方向相反——她们试图否认自认为低劣的直觉元素，转而拥抱自认为优越的逻辑元素。

quotes.text: In the exercise just given, you use the belief in effective change in any given area, and then allow your imagination freedom along those directed lines. Such an exercise automatically does even more, opening up the window of perception and letting in the knowledge and experience of other portions of the self. As this light and energy flows through, it will be tinted or colored by your own psychological reality, as the rays of the sun are through colored glass. This simply means that the other-dimensional information will often appear in ordinary guise, through an intuitive hunch, a sudden idea, or some solution that has already occurred to you but has not been acted upon.
quotes.translation: 在刚才的练习中，你运用了在特定领域有效改变的信念，然后让想象力沿着这些定向路径自由驰骋。这样的练习会自动产生更多效果，打开感知之窗，让自我其他部分的知识与经验涌入。当这光与能量流过时，会像阳光穿过彩色玻璃般，被你的心理实相染上色彩。这仅意味着，其他维度的信息常会以寻常伪装显现——通过直觉的预感、突发的灵感，或是你早已想到却未付诸行动的某个解决方案。

quotes.text: Creativity rides the tides of love. When love is denied its natural expression, creativity suffers. Your beliefs lead you to suppose that a natural bisexuality would result in the death of the family, the destruction of morals, rampant sexual crimes, and the loss of sexual identity. I would say, however, that my last sentence adequately describes your present situation (with dry humor). The acceptance of the species' natural bisexuality would ultimately help solve not only those problems but many others, including the large instances of violence, and acts of murder. In your terms, however, and in your circumstances, there is not apt to be any easy transition.
quotes.translation: 创造力乘着爱的潮汐而行。当爱被剥夺自然表达时，创造力便遭受损害。你们的信念让你们以为，天生的双性倾向会导致家庭瓦解、道德沦丧、性犯罪猖獗以及性别认同的丧失。然而我要说，我最后这句话恰恰精准描述了你们当前的处境（带着冷幽默）。接受人类天生的双性倾向，最终不仅有助于解决这些问题，更能化解包括大规模暴力与谋杀行为在内的诸多困境。不过，以你们所处的时代与环境而言，恐怕难以轻易实现这种转变。

quotes.text: The private psyche, the part of you that you do not recognize, is aware of those patterns even as it is aware of private physical biological patterns about which it forms your image. Certain leanings, inclinations, and probabilities are present then in your biological structure, to be triggered or not according to your purposes and intents. You may personally have the ability to be a fine athlete, for example. Yet your inclinations and intents may carry you in a different direction, so that the necessary triggers are not activated. Each individual is gifted in a variety of ways. His or her own desires and beliefs activate certain abilities and ignore others.
quotes.translation: 私人心灵——你尚未认知的那部分自己——觉察到那些模式，正如它觉察到构成你形象的私人生理生物模式。某些倾向、偏好与可能性存在于你的生物结构中，是否被触发取决于你的目的与意图。例如，你个人可能具备成为优秀运动员的潜能，但你的倾向与意图可能将你引向不同方向，因此必要的触发机制未被激活。每个个体都拥有多种天赋，其自身的欲望与信念会激活某些能力而忽略其他。

quotes.text: Now all of these ideas were quite conscious, but he held each group separately. The conscious mind, again, tries to obtain overall integrity and unity, lining up its beliefs into some kind of consistent system. When opposing beliefs that directly contradict each other are held for any length of time, and little attempt is made to reconcile them, then a "battle" begins within the conscious mind itself.
quotes.translation: 所有这些观念原本都是相当有意识的，但他将每组观念分别存放。意识心再次试图获得整体的一致性与统一性，将其信念排列成某种连贯的系统。当直接相互矛盾的信念被长期持有，且几乎不做任何调和尝试时，意识心内部便会开始一场"战斗"。

quotes.text: Although time does not basically exist as you "know" it, you are neurologically forced to perceive your life as a series of passing moments. As creatures you are born young and grow older. Yet the animals, as creatures, are not as limited in their experience in that regard. They have no beliefs in old age that automatically shut down their abilities; so left alone, while they do physically die as all creatures must in those terms, they do not deteriorate in the same way.
quotes.translation: 虽然时间本质上并不如你所"知"那般存在，但你的神经系统迫使你将生命感知为一系列流逝的瞬间。作为生物，你生而年轻并逐渐衰老。然而动物作为生物，在这方面所受的限制较少。它们没有关于衰老会自动关闭能力的信念；因此若不受干扰，尽管它们作为生物必然在肉体层面死亡，却不会以同样的方式退化。

quotes.text: The "laws" of supply and demand are misconceptions based upon a quite uncomplimentary belief in man's basic greedy nature. In the past you treated the land in your country as if your species, being the "fittest," had the right to survive at the expense of all other species, and at the expense of the land itself. The ideal of the country was and is an excellent one: the right of each individual to pursue an equitable, worthy existence, with dignity. The means, however, have helped erode that ideal, and the public interpretation of Darwin's principles was, quite unfortunately, transferred to the economic area, and to the image of man as a political animal.
quotes.translation: 供需"法则"是基于对人类贪婪本性的贬低性信念而产生的误解。过去你们对待本国土地的方式，仿佛作为"最适者"的人类有权以牺牲所有其他物种和土地本身为代价来生存。这个国家的理想过去和现在都堪称卓越：每个人都有权以尊严追求公平、有价值的存在。然而，实现手段却逐渐侵蚀了这一理想，达尔文原理被公众解读后不幸被移植到经济领域，并塑造了人类作为政治动物的形象。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: "How a belief in reincarnation and immortality added support to life, so that it didn't seem dead-ended. That I could relax now, admitting and realizing that I did have certain fears per the sinful self's document (instead of pretending that I didn't). Because brought into the light I really could handle them and see how they originated. Stuff on how society operated, whether it knew it or not, on a reincarnational basis, and how association was many-lives-thick, so to speak. Only these are thoughts I'm left with, connected to the experience while I've forgotten the events themselves, and the scenes that were extremely colorful and emotionally charged."
quotes.translation: "对轮回与不朽的信念如何为生命增添支撑，使其不再显得死路一条。我现在可以放松下来，承认并意识到自己确实对'罪恶自我'文件怀有某些恐惧（而非假装没有）。因为当这些恐惧被带到光明中时，我真正能够处理它们并看清其起源。关于社会如何运作——无论它是否意识到——都建立在轮回基础上，而关联性可以说是多生累世累积的。这些只是我留存的想法，与体验相连，却已遗忘事件本身，以及那些色彩斑斓、情感充沛的场景。"
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: It is impossible to separate your daily experience in any of its aspects from your beliefs and those judgments that you place upon them. The beliefs boil down to your ideas of right and wrong, and they involve all of your attitudes concerning illness and health, wealth and poverty, the relationships of the races, religious conflicts, and more important, your intimate day-by-day psychological reality.
quotes.translation: 你日常体验的任何方面都无法与你的信念及你对这些信念所做的判断分离。这些信念归根结底是你对是非的看法，涉及你对疾病与健康、贫富、种族关系、宗教冲突的所有态度，更重要的是，你每日亲密接触的心理实相。

quotes.text: Now: Your beliefs and intents cause you to pick, from an unpredictable group of actions, those that you want to happen. You experience those events. (To me:) "Your" desire to live straddled the death of the child in an operation. The child's desire to die chose that event. People are as free as atoms are. Give us a moment ... In no way could you predict what would happen to the child in that photograph of yourself.6 In no way now can you "predict" what will happen to you now. You can choose to accept as your reality any number of given unpredictable events. In that respect, the choice is yours, but all the events you do not accept occur nevertheless.
quotes.translation: 现在：你的信念与意图，会从一组不可预测的行动中，挑选出你希望发生的事件。你体验这些事件。（对我而言：）你"想要活下去"的渴望，与手术中那个孩子的死亡并存。那个孩子求死的意愿选择了这个事件。人与原子同样自由。请稍等片刻……你完全无法预测那张童年照片里的孩子会遭遇什么。此刻你也完全无法"预测"自己将经历什么。你可以选择接受任何数量的不可预测事件作为你的实相。就此而言，选择权在你手中，但所有未被你接纳的事件依然会发生。

quotes.text: Physically you have at your fingertips certain accumulations of knowledge, objectified through the passage of information verbally through the ages, in records or books, and through television. [Now] you use computers to help you process information, and you have a more or less direct access to physical knowledge. You acquired it through the use of your senses. There is systematized knowledge, where men have accumulated facts in one particular field, processing it in one way or another. Your own senses bring you information each moment, and that information is in a way already invisibly processed according to your own beliefs, desires, and intents.
quotes.translation: 在物质层面，你指尖可及之处积累着特定知识——这些知识通过世代口耳相传、记录在书籍或电视中而客观化。如今你借助电脑处理信息，对物质知识的获取近乎直接。你通过感官获取这些知识。当人类在特定领域积累事实并以各种方式处理时，便形成了系统化知识。你的感官每时每刻都在传递信息，而这些信息早已根据你自身的信念、欲望与意图，以无形的方式被处理过。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: When we began, he found it difficult to believe that so many answers were available in the conscious mind, and was astonished as he proceeded to discover that this was the case. I will use him here as an example to some extent, to show how a bridge belief appeared to assimilate what seemed to be diametrically opposed ideas. The same procedures will operate regardless of the particular beliefs held.
quotes.translation: 当我们开始时，他难以相信有如此多的答案存在于意识心之中，并在逐步发现事实确实如此时感到惊讶。我将在某种程度上以他为例，展示一个桥梁信念如何出现以整合看似截然对立的观念。无论持有何种特定信念，同样的程序都会运作。

quotes.text: (For some years now, organized religion as a whole has been suffering from a loss of faith and members, stripped of its mysteries by science, which, with the best of intentions, offers in religion's place a secular humanism -- the belief that one doesn't need blind faith in a god in order to be morally concerned for the common welfare; paradoxically, however, this concern is most of the time expressed in religious terms, or with religious feeling. Yet science too has experienced many failures in theory and technology, and knows a new humility; at least partly because of these failures, anti-intellectualism has grown noticeably in recent years.
quotes.translation: （多年来，有组织的宗教整体上一直遭受信仰和信徒流失之苦，其神秘性被科学剥夺——科学怀着最好的意图，以世俗人文主义取代宗教的位置：即相信一个人无需盲目信仰神祇，也能在道德上关怀公共福祉；然而矛盾的是，这种关怀大多时候却以宗教术语或宗教情感来表达。但科学本身在理论与技术上也经历了许多失败，并懂得了一种新的谦卑；至少部分由于这些失败，反智主义近年来显著增长。）

quotes.text: There are tests to pass before entry. There are rituals to be acted out. Now, all of this is highly distorted. Any attempt to so rigorously and precisely express inner reality is bound to be abortive, highly misleading, and in your terms sometimes dangerous; for you do create your own reality and live it according to your inner beliefs. Therefore, be careful also of those beliefs that you accept.
quotes.translation: 进入之前有考验要过。有仪式要执行。现在，这一切都被高度扭曲了。任何如此严格而精确地表达内在实相的尝试都注定失败、极具误导性，并且在你们看来有时是危险的；因为你们确实创造了自己的实相，并根据内在信念来活出它。因此，也要小心你所接受的信念。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: (10:08.) Sexuality is the only strong area of energy with which some people are connected, so it becomes the focal point for all of their beliefs about the self in general. In doing some of these exercises, you might come across images of masturbation, homosexual or lesbian encounters, or simply old sexual fantasies, and immediately backtrack because your beliefs may tell you that these are evil.
quotes.translation: 性欲是某些人与能量相连的唯一强烈领域，因此它成为他们所有关于自我信念的焦点。在进行这些练习时，你可能会遇到手淫、同性恋或女同性恋接触的意象，或仅仅是旧的性幻想，并立即退缩，因为你的信念可能告诉你这些是邪恶的。

quotes.text: Now, there's much confusion on the part of women over whether to have mammograms. The process isn't infallible, unfortunately; also, misinterpretations of its results have caused a number of cancer-free women to undergo mastectomies -- often radical ones -- when they didn't have to. Moreover, each of these individuals has to live with the belief that they've had cancer, and must constantly be on the alert for any signs of its recurrence -- signs they do not find. At the same time, they are subjected to even more X-ray examinations on a regular basis. They can also have insurance and employment problems (as can many other cancer patients).
quotes.translation: 现在，女性对于是否进行乳房X光检查存在许多困惑。不幸的是，这个过程并非万无一失；此外，对其结果的误解导致许多没有癌症的女性接受了不必要的乳房切除术——通常是根治性的。而且，这些个体中的每一个都必须带着他们曾患癌症的信念生活，并必须时刻警惕任何复发的迹象——这些迹象他们并未发现。同时，他们还要定期接受更多的X光检查。他们也可能面临保险和就业问题（就像许多其他癌症患者一样）。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Like the entire American hostage affair (in Iran), any physical event serves as a focus that attracts all of its probable versions and outcomes. The hostage situation (now in day 53) is a materialized mass dream, meant to be important and vital on political and religious platforms of reality, meant to dramatize a conflict of beliefs, and to project that conflict outward into the realm of public knowledge. Everyone involved was consciously and unconsciously a willing participant at the most basic levels of human behavior, and it is of course no coincidence that 1980 is immediately foreshadowed by that event. What will the world do with it?
quotes.translation: 就像整个美国人质事件（在伊朗）一样，任何物理事件都作为一个焦点，吸引其所有可能的版本和结果。人质局势（现已进入第53天）是一个物质化的集体梦境，旨在在现实的政治和宗教平台上具有重要性和活力，旨在戏剧化信念的冲突，并将该冲突投射到公共知识的领域。每个参与者都在人类行为的最基本层面上有意识或无意识地成为自愿参与者，当然，1980年由该事件直接预示绝非巧合。世界将如何应对它？
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: (10:03.) You must return, wiser creatures, to the nature that spawned you -- not only as loving caretakers but as partners with the other species of the earth. You must discover once again the spirituality of your biological heritage. The majority of accepted beliefs -- religious, scientific, and cultural -- have tended to stress a sense of powerlessness, impotence, and impending doom -- a picture in which man and his world is an accidental production with little meaning, isolated yet seemingly ruled by a capricious God. Life is seen as "a valley of tears" -- almost as a low-grade infection from which the soul can be cured only by death.
quotes.translation: （10点03分）你们必须作为更智慧的生灵回归孕育你们的自然——不仅是充满爱心的守护者，更是地球其他物种的伙伴。你们必须重新发现生物传承中的灵性本质。大多数被接受的信念——无论是宗教、科学还是文化方面的——都倾向于强调无力感、无能与迫在眉睫的厄运——描绘出人类及其世界是偶然产物、毫无意义、孤立无援却似乎被反复无常的神明统治的图景。生命被视为"涕泣之谷"——几乎像一种低级感染，唯有死亡才能治愈灵魂。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Your beliefs tell you, again, that the body is primarily a mechanism--a most amazing machine, but a machine (louder), without its own purpose, without any intent, a mindless assembly plant of assorted parts that simply happened to grow together in a certain prescribed fashion. Science says that there is no will, yet it assigns to nature the will to survive--or rather, a will-less instinct to survive. To that extent it does admit (underlined) that the machine of the body "intends" to insure its own survival--but a survival which has no meaning beyond itself. And because [the body] is a machine, it is expected to decay after so much usage.
quotes.translation: 你们的信念再次告诉你们：身体本质上是一台机器——一台最神奇的机器，但终究是机器（提高音量），没有自身目的，没有意图，只是各种零件按特定方式偶然拼凑而成的无意识装配厂。科学声称没有意志，却赋予自然求存的意志——更确切地说，是无意志的求生本能。就此而言，它确实承认（加重语气）身体这部机器"意图"确保自身存活——但这种存活本身毫无超越性意义。正因它是机器，人们便预期它在使用过度后会腐朽。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Television interacts with your lives, but it does not cause your lives. It does not cause the events that it depicts. With your great belief in technology, it often seems to many people that television causes violence, for example, or that it causes a love of overmaterialism, or that it causes "loose morals." Television reflects. In a manner of speaking it does not even distort, though it may reflect distortions. The writers and actors of television dramas are attuned to the "mass mind." They are not leaders or followers. They are creative reflectors, acutely aware of the overall, generalized emotional and psychic patterns of the age.
quotes.translation: 电视与你们的生活相互作用，但它并不创造你们的生活。它不制造所描绘的事件。由于你们对技术的极度信仰，许多人常认为电视引发暴力，或导致过度物质主义，或造成"道德沦丧"。电视只是反映。从某种角度说，它甚至不会扭曲，尽管可能反映扭曲。电视剧的编剧和演员与"大众心智"相协调。他们既非引领者亦非追随者，而是创造性的反映者，敏锐感知着这个时代整体性的情感与心灵模式。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: "Being your own natural and magical self when you dream, you utilize information that is outside of the time context experienced by the so-called rational mind. The creative abilities operate in the same fashion, appearing within consecutive time, but with the main work done outside of it entirely.... When you were both working on your projects, your cultural time was taken up in a way you found acceptable. When the projects were done, particularly with Ruburt, there was still the cultural belief that time should be so used (underlined), that creativity must be directed and disciplined to fall into the proper assembly-line time slots.
quotes.translation: 当你做梦时，你展现了自己自然且神奇的本性，运用了所谓理性心智所体验的时间脉络之外的信息。创造能力以同样的方式运作，看似出现在连续的时间中，但主要工作却完全在时间之外完成……当你们两人都在进行各自的项目时，你们发现文化时间以可接受的方式被占用。当项目完成后，尤其是对鲁柏而言，仍然存在这样的文化信念：时间应该如此使用（划线强调），创造力必须被引导和约束，以落入适当的流水线时间槽中。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: In writing down your list of personal beliefs, therefore, leave nothing out. Examine the list as though it belonged to someone else. I did not want to imply that you make a list of specifically negative ideas, however. It is of supreme importance that you recognize the existence of joyful beliefs, and take into consideration those elements of your own experience with which you have had success.
quotes.translation: 因此，在写下你的个人信念清单时，不要遗漏任何内容。像检视别人的清单那样检视这份清单。然而，我并非暗示你要列出一份专门针对负面想法的清单。至关重要的是，你要认识到喜悦信念的存在，并考虑你自身经验中那些你已取得成功的要素。

quotes.text: This will last very briefly. Heaven and hell, indeed, are mere representations. They represented originally intuitive insights. But no heaven or hell exist in those terms. There is no place within the universe or within any universe or system for them. You create reality according to your beliefs and expectations. Therefore, it behooves you to examine your beliefs and expectations very clearly.
quotes.translation: 这只会持续很短的时间。天堂和地狱，确实只是表象。它们最初代表的是直觉洞见。但天堂或地狱并不以那些术语存在。宇宙之内，或任何宇宙或系统之内，都没有它们的位置。你根据你的信念和期望创造实相。因此，你应当非常清晰地检视你的信念和期望。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: (Within such a gloomy framework, then, I think it legitimate to ask how the species can consciously stress its accidental presence in the cosmos, yet demand that its members be the most "moral" of creatures. If science insists that there was, and is, no design or planner behind man's emergence, then how can man be expected to act as if there was, or is? Seth hasn't said so yet, but I think such contradictions play an important negative role in present world conditions. The attitude that life is a godless thing is so pervasive -- and not only in Western cultures -- that in Seth's terms it can be called an invisible mass core belief.
quotes.translation: 在这样的阴郁框架下，我认为有理由质疑：人类物种如何能一边有意识地强调自己在宇宙中的偶然存在，一边却要求其成员成为最"道德"的生物？如果科学坚持人类出现背后从未有过、也不存在任何设计或规划者，那么又如何期望人类表现得仿佛存在过或存在着？赛斯尚未明说，但我认为这类矛盾在当今世界局势中扮演着重要的负面角色。生命是无神之物的态度如此普遍——不仅限于西方文化——用赛斯的话说，这可以被称为一种无形的群体核心信念。

quotes.text: In some cases of spiritual healing, belief is not necessary. In Ruburt's case belief and unbelief were part of the problem itself. Belief therefore was necessary, for unbelief of spontaneity, fear of spontaneity, was a basic cause of the difficulty- ....
quotes.translation: 在某些灵性疗愈案例中，信念并非必要。但在鲁柏的案例中，信念与不信念本身就是问题的一部分。因此信念是必要的，因为对自发性的不信任、对自发性的恐惧，正是困境的根本原因——
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: Ruburt has been working with alterations of consciousness (for Psychic Politics), and wondering about the basic validity of religion. He has been trying to reconcile intellectual and emotional knowledge. James is far from one of his favorite writers, yet Ruburt's interests, intent, and desire were close enough so that under certain conditions he could experience the world view held by James. The unknown reality is unknown only because you believe it must be hidden. Once that belief is annihilated, the other quite-as-legitimate views of reality can appear to your consciousness, and worlds just as valid as your own swim into view.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏一直在研究意识的变化（为《心灵政治》一书），并质疑宗教的基本有效性。他试图调和理性知识与情感知识。詹姆斯远非他最喜欢的作家之一，但鲁柏的兴趣、意图和渴望足够接近，以至于在特定条件下，他能体验到詹姆斯所持的世界观。未知实相之所以未知，只因为你相信它必然隐藏。一旦这个信念被摧毁，其他同样合理的实相观点便能进入你的意识，与你自己的世界同样真实的世界便会浮现眼前。

quotes.text: You apply this belief to physical systems and psychological ones. In terms of sex, you insist upon a picture that shows you a growth into a sexual identity, a clear focus, and then in old age a falling away of clear sexual identification into "sexual disorder." It does not occur to you that the original premise or focus, the identification of identity with sexual nature, is "unnatural." It is you, then, who form the entire framework from which your judgment is made. In many cases the person is truer to his or her own identity in childhood or old age, when greater individual freedom is allowed, and sexual roles are more flexible.
quotes.translation: 你将这个信念应用于物质系统和心理系统。在性别方面，你坚持一种画面，显示你成长为一个性别身份，一个清晰的焦点，然后在老年时，清晰的性别认同逐渐消退，陷入"性别混乱"。你从未想到，最初的前提或焦点——将身份与性别本质等同——是"不自然的"。因此，是你自己构建了整个框架，从中做出你的判断。在许多情况下，人在童年或老年时更忠于自己的身份，因为那时允许更大的个人自由，性别角色也更加灵活。

quotes.text: The body's innate knowledge, then, will try to translate itself often into psychological activity that may result in hunches, premonitions, and so forth. The senses may be utilized to clarify the message. You might hear a voice mentally, for example, or see a flashing image. According to your beliefs, you may interpret such data in any of many ways, but because such experiences are not an accepted part of recognized, official activity, they can appear frightening. Period. You may assign them to "spirits" or disembodied personalities, but in such a way that these are thrown together in a confusing mass of dogma or superstition.
quotes.translation: 因此，身体的内在知识会试图将自身转化为心理活动，可能表现为直觉、预感等。感官可能被用来澄清这些信息。例如，你可能会在脑海中听到一个声音，或看到一闪而过的影像。根据你的信念，你可能以多种方式解读这些数据，但由于这类经历并非公认的官方活动的一部分，它们可能显得令人恐惧。句号。你可能将它们归因于“灵体”或无形的人格，但这种方式却使它们混杂在教条或迷信的混乱堆中。

quotes.text: (10:16.) Now: In almost all instances, demons in dreams represent the dreamer's belief in evil, instantly materialized. They are not the inhabitants of some nether world, then, or underground. We will be giving some instructions that will enable readers to experiment with the projection of consciousness at least to some extent. It is very important for you to realize that even in dreams you form your own reality. Your state of mind, freed from its usual physical focus, creatively expresses itself in all of its power and brilliance. The state of mind itself serves as an intent, propelling you into realities of like conditions.
quotes.translation: （10:16。）现在：在几乎所有情况下，梦中的恶魔代表做梦者对邪恶的信念，并立即具象化。因此，它们并非某个地下世界的居民。我们将提供一些指导，使读者至少能在某种程度上尝试意识投射。认识到即使在梦中你也形成自己的现实，这一点非常重要。你的心智状态，从通常的物质焦点中解放出来，以其全部力量和光辉创造性地表达自己。心智状态本身作为一种意图，将你推向相似条件的现实。

quotes.text: You may find yourself thinking, "I am no one to begin with," or "The rich get richer and the poor get poorer," or, "The world is against me," or, "Money is wrong. People who have it are not spiritual." You may discover, again, one of numerous beliefs that all lead to the fact that you do not want to have money or are afraid of it. In any case your imagination and your beliefs go hand in hand.
quotes.translation: 你可能会发现自己想：“我本来就是个无名小卒”，或“富者愈富，穷者愈穷”，或“世界与我为敌”，或“金钱是邪恶的。拥有金钱的人不灵性。”你可能会再次发现众多信念之一，这些信念都导致你不想拥有金钱或害怕金钱的事实。无论如何，你的想象力和你的信念是相辅相成的。

quotes.text: The mother could not consciously control the bodily processes that lead to birth. In the truest sense, the birth magically happens, as miraculous in those terms as the so-called initial emergence of life upon the planet itself. Scientific analysis of the brain will tell you nothing about the power that moves your thoughts, or hint at the source of the brain's abilities. However, the constant activity between Frameworks 1 and 2 is constantly apparent in the very existence of your world, and in the relationships involving your imagination, feelings, and beliefs, and those private and shared events that compose your experience.
quotes.translation: 母亲无法有意识地控制导致分娩的身体过程。从最真实的意义上说，分娩是神奇地发生的，其奇迹程度不亚于所谓生命在地球上的最初出现。对大脑的科学分析不会告诉你是什么力量在驱动你的思想，也不会暗示大脑能力的来源。然而，框架1与框架2之间的持续活动，在你世界的存在本身中，以及在你想象、情感、信念之间的关系中，在构成你经验的那些私密与共享事件中，始终清晰可见。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: One is the cancer drive literature, and television "public service" announcements, in which the seven danger signals of cancer are given. Unfortunately, again, within the framework of your beliefs this also becomes almost a necessity for many -- especially for those who, because of previous experience of one kind or another with the disease, are almost irrational in their fear of it. The literature and announcements act as strong negative suggestions, following the nature of natural hypnosis -- as a conditioning process, you see, where you are looking for specific symptoms, and examining your body under the impetus of fear.
quotes.translation: 其一是癌症宣传文献和电视"公益广告"，其中列出了癌症的七大危险信号。不幸的是，在你的信念体系内，这对许多人来说几乎也成了一种必要——尤其是对那些因过去某种疾病经历而对癌症怀有近乎非理性恐惧的人。这些文献和广告遵循自然催眠的本质，作为条件反射过程，充当了强烈的负面暗示——你看，你在恐惧的驱使下寻找特定症状，检查自己的身体。

quotes.text: This is most difficult to explain, but the capacity for full conscious life is inherent in each portion of the body itself. Otherwise, in fact, its smooth synchronicity would be impossible. The brain has abilities you do not use consciously because your beliefs prevent you from initiating the proper neural habits. Certain portions of the brain seem dominant only because of those neural habits that are adopted in any given civilization or time. But other cultures in your past have experienced reality quite differently as a result of encouraging different neural patterns, and putting experience together through other focuses.
quotes.translation: 这最难解释，但充分意识生命的能力内在于身体的每个部分本身。否则，事实上，其顺畅的同步性将不可能实现。大脑拥有你未意识使用的能力，因为你的信念阻止你启动适当的神经习惯。大脑的某些部分之所以显得占主导地位，仅仅是因为任何特定文明或时代所采用的神经习惯。但你过去其他文化因鼓励不同的神经模式，并通过其他焦点整合经验，而以截然不同的方式体验现实。

quotes.text: You attempt then to further banish the characteristic enjoyment of your own creaturehood, denying the lusty spirituality of your flesh and the strong present corporeal leanings of your soul. You will try to rid yourself of very natural emotions, and so be cheated of their great spiritual and physical motion. (Pause.) On the other hand, some leaders may give little consideration to such issues, but still be deeply convinced of the misery of the human condition, focusing upon all the "darker" elements, seeing the world's destruction ever closer to hand without really examining the beliefs that arouse such constant feelings.
quotes.translation: 于是你试图进一步驱逐自身生物性所固有的欢愉特质，否认你肉体蓬勃的灵性，以及你灵魂强烈而现世的肉体倾向。你会试图摆脱非常自然的情感，从而被剥夺其伟大的灵性与物质动能。（停顿）另一方面，某些领袖或许很少考虑这类议题，却仍深信人类境遇的悲惨，聚焦于所有"阴暗"元素，眼见世界毁灭日益逼近，却未曾真正检视引发这些持续感受的信念。

quotes.text: (10:00.) They were left to observe the physical phenomenon, still watching the water's rise but with the inner knowledge of safety. Ruburt needed the experience in order to gain added faith in his own abilities. Both of them needed the assurance that those abilities are natural, and can be used in private dealings with nature. Ruburt also found that he had put himself in a position in which he had underrated the importance of physical manipulation. Both Ruburt and Joseph are very mental people, however, and so they sought out this physical meeting with material phenomena and solved the problem according to their beliefs.
quotes.translation: （10:00。）他们被留下来观察物理现象，仍注视着水位的上升，但内心知晓安全无虞。鲁柏需要这次经历，以增强对自己能力的信心。他们两人都需要确信这些能力是自然的，并能在与自然的私人互动中运用。鲁柏也发现，他让自己处于低估身体操作重要性的位置。不过，鲁柏和约瑟都是非常心智型的人，因此他们寻求与物质现象的这种物理会面，并根据他们的信念解决了问题。

quotes.text: (9:39.) There is nothing inherent in the waking state that causes it to be so limited. The boundaries set are your own. The body naturally heals itself, for example. Many give lip service to such a belief. In actuality, however, most of you believe -- and experience -- a far different picture, in which the body must be protected at all costs from a natural leaning toward disease and poor health. Viruses must be warded off, as if you had no protection against them. The natural healing that often occurs in the dream state is undone in the waking state, in which any such cure is seen as "miraculous" and against "the rules."
quotes.translation: （9:39。）清醒状态本身并无任何固有特性使其如此受限。所设的界限是你自己的。例如，身体自然会自我疗愈。许多人只是口头上相信这种信念。然而，实际上，你们大多数人相信——并体验到——一幅截然不同的画面，其中身体必须不惜一切代价保护，免受自然倾向疾病和健康不佳的影响。病毒必须被抵御，仿佛你对此毫无防护。在梦境状态中经常发生的自然疗愈，在清醒状态中被抵消，任何此类疗愈被视为“奇迹”且“违反规则”。

quotes.text: The unknown reality: Much of that reality is unknown simply because your beliefs close you off from your own knowledge. The reaches of your own consciousness are not limited. Because you accept the idea of a straight-line movement of time, you cannot see before or after what you think of as your birth or death,* yet your greater consciousness is quite aware of such experience. Ideally it is possible not only to remember "past" lives, but to plan future ones now. In greater terms, all such lives happen at once. Your present neurological structure makes this seem impossible, yet your inner consciousness is not so impeded.
quotes.translation: 未知的现实：那现实的很大部分之所以未知，仅仅是因为你的信念使你与自己的知识隔绝。你自身意识的范围并不受限。因为你接受时间直线运动的概念，你无法看到你所认为的出生或死亡之前或之后，*然而你的更大意识完全知晓此类体验。理想情况下，不仅可能记住“过去”的转世，还能现在规划未来的转世。从更大层面来说，所有此类转世同时发生。你当前的神经结构使这看似不可能，然而你的内在意识并不受此阻碍。

quotes.text: (9:35.) Impulses arise in a natural, spontaneous, constructive response to the abilities, potentials, and needs of the personality. They are meant as directing forces. Luckily, the child usually walks before it is old enough to be taught that impulses are wrong, and luckily the child's natural impulses toward exploration, growth, fulfillment, action and power are strong enough to give it the necessary springboard before your belief systems begin to erode its confidence. You have physical adult bodies. The pattern for each adult body existed in the fetus -- which again, "luckily," impulsively, followed its own direction.
quotes.translation: （9点35分。）冲动是人格对其能力、潜能与需求所做出的自然、自发且具有建设性的回应。它们本应作为引导力量。幸运的是，儿童通常在尚未被教导"冲动是错误的"之前就已学会行走；更幸运的是，儿童朝向探索、成长、实现、行动与力量的自然冲动足够强烈，能在你们的信念体系开始侵蚀其自信之前，为其提供必要的跳板。你们拥有成人的物质身体。每个成人身体的模式早已存在于胎儿之中——而胎儿同样"幸运地"凭借冲动遵循着自身的方向。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: There were no schizophrenics in the time of the pagans, for the belief systems did not support that kind of interpretation. This does not mean that certain behavior did not occur that you would now call schizophrenic. It means that generally speaking such behavior fit within the psychological picture of reality. It [did so] because many of the behavior patterns associated, now, with schizophrenia, are "distorted and debased" remnants of behavior patterns that are part and parcel of man's heritage, and that harken back to activities and abilities that at one time had precise social meaning, and served definite purposes.
quotes.translation: 异教徒时代不存在精神分裂症患者，因为当时的信念体系不支持这种解读。这并非意味着现今被称为精神分裂症的某些行为在当时从未发生，而是指一般而言，这类行为符合当时对现实的心理认知框架。之所以如此，是因为现今与精神分裂症相关的许多行为模式，实为人类与生俱来的行为模式中"扭曲且降格"的残余——这些模式可追溯至曾具有明确社会意义并服务于特定目的的活动与能力。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: If this is not apparent to you, then it is because you have not as yet completely examined your beliefs. Let us take a simple example: You are overweight. You have tried diets to no avail. You tell yourself that you want to lose weight. You follow what I have said so far. You change the belief. You say, "Because I believe I am overweight, I am, so I will think of myself at my ideal weight."
quotes.translation: 如果这对你而言并不明显，那是因为你尚未完全检视你的信念。让我们举个简单的例子：你超重了。你尝试过节食却毫无成效。你告诉自己你想减肥。你遵循了我至今所说的内容。你改变了信念。你说："因为我相信自己超重，所以我确实如此，因此我将把自己想象成理想体重。"

quotes.text: They are made material through quite automatic processes. Change your beliefs and you change your material circumstances. Your beliefs now concerning finances and the automobile are actually keeping you from bettering your financial situation. This is directly caused by the fact that you suspect such ideas as these, beneficial on theoretical grounds, but quite impractical in your actuality.
quotes.translation: 它们通过相当自动化的过程被物质化。改变你的信念，你就改变了你的物质环境。你现在关于财务和汽车的信念，实际上正在阻止你改善自己的财务状况。这直接源于你怀疑这些想法——理论上虽有益处，但在你的现实中却相当不切实际。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: Over the phone Ruburt reminded Andrea of her own basic uniqueness, and also of the fact that she was creating her reality through beliefs. Ruburt reinforced other ideas that Andrea had momentarily forgotten -- the fact, among others, of her own true worth; and because Ruburt believed in Andrea's worth, and because Andrea knew it, this more positive belief rose up to shove the others aside.
quotes.translation: 透过电话，鲁柏提醒安德烈亚她自身的基本独特性，以及她正通过信念创造自己实相的事实。鲁柏强化了安德烈亚暂时遗忘的其他观念——尤其是她自身真实价值的真相；因为鲁柏相信安德烈亚的价值，而安德烈亚也深知这一点，这个更积极的信念便升起，将其他信念推到一旁。

quotes.text: Now: Ruburt's mind is far more aware of other realities than his brain is, but he consciously believes in the greater reality of himself and his perceptions. The brain also possesses this belief, and so it opens itself as much as possible to the mind's activities. Because it does, certain intuitive psychic and "intellectually spacious" experiences can be physically felt to some extent. The knowledge is interpreted through alterations in body sensation, which give it an important corporeal validity. In such cases high mental and psychic activity is reflected in the body's experience, providing a beneficial unity.
quotes.translation: 现在：鲁柏的心智远比他的大脑更清楚其他实相，但他有意识地相信自己和感知的更广大实相。大脑也拥有这种信念，因此它尽可能向心智的活动敞开。正因如此，某些直觉性的灵性及"智性开阔"的体验在某种程度上能被身体感知到。这些知识通过身体感觉的变化被诠释，赋予其重要的肉体真实性。在这种情况下，高度的心智与灵性活动反映在身体的体验中，形成有益的统合。

quotes.text: You are setting out to experience the most fulfilled reality that you can. To do this you have, hopefully, begun to examine your beliefs. You may want others to change. In doing so you begin with yourself. I told you (in the 619th session) to imagine a game in which you see yourself acting in line with the new desired belief. As you do so, see yourself affecting others in the new fashion.
quotes.translation: 你正着手体验所能达到的最圆满的实相。为此，你已开始审视自己的信念。你可能希望他人改变，但这样做时，你从自己开始。我告诉过你（在第619节中）想象一个游戏，在其中你看到自己依照新的理想信念行事。当你这样做时，看到自己以新的方式影响他人。

quotes.text: According to your intent, your desire, and your beliefs, your ideas intersect with the reality that you know, with physical space and physical time -- they become real, in historic terms. In other realities there are different historical terms. A war won here, with a treaty, is not won somewhere else, and there is a different treaty. Even wars seemingly won here are not nearly as clear-cut as would appear; you make history as you go along. You use, in your terms now only, the past as source material. You rewrite it as you go along. As records are lost, you do not even realize that you have rewritten the past.
quotes.translation: 根据你的意图、渴望和信念，你的理念与你所知的实相、物质空间和物质时间相交织——它们变得真实，以历史的角度而言。在其他实相中，有不同的历史角度。此处通过条约赢得的战争，在别处并未获胜，且存在不同的条约。即使看似在此处赢得的战争，也远非表面那般明确；你一边前行，一边创造历史。以你目前的角度而言，你只将过去作为素材。你一边前行，一边重写它。当记录遗失时，你甚至未意识到自己已重写了过去。

quotes.text: From the 742nd session in Section 6: "Atlantis is a land that you want to inhabit, appearing in your literature, your dreams, and your fantasies, serving as an impetus for development ... It carries also, however, the imprint of your fears, for the tales say that Atlantis was destroyed. You place it in your past while it exists in your future. Not the destruction alone, but the entire pattern seen through the framework of your beliefs. Beside this, however, many civilizations have come and gone in somewhat the same manner, and the "myth" [of Atlantis] is based somewhat then on physical fact in your terms."
quotes.translation: 来自第742节第六部分：“亚特兰蒂斯是一片你们想要居住的土地，出现在你们的文学、梦境和幻想中，作为发展的动力……然而，它也承载着你们的恐惧印记，因为传说中亚特兰蒂斯被毁灭了。你们将它置于过去，而它却存在于你们的未来。不仅是毁灭本身，而是通过你们信念的框架所看到的整个模式。然而，除此之外，许多文明也以类似的方式兴衰，而（亚特兰蒂斯的）‘神话’在你们的说法中，多少基于物理事实。”

quotes.text: In a manner of speaking, the conscious mind is a window through which you look outward -- and looking outward, perceive the fruits of your inner mind. Often you let false beliefs blur that great vision. Your joy, vitality and accomplishment do not come from the outside to you as the result of events that "happen to you." They spring from inner events that are the result of your beliefs.
quotes.translation: 可以说，意识心是一扇窗户，你们透过它向外看——而向外看时，你们感知到内在心智的果实。你们常常让虚假的信念模糊了那伟大的视野。你们的喜悦、活力与成就并非来自外界，作为‘发生在你身上’的事件的结果。它们源于内在事件，而这些事件是你们信念的结果。

quotes.text: (A one-minute pause at 9:59.) It is your method of assessing temporal experience according to the beliefs that it holds about the nature of reality. It automatically causes the body to react in certain ways. I cannot say this often enough: Your beliefs form your reality, your body and its condition, your personal relationships, your environment, and en masse your civilization and world.
quotes.translation: （9:59时停顿一分钟。）这是你们根据其对实相本质所持的信念来评估时间性经验的方法。它自动导致身体以特定方式反应。我再怎么强调也不为过：你们的信念形成你们的实相、你们的身体及其状况、你们的人际关系、你们的环境，以及整体而言，你们的文明和世界。

quotes.text: Ruburt advised Andrea to accept the validity of such feelings as feelings -- not to inhibit them, but to follow their flow with the understanding that they are feelings about reality. As themselves they are real. They express emotional reactions to beliefs. The next time Andrea feels inadequate, for example, she is to actively experience that feeling, realizing that even though she feels inferior this does not mean that she is inferior. She is to say, "I feel inferior," and at the same time to understand that the feeling is not a statement of fact but of emotion. A different kind of validity is involved.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏建议安德莉亚接受这类感受的真实性——不压抑它们，而是跟随它们的流动，同时理解这些感受是对信念的情感反应。它们作为感受本身是真实的，表达了对信念的情绪反应。例如，下次安德莉亚感到不足时，她应主动体验那种感受，意识到即使感到自卑，这也不意味着她真的自卑。她要说"我感到自卑"，同时理解这种感受并非事实陈述，而是情绪表达，涉及另一种层面的真实性。

quotes.text: His belief, illogical as it sounded when spoken, contradictory as it seemed when applied to daily life, stated that the individual somehow could perceive the nature of reality on his or her own by virtue of innate capacities that belonged to the individual by right -- capacities that were a part of man's heritage. In other words, Ruburt felt that there was a slim chance of opening doors of knowledge that had been closed, and he decided to take that chance.
quotes.translation: 他的信念——尽管说出来显得不合逻辑，应用于日常生活时看似矛盾——主张个体凭借与生俱来的天赋能力，能够自行感知实相的本质。这些能力是人与生俱来的权利，是人类传承的一部分。换言之，鲁柏觉得存在一线机会打开那些被关闭的知识之门，他决定抓住这个机会。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Oddly enough, the fact that the movement is conservative will work to his advantage in very strange ways. Though they may not agree with his political beliefs, they will on the other hand trust his integrity because the movement is conservative. They do not believe that he would follow an unpopular cause unless he believed in it. They will want a man who is sincere. They will also want a man who can lead. They will be afraid of a liberal. I hope that this is sufficient. After our next break I will give you what additional information I have. You may take a break, or we will continue with our session.
quotes.translation: 奇怪的是，该运动的保守性质反而会以非常奇特的方式对他有利。尽管他们可能不认同他的政治信念，但另一方面会因运动的保守性而信任他的正直。他们相信他不会追随不受欢迎的事业，除非他真心相信。他们想要一个真诚的人，也想要一个能领导的人。他们会害怕自由派。希望这些信息足够。下次休息后，我会提供额外的信息。你们可以休息，或者我们继续课程。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: I told you (in the private 836th session) that viruses mutate. Such is often the case. It seems quite scientific to believe in inoculations against such dangerous diseases -- and certainly, scientifically, inoculations seem to work: People in your time right now are not plagued by smallpox, for example. Some cultures have believed that illnesses were caused by demons. Medicine men, through certain ceremonies, would try to rid the body of the demons -- and those methods worked also. The belief system was tight and accepted, and it only began to fail when those societies encountered "civilized views."
quotes.translation: 我告诉过你（在第836节私人课中），病毒会突变。情况往往如此。相信接种疫苗能预防这类危险疾病似乎相当科学——当然，从科学角度看，接种疫苗似乎有效：例如，你们这个时代的人不再受天花困扰。有些文化曾认为疾病由恶魔引起。巫医通过特定仪式试图驱除体内恶魔——那些方法也同样有效。当时那个信念体系严密且被广泛接受，直到这些社会遭遇"文明观点"时才开始失效。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: For Seth, Framework 1 is simply a term representing the everyday, linear, conscious "working reality" we take for granted, the one in which "time" and events automatically unfold in moment after undeniable moment. It's the milieu in which most of us unthinkingly live out our physical lives. Beyond Framework 1, however, exists Framework 2, and it represents the great timeless or simultaneous spacious present that's so dearly a manifestation of All That Is. All of our dreams, plans, thoughts, actions, and choices live in Framework 2; all flow from Framework 2 into Framework 1 according to our beliefs.
quotes.translation: 对赛斯而言，架构一只是代表我们视为理所当然的日常线性意识"运作实相"的术语，在这个实相中，"时间"与事件在无可否认的连续刹那中自动展开。这是大多数人不假思索地度过物质生活的环境。然而，在架构一之外存在着架构二，它代表着那伟大的无时间性或同时性的广阔当下，这如此珍贵地彰显了万有即一切的本质。我们所有的梦境、计划、思想、行动与选择都存在于架构二中；根据我们的信念，一切从架构二流入架构一。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: (11:39.) You must, therefore, willingly suspend that belief. Using the exercises given in this chapter, you must make a conscious effort to insert a different belief; employ natural hypnosis in this new way. If you realize your own worth after reading this book, then that realization in the present can negate any past ideas of unworthiness that may have attracted you to the condition.
quotes.translation: 因此，你必须心甘情愿地搁置那个信念。运用本章提供的练习，你必须刻意努力植入一个不同的信念；以这种新方式运用自然催眠。如果你在阅读本书后认识到自己的价值，那么当下的这份认知就能否定过去任何可能吸引你陷入困境的无价值感。

quotes.text: There would be no psychological avenues to connect my world and yours. There would be no extensions of the self that would allow you to travel such a psychological distance to those thresholds of reality that form my mental environment. If the universe were structured as you have been told, the probability of my existence would be zero as far as you are concerned. There would have been no unofficial roads for Ruburt to follow, to lead him from the official beliefs of his time. He would never have acknowledged the original impulse to speak for me, and my voice would have been unheard in your world.
quotes.translation: 将没有任何心理通道连接我的世界与你的世界。将没有任何自我延伸能让你跨越如此遥远的心理距离，抵达构成我心灵环境的那些实相门槛。如果宇宙真如你所被告知的那样被构建，那么对你而言，我存在的概率将为零。将没有非官方路径可供鲁柏遵循，引领他脱离所处时代的官方信念。他永远不会承认最初为我发声的冲动，而我的声音将在你的世界无人听闻。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: (Very actively delivered:) In your current beliefs, again, consciousness is equated in very limited terms with your conception of intellectual behavior: you consider this to be a peak of mental achievement, growing from the "undifferentiated" perceptions of childhood, and returning ignominiously to them again in old age. Such wake-sleep patterns as I have suggested would acquaint you with the great creative and energetic portions of psychological behavior -- that are not undifferentiated at all, but simply distinct from your usual concepts of consciousness; and these operate throughout your life.
quotes.translation: （以非常活跃的方式传达：）在你当前的信念中，意识再次被以极其有限的术语等同于你对智力行为的认知：你视此为心智成就的巅峰，从童年"未分化"的感知中发展而来，又在老年时屈辱地回归其中。我所建议的这类醒睡模式，将让你熟悉心理行为中那些伟大而充满创造力的部分——它们绝非未分化，而只是与你通常的意识概念截然不同；并且这些部分贯穿你整个生命运作。

quotes.text: I am not saying here that any given sexual performance is "wrong," or meaningless, or debased, if it is not accompanied by the sentiments of love and devotion. Over a period of time, however, the expression of sex will follow the inclinations of the heart. These inclinations will color sexual expression, then. To that degree, it is "unnatural" to have sexual desire for someone whom you dislike or look down upon. The sexual ideas of domination and submission have no part in the natural life of your species, or that of the animals. Again, you interpret animal behavior according to your own beliefs.
quotes.translation: 我并非在此表示，任何特定的性行为若未伴随爱与奉献的情感，就是"错误的"、无意义的或低贱的。然而，随着时间的推移，性的表达会追随心灵的倾向。这些倾向随后会为性的表达染上色彩。就此程度而言，对你不喜欢或轻视的人产生性欲是"不自然的"。支配与臣服的性观念在你们物种或动物的自然生活中毫无立足之地。再次强调，你们是根据自己的信念来解读动物行为的。

quotes.text: In these continuous exterior religious dramas, the Hebrews played a strange role. Their idea of one god was not new to them. Many ancient religions held the belief of one god above all others. This god above all others was a far more lenient god, however, than the one the Hebrews followed. Many tribes believed, quite rightly, in the inner spirit that pervades each living thing. And they often referred to, say, the god in the tree, or the spirit in the flower. But they also accepted the reality of an overall spirit, of which these lesser spirits were but a part. All worked together harmoniously.
quotes.translation: 在这些持续的外在宗教戏剧中，希伯来人扮演了一个奇特的角色。他们关于独一神的观念对他们而言并非新鲜事。许多古老宗教都相信有一位超越众神的神。然而，这位超越众神的神远比希伯来人所追随的那位神更为宽容。许多部落相当正确地相信遍及每个生命体的内在灵性。他们常提及，比如说，树中的神或花中的灵。但他们也接受一个整体灵性的实相，这些较小的灵性只是其中的一部分。所有一切都和谐地共同运作。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Now. (Pause.) Mental "diseases" often point out the nature of your beliefs as they agree or conflict with those held by others. Here the belief systems are different than those of society to such a degree that obvious effects show in terms of behavior. There are crisis points here as with many physical illnesses, and left alone an individual may well work through to his own solution.
quotes.translation: 现在。（停顿）心理"疾病"常常指出你们信念的本质，当这些信念与他人所持的信念一致或冲突时。在此，信念系统与社会信念的差异达到如此程度，以至于在行为上显现出明显的效应。如同许多身体疾病一样，这里存在危机点，若不加干预，个体很可能自行找到解决方案。

quotes.text: They were not only his private religious beliefs, but those of his contemporaries generally -- and (loudly:) the foundations upon which your present civilization was made. He had to find the courage to encounter those old beliefs boldly, and he is finally doing so. I will have more to say to him in the dream state this evening, and I will shortly explain his experience with my voice.
quotes.translation: 那些不仅是他个人的宗教信仰，也是他同时代人的普遍信仰——并且（大声地：）是你们现今文明赖以建立的基础。他必须鼓起勇气勇敢面对那些旧有信念，而他终于开始这样做了。今晚我会在梦境状态中对他多说一些，并且很快就会解释他对我声音的体验。

quotes.text: You have reconstructed it. So when I tell you to restructure your past, I am not telling you to do something that you have not already done. Hypnosis, again, is merely a state of concentrated attention, in which you focus upon beliefs. Popular demonstrations lead the public to believe that the subject must fall asleep or be completely relaxed, yet this is not the case. The one prerequisite is an intense concentration upon specific incoming data to the exclusion of everything else. Therefore the orders given are clear-cut, to the point. No conflicting information is received, no cross messages.
quotes.translation: 你们已经重构了它。所以当我告诉你们重组你们的过去时，我并非在要求你们去做一件你们尚未做过的事。催眠，再次强调，只是一种专注的状态，在其中你们聚焦于信念。大众演示让公众相信受试者必须入睡或完全放松，然而事实并非如此。唯一的前提是对特定输入信息的高度专注，排除一切其他干扰。因此所下的指令清晰明确，直指要点。没有接收到相互矛盾的信息，也没有交叉的信息。

quotes.text: 28. Seth's remarks here are actually an extension of a long discussion on individual beliefs and spontaneity that he'd initiated in a class session two weeks ago: "Now, my words will not, I hope, be used to begin a new dogma. My dogma is the freedom of the individual (my emphasis). My dogma is the sacrilegious one -- that each of you is a good individual. There is nothing wrong with your emotions, or feelings, or being. When you know yourself then you are joyfully -- joyfully -- responsive, and, being joyfully responsive, you can carry your society to the furthest reaches of its creativity."
quotes.translation: 28. 赛斯在此处的发言，实际上是他两周前在课堂讨论中发起的一场关于个人信念与自发性的长篇讨论的延伸："现在，我希望我的话不会被用来开启新的教条。我的教条是个体的自由（我的强调）。我的教条是亵渎神圣的——你们每个人都是良善的个体。你们的情绪、感受或存在本身都没有任何问题。当你认识自己时，你就会喜悦地——喜悦地——回应，而由于能喜悦地回应，你便能将你的社会带向其创造力的最远疆界。"

quotes.text: New paragraph: Despite all "realistic" pragmatic tales to the contrary, the natural state of life itself is one of joy, acquiescence with itself -- a state in which action is effective, and the power to act is a natural right. You would see this quite clearly with plants, animals, and all other life if you were not so blinded by beliefs to the contrary. You would feel it in the activity of your bodies, in which the vital individual affirmation of your cells brings about the mass, immensely complicated achievement of your physical being. That activity naturally promotes health and vitality.
quotes.translation: 尽管所有"现实"的实用主义故事与此相反，生命本身的自然状态是一种喜悦、一种与自身和谐的状态——在这种状态中行动是有效的，而行动的能力是一种天赋权利。若非你被相反的信念蒙蔽，你会从植物、动物及所有其他生命中清楚看到这一点。你会在自己身体的活动里感受到它——在那里，你细胞中充满活力的个体肯定，带来了你物质实相那庞大而极其复杂的成就。这种活动自然促进健康与活力。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: An automobile accident, suicide, or another kind of accident might result. The person might fall prey to an epidemic, but the smoothness of biological motion or psychological motion has been lost. I am not here condoning suicide, for too often in your society it is the unfortunate result of conflicting beliefs -- and yet it is true to say that all deaths are suicide, and all births deliberate on the part of child and parent. To that extent, you cannot separate issues like a population explosion on the part of certain portions of the world, from epidemics, earthquakes, and other disasters.
quotes.translation: 一场车祸、自杀或其他意外可能因此发生。这个人可能成为流行病的猎物，但生物运动或心理运动的顺畅性已然丧失。我在此并非宽恕自杀，因为在你们社会中，它往往是冲突信念的不幸结果——然而，诚然可以说所有死亡都是自杀，所有出生都是孩子与父母双方的刻意选择。就此而言，你无法将世界某些地区的人口爆炸等问题，与流行病、地震及其他灾难分开看待。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: "The church was quite real to Ruburt as a child, through the priests who came (to the house) regularly, and through direct contact with the religious (grade) school, and the support offered to the (fatherless) family. Ruburt's very early poetry offended Father (Boyle), who burned his books on the fall of Rome, so he had more than a hypothetical feeling about such issues. Many of his fears originated long before the sessions, of course, and before he realized that there was any alternative at all between, say, conventional religious beliefs and complete disbelief in any nature of divinity.
quotes.translation: "对鲁柏而言，教堂在童年时非常真实——通过定期（到家中）来访的神父，通过直接接触宗教（小学）教育，以及（失去父亲的）家庭获得的资助。鲁柏早期的诗歌冒犯了（博伊尔）神父，他烧掉了鲁柏关于罗马陷落的书籍，因此鲁柏对这些议题的感受绝非空穴来风。当然，他的许多恐惧早在这些课开始之前就已存在，早在他意识到在传统宗教信仰与完全否定任何神性本质之间还存在其他可能性之前。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: When you are dealing with normal waking reality, you are operating at one level of the many that are native to your psyche. When you are dreaming, from your viewpoint you are entering other levels of reality quite as native to your psyche, but usually you are still experiencing those events through your current "waking station" The dreams that you remember are colored or altered or even censored to a certain extent. There is no inherent psychological or biological necessity for this. Your ideas and beliefs, however, about the nature of reality, and sanity, have resulted in such a schism.
quotes.translation: 当你处理正常的清醒实相时，你是在你心灵所固有的众多层次之一上运作。当你做梦时，从你的观点来看，你正在进入同样为你心灵所固有的其他实相层次，但通常你仍通过你当前的"清醒站"来体验那些事件。你所记得的梦境在某种程度上被着色、改变甚至审查。这并非固有的心理或生物学上的必然性。然而，你对实相本质和心智健全的观念与信念导致了这样的分裂。

quotes.text: Now those beliefs separate man from his own nature.1 He cannot trust himself -- for who can rely upon the accidental bubblings of hormones and chemicals that somehow form a stew called consciousness (louder and quite ironic) -- an unsavory brew at best, so the field of science will forever escape opening up into any great vision of the meaning of life. (Long pause.) It cannot value life, and so in its search for the ideal it can indeed justify in its philosophy the possibility of an accident that might kill many many people through direct or indirect means, and kill the unborn as well.2
quotes.translation: 这些信念将人与自身本性分离。他无法信任自己——因为谁能依赖那些偶然冒出的激素和化学物质，它们莫名其妙地炖成一锅称为意识的汤（更大声且相当讽刺）——充其量只是一锅难喝的杂烩，因此科学领域将永远无法开启对生命意义的任何伟大洞见。（长停顿。）它无法珍视生命，因此在追求理想的过程中，它确实能在其哲学中证明一种可能性：一场意外可能通过直接或间接手段杀死许多人，并杀死未出生的胎儿。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Basically the species is relatively so freewheeling, with so many potentials, that it is necessary that the mother's beliefs provide a kind of framework in the beginning, allowing the child to focus its abilities in desired directions. It knows ahead of time then the biological, spiritual, and social environment into which it is born. It is somewhat prepared to grow in a certain direction -- a direction that is applicable and suited to its conditions.
quotes.translation: 基本上，这个物种相对如此自由奔放，拥有如此多的潜能，以至于母亲的信念必须在最初提供一种框架，允许孩子将其能力聚焦于期望的方向。因此，它事先就知道自己出生时的生物、灵性与社会环境。它已有所准备，朝着某个特定方向成长——一个适用于并适合其条件的方向。

quotes.text: Again, it is important that you realize the initial nonphysical reaction to stimuli that sparks off all physical reactions. There is constant interplay and communication between the areas of consciousness that are connected to the brain and those that are not. The "deeper" purposes of the consciousness involved "circulate," sometimes arising in the awareness that is joined with the brain. Information coming from those deeper sources of the self, reaching the areas connected to the brain, will be interpreted according to the beliefs of that most physically focused segment of the self.
quotes.translation: 再次强调，重要的是要认识到，最初对刺激的非物质反应会引发所有物质反应。与大脑相连的意识领域和未与大脑相连的意识领域之间存在着持续的相互作用与交流。所涉意识的"更深层"目的会"循环"，有时会浮现于与大脑相连的觉知中。来自自我更深层源头的讯息，在抵达与大脑相连的区域时，将根据自我最聚焦于物质层面的那部分的信念而被解读。

quotes.text: Again, it is natural to express love through sexual acts -- natural and good. It is not natural to express love only through sexual acts, however. Many of Freud's sexual ideas did not reflect man's natural condition. The complexes and neuroses outlined and defined are products of your traditions and beliefs. You will naturally find some evidence for them in observed behavior. Many of the traditions do come from the Greeks, from the great Greek play-writers, who quite beautifully and tragically presented the quality of the psyche as it showed itself in the light of Grecian traditions.
quotes.translation: 再次强调，通过性行为表达爱是自然的——自然且美好。然而，仅通过性行为表达爱则是不自然的。弗洛伊德的许多性观念并未反映人类的自然状态。他所概述和定义的种种情结与神经症，实为你们传统与信念的产物。在观察到的行为中，你们自然会找到一些证据来支持它们。许多传统确实源自希腊人，源自伟大的希腊剧作家——他们优美而悲剧性地呈现了在希腊传统之光下所显现的心灵特质。

quotes.text: Each individual must examine his or her individual beliefs, or begin with feelings which will inevitably lead to them. In this area, as in all others, those of you who are proficient verbally might use the method of writing. Either write down your beliefs as they come to you, or make lists of your intellectual and emotional assumptions. You may find that they are quite different.
quotes.translation: 每个人必须检视自己个人的信念，或从感受入手，而感受必将引导至信念。在此领域，如同在其他所有领域一样，你们中那些擅长言辞的人可使用书写的方法。要么将浮现于心的信念写下来，要么列出你们的理智与情感假设。你们可能会发现两者截然不同。

quotes.text: The same kind of dilemma can arise in any reader's experience whenever two strongly conflicting core beliefs meet. Ruburt also believed in his psychic work, you see, and was fully committed to it. He developed some physical symptoms, and following through with his beliefs he is working them out on his own. He saw for himself how they perfectly mirrored his inner image of himself.
quotes.translation: 当两个强烈冲突的核心信念相遇时，任何读者都可能在自己的经历中遇到类似的困境。鲁柏也相信自己的通灵工作，你瞧，并且全身心投入其中。他出现了一些身体症状，遵循自己的信念，他正在自行解决这些问题。他亲眼看到这些症状如何完美地映照出他内在的自我形象。

quotes.text: The natural person is indeed the magical person, and you have both to some extent had very recent examples of such activity. You were, and are, trying to teach yourselves something. This is somewhat lengthy to unravel, but your behavior and experience, of course, is the result of your beliefs. Framework 22 has been a rather fascinating but mainly (underlined) hypothetical framework, in that neither of you have really been able to put it to any perceivable use in your terms. This is not to say it has not been operating. You have not had the kind of feedback, however, that you want.
quotes.translation: 自然之人确实是神奇之人，你们两人最近都在某种程度上经历过此类活动的实例。你们一直在试图教导自己一些东西。这需要相当篇幅来厘清，但你们的行为和经历自然是你们信念的结果。框架22一直是个相当迷人但主要（加着重号）是假设性的框架，因为你们两人都未能真正按自己的理解将其付诸任何可感知的运用。这并非说它没有在运作。然而，你们尚未获得想要的那种反馈。

quotes.text: Such knowledge requires not only more responsive and responsible behavior, but involves a sympathy with life that may earlier have been lacking. The sympathy brings with it a sensitivity that is strong, challenging and intense. Many individuals have experienced unusual, quite valid and intense expansions of consciousness, but found themselves unable to correlate the new knowledge with past beliefs, to make the changes necessary to handle the sensitivity. Indeed, they were not strong enough to contain the experience. In such cases, they tried to close it off, deny it, forget it.
quotes.translation: 这种知识不仅需要更敏锐且负责任的行为，还涉及一种可能先前缺乏的对生命的同情。这种同情带来一种强烈、具有挑战性且深刻的敏感度。许多人经历过不寻常、相当真实且强烈的意识扩展，却发现自己无法将新知识与过去的信念联系起来，无法做出必要的改变来应对这种敏感度。事实上，他们不够强大到足以容纳这种体验。在这种情况下，他们试图关闭它、否认它、忘记它。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Your sense data, again, will most definitely reinforce your ideas. You will also react clairvoyantly and telepathically to inner information at an unconscious level that is, once more, "collected" under the organization of your quite conscious concepts concerning existence in general, and your own in particular. So you are locked into physical situations that are corroborated by the great evidence of sense data -- and of course it is convincing because it reflects so beautifully, so creatively, and so actively, your own ideas and beliefs, whether they are positive or negative.
quotes.translation: 再次强调，你的感官资料必定会强化你的想法。你也会在潜意识层面以千里眼和心灵感应的方式对内在信息做出反应，这些信息再次在你有意识的概念组织下被"收集"——这些概念涉及一般存在，特别是你自己的存在。因此，你被锁在由感官资料的大量证据所证实的物质情境中——这当然令人信服，因为它如此美丽、如此创造性地、如此活跃地反映了你自己的观念和信念，无论它们是正面的还是负面的。

quotes.text: At this point do not think of the future, but only of the present. If you are overweight, insert the weight that you think is ideal for you while you are following this exercise. Imagine that you are healthy if you have the belief that you are not. If you are lonely, believe that you are filled with the feeling of companionship instead. Realize that you are exerting your initiative to imagine such situations. Here there can be no comparison with your normal situation. Use visual data, or words -- whatever is most natural to you. And again, no more than ten minutes is required.
quotes.translation: 此时不要思考未来，只关注当下。如果你超重，在跟随这个练习时，插入你认为理想的体重。如果你持有不健康的信念，就想象自己是健康的。如果你感到孤独，就相信你充满了陪伴的感觉。认识到你正在发挥主动性去想象这些情境。这里不能与你正常的情况进行比较。使用视觉资料或语言——任何对你来说最自然的方式。同样，不需要超过十分钟。

quotes.text: Those of you who believe in reincarnation in more or less conventional terms, can make the error of using or blaming "past" lives, organizing them through your current beliefs. It is bad enough to believe that you are at the mercy of one past, but to consider yourself helpless before innumerable previous errors from other lives puts you in an impossible situation; the conscious will is robbed of its power to act. Such lives exist simultaneously. They are other expressions of yourself, interacting, but with each conscious self possessing the point of power in its own present.
quotes.translation: 那些或多或少以传统方式相信转世的人，可能会犯下利用或责怪"前世"的错误，通过你们当前的信念来组织它们。相信你们受制于一个过去已经够糟了，但若认为自己在无数来自其他生命的先前错误面前无能为力，则会使你们陷入不可能的境地；意识意志被剥夺了行动的力量。这样的生命同时存在。它们是你们自己的其他表达，相互互动，但每个有意识的自我都在其自身的当下拥有力量点。

related_concepts.name_en: The point of power is in the present

quotes.text: Your beliefs so structure your experience individually and en masse, however, that evidential material contrary to those ideas shows itself but seldom, or in distorted or exaggerated form. It is quite natural, biologically and psychologically, to operate in certain fashions that are not acceptable in your society, and that seem to run counter to your picture of mankind's history. In terms of your definitions, then, it is quite natural for some people to behave as males sexually and as females psychologically. It is quite "natural" for others to operate in a reverse fashion.
quotes.translation: 然而，你们的信念会如此结构个人与集体的经验，以至于与这些观念相悖的证据材料极少显现，或仅以扭曲夸张的形式出现。从生物学与心理学角度而言，以某些不被社会接纳的方式运作本是极为自然的，这些方式看似与你们对人类历史的认知相悖。因此，按照你们的定义，某些人在性行为上表现为男性、心理上表现为女性是极为自然的；另一些人以相反方式运作也同样"自然"。

quotes.text: This will result in complete belief on his part, and this belief will enable us to achieve results denied us in the past. He will fight for us, you see. He is now completely determined upon health, and the recovery will be remarkably fast, now that it has truly begun, simply because his released energies will now work at it with their accustomed vigor--only now for his benefit.
quotes.translation: 这将使他产生完全的信念，而这份信念将使我们达成过去无法企及的成果。你看，他会为我们而战。他现在已完全决心追求健康，既然康复真正开始，恢复速度将异常迅速——只因他释放的能量将以其惯有的活力投入其中，只是这次是为了他自身的福祉。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: Generally speaking, for example, if you are seriously worried about a physical condition, go to a doctor, because your own beliefs may overfrighten you otherwise. Begin with innocuous but annoying physical conditions, however, and try to work those out for yourself. Try to discover why you are bothered. When you have a headache or a simple stomach upset, or if you have a chronic, annoying but not serious condition, such as trouble with your sinuses, or if you have hay fever -- in those situations, remind yourself that your body does indeed have the capacity to heal itself.
quotes.translation: 一般而言，举例来说，如果你严重担忧某种身体状况，就去看医生，否则你自己的信念可能会过度惊吓你。不过，先从无害但恼人的身体状况开始，试着自行解决。试着找出你为何感到困扰。当你头痛或轻微胃部不适，或患有慢性、恼人但不严重的状况，如鼻窦问题，或花粉热时——在这些情况下，提醒自己你的身体确实拥有自我疗愈的能力。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Let me take this moment to state again that there are no devils or demons, except as you create them out of your belief. As mentioned earlier, good and evil effects are basically illusions. In your terms all acts, regardless of their seeming nature, are a part of a greater good. I am not saying that a good end justifies what you would consider an evil action. While you still accept the effects of good and evil, then you had better choose the good.
quotes.translation: 让我再次说明，没有魔鬼或恶魔，除非你根据自己的信念创造出它们。如前所述，善与恶的效果基本上是幻象。就你们而言，所有行为，无论其表面性质如何，都是更大善的一部分。我并非说好的结果能合理化你们所谓的邪恶行为。当你们仍接受善恶的效果时，最好选择善。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: When the intellect is improperly used, however, it is as if the intellect feels required to somehow know or personally direct all of those inner processes. When the erroneous belief systems and negativity connected with so-called rational reason apply, then it is as if our person sees the target, but instead of directing his attention to it he concentrates upon all of the different ways that his arrow could go wrong: It could fall to the left or the right, go too far or not far enough, break in the air, fall from his hand, or in multitudinous other ways betray his intent.
quotes.translation: 然而，当智力被不当使用时，仿佛智力觉得必须知道或亲自指挥所有那些内在过程。当错误的信念系统及与所谓理性推理相关的负面性被应用时，就好比这个人看到了目标，但他不是将注意力集中在目标上，而是专注于箭可能出错的所有不同方式：它可能偏左或偏右，飞得太远或不够远，在空中折断，从手中掉落，或以无数其他方式背叛他的意图。

quotes.text: Questions you cannot seem to answer as you study your own ideas, for example, may lead you to suspect the existence of such invisible core beliefs. Let me emphasize that they are consciously available. You can find them through the approaches mentioned earlier (in the last session), working from your feelings or by beginning with the beliefs that become most readily available.
quotes.translation: 例如，当你审视自己的观念时，那些看似无法解答的问题，可能会让你怀疑是否存在这种无形的核心信念。我要强调的是，这些信念是意识可及的。你可以通过之前（上一节）提到的方法找到它们——从你的感受入手，或者从那些最容易触及的信念开始。

quotes.text: In the culture that you know, such information remains hidden from you. Your main belief systems lead you to feel that your present life is singular, unsupported by any knowledge of prior experience with existence, and fated to be cut off or dead-ended without a future. Instead, you always carry the inner knowledge of innumerable available futures (emphatically). Your emotional life at certain levels is enriched by the unconscious realization that those who love you from past or future are connected to you by special ties that add to your emotional heritage and support.
quotes.translation: 在你们所熟知的文化中，这类信息始终被隐藏。你们的主要信念体系让你觉得此生是孤立的，没有任何先前存在经验的知识支撑，注定被切断或陷入没有未来的死胡同。但事实上，你始终携带着无数可用未来的内在知识（强调）。在特定层面上，你的情感生活因无意识认知而丰富——那些来自过去或未来的爱你之人，通过特殊纽带与你相连，这些纽带为你的情感遗产与支持增添了厚度。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: The same artificial need to vindicate being is present in many of my readers, and various core beliefs may be built up to hide this inner insecurity. You may "justify your life" by biological creativity, and then latch onto your children and never want to let them go. You may use your career instead. But in all cases you must come to grips with such unnecessary ideas, face the reality of your creaturehood, and see that you certainly have as much of a place in the universe as a squirrel, an ant or a leaf. You do not question their right to exist. Why question your own?
quotes.translation: 许多读者心中同样存在着这种为存在辩护的人为需求，并可能建立起各种核心信念来掩盖内在的不安全感。你可能通过生物性创造力"证明自己存在的价值"，然后紧紧抓住孩子不愿放手；也可能转而利用事业。但在所有情况下，你都必须正视这些不必要的想法，面对自己身为生物的现实，看清你在这个宇宙中与松鼠、蚂蚁或树叶同样拥有立足之地。你从不质疑它们存在的权利，又何必质疑自己的存在？

quotes.text: (Our beliefs and intents cause us to pick "from an unpredictable group of actions," or probabilities, those that we want to happen, as Seth tells us in the 681st session in Volume 1; therefore, from my physically oriented probability the considerable work I've put into this paper is an examination of evolution in connection with a number of Seth's concepts. Religious questions connected with evolution aren't stressed as much as some might like, although they aren't ignored either -- but to go very far into religious history would lead away from the focus I've chosen.
quotes.translation: 我们的信念与意图促使我们从"不可预测的行动群"或概率中，挑选出那些我们想要发生的事件——正如赛斯在第一册第681节告诉我们的那样。因此，从我这个以物质为导向的概率角度来看，我为这篇论文投入的大量心血，正是对进化论与赛斯诸多概念关联性的探究。与进化论相关的宗教问题虽未如某些人所愿被着重强调，但也并未被忽视——不过若深入宗教史领域，就会偏离我所选定的焦点。

quotes.text: (10:01.) Any animal knows better than to distrust the nature of its own life, and so does any infant. Nature exists by virtue of faith. The squirrels gather nuts in the faith that they will have provisions, in the faith that the next season will come, and that spring will follow winter. Your impulses are immersed in the quality called faith, for they urge you into action in the faith [that] the moment for action exists. Your beliefs must interact with your impulses, however, and often they can erode that great natural beneficial spontaneity that impulses can provide.
quotes.translation: （十点零一分）任何动物都比人类更懂得不怀疑自身生命的本质，任何婴儿也是如此。自然因信念而存在。松鼠收集坚果时，深信自己将拥有储备，深信下一个季节必将到来，深信春天终将紧随寒冬。你们的冲动浸润在名为信念的特质中，因为它们驱使你们在"行动时机已然存在"的信念中付诸行动。然而，你们的信念必须与冲动互动，而它们往往能侵蚀冲动所能提供的、那种伟大而自然的自发性。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: The Nature of Personal Reality [our last book] is there for others to follow. Others, however, did not have a hand in producing it. They will try out the ideas, many of them, to the best of their ability, and learn and gain much -- all the time (much more forcefully.) hanging on safely to the banners of conventional beliefs. Ruburt has allowed himself no such comforts. He should remember that many people have far greater hassles ... with health, personal relationships, finances and vocations, and without any satisfying accomplishments to offset their misfortunes.
quotes.translation: 《个人实相的本质》[我们上一本书]可供他人参考。但其他人并未参与其创作过程。他们中的许多人会尽力尝试书中的理念，学习并收获良多——却始终（更加强烈地）紧抓传统信念的旗帜以求安稳。鲁柏却不允许自己享有这般慰藉。他应当铭记，许多人在健康、人际关系、财务和职业方面面临远比他更大的困境，且没有任何令人满意的成就来抵消他们的不幸。

quotes.text: (8:59.) You both believed it was quite possible to have clairvoyant dreams, out-of-body experiences, creative adventures in the arts -- but to some extent both of you doubted that the same power or energy could be directed effectively in the physical realm, so-called, of bodily health, or situations of the nitty-gritty (with emphatic amusement). Again, the material is indeed dealing with a far more valid explanation for the working ways of reality than the old official beliefs -- and again, we are not just (underlined) dealing with evocative, creative hypotheses.
quotes.translation: （8:59）你们俩都相信，拥有千里眼式的梦境、出体体验、艺术领域的创意冒险是完全可能的——但某种程度上，你们俩都怀疑同样的力量或能量能否有效导向所谓肉体健康或现实琐事的物质领域（带着强调的戏谑）。再次强调，这些资料确实为现实运作方式提供了远比旧有官方信念更有效的解释——而且我们并非仅仅（着重强调）在探讨唤起性的创意假说。

quotes.text: Now in moments of solitude you may become aware of some of these other streams of consciousness. You may at times for example, hear words, or see images that appear out of context with your own thoughts. According to your education, beliefs, and background you may interpret these in any number of ways. For that matter, they may originate from several sources. On many occasions, however, you have inadvertently tuned in on one of your other streams of consciousness, opened momentarily a channel to those other levels of reality in which other portions of you dwell.
quotes.translation: 在独处的时刻，你可能会觉察到其他意识流的存在。例如有时你会听到话语，或看到与你自身思绪无关的影像。根据你的教育背景、信念和成长经历，你可能会以多种方式解读这些现象。就此而言，它们可能源自多个源头。然而在许多情况下，你无意间调频到了自己的另一条意识流，短暂开启了通往你其他自我所栖居的那些现实层次的通道。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... You are a part of nature that has learned to make choices, a part of nature that naturally and automatically produces dreams and beliefs about which you then organize your reality. There are many effects which you do not like, but you possess a unique kind of consciousness, in which each individual has a hand in the overall formation of a world reality, and you are participating at a level of existence in which you are learning how to transform the imaginative realm of probabilities into a more or less specific, physically experienced world.
quotes.translation: 稍等片刻……你是自然中学会做出选择的那部分，是自然中自然而然、自动产生梦境与信念的那部分，然后你依据这些信念组织你的现实。虽然存在许多你不喜欢的效果，但你拥有独特的意识形态——每个个体都在共同塑造世界现实的整体图景，而你正参与其中的存在层面，正是在学习如何将可能性的想象领域转化为相对具体、可亲身经历的世界。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: In such dreams you tune in to other frequencies that are, indeed, closer to your biological integrity, but there is no reason why you cannot do so in the waking state. When such seeming miracles occur, it is because you have transcended your usual official beliefs about your body and its health, and disease, and so allowed nature to take its course. Often in the dream state you become truly awake, and grab ahold of your spirithood and creaturehood with both hands, so to speak, understanding that each has a far greater reality then you have been led to suppose.
quotes.translation: 在这种梦境中，你调频进入其他频率，这些频率实际上更贴近你的生物完整性，但你没有理由不能在清醒状态下做到这一点。当这类看似奇迹的事件发生时，是因为你超越了关于身体、健康与疾病的惯常官方信念，从而让自然顺其道而行。在梦境状态中，你常常真正清醒过来，可以说双手紧握你的灵性本质与生物本质，明白两者都拥有远超你被引导所认为的实相。

quotes.text: The beliefs of course will be accepted by you not as beliefs, but as reality. Once you understand that you form your reality, then you must begin to examine these beliefs by letting the conscious mind freely examine its own contents.
quotes.translation: 这些信念当然不会被你视为信念，而是被当作实相。一旦你明白是你形成了自己的实相，就必须开始检视这些信念，让意识心自由审视其自身内容。

quotes.text: The most rejuvenating idea of all, and the greatest step to any true illumination, is the realization that your exterior life springs from the invisible world of your reality through your conscious thoughts and beliefs, for then you realize the power of your individuality and identity. You are immediately presented with choices. You can no longer see yourself as a victim of circumstances. Yet the conscious mind arose precisely to open up choices, to free you from a one-road experience, to let you use your creativity to form diversified, varied comprehensions.
quotes.translation: 最具更新力量的理念，也是通往任何真正启蒙的最大一步，就是认识到你的外在生活源自你实相的无形世界，通过你有意识的念头和信念——因为这样你就能意识到你个体性与身份的力量。你立刻面临选择。你再也无法将自己视为环境的受害者。然而，有意识的头脑恰恰是为了开启选择而出现的，是为了将你从单一道路的体验中解放出来，让你运用创造力形成多样化、多角度的理解。

quotes.text: What sin did the poor person or the sick person commit? That question, often asked unconsciously -- if not consciously -- brings you back to beliefs in punishment that have nothing to do with the concept of natural guilt, but with those distortions placed upon it. There is also a connection with misinterpretation of the Bible. Christ as you think of him was simply saying that you form your own reality. He tried to rise above the idea-systems of those times, yet even he had to use them, and so the connotations of sin and punishment distorted the message given.
quotes.translation: 穷人或有病的人犯了什么罪？这个问题——即使不是有意识地，也常常是无意识地提出——将你带回到与惩罚相关的信念，这些信念与自然罪疚的概念毫无关联，而是与强加其上的扭曲有关。这也与对《圣经》的误解有关。你所认为的基督只是在说：你创造你自己的实相。他试图超越那个时代的观念体系，然而连他也不得不使用这些体系，因此罪与惩罚的含意扭曲了他所传达的信息。

quotes.text: (A one-minute pause at 10:05.) Bridge beliefs may become available to you in the dream state. If so, the conscious knowledge may appear suddenly in the middle of your waking day. A reconciliation will be felt within the self following such a conscious understanding, though the dream itself may not be consciously remembered. In the dream various symbols may be used. Each person will vary in this regard. When such dreams are remembered, however, individual symbols, such as crossing a river safely, or an ocean, or bridging a gap or an abyss, are often involved.
quotes.translation: （10:05停顿一分钟。）在梦境状态中，你们可能获得桥梁信念。若如此，清醒的认知可能会在白天突然浮现。在这种清醒理解之后，自我内部会感受到和解，尽管梦境本身可能不被有意识地记住。梦境中可能使用各种象征符号，每个人在这方面都会有所不同。然而，当这类梦境被记住时，通常涉及诸如安全渡河、渡海、跨越鸿沟或深渊等个体象征。

quotes.text: In daily practical experience, try to concentrate for a while upon seemingly subordinate abilities, ones that you think of as latent. If you do so consistently, using your imagination and will, then those abilities will become prominent in your present. The current beliefs will reprogram and alter past experience. It is not simply that past, forgotten, unconsciously perceived events will be put together in a new way and organized under a new heading, but that in that past (now not perceivable), the entire bodily response to seemingly past events will change.
quotes.translation: 在日常实际经验中，试着暂时专注于那些看似次要的、你认为处于潜伏状态的能力。如果你持续这样做，运用你的想象力和意志，那么这些能力将在你的当下变得突出。当前的信念将重新编程并改变过去的经验。这不仅仅是过去被遗忘、无意识感知的事件会以新的方式重组并归入新的标题下，而是在那个过去（现在不可感知的）中，整个身体对看似过去事件的反应都会改变。

quotes.text: This operates in individual and mass terms. Suppose that today your home was robbed. Yesterday, the theft was one of innumerable probable events. I chose such an example because more than one person would have to be involved -- the victim and the robber. (Pause.) Why was your home ransacked, and not your neighbor's? In one way or another, through your conscious thought you attracted such an event, and drew it from probability into actuality. The occurrence would be an accumulation of energy -- turned into action -- and be brought about by corollary beliefs.
quotes.translation: 这既在个人层面也在集体层面运作。假设今天你家遭窃，而昨天这桩盗窃案还只是无数可能事件之一。我选择这个例子是因为它涉及不止一个人——受害者和窃贼。（停顿）为何是你家被洗劫而非邻居家？通过某种方式，你经由自己的意识思想吸引了这样的事件，并将其从可能性转化为现实。这个事件会是能量的累积——转化为行动——并由衍生信念所促成。

quotes.text: (With amusement:) The wrong kinds of questions are the right ones for you, however, in your civilization and with your beliefs, because you want to stay within that structure to that extent. Only now are you beginning to question your methods, and even your questions.5 The true physicist would be able to ask his questions from his usual state of consciousness, and then turn that consciousness in other directions where he himself would be led into adventures-with-reality, in which the questions would themselves be changed. And then the answers would be felt.
quotes.translation: （带着笑意）然而，在你们的文明与信念体系里，错误的问题反而对你们最合适，因为你们想要停留在那个结构框架内。直到现在你们才开始质疑自己的方法，甚至质疑你们提出的问题。真正的物理学家应当能从惯常的意识状态发问，然后将意识转向其他方向，让自己进入与实相冒险的旅程，届时问题本身也会随之改变。而答案将会被感知。

quotes.text: The same beliefs often spread to economic areas in which (long pause) people who met pleasure in God's eyes were therefore gifted with wealth and prosperity, as well as good health. Therefore God was seen to be on the side of those who competed most strenuously, so that to be poor or sick was almost seen as a sign of God's disfavor. All such concepts appear in one form or another at most official levels of thought and education. The whole idea of the esthetics of nature is forgotten -- a subject that we will touch upon further as we continue our discussion.
quotes.translation: 相同的信念常蔓延至经济领域，其中（长停顿）那些在上帝眼中获得喜悦的人，因此被赐予财富、繁荣与健康。于是上帝被视为站在最激烈竞争者的那一边，以至于贫穷或疾病几乎被看作上帝不悦的征兆。所有这些概念都以某种形式出现在思想与教育的主流层面。自然美学的整体概念已被遗忘——这是我们后续讨论中会进一步触及的主题。

quotes.text: Dineen is in excellent physical health, however, and is an extremely attractive woman. She did not choose a situation in which either her health or beauty would be imperiled. She also stayed clear of any sexual involvement outside of marriage. She chose the psychic arena because she felt it to be out of the ordinary to begin with, and invested with all kinds of mystery.
