Any difficulties encountered there would automatically have a kind of glamour and distinction. The more she was reassured by others with the same beliefs, the deeper her involvement grew.
quotes.translation: 然而，迪宁的身体健康状况极佳，且是一位极具魅力的女性。她并未选择任何会危及自身健康或美貌的情境。她也避免了婚姻之外的任何性关系。她选择了心灵领域，因为她一开始就觉得这非同寻常，且充满了各种神秘色彩。在那里遇到的任何困难，都会自动带有一种魅力和独特之处。她越是得到其他持有相同信念之人的肯定，她的卷入就越深。

quotes.text: Now: Much has been written saying that if imagination and willpower are in conflict, imagination will win. Now I tell you, if you examine yourself you will find (deeper and louder) that imagination and willpower are -- underlined twice -- in conflict. Your beliefs may conflict, but your imagination will always follow your willpower and your conscious thoughts and beliefs.
quotes.translation: 现在：有许多文章都提到，如果想象力和意志力发生冲突，想象力会获胜。现在我告诉你们，如果你审视自己，你会发现（声音更深沉、更响亮）想象力和意志力是——下划线强调——相互冲突的。你的信念可能相互冲突，但你的想象力永远会追随你的意志力以及你有意识的思想和信念。

quotes.text: Let us become more practical, and see how these issues merge in your reality. Some of this requires a great honesty on your own parts, as you try to recall some feelings and daydreams that you have tried to put away or forget or disown. Why are some people, then, born with conditions that are certainly experienced as genetically defective, granting even the overall value of such variances on the part of the species? For, again, I must stress the fact that in its way nature makes no such judgments, regardless of the beliefs of your science or religions.
quotes.translation: 让我们更实际些，看看这些问题如何在你们的实相中融合。这需要你们对自己极为诚实，试着回忆那些你们曾试图搁置、遗忘或否认的感受与白日梦。那么，为什么有些人天生就带着被确认为基因缺陷的状况——即便考虑到物种层面这类变异的整体价值？因为我要再次强调：大自然以其自身方式从不做此类评判，无论你们的科学或宗教持有何种信念。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: On the other hand there are those who stress the great value of the inner self, the emotional being, at the expense of the conscious mind. These theories hold that the intellect and usual consciousness are far inferior to the inner "unconscious" portions of being, and that all the answers are hidden from view. (Pause.) The followers of this belief consider the conscious mind in such derogatory terms that it almost seems to be a supercilious cancer that sprouted like a growth upon man's psyche-impeding rather than aiding his progress and understanding.
quotes.translation: 另一方面，有些人以牺牲意识心为代价，过分强调内在自我与情感存在的价值。这些理论认为，智识与通常的意识远逊于存在中内在的"无意识"部分，所有答案都隐藏于视野之外。（停顿）这类信念的追随者以如此贬抑的言辞看待意识心，几乎将其视为一种傲慢的癌变——像赘生物般在人类心灵上萌发，阻碍而非促进人类的进步与理解。

quotes.text: ("Many of those old beliefs still have an emotional hold, however, and some helpful beliefs have also been overdone, or carried on too long. Because you can see so clearly the failings of your age, you each have a tendency to exaggerate them, or rather to concentrate upon them, so that you do not have an emotional feeling of safety. You react by setting up defenses....")
quotes.translation: "然而许多旧信念仍具有情感上的掌控力，一些有益的信念也被过度使用或延续过久。正因为你们能如此清晰地看到时代的缺陷，每个人都倾向于夸大它们——更确切地说，是过度聚焦于它们——以至于无法获得情感上的安全感。你们的反应是建立防御机制......"
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: I would like each of my readers to be a practicing idealist, and, if you are then you will automatically be tolerant of the beliefs of others. You will not be unkind in the pursuit of your own ideals. You will look upon the world with a sane compassion, with some humor, and you will look for man's basic good intent. You will find it. It has always been there. You will discover your own basic good intent, and see that it has always been behind all of your actions -- even in those least fitted to the pursuit of your private ideals (with gentle irony).
quotes.translation: 我希望我的每一位读者都成为实践的理想主义者，如果你做到了，那么你自然会宽容他人的信念。你不会在追求自己理想的过程中变得刻薄。你会以清醒的慈悲和些许幽默看待世界，你会寻找人类基本的善意。你会找到它。它一直都在那里。你会发现你自己基本的善意，并看到它始终存在于你所有的行为背后——即使是在那些最不适合追求你个人理想的行为中（带着温和的讽刺）。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: He heard tomorrow's weather report (yesterday), groaned, thought of a very uncomfortable 90-degree temperature tomorrow [and] imagined himself miserable with the heat. Indeed, he began to feel warmer. In a flash he remembered previous days of discomfort, and in the next moment he projected those into the weekend. He felt trapped. Midway through this process he tried to catch himself, but he believed that his body could not handle the heat -- and that belief outweighed his intent to change his thoughts, so they kept returning for perhaps ten minutes.
quotes.translation: 他（昨天）听到了明天的天气预报，呻吟着，想到明天90华氏度的酷热天气，想象自己在高温中痛苦不堪。确实，他开始觉得更热了。一瞬间他记起了过去那些不适的日子，下一刻他又把这些投射到了周末。他感到被困住了。在这个过程中途他试图抓住自己，但他相信自己的身体无法承受高温——这个信念压过了他改变想法的意图，所以这些念头又持续了大约十分钟。

quotes.text: Now, in the world you (to me) early formed your own beliefs and strategies. In midlife you were presented with our sessions -- or [the two of] you presented yourselves with them, if you prefer. You recognized the overall vitality of our material (long pause) -- but again, you did not realize that it meant a complete reorientation of your attitudes. You did not realize that you were being presented, not merely with an alternate view of reality, but with the closest approximation you could get of what reality was, and how it worked, and what it meant.
quotes.translation: 现在，在你们的世界里，你（对我而言）很早就形成了自己的信念和策略。在中年时期，你们接触到了我们的课程——或者如果你愿意，也可以说是你们自己安排了这些课程。你们认识到了我们资料的整体活力（长停顿）——但再次强调，你们没有意识到这意味着你们态度的彻底重新定向。你们没有意识到，你们所接触到的，不仅仅是关于实相的另一种观点，而是你们所能获得的最接近实相本质、运作方式及其意义的描述。

quotes.text: The psyche is not male or female. In your system of beliefs, however, it is often identified as feminine, along with the artistic productions that emerge from its creativity. In that context, the day hours and waking consciousness are thought of as masculine, along with the sun -- while the nighttime, the moon, and the dreaming consciousness are considered feminine or passive. In the same manner, aggression is usually understood to be violent assertive action, male-oriented, while female elements are identified in terms of the nurturing principle.
quotes.translation: 心灵没有性别之分。然而，在你们的信念体系中，它常被认定为女性特质，连同其创造力所生出的艺术产物也是如此。在这种语境下，白昼时光和清醒意识被视为男性特质，与太阳一样——而夜晚、月亮和做梦意识则被视为女性或被动特质。同样地，侵略性通常被理解为暴力的、果断的行动，以男性为导向，而女性元素则被认定为滋养原则。

quotes.text: If you have a physical symptom, do not run away from it. Feel its reality in your body. Let the emotions follow freely. These will lead you, if you allow them to flow, to the beliefs that cause the difficulty. They will take you through many aspects of your own reality that you must face and explore. These methods release your withheld natural aggressiveness. You may feel that you are swamped by emotion, but trust it -- again, it is the motion of your being, and it arouses your own creativity. Followed, it will seek the answers to your problems.
quotes.translation: 如果你有身体症状，不要逃避它。感受它在身体中的实相。让情绪自由跟随。如果你允许它们流动，它们会带你找到造成困扰的信念。它们会带你穿越你必须面对和探索的自身实相的许多层面。这些方法释放了你被压抑的自然攻击性。你可能会觉得自己被情绪淹没，但信任它——再次强调，它是你存在的动能，并激发你自身的创造力。跟随它，它会寻找你问题的答案。

quotes.text: Those who understand thoroughly that reality is self-created will have least difficulty. Those who have learned to understand and operate in the mechanics of the dream state will have great advantage. A belief in demons is highly disadvantageous after death, as it is during physical existence. A systematized theology of opposites is also detrimental. If you believe, for example, that all good must be balanced by evil, then you bind yourself into a system of reality that is highly limiting, and that contains within it the seeds of great torment.
quotes.translation: 那些彻底明白实相是自我创造的人，遇到的困难最少。那些学会理解并运作梦境状态机制的人，将拥有巨大优势。对恶魔的信仰在死后极为不利，如同在肉身存在期间一样。系统化的对立神学也是有害的。例如，如果你相信所有善必须由恶来平衡，那么你就将自己束缚在一个高度限制性的实相系统中，其中蕴含着巨大折磨的种子。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Now: It is true that habitual thoughts of love, optimism and self-acceptance are better for you than their opposites; but again, your beliefs about yourself will automatically attract thoughts that are consistent with your ideas. There is as much natural aggressiveness in love as there is in hate. Hate is a distortion of such a normal force, the result of your beliefs.
quotes.translation: 现在：的确，充满爱、乐观与自我接纳的习惯性想法比其对立面更有利于你；但同样地，你对自己的信念会自动吸引与你的观念一致的想法。爱中蕴含的攻击性与恨中一样自然。恨是这种正常力量的扭曲，是你信念的结果。

quotes.text: Touching is considered so basically sexual that the most innocuous touching of any portion of the body by another person is considered potentially dangerous. On the one hand you are too specific in your use of the term "sexuality"; yet in another way, and in that context, you feel that any kind of affection must naturally lead to sexual expression, if given its way. Your beliefs make this sexual eventuality appear as a fact of experience.
quotes.translation: 触碰被视为本质上如此具有性意味，以至于他人对身体任何部位最无害的触碰都被认为具有潜在危险。一方面，你对"性"一词的使用过于狭隘；另一方面，在这种语境下，你觉得任何形式的爱意若任其发展，必然会导致性表达。你的信念使这种性的必然性显得像是经验事实。

quotes.text: I am going to take this instance of your friend's illness however to make a few points of my own, some that may not be too well received. A cure of any kind will never depend upon any given treatment. It will in all cases depend upon the belief on the part of the sufferer that he can be cured. It will depend upon his desire to be cured. It will depend upon the strength of the purpose that an illness serves. It will depend upon, in the last analysis, the individual's own ability to mobilize his own energies, for only these will effect a cure.
quotes.translation: 我要以你朋友生病的这个例子，提出我自己的几点看法，有些可能不太受欢迎。任何形式的疗愈都绝不取决于特定的治疗方法。在所有情况下，它都取决于受苦者相信自己能被治愈的信念，取决于他想要被治愈的渴望，取决于疾病所服务的目的的强度，而最终分析起来，则取决于个人调动自身能量的能力——因为只有这些能量才能带来疗愈。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: If you desperately try to remain young, it is usually to hide your own beliefs about age, and to negate all of those emotions connected with it. (Pause.) Whenever you refuse to accept the reality of your creaturehood, you also reject aspects of your spirit. The body exists in the world of space and time. The experiences you may encounter in your sixties are as necessary as those in your twenties. Your changing image is supposed to tell you something. When you pretend alterations do not occur you block both biological and spiritual messages.
quotes.translation: 如果你拼命想保持年轻，通常是为了隐藏自己对年龄的信念，并否定所有与之相关的情绪。（停顿）每当你拒绝接受自己作为生物体的现实时，你也拒绝了自身灵性的某些面向。身体存在于时空世界中。你在六十岁时可能遇到的体验，与二十岁时的体验同样必要。你不断变化的形象本应告诉你一些事情。当你假装改变没有发生时，你就阻断了生物与灵性的讯息。

quotes.text: (Still to Warren:) So forget all of the histories, my dear friend, and listen to your own thoughts, which are today as alive and vital as those of any man ever born, in whatever time. Forget the dusty old records and feel your reality in the moment as you are. In that moment can you hear the insects sweeping across the continents and the voices of the leaves speak, and feel their echoes in your blood -- and that blood lives, beyond the time. It throbs beyond destiny, even as the masses of those people live beyond the beliefs of those gurus.
quotes.translation: （仍对华伦说：）所以，忘掉所有那些历史吧，我亲爱的朋友，倾听你自己的想法，它们在今天与任何时代出生的任何人一样鲜活有力。忘掉那些尘封的旧记录，在当下感受你此刻的真实。在那一刻，你能听到昆虫横越大陆的嗡鸣，树叶的言语，并在你的血液中感受到它们的回响——而那血液，超越时间而活着。它超越命运而搏动，正如那些群众超越那些导师的信念而活着。

quotes.text: When physical conditions are adverse, such social traditions have often emerged. In times of overpopulation, so-called homosexual and lesbian tendencies come to the surface -- but also there is the tendency to express love in other than physical ways, and the emergence of large social issues and challenges into which men and women can throw their energies. There are "lost" portions of the Bible having to do with sexuality, and with Christ's beliefs concerning it, that were considered blasphemous and did not come down to you through history.
quotes.translation: 当物质条件不利时，这类社会传统往往会出现。在人口过剩时期，所谓的同性恋倾向会浮出水面——但同时也出现了以非肉体方式表达爱的倾向，以及男女可以投入精力应对的重大社会议题与挑战。《圣经》中有些关于性及基督对此看法的"失落"篇章，曾被视作亵渎神明而未能流传至今。

quotes.text: You form the fabric of your experience through your own beliefs and expectations. These personal ideas about yourself and the nature of reality will affect your thoughts and emotions. You take your beliefs about reality as truth, and often do not question them. They seem self-explanatory. They appear in your mind as statements of fact, far too obvious for examination.
quotes.translation: 你通过自己的信念和期望编织出经验的织锦。这些关于你自己和实相本质的个人想法会影响你的思想和情绪。你将关于实相的信念视为真理，且往往不加质疑。它们看似不言自明。它们以事实陈述的形式出现在你脑海中，明显到无需检验。

quotes.text: (10:01.) Augustus Two, you see, believes that his body is nearly invincible, and following this belief the body does perform much better. Augustus Two believes that he is an alien. In this case the rationale -- because there must be one -- is that he is a being from another planet, in fact from another galaxy. His purpose in this case is quite clear and simple: He is to help Augustus One, to use his power on the latter's behalf, rewarding his friends and terrifying his enemies. Augustus One quite deeply believes he needs this kind of help.
quotes.translation: （十点零一分）奥古斯都二世相信他的身体几乎不可战胜，遵循这个信念，身体确实表现得更好。奥古斯都二世相信自己是外星人。在这种情况下，其理由——因为必然存在理由——是他来自另一个星球，实际上来自另一个星系。他此生的目的相当清晰简单：他要帮助奥古斯都一世，运用自己的力量为后者服务，奖赏他的朋友，震慑他的敌人。奥古斯都一世深信自己需要这种帮助。

quotes.text: The mind is a system of checks and balances even as the body, and so often a set of beliefs that can be seen as highly negative will often serve beneficial ends in countering other beliefs. For some time Western civilization stressed a distorted version of intellectual reasoning, for example, and so the current stress about other portions of the self serves a purpose.
quotes.translation: 心灵如同身体一样，是一套制衡系统，因此常被视为高度负面的信念组合，往往在对抗其他信念时发挥有益作用。例如，西方文明曾长期强调智性推理的扭曲版本，因此当前对自我其他部分的关注自有其目的。

quotes.text: If you allow yourself to be more and more aware of your own beliefs, you can work with them. It is silly to try to fight what you think of as negative beliefs, or to be frightened of them. They are not mysterious. You may find that many served good purposes at one time, and that they have simply been overemphasized. They may need to be restructured rather than denied.
quotes.translation: 如果你允许自己越来越觉察自己的信念，就能与它们共事。试图对抗你认为的负面信念，或对其感到恐惧，都是愚蠢的。它们并不神秘。你可能会发现许多信念曾一度发挥良好作用，只是被过度强调了。它们需要被重组而非否定。

quotes.text: This can show you what was missing from your home station if you know how to read the clues. You form your home station according to your beliefs. If you firmly believe, again, that sex is wrong, then your home station may involve you in a life "programming" in which you constantly try to deny the vitality of the flesh. The sight of a nude body might upset you. You might undress in the dark, or think, if you are married, of the sexual act of intercourse as dirty. If you are a man, you might be ashamed of what you consider to be your need.
quotes.translation: 如果你懂得解读线索，这能向你揭示你的"家园站"缺失了什么。你根据信念构建自己的"家园站"。若你坚信性是不洁的，那么你的"家园站"可能让你陷入一种"程序化"生活——不断试图否定肉体的活力。裸体景象可能令你不安，你可能在黑暗中更衣，或认为（若已婚）性交行为是肮脏的。若你是男性，你可能会为自己所谓的"需求"感到羞耻。

quotes.text: (A one-minute pause at 9:23.) You may need some time before the old beliefs become less prominent, and finally fall into their proper decay--a decay, incidentally, that does indeed have its own kind of majesty, energy, and beauty. But the inner natural leanings of all of consciousness within the realms of your being now yearn for constructive change, clearer vision, to experience again their inherent sense of corporal spirituality, physical and psychic grace. They want to sense again the effortless motion that is their natural birthright.
quotes.translation: （9:23停顿一分钟）旧信念需要时间才能逐渐淡化，最终归于其应有的衰败——这种衰败，顺带一提，确实具有其独特的庄严、能量与美感。但此刻，你存在领域内所有意识的内在自然倾向，正渴求建设性的改变、更清晰的视野，渴望重新体验与生俱来的肉体灵性、身体与心灵的优雅。它们想要再次感受那与生俱来的不费力运动——这本就是它们与生俱来的权利。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: The dreamer, whatever his age, job or family background, is considered most suspect, for it seems that he doesn't even have a craft to excuse his moral laziness. People with such beliefs will find it most difficult to understand the creativity of their own being. The work done in dreams, the multitudinous experience encountered there, will be invisible to them. They will have little regard or respect for the dreamers or visionaries of the world, and will be the first to leap upon those in their own generation who display such tendencies.
quotes.translation: 无论做梦者年龄、职业或家庭背景如何，都被视为最可疑之人，仿佛连一项手艺都无法为其道德懈怠开脱。持有此类信念者将最难理解自身存在的创造力。梦中完成的工作、其中经历的万千体验，对他们而言将隐而不见。他们不会重视或尊重世间的做梦者与远见者，且会率先抨击同代人中显露此类倾向者。

quotes.text: You are not dealing with material specifics, or even with the psychic specifics. You are dealing instead with the initiation of a framework of beliefs far better than those now current -- a framework that is large enough to contain all specifics, through which people can indeed learn to understand themselves better. You are providing an overall aura of spiritual and intellectual light which would help people precisely because you are not tied to specifics, but engaged with the larger levels of reality from which the specifics emerge ...
quotes.translation: 你处理的并非物质细节，甚至不是心灵细节。你着手建立的是一套远胜当下流行的信念架构——一个足以容纳所有细节的宏大框架，人们借此方能真正学会更深刻地理解自身。你正提供灵性与智识的整体光晕，这之所以能助益世人，恰因你不囿于细节，而是与孕育细节的更大实相层面打交道。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: A human being, however, has another dimension to deal with, a new area of creativity, a diverse mixture of beliefs. His or her ideas about the self must be examined, for they are being materialized in flesh. Again, the situation has great complexity, for the condition is still a healthy attempt on the part of the body to maintain balance. Overall there is also the world situation to be taken into consideration -- the status of the species on the planet, in which, say, overpopulation problems will bring about death to insure new growth.
quotes.translation: 然而，人类还必须处理另一个维度，一个全新的创造领域，以及各种信念的混合体。他或她对自我的想法必须加以审视，因为这些想法正在肉身中具体化。再次强调，这种情况极为复杂，因为这种状态仍是身体维持平衡的健康尝试。整体而言，还必须考虑世界局势——这个物种在地球上的状况，例如，人口过剩问题将导致死亡，以确保新的生长。

quotes.text: (11:13.) Only an understanding of this inherent bisexual nature will release those qualities in each individual, regardless of sex. Those same abilities are natural characteristics of people in each race, of course, yet you have consistently made the same kind of distinctions in racial terms as you have in sexual ones, so that certain races appear as feminine or masculine to you. You project your sexual beliefs outward upon the nations, then, and often the terminology of the nations and of wars is the same as that used to describe sex.
quotes.translation: （11:13。）只有理解这种与生俱来的双性本质，才能释放每个人身上的那些特质，无论其性别如何。当然，这些相同的能力是每个种族人群的自然特征，然而你们一直以与性别区分相同的方式，在种族方面做出同样的区分，以至于某些种族在你们眼中显得女性化或男性化。于是，你们将性信念投射到国家之上，而国家与战争的术语往往与描述性的术语相同。

quotes.text: Each person experiences a unique reality, different from any other individual's. This reality springs outward from the inner landscape of thoughts, feelings, expectations and beliefs. If you believe that the inner self works against you rather than for you, then you hamper its functioning -- or rather, you force it to behave in a certain way because of your beliefs.
quotes.translation: 每个人体验到的实相都是独一无二的，与其他任何个体都不同。这个实相从思想、情感、期待与信念的内在景观中向外涌现。如果你相信内在自我与你作对而非为你效力，那么你就阻碍了它的运作——或者更确切地说，你因自己的信念而迫使它以特定方式行事。

quotes.text: But pretend that you go beyond this point. In sheer desperation you say, "All right, I will examine my beliefs further!" Now this is a hypothetical case so you may find one of innumerable beliefs. You may, for instance, find that you believe you are not worthy, and hence should not look attractive. Or that health means physical weight and it is dangerous to be slim.
quotes.translation: 但假设你超越了这个阶段。在彻底的绝望中你说："好吧，我要进一步检视我的信念！"这只是一个假设案例，因此你可能会发现无数信念中的某一个。例如，你可能会发现自己相信自身毫无价值，因此不该显得有吸引力；或者健康意味着身体重量，苗条是危险的。

quotes.text: (My thought is that because of that choosing, common denominators must lie beneath the clashing beliefs about evolution, and that a good place to start looking for such unifying factors is within the theory, or the framework or idea, of simultaneous time -- however one wants to try to express such a quality within serial terms. The search would be a complicated one. At the same time, I admit that ideas like this always remind me of Seth's comments in the class session for June 23, 1970, as excerpted in the Appendix for Seth Speaks:)
quotes.translation: （我的想法是：由于这种选择，关于进化的冲突信念之下必然存在共同分母，而寻找这类统一因素的最佳起点，正在于同时性时间的理论、框架或概念中——无论人们如何试图用序列性术语来表达这种特质。这项探索将十分复杂。同时，我承认这类想法总让我想起1970年6月23日赛斯在课堂上的评论，正如《赛斯资料》附录所摘录的那样：）

quotes.text: One belief, of course, can be dependent upon many others, each generating its own emotion and imaginative reality. The belief in illness itself depends upon a belief in human unworthiness, guilt and imperfection, for example.
quotes.translation: 当然，一个信念可能依赖于许多其他信念，每个信念都会产生自己的情感和想象实相。例如，对疾病本身的信念依赖于对人类无价值、有罪和不完美的信念。

quotes.text: (11:09.) The habit of not facing problems, which indeed are challenges, can be addictive. A feeling of powerlessness in one field can be transferred to others. When this happens through natural hypnosis, then even the psychic territory of power can be assailed. Here the individual becomes thoroughly aroused, threatened, and realizes for the first time perhaps the nature of belief and his or her predicament. Here you have life and death struggles in creative terms. Some miraculous cures or change-abouts in midlife occur as a result.
quotes.translation: (11:09.) 不面对问题（实为挑战）的习惯可能成瘾。在一个领域感到无力感可能转移到其他领域。当这种情况通过自然催眠发生时，甚至连力量的灵性领域都可能受到攻击。此时个体完全被唤醒、受到威胁，或许第一次认识到信念的本质以及他或她的困境。从创造性角度来看，这里存在着生死搏斗。一些奇迹般的疗愈或中年时期的转变就是由此产生的。

quotes.text: "They were not asleep to themselves, only from your viewpoint. There were several such races of human beings. To them the real was the dream life, which contained the highest stimuli. This is the other side of your own experience. Such races left the physical earth much as they found it. In what you would call the physical waking state, these individuals slept, yet they behaved with great natural physical grace. They did not saddle the body with negative beliefs of disease or limitation. They did not age to the extent that you do."
quotes.translation: "他们并非对自己无意识，只是从你们的视角来看如此。曾有过几支这样的人类种族。对他们而言，真实的是包含最高刺激的梦境生活。这是你们自身经验的另一面。这些种族离开物质地球时几乎保持原样。在你们所谓的物质清醒状态中，这些个体处于睡眠状态，却展现出极大的自然身体优雅。他们不会用疾病或限制的负面信念来束缚身体。他们衰老的程度也不及你们。"
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: (9:44 in a fast delivery.) Give us a moment... .(Long pause.) To some extent Ruburt's dissatisfaction with laying down after dinner also means that he is learning more about his own natural rhythms, for he does feel accelerated at that time, and by the evening, as you do. This is because many of the beliefs that you have individually and jointly are somewhat relieved in the evening, in that they so often apply to the day's activities, when the rest of the world seems to be engaged in the nine-to-five assembly-line world experience.
quotes.translation: （以较快语速在9:44处）请稍等……（长停顿）某种程度上，鲁柏对饭后躺下休息的不满也意味着他正在更深入地了解自己的自然节律，因为他在那个时段确实感到精力充沛，而到了晚上则和你一样。这是因为你们各自及共同持有的许多信念在晚间会有所缓解——这些信念通常与白天的活动相关，那时世界上的其他人似乎都沉浸在朝九晚五的流水线式生活体验中。

quotes.text: Now, there's very recent discussion in medical circles that many cases of senility are caused by a "slow virus infection," rather than just heredity or the traditional aging and oxygen starvation of the brain. The hope, and the unproven speculation, are that eventually such an infection might be treatable medically. But either way (whether senility arises through aging or infection), beliefs would come first, helping the whole body maintain its healthy performance well into old age, or encouraging it to deteriorate unnecessarily.
quotes.translation: 现在医学界有最新讨论指出，许多衰老案例是由"慢病毒感染"引起的，而不仅仅是遗传因素或传统的脑部老化和缺氧。尚未证实的推测和希望是，这种感染最终可能通过医学手段治疗。但无论哪种情况（衰老源于老化还是感染），信念都居于首位——它要么帮助整个身体在老年期保持健康机能，要么促使身体不必要地加速衰退。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: For that matter, [the same is true of] environment, as it is usually understood. Your cultural beliefs predispose you to interpret experience in terms of heredity and environment, however, so that you focus primarily upon them as prime causes of behavior. This in turn results in much more structured experience than necessary. You do not concentrate upon the exceptions -- the children who do not seem to fit the patterns of their families or environments, so of course no attempts are made to view those kinds of unofficial behavior.
quotes.translation: 就此而言，环境也是如此——按通常的理解。然而，你们的文化信念使你们倾向于以遗传和环境来解释经验，因此你们主要将它们视为行为的首要成因。这反而导致经验结构比实际所需更为固化。你们不关注那些例外——那些似乎不符合家庭或环境模式的孩子，自然也就无人试图审视这类非官方行为。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Only by examining these ideas of your own can you learn where you stand with yourself. Now I do not mean to stress the negative by any means, so I suggest that you look to those areas of your life in which you are pleased and have done well. See how emotionally and imaginatively you personally reinforced those beliefs and brought them to physical fruition -- realize how naturally and automatically the results appeared. Catch hold of those feelings of accomplishment and understand that you can use the same methods in other areas.
quotes.translation: 唯有审视这些自身观念，你才能了解自己与内在的立场。我绝非刻意强调负面，因此建议你检视生活中那些令你满意且做得好的领域。观察你如何以情感和想象力强化那些信念，并使其在物质层面开花结果——领悟这些结果是如何自然而然地显现。抓住那些成就感，明白你可以在其他领域运用同样的方法。

quotes.text: The male-female, female-male orientation is not nearly as separate as it appears to be in your present experience. It is not nearly as tied to psychological characteristics as you suppose. Nor is it inherently focused in the particular age period in which it now shows itself. Puberty arrives, so to speak, but the time of its arrival varies according to the needs of the species, its conditions and beliefs. You are an individual for life. You operate as a reproducing individual, generally speaking, for only a portion of that time.
quotes.translation: 男女与女男的取向，并不像你们当前经验中显现的那样截然分离。它远不如你们所假设的那样与心理特征紧密相连。它本身也并非固着于目前所显现的特定年龄阶段。可以说，青春期会到来，但其到来的时间因物种的需求、条件及信念而异。你终生都是一个个体。一般而言，你作为具有生殖能力的个体运作，仅占生命中的一部分时间。

quotes.text: Regardless, there are certain tendencies, mental stances, that you will take about yourself, your body and your life to one degree or another. Many of these will be directly or indirectly connected with old myths and beliefs of your forefathers. Your ideas of good and evil as applied to health and illness are highly important, for instance. (Pause.) Few can escape putting value judgments in such areas. If you consider illness as a kind of moral stigma, then you will simply add an unneeded quality to any condition of ill health.
quotes.translation: 无论如何，关于你自己、你的身体及你的生命，你总会或多或少采取某些特定的倾向与心智立场。其中许多将直接或间接与你祖先的古老神话和信念相关。例如，你应用于健康与疾病的善恶观念极为重要。（停顿。）很少有人能避免在这些领域做出价值判断。若你将疾病视为一种道德污点，那么你只会为任何不健康的状态增添一种不必要的特质。

quotes.text: Examine the literature that you read, the television programs that you watch, and tell yourself to ignore those indications given of the body's weaknesses. Tell yourself to ignore literature or programs that speak authoritatively about the species' "killer instincts." Make an effort to free your intellect of such hampering beliefs. Take a chance on your own abilities. If you learn to trust your basic integrity as a person, then you will be able to assess your abilities clearly, neither exaggerating them or underassessing them.
quotes.translation: 检视你阅读的书籍、收看的电视节目，并告诉自己忽略那些关于身体弱点的暗示。忽略那些权威地谈论物种"杀戮本能"的书籍或节目。努力让你的心智摆脱这些限制性信念。相信你自己的能力。如果你学会信任自己作为个体的基本正直，你就能清晰评估自己的能力，既不夸大也不低估。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Now on the surface there is nothing wrong with that belief. If you hold to it and do not examine it, however, you may find that the word "responsible" is quite loaded, and collects other ideas that are equally unexamined by you. What is your idea of being responsible? According to your answer you can discover whether the core belief works to your advantage or not.
quotes.translation: 表面上这个信念并无不妥。然而，若你坚持它却不加以检视，可能会发现"负责任"这个词承载了太多含义，并会吸引其他同样未经你检视的观念。你对负责任的理解是什么？根据你的答案，你可以发现这个核心信念是否对你有利。

quotes.text: Such predictions can affect the probabilities, of course, and reinforce a present line of belief. Physicians often wonder whether they should tell terminal patients of their impending deaths. There is great controversy. In some cases such a prediction can make death a fact -- while its opposite can regenerate the patient's belief in his or her own ability to live.
quotes.translation: 当然，这类预测会影响可能性，并强化现有的信念路线。医生们常困惑是否应告知绝症患者其即将死亡。这存在巨大争议。在某些案例中，这样的预测可能使死亡成为事实——而相反的预测则能重燃患者对自己生存能力的信念。

quotes.text: ("Because of your individual and joint intuitive understanding and intellectual discrimination, you were able from an early age to clearly perceive the difficulties of your fellows. This helped incite stimuli that made you question the entire framework of your civilization. You were able to do something few people can: leap intuitively and mentally above your own period -- to discard intellectually and mentally, and sometimes emotionally, the shortsighted, unfortunate religious, scientific, and social beliefs of your fellows.
quotes.translation: "由于你们个人与共同的直觉理解及智性辨别力，你们自幼便能清晰洞察同伴们的困境。这激发了促使你们质疑整个文明框架的刺激。你们能做到很少有人能做到的事：在直觉与心智上跃升到自身时代之上——在智性、心智上，有时甚至在情感上，摒弃同伴们短视且不幸的宗教、科学与社会信仰。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: There is no battle between the intuitive self and the conscious mind. There only seems to be when the individual refuses to face all the information that is available in his conscious mind. (Pause.) Sometimes it seems easier to avoid the frequent readjustments in behavior that self-examination requires. In such cases an individual collects many secondhand beliefs. Some contradict each other; the signals given to the body and to the inner self are not smoothly flowing or clear-cut, but a muddied jumble of counter-directions.
quotes.translation: 直觉自我与意识心之间并无战争。只有当个体拒绝面对意识心中所有可得信息时，这种对立才看似存在。（停顿）有时，逃避自我检视所要求的频繁行为调整似乎更容易。在这种情况下，个体收集了许多二手信念。有些信念相互矛盾；传递给身体与内在自我的信号并非顺畅流动或清晰明确，而是混杂着相互矛盾的指令。

quotes.text: Use your conscious mind and its logic. If you discover that you feel unworthy, then do not simply try to apply a more positive belief over that one. Instead discover the reasons for your first belief. If you have not already done so, write down your feelings about yourself. Be perfectly honest. What would you say if someone else came to you with the same reasons?
quotes.translation: 运用你的意识心及其逻辑。若你发现自己感到不配，不要简单地在那个信念之上强加一个更积极的信念。相反，找出你最初信念的原因。若你尚未如此做，请写下你对自己的感受。要完全诚实。若别人带着同样的理由来找你，你会说什么？

quotes.text: Quite literally, you live in the body of your beliefs. You perceive through the body of your beliefs. Your beliefs can increase your vision or diminish it. They can increase or diminish your hearing, or any sense function.
quotes.translation: 确切地说，你活在信念的身体里。你透过信念的身体来感知。你的信念能增强或削弱你的视力，能增强或削弱你的听力，或任何感官功能。

quotes.text: Now when you leave your home station and alter your consciousness, you are always a tourist if you take your own baggage of ideas along with you, and interpret your experiences through your own personal, cultural beliefs. There is nothing unconventional about gods and demons, good spirits or bad spirits. These are quite conventional interpretations of experience, with religious overtones. Cults simply represent counter-conventions, and they are as dogmatic in their way as the systems they reject. Underline that sentence.
quotes.translation: 当你离开自己的家园驿站并改变意识时，若随身携带自己的观念行李，并通过个人及文化信念来诠释经历，你永远只是个观光客。神魔与善恶灵体并无非常规之处——这些不过是带有宗教色彩的常规经验诠释。教派只是反常规的代表，其教条程度与所排斥的体系如出一辙。请在此句下划线标注。

quotes.text: The belief in guilt therefore would be a cementing structure that would hold together other similar core beliefs, and add to their strength. You must understand that these are not simply dead ideas, like debris, within your mind. They are psychic matter. In a sense then they are alive. They group themselves like cells, protecting their own validity and identity.
quotes.translation: 因此，对罪咎的信念会成为粘合其他类似核心信念的结构，并增强其力量。你必须明白，这些并非如残骸般静止于心智中的死板观念，而是心灵物质。从某种意义上说，它们具有生命力，会像细胞般聚合，捍卫自身的有效性与独特性。

quotes.text: In times of overpopulation, this mechanism is hardly desirable, but it is a part of the species held in abeyance now, representing nature's capabilities. In some areas of your world, isolated peoples live on past a hundred years, vital and strong, because they are untouched by your beliefs, and because they live in sympathy and accord with the world as they know and understand it. Occasionally such second puberties happen then, with resulting childbirth, as a small group attempts to maintain its own biological stance.
quotes.translation: 在人口过剩时期，这种机制虽不可取，但作为物种潜能的一部分，它目前处于休眠状态，代表着自然的能力。在你们世界的某些区域，与世隔绝的族群能活力充沛地活过百岁，因为他们未受你们信念的侵染，且以同情与和谐的方式生活在他们所认知的世界中。偶尔会出现这种第二次青春期现象并导致生育，这是小群体试图维持自身生物立场的表现。

quotes.text: Mental images therefore are extremely powerful, combining inner sound and its effects with a clear mental picture which will seek physical form. Your imagination adds motivating and propelling power to such images, and so you will find that many of your beliefs are entertained by you in an inner visual manner. They will have mental pictures connected with them.
quotes.translation: 因此，心象具有极强大的力量，它将内在声音及其效应与寻求物质形态的清晰心象相结合。你的想象力为这些心象注入推动与驱策之力，因此你会发现许多信念皆以内在视觉方式被你接纳，并与相关的心象相连。

quotes.text: Again, you make your own reality. When you view the world, social groups, political groups, your friends, your private experience -- these are all attracted into your realm of activity by your beliefs. Natural hypnosis, as explained in the last chapter, leads you to seek out those situations that will confirm your beliefs, and to avoid those that threaten them.
quotes.translation: 再次强调，你创造自己的实相。当你观察世界、社会群体、政治团体、朋友及个人经验时——这些皆因你的信念而被吸引至你的活动领域。如前一章所述，自然催眠引导你寻求那些能印证你信念的情境，同时回避那些威胁信念的处境。

quotes.text: Now: In those terms he may or may not be as attractive, feature by feature, as some other individuals who believe, in fact, that they are unattractive. The belief in his own comeliness is so important that others will react to him in the same fashion. An individual can have great native beauty, for example, but this beauty is not apparent to others, or to the individual. The person does not believe that he or she possesses it, and mars the actual physical features so that the comeliness becomes literally invisible.
quotes.translation: 现在：就这些条件而言，他或许在容貌特征上并不如某些自认缺乏吸引力的人出众。但对自己容貌的信念如此重要，以至于他人会以同样方式回应他。举例而言，一个人可能天生丽质，但这美色对他人或本人而言并不明显。此人并不相信自己拥有这份美，反而损毁了实际的身体特征，使这份俊美变得几乎不可见。

quotes.text: 2. Seth's creative use of "hallucinations" here is certainly at variance with the concepts ordinarily associated with the word. In a dictionary, for instance, hallucinations may be described as sights and sounds apparently perceived. Hallucinations are tied in with some mental disorders; with objects not actually present. Logically enough, then, in the dictionary one of the synonyms for hallucination will be a word like "delusion": a belief not true, a persistent opinion without corresponding physical evidence.
quotes.translation: 赛斯在此对"幻觉"一词的创造性运用，显然与这个词通常关联的概念大相径庭。例如在字典中，幻觉可能被描述为明显感知到的声音与影像。幻觉与某些精神障碍有关；与实际上不存在的物体有关。因此很合乎逻辑的是，在字典中"幻觉"的同义词之一会是"妄想"：不真实的信念，缺乏相应物理证据的顽固观点。

quotes.text: "A belief in a 'god who provides,' by whatever name, is indeed a psychological requirement for the good health of the body and mind. Ruburt did not want to face such issues. (Long pause.) He felt that they opened the door to all of organized religion's psychological quicksand of emotionalism. The sinful-self material is doing its work, opening the necessary doorways of desire and intent. When Ruburt has typed those small later poems, the path will seem much clearer to him. The innocent self is being uncovered.
quotes.translation: "相信'提供一切的上帝'，无论以何种名号，确实是身心健康所需的心理条件。鲁柏不愿面对这类议题。（长停顿。）他感觉这会打开通往所有组织化宗教情绪主义心理流沙的大门。罪孽自我材料正在发挥作用，开启了欲望与意图的必要通道。当鲁柏打完那些后期短诗时，道路对他而言将显得清晰得多。纯真自我正在被揭示。"
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: In actuality, the body's response to such information is always instantaneous, whether or not the results show at once. Ruburt is beginning to hold a more "realistic" picture of how overall reality works. He is managing to disentangle himself from many disadvantageous cultural beliefs -- beliefs that both of you for years, like other people, took for granted.
quotes.translation: 实际上，身体对此类信息的反应始终是即时的，无论结果是否立即显现。鲁柏正开始对整体实相如何运作持有更"务实"的认知。他正努力摆脱许多不利的文化信念——这些信念多年来被你们两人（如同他人一般）视为理所当然。

quotes.text: My definition of magic is this: Magic is nature unimpeded, or magic is life unimpeded. It is true that your thoughts and emotions and beliefs form the reality that you experience -- but it is also true that this creative construction is, in a manner of speaking, magically formed. That is, the construction of your body and the construction of a world (pause) are produced with the greatest combination of order and spontaneity -- an order and spontaneity that seems hidden rather than apparent (all intently).
quotes.translation: 我对魔法的定义是：魔法是未被阻碍的自然，或魔法是未被阻碍的生命。确实，你的思想、情感和信念形成了你所体验的现实——但同样真实的是，这种创造性的建构，从某种意义上说，是以魔法的方式形成的。也就是说，你身体的建构和世界的建构（停顿）是以秩序与自发性最完美的结合产生的——这种秩序与自发性看似隐藏而非明显（全部专注地说）。

quotes.text: Any deep exploration of the self will lead you into areas that will confound conventional beliefs about sexuality. You will discover an identity, a psychological and psychic identity, that is in your terms male and female, one in which those abilities of each sex are magnified, released, and expressed. They may not be so released in normal life, but you will meet the greater dimensions of your own reality, and at least in the dream state catch a glimpse of the self that transcends a one-sex orientation.
quotes.translation: 对自我的任何深入探索，都会将你带入那些会颠覆传统性观念的领域。你会发现一个身份——一个心理与灵性的身份——用你们的说法既是男性也是女性，其中两性的能力都被放大、释放并表达出来。这些能力在正常生活中或许不会如此释放，但你会遇见自己实相的更大维度，至少在梦境状态中一瞥那超越单一性别取向的自我。

quotes.text: Your thoughts and beliefs and desires form the events that you view on television. If you want to change your world, you must first change your thoughts, expectations, and beliefs. If every reader of this book changed his or her attitudes, even though not one law was rewritten, tomorrow the world would have changed for the better. The new laws would follow.
quotes.translation: 你的思想、信念和欲望形成了你在电视上看到的事件。如果你想改变你的世界，你必须首先改变你的思想、期待和信念。如果这本书的每一位读者都改变其态度，即使没有一条法律被重写，明天世界也会变得更好。新的法律将会随之而来。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Many of the contradictory beliefs and negative ones discussed in yesterday's session led to strong feelings of self-disapproval. The beliefs were so contradictory that in living up to any one of them you actually seemed to be denying others in which you also believed. Hence, no matter what you did you were both left with strong feelings of self-disapproval.
quotes.translation: 昨天课程中讨论的许多矛盾信念和负面信念，导致了强烈的自我不认同感。这些信念如此矛盾，以至于当你符合其中任何一个时，实际上似乎否定了你同样相信的其他信念。因此，无论你做什么，你都会留下强烈的自我不认同感。

quotes.text: You consciously give the signals for reaction. It is not the other way around. Past events do not intrude in this manner unless they are beckoned by the conscious expectations and thoughts that exist within your mind. (Pause.) Those unconscious memories will be activated according to your current beliefs. You will be replenished and renewed as your thoughts motivate joyful body sensations and physical events, or you will be depressed as you bring into your awareness unpleasant past body happenings.
quotes.translation: 你有意识地发出反应的信号。情况并非相反。过去的事件不会以这种方式侵入，除非它们被你心中存在的有意识的期望和思想所召唤。（停顿。）那些无意识的记忆将根据你当前的信念被激活。当你的思想激发愉悦的身体感觉和物理事件时，你将得到补充和更新；或者，当你将不愉快的过去身体经历带入意识时，你会感到沮丧。

quotes.text: "With some women, not conducting regular self-examinations would rouse as many fears as doing them -- and since those women's beliefs follow official medical ones so strictly, they're much better off with the examinations. In this and all instances regarding health, each woman should weigh all the evidence, examine her beliefs, and make her own decisions."
quotes.translation: “对某些女性来说，不进行定期自我检查会引发与进行检查同样多的恐惧——而且由于这些女性如此严格地遵循官方医学信念，她们进行检查反而更好。在这一点以及所有与健康相关的情况下，每位女性都应权衡所有证据，审视自己的信念，并做出自己的决定。”
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Many past beliefs were automatically shattered in the reality of the moment. Powers of initiation and action, long buried, were released in numberless individuals. Federal funds were directed instantly to this region. The spotlight was turned on to the section. (Pause.) Many lonely people were forced, or rather forced themselves, into a situation where it was imperative that they relate with others. Since this is not the main topic of this book, I cannot go deeply into the ways and means involved.
quotes.translation: 许多过去的信念在当下的现实中被自动粉碎。长期埋藏的主动与行动力量，在无数个体中释放出来。联邦资金立即拨往该地区。聚光灯转向了这片区域。（停顿）许多孤独的人被迫——更确切地说，是强迫自己——进入一种必须与他人建立联系的处境。由于这并非本书的主题，我无法深入探讨其中涉及的方式与方法。

quotes.text: (9:10.) Seth couldn't lead my life for me, of course. He couldn't lead other people's lives for them (underlined), either--yet through the years I began to feel a greater and greater sense of responsibility for people with physical problems who wrote requiring Seth's aid, or mine. Their needs--and my own--seemed to blot out the great hope that Seth could and did offer: the infusion of understanding and comprehension that could clear away the old belief patterns that held the individual in bounds.
quotes.translation: （9:10分）赛斯当然不能替我过我的生活。他也不能替别人过他们的生活（强调）——然而多年来，我开始对那些写信寻求赛斯或我帮助的身体有问题的个体，感到越来越大的责任感。他们的需求——以及我自己的需求——似乎遮蔽了赛斯能够且确实提供的巨大希望：注入理解与领悟，以清除那些将个体束缚在局限中的旧有信念模式。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: If you call the demons "negative beliefs," however, then you have taken strides forward. People continue to die of diseases. Many of your scientific procedures, including inoculations, of themselves "cause" new diseases. It does not help a patient inoculated against smallpox and polio if [eventually] he dies of cancer as a result of his negative beliefs.4
quotes.translation: 然而，若你将恶魔称为"负面信念"，便已向前迈进了一大步。人们仍在死于疾病。许多科学程序，包括疫苗接种，其本身就会"引发"新疾病。若一位接种了天花和小儿麻痹症疫苗的患者最终因负面信念死于癌症，那么这种接种对他毫无助益。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Events are processed in dreams, put in the necessary perspective, sorted and arranged. This is done when the conscious mind is separated from direct involvement with physical events. Dreams serve to dull the impact of the day's events just past, while the meaning of those activities sifts through the various levels of the personality, settling into compartments of intent and belief. Often the true impact of an event does not occur until it has been interpreted or reexperienced through a dream.
quotes.translation: 事件在梦境中被处理，被置于必要的视角中，被分类和整理。这是在意识心智与物质事件的直接参与分离时完成的。梦境起到缓冲刚过去一天事件冲击的作用，同时这些活动的意义会渗透到人格的各个层面，沉淀到意图和信念的隔间中。通常，一个事件的真正冲击要等到通过梦境被解读或重新体验后才会显现。

quotes.text: He believed in heroic characteristics, and became blinded by an idealized superman version of an Aryan strong in mind and body. To attain that end, Hitler was quite willing to sacrifice the rest of humanity. "The evil must be plucked out." That unfortunate chant is behind the beliefs of many cults -- scientific and religious -- and Hitler's Aryan kingdom was a curious interlocking of the worst aspects of religion and science alike, in which their cultish tendencies were encouraged and abetted.
quotes.translation: 他相信英雄特质，并被一种理想化的、身心强健的雅利安超人版本所蒙蔽。为达此目的，希特勒甘愿牺牲其余人类。"必须铲除邪恶。"这句不幸的口号是许多邪教（无论是科学还是宗教的）信念背后的驱动力，而希特勒的雅利安王国正是宗教与科学最恶劣方面的奇特交织，其中它们的邪教倾向受到鼓励和纵容。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: A belief in hell fires can cause you to hallucinate Hades' conditions. A belief in a stereotyped heaven can result in a hallucination of heavenly conditions. You always form your own reality according to your ideas and expectations. This is the nature of consciousness in whatever reality it finds itself. Such hallucinations, I assure you, are temporary.
quotes.translation: 对地狱烈火的信念会导致你幻觉地狱的景象。对刻板天堂的信念则会导致天堂景象的幻觉。你总是根据你的观念和期望形成自己的实相。这是意识在任何它所处的实相中的本质。我向你保证，这类幻觉是暂时的。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Quite simply, a belief in the good without a belief in the evil, may seem highly unrealistic to you. This belief, however, is the best kind of insurance that you can have, both during physical life and afterward.
quotes.translation: 简单来说，相信善而不相信恶，对你而言可能显得极不现实。然而，这种信念却是你所能拥有的最佳保障，无论是在物质生命期间还是之后。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: In this probability of which I speak, the species will begin to encounter the great challenge inherent in fulfilling the vast untouched (forcefully) -- underlined -- potential of the human body and mind. (Long pause.) In that probable reality, to which each of you can belong to some extent, each person will recognize his or her inherent power of action and decision, and feel an individual sense of belonging with the physical world that springs up in response to individual desire and belief.7
quotes.translation: 在我所谈论的这个可能性中，人类物种将开始面对实现身心巨大未开发（强调地）——下划线——潜力所固有的重大挑战。（长停顿。）在那个你们每个人都能在一定程度上归属的实相可能性中，每个人都会认识到自己与生俱来的行动与决定的力量，并感受到与物质世界的个体归属感——这个世界因个体的欲望与信念而涌现。

quotes.text: It is always because you do not trust the natural self that you resort to such drug therapy. The individuals who seek out treatment fear the nature of their own identity more than anything else. They are then only too willing to sacrifice it. (Pause, then smiling:) Your thoughts and beliefs form your reality. There is, as Joseph (Seth's name for me) said in our break, no magic therapy -- only an understanding of your own great creativity, and the knowledge that you yourself make your world.
quotes.translation: 正是因为你们不信任自然自体，才会诉诸这种药物疗法。寻求治疗的个体最恐惧的是自己身份的本质。于是他们太乐意牺牲它了。（停顿，然后微笑：）你的思想和信念形成你的实相。正如约瑟（赛斯对我的称呼）在我们休息时所说，没有神奇的疗法——只有对你自身巨大创造力的理解，以及你明白自己创造了自己世界的知识。

quotes.text: Now: Churchmen of the Middle Ages could draw diagrams of various portions of the human body that were afflicted as the result of indulging in particular sins. Logical minds at one time found those diagrams quite convincing, and patients with certain afflictions in certain areas of the body would confess to having committed the various sins that were involved. The entire structure of beliefs made sense within itself. A man might be born deformed or sickly because of the sins of his father.
quotes.translation: 中世纪的神职人员能绘制人体各部位的图解，指出沉溺特定罪孽会导致哪些部位受苦。逻辑思维缜密者一度认为这些图解极具说服力，而身体特定部位患有某些病痛的患者，也会承认自己犯下了相关的各种罪孽。整个信念体系在其内部是自洽的。一个人可能因父亲的罪孽而天生畸形或体弱多病。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: While you believe that only doctors can cure you, you had better go to them, because in the framework of your beliefs they are the only people who can help you. But the framework itself is limiting; and again, while you may be cured of one difficulty, you will only replace it with another as long as your beliefs cause you to have physical problems.
quotes.translation: 当你相信只有医生能治愈你时，你最好去找他们，因为在你信念的框架内，他们是唯一能帮助你的人。但这个框架本身具有局限性；同样，即使你被治愈了某个病症，只要你的信念继续导致你产生身体问题，你只会用另一个病症来替代它。

quotes.text: In her thirties, it seems to her that youth is fast fleeing, and in line with her beliefs she cannot see a woman who is much older being desirable. So her beliefs put her in a state of crisis. Change them and no crisis exists. The body would then cease reacting to such stress, and almost immediately the exterior situation itself would be altered.
quotes.translation: 在她三十多岁时，青春似乎正迅速消逝，根据她的信念，她无法想象一个年长许多的女性仍具吸引力。因此，她的信念使她陷入危机状态。改变这些信念，危机便不复存在。身体将不再对这种压力产生反应，而外在情境本身几乎会立即改变。

quotes.text: If all of your beliefs, not just your "fortunate" ones, were not materialized, you would never thoroughly understand on a physical level that your ideas create reality. If only your "positive" beliefs were materialized then you would never clearly comprehend the power of your thought, for you would not completely experience its physical results.
quotes.translation: 如果你们所有的信念——不仅是那些"幸运的"信念——都没有被物质化，你们就永远无法在物质层面彻底理解自己的思想创造了实相。如果只有"正面"的信念被物质化，你们就永远无法清楚领悟思想的力量，因为你们无法完整体验其物质结果。

quotes.text: You want to clear the road. The free association is valuable because it helps to point out those conflicting feelings and beliefs, brings them into consciousness, and into the present moment, where they can indeed be understood in the light of knowledge that has been acquired since -- but not been allowed to act upon the old conflicting beliefs.
quotes.translation: 你们想要清理道路。自由联想之所以珍贵，是因为它有助于指出那些冲突的感受与信念，将它们带入意识层面，带入当下这一刻——在那里，它们确实能被后来获得的知识所理解，但这些知识此前一直不被允许作用于那些旧有的冲突信念。

quotes.text: What you consider conscience is often an applied-from-without sense of right and wrong instilled in you in your youth. As a rule these ideas represent your parents' conceptions of natural guilt, distorted by their own beliefs. (See the 619th session in Chapter Four, as well as the first session in this chapter.) You accepted those ideas for a reason, individually and en masse, for mankind at any given "time" has a strong idea of the particular sort of world experience it will create.
quotes.translation: 你们所谓的良知，往往是童年时被灌输的、从外部强加的是非观念。通常这些观念代表你们父母对自然罪疚的看法，又经过他们自身信念的扭曲（参见第四章第619节及本章第一节）。你们接受这些观念自有其理由——无论个体还是集体层面，因为人类在任何特定"时间"都对将要创造的世界经验类型持有强烈看法。

quotes.text: You concentrate with great vigor upon one idea, usually to the exclusion of others. It is a quite conscious performance. As such it also portrays the importance of belief, for using hypnosis you "force-feed" a belief to yourself, or one given to you by another -- a "hypnotist"; but you concentrate all of your attention upon the idea presented.
quotes.translation: 你全神贯注于一个想法，通常排斥其他想法。这是一种完全有意识的行为。正因如此，它也展现了信念的重要性——通过催眠，你"强行灌输"一个信念给自己，或他人（"催眠师"）给予你的信念；但你会将所有注意力集中于呈现出的那个想法上。

quotes.text: (10:05.) Some of the components are more charged than others. A Catholic or a Jew possessing these beliefs is obviously out of step to some extent, and will feel guilty as he measures himself against them. (Intently:) A black man who accepts the same system is indeed in difficulty. If he happens to be a poor black man he is in double jeopardy.
quotes.translation: （10:05分）某些组成部分比其他部分更具能量。持有这些信念的天主教徒或犹太人显然在某种程度上格格不入，当他们以这些标准衡量自己时会产生罪恶感。（专注地）接受同一体系的黑人确实处境艰难。若他恰巧是个贫穷的黑人，则面临双重困境。

quotes.text: (10:01.) For example: I told you time does not consist of a series of moments, one before the other, though you do perceive it now in that fashion. Events are not things that happen to you. They are materialized experiences formed by you according to your expectations and beliefs. Inner portions of your personality realize this now. After death you will not concentrate upon the physical forms taken by time and events. You may use the same elements, as a painter might use his colors.
quotes.translation: 例如：我曾告诉你们，时间并非由一连串前后相继的片刻所组成，尽管你们现在确实以那种方式感知它。事件并非发生在你身上的事，而是你根据自身的期望与信念所形成的具体化经验。你人格的内在部分此刻便明白这一点。死亡之后，你将不再专注于时间与事件所采取的物质形式。你可以运用相同的元素，如同画家运用他的颜料一般。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: "As a Roman, you pretended to be a follower while you were a man of rank in the military. You had no belief in the conventional gods, yet you were supposed to be conquering lands in their names. You traveled even to Africa. You had a disdain for leaders as liars, and of the masses as followers, and so you were always in one kind of dispute or another with your fellows, and even with the authorities. You were of a querulous nature, yet highly curious, and, again, physically involved.
quotes.translation: "身为罗马人，你在军中身居高位时却假装是信徒。你并不信奉传统的神祇，却要假借其名征服疆土。你甚至远赴非洲。你鄙视领袖为说谎者，视群众为盲从者，因此你总是与同僚乃至当权者争执不休。你生性爱抱怨，却又极度好奇，且再次强调，你是个身体力行的人。

quotes.text: If you accept the idea that knowledge is "bad," for instance, then in line with that belief all of your efforts to learn will be futile, or bring you great discomfort. You will not trust any knowledge that comes easily for you will feel that you have to pay, do penance for the attainment of any wisdom. Fundamental interpretations of the Bible often lead to such conclusions, so that the pursuit of knowledge itself, which has a built-in biological impetus, becomes a taboo activity.
quotes.translation: 例如，如果你接受"知识是邪恶的"这一观念，那么根据这个信念，你所有的学习努力都将徒劳无功，或带来极大的不适。你不会信任任何轻易获得的知识，因为你会觉得必须付出代价、为获得任何智慧而赎罪。对《圣经》的基本解读常常导致这样的结论，以至于追求知识本身——这本是内在的生物性驱动力——变成了禁忌行为。

quotes.text: So, from other streams of actuality you choose those events that you want physically materialized; and you do this according to your beliefs about the nature of reality. A photograph is taken, and you have before you then a picture of an event that in your terms has already happened. In dreams you take many subjective "photographs," and decide which ones among them you want to materialize in time. To a certain extent, therefore, the dreams are blueprints for your later snapshots.
quotes.translation: 因此，从其他实相之流中，你选择那些你想要具体物质化的事件；而你是根据自己对实相本质的信念来做这件事的。一张照片被拍下，你眼前便呈现一个在你们看来已经发生的事件画面。在梦境中，你拍摄许多主观的"照片"，并决定其中哪些你想要在时间中物质化。因此，在某种程度上，梦境是你后来快照的蓝图。

quotes.text: (9:47.) That good intent is apparent within your world. It is obvious in the cooperative ventures that unite, say, the mineral, plant, and animal kingdoms, the relationship of bee to flower. And your beliefs to the contrary, you have closed your minds to man's own cooperative nature, to his innate desire for fellowship, his natural bent for taking care of others, and (with elaborate, if gentle emphasis) for altruistic behavior. But we will discuss those matters later in our book.
quotes.translation: （9:47）那善意的意图在你们的世界中显而易见。它显现在那些联合矿物、植物与动物王国的合作性冒险中，显现在蜜蜂与花朵的关系中。而你们持有相反的信念，因此对人类的合作本性、对与生俱来的情谊渴望、对照顾他人的自然倾向，以及（以细致而温和的强调）对利他行为的天然禀赋，都视而不见。但我们将在本书后面讨论这些议题。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Seth's material largely opposes science's mechanistic model of the body wearing down within certain age limits, abetted as that model is by the power of the beliefs that say it will. He had much to say about how the out-of-place stresses we impose upon ourselves through our fearful projections into the future adversely affect our body consciousnesses, which are focused in the present. Telepathy, "molecular mentality," and cellular consciousness are deeply involved in all of this.
quotes.translation: 赛斯资料在很大程度上反对科学将身体视为在特定年龄范围内磨损的机械模型——而该模型又因"身体必将如此"的信念力量而助长。他大量论述了我们因对未来恐惧投射而强加于自身的错位压力，如何对聚焦于当下的身体意识产生负面影响。心灵感应、"分子心智"与细胞意识都深深牵涉其中。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Latently, your consciousness is capable of performing these feats, but the work cannot be done with the part of your consciousness that is strongly attached to the space-time relationship. What you think of as your conscious mind is given the task of assessing the "facts" of daily living. It then forms beliefs about reality, and these are used in the dream state as one of the main yardsticks, so to speak, that activate the emergence of certain probable events rather than others.
quotes.translation: 潜在地，你的意识能够完成这些壮举，但这项工作无法由你意识中强烈依附于时空关系的部分来完成。你所谓的意识心被赋予评估日常生活的“事实”的任务。它随后形成关于实相的信念，而这些信念在梦境中被用作主要衡量标准之一，可以说，它们激活了某些可能事件而非其他事件的出现。

quotes.text: This does not mean that an alienation results in those realities -- simply a relationship in which the body and consciousness relate to other events. Only your beliefs, training, and neurological indoctrination prevent you from recognizing the true nature of your consciousness while you sleep. You close out those data. In that period, however, at an inner order of events, you are highly active and do much of the interior mental work that will later appear as physical experience.
quotes.translation: 这并不意味着那些实相会导致疏离——只是身体和意识与其它事件产生关联的一种关系。只有你的信念、训练和神经系统的灌输，阻止你在睡眠时认识到意识的真实本质。你屏蔽了那些数据。然而，在那段时间里，在事件的内在秩序中，你高度活跃，完成了许多后来将显现为物质体验的内在心智工作。

quotes.text: I say wrong, meaning no moral judgment, but the application of one method to a pursuit that cannot be adequately expressed in such a fashion. The assembly-line time and the beliefs that go along with it have given you many benefits as a society, but it should not be forgotten that the entire framework was initially set up to cut down on impulses, creative thought, or any other activities that would lead to anything but the mindless repetition of one act after another (intently).
quotes.translation: 我所谓的错误，并非道德评判，而是指将一种方法应用于无法以这种方式充分表达的目标。流水线时间及其伴随的信念，作为社会确实给你们带来了许多益处，但不应忘记，整个框架最初是为了抑制冲动、创造性思维，或任何会导致除了机械重复一个接一个动作之外的活动而建立的（专注地）。

quotes.text: For the body of Augustus is once again under the sway of beliefs about himself that are highly contradictory. Before, he was physically powerful when he was Augustus Two, and weak when he was Augustus One. Now as Augustus he is alternately strong and weak, and the body stresses are apparent. As Augustus Two he could stay up night and day and perform physical tasks quite difficult for the normal human being to do, for he operated under the indivisible idea of power and strength.
quotes.translation: 因为奥古斯都的身体再次受到关于他自己的高度矛盾的信念的支配。之前，当他作为奥古斯都二世时，他身体强健；作为奥古斯都一世时，他身体虚弱。现在作为奥古斯都，他时而强壮时而虚弱，身体压力显而易见。作为奥古斯都二世时，他可以日夜不眠，执行常人难以完成的体力任务，因为他是在力量与强韧不可分割的信念下运作的。

quotes.text: I am also quite aware of the cruelty that would be involved if I led you on in this endeavor without due consideration. I can only tell you that I appreciate both your objectivity and your beliefs. I will do my best, my dear doctor, to satisfy you in both respects. You may call it chance. You may call it if you choose coincidence. You may name it in whatever way pleases you: nevertheless, it is because of my personal rapport with you that I will bother with any displays at all.
quotes.translation: 我也很清楚，如果我在没有适当考虑的情况下引导你进行这项努力，将会涉及残酷。我只能告诉你，我欣赏你的客观性和你的信念。我亲爱的医生，我会尽最大努力在这两方面满足你。你可以称之为机遇。如果你愿意，也可以称之为巧合。你可以用任何你喜欢的方式命名它：然而，正是因为我与你个人的融洽关系，我才会费心进行任何展示。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: As Ruburt would put it, your sidekick over here (Jim's wife, Jean) did not go along with your ideas at all in that life. She was a male at that time, however, and you were a female and a priestess. So was your friend (Bert C.). As a male in that life she had an expanding effect upon your personality, but you were very given to ritual and a belief in magic acts, and to the idea that existence in itself was evil and wrong. You were indeed a member of the sect now called Gnostic.
quotes.translation: 如鲁柏所言，你身边的伙伴（吉姆的妻子珍）在那世完全不同意你的想法。不过她当时是男性，而你则是女性祭司。你的朋友（伯特·C）也是如此。作为男性，她在那一世对你的性格产生了扩展性的影响，但你却极度沉迷于仪式，相信魔法行为，并认为存在本身是邪恶错误的。你确实是如今被称为诺斯替教派的成员。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: The interaction is constant, however, and in all of your presents, creative. You draw on their knowledge as they draw on yours, and this of course applies to personalities that you would consider future. You have a gigantic pool of information and experience to draw upon, but this will be utilized according to your present conscious beliefs. If you understand that the point of power is in the present, then you have an inexhaustible realm of ability and energy at your command.
quotes.translation: 然而，这种互动是持续不断的，并且在你们所有的当下中都具有创造性。你们汲取他们的知识，正如他们汲取你们的知识，这当然也适用于你们认为未来的人格。你们拥有一个庞大的信息与经验库可供汲取，但这将根据你们当下的意识信念来运用。如果你明白力量之点就在当下，那么你就拥有取之不竭的能力与能量供你调遣。

related_concepts.name_en: The point of power is in the present

quotes.text: The universe is with you and not against you. Your fellow men are with you and not against you. When [each of] you realize that, then you reach those portions of your fellows that are with you. You meet them at a different level that is illuminating to them also, and starts them developing.... The will's power is impressive, and it is "distributed" throughout the body. The body depends upon it for direction. The will's beliefs, again, activate the body's automatic resources.
quotes.translation: 宇宙与你同在而非对立。你的同胞与你同在而非对立。当你们每个人领悟到这一点时，就能触及那些与你同频的同胞。你们在能照亮他们的不同层面相遇，并促使他们开始成长……意志的力量令人印象深刻，它"分布"于整个身体。身体依赖它来指引方向。而意志的信念，再次激活身体自动的资源。

quotes.text: You are not to hammer at yourself consciously. Imagination and emotion are your great allies. Your conscious direction will automatically bring them into play. You can see why it is so important that you examine all of your beliefs about yourself and the nature of your reality; and one belief, if you let it, will lead you to another.
quotes.translation: 你无需刻意苛责自己。想象力与情感是你强大的盟友。你的意识引导会自动将它们调动起来。你当明白，检视所有关于自我及现实本质的信念何其重要；若你允许，一个信念自会引领你触及另一个。

quotes.text: Your beliefs close you off from much otherwise quite-available knowledge concerning man's psychology--knowledge that would serve to answer many questions usually asked about the reasons for suffering. Other questions, it is true, are more difficult to answer. Men and women are born, however, with curiosity about all sensations, and about all possible life experiences. They are thirsty for experience of all kinds. Their curiosity is not limited to the pretty or the mundane.
quotes.translation: 你的信念将你隔绝于许多本可轻易获取的人类心理学知识——这些知识本可解答关于苦难缘由的诸多常见疑问。诚然，另一些问题更难解答。但人类生来便对一切感知与所有可能的人生体验充满好奇。他们渴求各类体验，其好奇心绝不局限于美好或平凡之事。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: You often avoid this natural treatment, however, and run from frightening conscious thoughts that would in their turn lead you to the source of "negative" beliefs, where they could be faced; you could then travel through them, so to speak, into feelings of joy and victory. Instead, for example, many of you accept the way of drugs, where such feelings and thoughts are thrust upon you, or forced out of you while you are denied the stabilizing comforts of the conscious mind.
quotes.translation: 然而，你们常常回避这种自然疗法，逃离那些会引导你们找到"负面"信念根源的可怕意识念头——在那里你们本可以面对它们；然后你们可以穿越它们，可以说，进入喜悦与胜利的感受。相反，例如，你们许多人接受药物之道，在那里这些感受和念头被强加于你们，或被逼出你们体外，而你们却被剥夺了意识心智的稳定慰藉。

quotes.text: All of this can be avoided through the realization that your point of power is in the present, as stated earlier (in the 657th session in Chapter Fifteen). Not only do you operate within your own personal beliefs, of course, but within a mass system to which you subscribe to one degree or another. Within that organization medical insurance becomes a necessity for most of you, so I am not suggesting that you drop it. Nevertheless, let us look more closely at the situation.
quotes.translation: 通过认识到你们的力量点就在当下——如先前（第十五章第657节）所述——这一切都可以避免。当然，你们不仅在自己个人信念的范围内运作，也在你们不同程度认同的集体系统内运作。在那个组织里，医疗保险对你们大多数人来说成为必需品，所以我并不是建议你们放弃它。不过，让我们更仔细地审视这个情况。

related_concepts.name_en: The point of power is in the present

quotes.text: All of the powers of your inner self are set into activation as a result of your conscious beliefs. You have lost a sense of responsibility for your conscious thought because you have been taught that it is not what forms your life. You have been told that regardless of your beliefs you are terrorized by unconscious conditioning.
quotes.translation: 你内在自我的所有力量都因你有意识的信念而被激活。你已失去对意识思维的责任感，因为你被教导说意识思维并不塑造你的生命。你被告知，无论你的信念如何，你都会受到无意识制约的恐吓。

quotes.text: As some of your reading material of late suggests, the more you know of the nature of reality the better equipped you are to deal with the point of transition. Here your inner beliefs are your only contact with reality. Believing for example that death is a complete end can result in a quite unnecessary period of unconsciousness.
quotes.translation: 正如你们近期某些读物所暗示的，对实相本质了解得越多，就越能妥善应对过渡点。在此，你们的内在信念是与实相唯一的接触点。例如，相信死亡是彻底的终结，可能导致一段完全不必要的无意识状态。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: Such a belief in extinction, such a certainty that identity is about to be blotted out in the next moment, is a severe psychological experience, that in itself can bring about unfortunate reactions. What happens instead is that you find consciousness quite intact, and its expression far less limited than it was before. Joseph chose subconsciously to interrupt those methods of expression he was using at the time simply so that their interference would claim due notice.
quotes.translation: 这种对湮灭的信念，这种确信自我即将在下一刻被抹除的执念，是一种严重的心理体验，其本身就会引发不幸的反应。实际发生的情况是，你会发现意识完好无损，且其表达方式比先前更少限制。约瑟夫潜意识地选择中断当时使用的那些表达方式，仅仅是为了让这些干扰引起应有的注意。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Such judgments are very simplistic, and ignore the great range of human motivation and experience. If you are bound and determined that "GOD" (in capitals and quotes) creates only "good," then any physical deficiency, illness or deformity becomes an affront to your belief, threatens it, and makes you angry and resentful. If you become ill you can hate yourself for not being what you think you should be -- a perfect physical image made in the likeness of a perfect God.
quotes.translation: 这类判断过于简单化，忽略了人类动机与经验的广阔范围。若你执意认定"神"（大写并加引号）只创造"善"，那么任何身体缺陷、疾病或畸形都会成为对你信念的冒犯，威胁它，使你愤怒怨恨。若你生病，你会憎恨自己未能成为自认为应有的模样——完美上帝形象所造的完美肉身。

quotes.text: Now: Your beliefs about what is desirable and what is not, what is good and what is evil, cannot be divorced from the condition of your body. Your own ideas of values can help you achieve good health or bring about disease, can bring into your experience success or failure, happiness or sadness. Yet each of you will interpret that last remark in line with your own value system. You will have definite ideas about what success or failure means, or what good or evil is.
quotes.translation: 现在：你关于什么是可欲的、什么不是，什么是善、什么是恶的信念，无法与你的身体状况分离。你自己的价值观既能帮助你获得健康，也能引发疾病；既能带来成功或失败，也能带来快乐或悲伤。然而，你们每个人都会根据自己的价值体系来解读这最后一句话。你们对成功或失败的意义，或善与恶的定义，都有明确的看法。

quotes.text: While that emotionally invisible belief is carried, then anything the self does must be scrutinized, put to the test; in the meantime beliefs that have sustained others are suspended. The development of Ruburt's abilities would, therefore, lead him away from comforting structures while he searched for others to sustain him ...
quotes.translation: 当这种情感上隐形的信念被携带时，自我所做的任何事都必须被审视、被检验；与此同时，那些曾支撑他人的信念则被悬置。因此，鲁柏能力的发展会将他带离那些令人安心的结构，同时他也在寻找其他能支撑他的东西……

quotes.text: Often in the aged you find such frameworks coming into being naturally, but those who awaken spontaneously after four hours consider themselves insomniacs because of their beliefs, and so cannot utilize their experience properly. Both the conscious and unconscious would operate far more effectively, however, under an abbreviated sleeping program, and for those involved in "creative" endeavors this kind of schedule would bring greater intuition and applied knowledge.
quotes.translation: 在老年人身上，你常会发现这种框架自然形成，但那些在四小时睡眠后自然醒来的人，却因自己的信念而自认患有失眠症，因此无法妥善运用他们的经验。然而，在缩短的睡眠计划下，意识与潜意识都能运作得更有效率，而对从事"创造性"工作的人来说，这种作息时间表会带来更强的直觉与实用知识。

quotes.text: "Wealth is everything." Now this idea is far from a truth. The person who accepts it completely, though, will be wealthy and in excellent health, and everything will fit in quite well with his beliefs. Yet the idea is still a belief about reality, and so there will be invisible gulfs in his experience of which he is ignorant.
quotes.translation: “财富就是一切。”这个观念远非真理。然而，完全接受它的人会变得富有且健康极佳，一切都会与他的信念相当吻合。但这个观念仍然是对实相的一种信念，因此在他的经验中会存在一些他未曾察觉的无形鸿沟。

quotes.text: You may become more and more efficient in that manner. This is why it is vital that you examine your beliefs for yourself and understand what they mean to you personally. If, using that example, you suddenly begin to realize your position and begin to express your love to your children directly, you may find them quite surprised, delighted but confused. It may take them a while to understand your reactions, but as the old reality had a cohesiveness so will the new.
quotes.translation: 你可能会以那种方式变得越来越有效率。这就是为什么你必须亲自审视自己的信念，并理解它们对你个人的意义。如果以那个例子来说，当你突然开始意识到自己的处境，并开始直接向孩子表达你的爱时，你可能会发现他们既惊讶又欣喜，但同时也感到困惑。他们可能需要一段时间才能理解你的反应，但正如旧实相有其连贯性，新实相也将如此。

quotes.text: If you consider yourself primarily physical then you may, in line with your beliefs, impede your spiritual or emotional aspects. In that case, working with your beliefs will lead you to greater experience along mental and spiritual paths. But all are interconnected, and you cannot ignore one except at the expense of others.
quotes.translation: 如果你主要将自己视为物质性的存在，那么根据你的信念，你可能会阻碍自己的灵性或情感层面。在这种情况下，处理你的信念将引导你在心智和灵性道路上获得更丰富的经验。但所有层面都是相互关联的，你无法忽视任何一个层面而不牺牲其他层面。

quotes.text: That intent may be confused, poorly executed, tangled amid conflicts of beliefs, strangled by the bloody hands of murders and wars -- and yet no man or woman ever loses it. That represents the hope of the species, and it has ever remained lit, like a bright light within each member of the species; and that good intent is handed down through the generations. It is far more potent, that illumination, than any hates or national grudges that may also be passed along.
quotes.translation: 那份意图可能被混淆、执行不当、在信念冲突中纠缠不清，被谋杀与战争的鲜血之手扼杀——但没有任何男人或女人会失去它。这代表了物种的希望，它始终如明亮的光芒般存在于每个物种成员之内；这份良善意图代代相传。那光芒远比任何可能传承的仇恨或民族积怨更为强大。

quotes.text: Such dreams will be greatly effective, but only for a short period of time unless the conscious mind faces the beliefs that have been causing the imbalance. The heavy doses of chemicals introduced from the outside, however, give you an entirely different kind of situation and add new stresses. These dilemmas condition consciousness to believe its position to be even more precarious than it was before, and its sense of power and effectiveness is greatly reduced.
quotes.translation: 这类梦境将非常有效，但仅能持续短暂时间，除非意识心智直面那些导致失衡的信念。从外界引入的大量化学物质会带来完全不同的情境并增加新的压力。这些困境会制约意识，使其相信自己的处境比以往更加岌岌可危，其力量与效能感也大幅降低。

quotes.text: The situation may be quite different in some ways. Large areas of life may not be touched by certain attitudes, while others are. One person may be completely free physically and in excellent health, and yet, because of certain experiences, begin to doubt his ability to get along with others. So he may begin to look into his past -- with that belief in mind, that he cannot relate -- and then find within previous conduct all kinds of reasons to support the idea.
quotes.translation: 在某些方面，情况可能大不相同。生活的某些领域可能完全不受特定态度的影响，而其他领域则不然。一个人可能在身体上完全自由且健康极佳，却因某些经历开始怀疑自己与人相处的能力。于是他可能带着"自己无法建立关系"的信念回顾过去——然后在过往行为中找出各种理由来支持这个想法。

quotes.text: Your own idea of the perilous nature of existence becomes so strong that the ego allows this data to emerge, even though it is "out of time," because your fearful beliefs convince it that you must be on guard. The incidents do not even have to involve you. From all the unconscious telepathic and clairvoyant data available, however, you will be aware of this particular grouping, and it will only serve to reinforce your idea that existence is above all perilous.
quotes.translation: 你对存在本质充满危险的观念变得如此强烈，以至于自我允许这些数据浮现——即便它们"不合时宜"——因为你恐惧的信念说服自我必须保持警惕。这些事件甚至不必与你直接相关。然而，从所有可用的无意识心灵感应与千里眼数据中，你会注意到这个特定组合，而这只会强化你"存在本质充满危险"的信念。

quotes.text: If you believed you were unworthy because you were scrawny and bullied, then in some way you undoubtedly used that belief for your own purposes. Admit it. Discover what the purposes were. Perhaps you compensated -- became athletic later, or used the impetus to go ahead in your own way. If your mother hated you, you may have used that to assert independence, to give you an excuse or a pathway; but in all cases you form your own reality, and so you agreed to it.
quotes.translation: 若你因瘦弱被欺而相信自己毫无价值，那么你无疑在某种程度上利用了这个信念达成自己的目的。承认它。找出这些目的为何。也许你后来通过成为运动员来补偿，或以此动力走自己的路。若你母亲憎恨你，你可能借此主张独立，将其作为借口或途径；但无论如何，你都在创造自己的实相，因此你曾同意如此。

quotes.text: You are familiar with your own view of the world. As you leave your usual orientation, however, altering the focus of your consciousness, you may very well structure your new experience just as you do your physical one. At the same time, you are more free. You have greater leeway. You are used to projecting your beliefs onto physical objects and events. When you leave your home station, those objects and events no longer present themselves in the same fashion.
quotes.translation: 你熟悉自己对世界的看法。然而，当你离开惯常的取向，改变意识的焦点时，你很可能会像构建物质实相那样构建你的新体验。同时，你更加自由，拥有更大的余地。你习惯于将信念投射到物质物体和事件上。当你离开自己的本位时，那些物体和事件便不再以同样的方式呈现。

quotes.text: It is also automatically transformed according to your beliefs, so that it makes sense to you at least to some degree. In energy terms, think of your selves as particles, and of your experiences as the waves that flow through the particles and gives each of them its sensations. When you are physical you are a particle. The form of the particle defines your experience as the waves permeate it, but your greater reality cannot be expressed in such limited terms.
quotes.translation: 它也会根据你的信念自动转化，因此至少在一定程度上对你来说是有意义的。从能量角度来说，把你的自我想象成粒子，把你的体验想象成流经粒子并赋予每个粒子其感觉的波。当你处于物质状态时，你就是一个粒子。粒子的形态定义了你的体验，因为波渗透其中，但你更大的实相无法用如此有限的术语来表达。

quotes.text: Now: While, generally speaking, the issues just mentioned operate as impediments, there are always exceptions. A belief in heaven that is not an obsessional belief can be used as a useful framework, as a basis of operation in which an individual will often accept easily then, the new explanations that will be offered.
quotes.translation: 现在：虽然一般来说，上述问题会构成障碍，但总有例外。对天堂的信念，如果不是一种执迷的信念，就可以被用作一个有用的框架，作为一个运作的基础，在这样的基础上，个体往往能轻易接受随后将提供的新解释。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: More and more, the quality of your lives is formed through the subjective realities of your feelings and mental constructions. Again, beliefs that foster despair are biologically destructive. They cause the physical system to shut down. If mass action against appalling social or political conditions is not effective, then other means are taken, and these are often in the guise of epidemics or natural disasters. The blight is wiped out in one way or another.
quotes.translation: 你们生活的品质，越来越透过你们感受与心智建构的主观实相所形成。再次强调，助长绝望的信念在生物学上具有破坏性。它们会导致身体系统关闭。如果针对骇人社会或政治状况的集体行动无效，便会采取其他手段，这些手段往往以流行病或自然灾害的形式出现。祸害会以某种方式被铲除。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: The sound of your inner thoughts is the medium that you actually use. This is far more than an analogy, however, for in simple terms it explains quite clearly the way in which your beliefs form your reality. In quiet moments the word "O-O-O-O-O-M-M-M-M-M," said slowly, mentally or aloud, will be of benefit in toning up your general physical condition. The sounds contain within them a built-in impetus toward energy and well-being, as I will explain shortly.
quotes.translation: 你们内在思想的声音，才是实际运用的媒介。然而这远非比喻，因为简单来说，它清楚说明了你们的信念如何形成实相。在宁静时刻，缓慢地默念或出声念诵"嗡-嗡-嗡-嗡-嗡-嘛-嘛-嘛-嘛-嘛"这个音，将有益于调养整体身体状况。这些声音本身蕴含着朝向能量与福祉的内在动力，稍后我会解释。

quotes.text: He proceeded to make two divisions in his life, one "psychic," and the other "the writing self." The writing self looked askance at any creative material that did not come from the kinds of inspiration with which it was previously familiar. It insisted that other creative material come outside of Ruburt's five-hour writing day. These beliefs generated their own emotions, of course, so that Ruburt would become angry when thought of as a "psychic" by others.
quotes.translation: 他进而将自己的生命划分为两个领域：一个是"通灵"，另一个是"写作自我"。写作自我对任何非来自先前熟悉灵感类型的创作材料都投以怀疑目光。它坚持其他创作材料必须来自鲁柏五小时写作时间之外。这些信念当然会引发相应情绪，因此当别人视鲁柏为"通灵者"时，他会感到愤怒。

quotes.text: "He began to search actually from childhood in a natural fashion toward some larger framework that would offer an explanation for reality, that bore at least some resemblance to the natural vision of his best poetry. I have said before that many creative people, highly gifted, have died young in one way or the other because their great gifts of creativity could find no clear room in which to grow. They became strangled by the beliefs of the cultural times.
quotes.translation: “他实际上从童年起就以自然的方式开始探索，寻找一个能解释现实的更宏大框架——这个框架至少与他最佳诗歌中的自然视野有几分相似。我之前说过，许多极具天赋的创造性人才以各种方式英年早逝，因为他们伟大的创造力找不到清晰的成长空间。他们被文化时代的信念扼杀了。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: One such image may represent one particular belief or it may stand for several. As you make lists of your beliefs you will find some of these pictures coming into your mind. Look at them as you would a painting you have created. If you do not like what you see then quite consciously change the picture in your mind.
quotes.translation: 这样的一个意象可能代表某个特定信念，也可能代表数个信念。当你列出自己的信念时，会发现这些画面浮现于脑海。不妨将它们视为你创作的画作来审视。若对所见的景象不满，便可有意识地改变心中的画面。

quotes.text: (10:55.) No sex will be considered better than the other, or any role in society, when each individual is aware of his own or her own experience at many levels of society and in many roles. An open-ended consciousness will feel its connections with all other living beings. (Pause.) The continuity of consciousness will become apparent. As a result of all this the social and governmental structures will change, for they are based upon your current beliefs.
quotes.translation: （10:55）当每个人都能在社会多重层面与多种角色中觉察自身经验时，便不会认为某种性别优于另一种，或某个社会角色更优越。开放的意识将感知到与所有生灵的联结。（停顿）意识的连续性将变得显而易见。这一切终将改变社会与政府结构，因为它们本就建立在你们当前的信念之上。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: There are indeed lost gospels, written by men in other countries in that time, relating to Christ's unknown life, to episodes not given in the Bible. These formed a quite separate framework of knowledge that could be accepted by people who had different beliefs than the Jews at that time. The messages were given in other terms, but again they reflected the affirmation of the self and its continued existence after physical death. Love was always stressed.
quotes.translation: 确实存在失传的福音书，由当时其他国家的作者撰写，记载了基督不为人知的生活，以及圣经未收录的片段。这些文本构成了独立的知识体系，能被当时持有与犹太人不同信念的人们所接纳。讯息虽以其他术语传达，却同样彰显了自我肯定及其在肉体死亡后的持续存在。爱始终被强调。

quotes.text: A belief in heaven or hell, under certain conditions, can be equally disadvantageous. Some will refuse to accept the idea of further work, development, and challenge, believing instead that conventional heaven situations are the only possibility. For some time they may indeed inhabit such an environment, until they learn through their own experience that existence demands development, and that such a heaven would be sterile, boring, and indeed "deadly."
quotes.translation: 对天堂或地狱的信念，在某些条件下可能同样不利。有些人会拒绝接受进一步工作、发展与挑战的想法，反而认为传统天堂情境是唯一可能。他们或许会暂时栖身于这样的环境，直到通过自身经验领悟到：存在要求发展，而这样的天堂将贫瘠、乏味，甚至"致命"。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: (10:20.) You were faced with what could be called a classic instance of secondary personality. I am discussing it here because it so beautifully illustrates the nature and power of beliefs, and the conflicts that can arise when an individual does not accept responsibility for his own thoughts. This is not a usual case -- but to some extent or another, such a division occurs physically or mentally when the contents of the conscious mind are not examined.
quotes.translation: （10点20分）你面对的是所谓的"次人格"的经典案例。我在此讨论它，是因为它完美诠释了信念的本质与力量，以及当个体不为自身思想负责时可能产生的冲突。这并非寻常案例——但在某种程度上，当意识心内容未经检视时，这种分裂会以身体或心灵的形式发生。

quotes.text: You will be merely doing what you have been doing all along, but with the aid of the ego you will be able to prove the extent of your achievement. If everyone on your plane were suddenly to believe that the physical world would end at a particular time, then so it would, you would simply cease your idea constructions. In our experiments therefore, we will have to begin with a strong belief that what we are setting out to achieve can in fact be achieved.
quotes.translation: 你只是在做你一直在做的事，但借助自我的帮助，你将能够证明你的成就程度。如果你那个层面上的每个人都突然相信物质世界会在某个特定时间终结，那么它就会终结——你只是停止你的观念建构。因此，在我们的实验中，我们必须从强烈的信念开始：我们着手要达成的目标确实能够实现。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: Look about you. Your entire physical environment is the materialization of your beliefs. Your sense of joy, sorrow, health or illness -- all of these are also caused by your beliefs. If you believe that a given situation should make you unhappy, then it will, and the unhappiness will then reinforce the condition.
quotes.translation: 环顾四周。你的整个物质环境就是你信念的具体化。你的喜悦、悲伤、健康或疾病——所有这些也都是由你的信念造成的。如果你相信某个特定情境应该让你不快乐，那么它就会让你不快乐，而这种不快乐又会强化那个情境。

quotes.text: The beliefs whose energy generated this "alternate self image" then appeared in the daylight, acting out their natural results in physical reality. Augustus One, now in manhood, was forced to perceive the nature of these beliefs to some extent, yet when he was here visiting Ruburt he still would not examine them.
quotes.translation: 这些信念的能量生成了这个“替代自我形象”，随后在光天化日之下显现，在物质实相中演绎出其自然结果。已成年的奥古斯都一号被迫在一定程度上看清这些信念的本质，但当他来拜访鲁柏时，仍不愿检视它们。

quotes.text: Here the exotic is romanticized, the foreign held up, the picturesque seen as the real.
