Black skin or brown skin becomes the criteria of spiritual perfection, and poverty a badge of honor to be worn not only proudly, but often to be used as an aggressive tool. The people who follow these belief systems think that they are right. Their living style, community affiliations, and political leanings will be in direct opposition to the "white-wealthy" ethic.
quotes.translation: 在此，异域风情被浪漫化，外来事物被推崇，如画景致被视为真实。黑皮肤或棕皮肤成为灵性完美的标准，贫穷则成为荣誉徽章——不仅可骄傲佩戴，更常被用作攻击性工具。信奉这些信念体系的人们自认正确，其生活方式、社群归属与政治倾向，必然与“白人-富裕”伦理直接对立。

quotes.text: A bond of hate will unite you, but the bond was originally based upon love. In this case however you aggravate and exaggerate all those differences from the ideal, and focus upon them predominantly. In any given case all of this is consciously available to you. It requires only an honest and determined attempt to become aware of your own feelings and beliefs. Even your hateful fantasies, left alone, will return you to a reconciliation and release love.
quotes.translation: 仇恨的纽带会将你们联结，但这纽带原本基于爱。然而在这种情况下，你们却加剧并夸大了所有与理想之间的差异，并主要聚焦于这些差异。在任何特定情况下，这一切都是你们有意识可得的。只需诚实而坚定的尝试，去觉察自己的感受与信念。即便是你们那些充满仇恨的幻想，若任其自然，也会将你们带回和解并释放爱。

quotes.text: Your belief otherwise automatically shuts you off so that the necessary climate, the psychic climate, is a barrier to what you want. The belief will cause attractions, in which physical circumstances will change. It is highly impractical to believe as you do, regarding your automobile or prospects, for another.
quotes.translation: 你相反的信念会自动将你隔绝，使得必要的气候——心灵气候——成为你达成愿望的障碍。这个信念会引发吸引力法则，使物质环境随之改变。像你那样看待自己的汽车或前景，对他人而言是极不切实际的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: Ruburt has been reading old poetry of his own, and he was appalled to find such beliefs in rather brutal, concentrated form. Until our sessions began, he followed the official line of consciousness, and though he railed against those precepts he could find no other solution. The self, so spectacularly alive, seemed equipped with reason to understand the great import of its own certain extinction. Such a tragedy to project upon the living personality.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏最近重读自己早年诗作，震惊地发现其中竟以相当残酷而集中的形式呈现这些信念。在课程开始前，他遵循着意识的官方路线，尽管他激烈抨击那些教条，却找不到其他出路。那看似生机勃勃的自我，似乎配备着理性来理解自身必然消亡的重大意义——将如此悲剧投射在活生生的人格上，是何等荒谬。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: It is the core belief which is strong enough to so focus your perception that you perceive from the physical world only those events that correlate with it. It is also the strength of the core belief that draws up from the vast bank of inner knowledge only those events that seem to fit within its organization.
quotes.translation: 正是核心信念强大到足以如此聚焦你的感知，使你只从物质世界中感知到与之相关的事件。也正是核心信念的力量，从内在知识的庞大库藏中只提取那些似乎符合其组织架构的事件。

quotes.text: (9:34.) If you habitually deny the expression of any emotions, to that degree you become alienated not only from your body but from your conscious ideas. You will bury certain thoughts and put up biological armor to prevent you from physically feeling their effects upon your body. In each case the answer lies in your personal system of beliefs, in those strong concepts you hold on an intimate level that brought about the inhibitions to begin with.
quotes.translation: (9:34) 如果你习惯性地压抑任何情绪的表达，你不仅会与自己的身体疏离，也会与自己的意识理念疏离。你会埋葬某些思想，并构筑生物性的盔甲，阻止自己从身体层面感受这些思想对肉体造成的影响。每个案例的答案都存在于你个人的信念系统中——那些你深信不疑的核心观念，正是它们最初导致了这些压抑。

quotes.text: ... in certain terms the theory of evolution, as it is conventionally held, has caused unfortunate beliefs. For how can you look at yourselves with self-respect, with dignity or with joy, if you believe that you are the end product of forces in which the fittest survive? Being the fittest implies those given most to what would appear to be murderous intent -- for you must survive at the expense of your fellows, be you leaf, frog, plant, or animal.
quotes.translation: ...从某种角度而言，传统意义上的进化论已引发不幸的信念。若你相信自己是"适者生存"法则的最终产物，又如何能以自尊、尊严或喜悦看待自身？所谓"最适者"暗示着那些最具杀戮意图的存在——因为无论你是叶片、青蛙、植物还是动物，都必须以牺牲同类为代价才能存活。

quotes.text: (9:14.) There are no magical methods, only natural ones that you use all of the time, although in some cases you use them for beliefs that you take for truths, when instead they are quite defective assumptions. A small example -- one, incidentally, that Ruburt finally realized; but it is a beautiful instance of natural methods. He used it beautifully, even though the results were not pleasing at first. It also shows Ruburt's growing understanding:
quotes.translation: (9:14) 不存在魔法般的方法，只有你始终在使用的自然方法——尽管在某些情况下，你会将它们用于那些被误认为真理的信念，而实际上这些信念是相当有缺陷的假设。举个鲁柏最终领悟的小例子：这恰是自然方法的绝佳范例。他运用得如此美妙，即便最初结果并不令人愉悦。这也展现了鲁柏日益增长的领悟力：

quotes.text: Until you change your belief, you will continue to utilize your food in the same fashion -- and to overeat. Momentary gains will not last. Your entire behavior pattern operates according to the strong hypnotic suggestions given, and then of course your appearance and experience always reinforce your belief.
quotes.translation: 除非你改变信念，否则你会继续以同样的方式利用食物——并且暴饮暴食。暂时的收获不会持久。你的整个行为模式遵循着所给予的强烈催眠暗示，而你的外表和经历当然总是强化你的信念。

quotes.text: Medicine men may do a rain dance. They understand the innate relationship that exists within all portions of nature. You are taught to believe that faith can move mountains, yet many of you will find it extremely difficult to accept your own relationship with the environment. Your beliefs (often as opposed to your desires) cause wars. Your feelings represent the inner reality behind what you think of as purely natural phenomena, such as weather.
quotes.translation: 巫医可能会跳祈雨舞。他们理解自然所有部分之间存在的内在关系。你被教导相信信念可以移山，但你们许多人会发现极难接受自己与环境的关系。你的信念（通常与欲望相反）引发战争。你的感受代表了你所认为纯粹自然现象（如天气）背后的内在实相。

quotes.text: In order to become physical, probable events must meet certain conditions, as it were. They must fall into the proper time and space slots. There must be a psychological fit also, certain intensities reached in terms of desire, belief, or intent. By intensity I do not necessarily mean effort, vehement desire, or determined conscious intent. I mean instead the collection of certain intangible qualities, precisely focused toward physical activity.
quotes.translation: 为了成为物质实相，或然事件必须满足某些条件。它们必须落入适当的时间和空间槽中，同时还需要心理上的契合，在欲望、信念或意图方面达到特定强度。我所说的强度并非指努力、强烈渴望或刻意的有意识意图，而是指某些无形特质的集合，精准地朝向物质活动聚焦。

quotes.text: -- and we will resume dictation. Give us a moment. (Pause.) Core beliefs are those about which you build your life. You are consciously aware of these, though often you do not focus your attention upon them. They become invisible, therefore, unless you become aware of the contents of your conscious mind.
quotes.translation: ——我们将继续口述。请稍等片刻。（停顿）核心信念是你赖以构建人生的那些信念。你虽能意识到它们的存在，却往往不加以关注。因此除非你觉察到意识心所包含的内容，否则它们会变得隐而不显。

quotes.text: Using it, he is only now in the process of assimilating the newly available energy. He understands that he is the self who holds all of those beliefs, and does not identify so completely with the one core belief any longer. That association was what had prevented its natural motion and expansion earlier.
quotes.translation: 他运用它，此刻才刚开始吸收新获得的能量。他明白自己是持有所有这些信念的那个自己，不再完全认同于那个核心信念。正是那种认同感先前阻碍了它的自然运动与扩展。

quotes.text: Both believe they need the other, of course. Behind this is the psychic pattern of beliefs in which the patient often assigns to the doctor the powers of knowledge and wisdom that his beliefs have taught him he does not have. Knowing otherwise, the patient still wants to consider the doctor omnipotent.
quotes.translation: 当然，双方都认为彼此需要对方。这背后存在着信念的心灵模式：病人常将信念教导他"自己不具备"的知识与智慧力量，投射到医生身上。即便明知并非如此，病人仍希望视医生为全知全能者。

quotes.text: Again, while the conscious mind is meant to direct the flow of your experience through your beliefs, and to materialize them, the actual mechanics are taken care of automatically by other portions of the self. You must indeed trust that your new beliefs will work as completely for you as your old ones.
quotes.translation: 再者，虽然意识心本应通过信念引导经验之流并使其具体化，但实际运作机制却由自我其他部分自动处理。你们必须确实信任：新信念会像旧信念一样，为你们完整运作。

quotes.text: You are not at the mercy of your emotions, either, for they are meant to follow the flow of your reasoning. Your mind is meant to perceive the physical environment clearly, and its judgments about the environment then activate the body's mechanisms to bring about proper response. If your beliefs about existence are fearful, then the emotional reactions will be those leading to stress. Your own value judgments need examination in such a case.
quotes.translation: 你也不受情绪的摆布，因为情绪本应追随你理性的流动。你的心智本应清晰地感知物质环境，其对环境的判断随后激活身体机制以产生适当反应。若你对存在的信念充满恐惧，情绪反应便会导向压力状态。在此情况下，你需要审视自己的价值判断。

quotes.text: No man will die simply because a physician tells him he is going to, however. No one is so at the mercy of another's beliefs. Each individual, generally speaking, knows his challenges and overall programs, and the time of his death. But even such decisions can be altered at any time in your "now" -- the entire body can be regenerated in a way that would be impossible to predict in usual medical terms. (See the 624th session in Chapter Five.)
quotes.translation: 没有人会仅仅因为医生说他将死就真的死去。没有人会如此受他人信念的摆布。一般而言，每个个体都知晓自己的挑战与整体规划，以及死亡的时间。但即便这样的决定，也能在你"当下"的任何时刻被改变——整个身体能以常规医学无法预测的方式重生。（参见第五章第624节）

quotes.text: We will later discuss the nature of mass reality, but for now we are dwelling upon the personal aspects. The main point I wanted to make in this chapter was that your conscious beliefs are extremely important, and that you are not at the mercy of events or causes that dwell far beneath your awareness.
quotes.translation: 我们稍后将讨论群体实相的本质，但目前我们聚焦于个人层面。本章我想强调的核心是：你有意识的信念极其重要，你并非受制于深藏于觉知之下的事件或原因。

quotes.text: Imaginatively you may see the next bill coming, with you unable to pay it. "I will have enough money," you say. "This is my new belief." But nothing changes so you think, "My conscious thoughts mean nothing." Yet upon examination of your beliefs you may find a deep conviction of your own unworthiness.
quotes.translation: 想象中你或许看见下一张账单即将到来，而你无力支付。“我会有足够的钱，”你说，“这是我的新信念。”但一切毫无改变，于是你想：“我有意识的念头毫无意义。”然而，若检视你的信念，你可能会发现自己深信自身毫无价值。

quotes.text: When that nature grew out of the framework, he left it. All the beliefs that had once seemed so legitimate were then seen as hampering, and all their defects became plain. While he followed the framework, nothing could swerve him from it, and here (touching the photo) in this child's picture, you already have the unswerving nature, the great spontaneity, looking for a structure that will allow it growth, and yet give the illusion of safety.
quotes.translation: 当那种本性成长出框架之外时，他便离开了它。所有曾看似合理的信念，此刻都被视为阻碍，其缺陷也昭然若揭。当他遵循框架时，没有任何事物能使他偏离；而在这张孩童照片中（触碰照片），你已看到那坚定不移的本性、那巨大的自发性，正在寻找一个既能容许它成长、又能给予安全幻象的结构。

quotes.text: (Softly amused:) As a matter of fact -- in case you may think sometimes that I am not fully aware of your mores -- I did indeed temper many of my remarks in Mass Events on several subjects, so that the book would not be found too objectionable in the context of your times. The implications are there, but your belief systems must be allowed to mellow and change in the light of new knowledge, rather than to be booted aside with an angry foot.
quotes.translation: （略带笑意地：）事实上——以防你们有时认为我并未完全了解你们的风俗——我确实在《群体事件》一书中就若干主题缓和了许多言论，以免该书在你们时代的语境中被视为过于令人反感。其中隐含的深意是存在的，但你们的信念体系必须在新知的光照下逐渐成熟与转变，而非被愤怒一脚踢开。

quotes.text: A belief must not possess. They were psychically adding to the unfortunate circumstances over which they argued, and both equally were at fault for this reason. A battle recreated in the mind reinforces the original psychic explosion and contemplation of war helps project that disaster into the future. To argue aggressively that war is wrong is ironic stupidity, for such an individual already displays those characteristics that lead to war.
quotes.translation: 信念不可占有。他们正在灵性上加剧自己所争论的不幸境况，双方因此同样有错。在脑海中重演的战斗强化了最初的心灵爆发，而对战争的沉思则助长将灾难投射到未来。激烈争辩战争是错误的，实为讽刺的愚蠢，因为这样的人已展现出导致战争的特质。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: By recognizing these differences and honestly following the feelings through -- in other words, by riding the emotions -- you will be led to the beliefs behind them. A series of self-revelations will inevitably result, each leading you to further creative psychological activity. At each stage you will be closer to the reality of your experience than you have ever been.
quotes.translation: 通过辨识这些差异并诚实地跟随感受——换言之，驾驭情绪——你们将被引向情绪背后的信念。一系列自我启示将不可避免地随之而来，每一步都将引领你们进入更富创造性的心理活动。在每个阶段，你们都将比以往任何时候更贴近自身经验的实相。

quotes.text: You have been taught that you are at the mercy of previous events, so your idea of looking for the source of personal difficulty is to examine the past, but -- to find what you did wrong there, or what mistakes occurred there, or what inadequate interpretations were made there! Again, regardless of what you have been taught, the point of power is in the present; and again, your present beliefs will be used to structure your recollections.
quotes.translation: 你们一直被教导说，自己受制于过去的事件，因此你们寻找个人困境根源的方式就是检视过去——但那是为了找出自己过去做错了什么，或那里发生了什么错误，或当时做出了哪些不恰当的解释！再次强调，无论你们受过何种教导，力量的关键点始终在当下；而你们当下的信念，也将被用来构建你们的回忆。

related_concepts.name_en: The point of power is in the present

quotes.text: That reality represents your origin, and is the natural environment in which the psyche resides. Your beliefs, cultural background, and to some extent your languages, set up barriers so that this dream dimension seems unreal to you. Even when you catch yourselves in the most vivid of dream adventures, or find yourselves traveling outside of your bodies while dreaming, you still do not give such experiences equal validity with waking ones.
quotes.translation: 那个实相代表了你的本源，是心灵栖息的天然环境。你们的信念、文化背景，以及某种程度上你们的语言，设置了屏障，使得这个梦境维度对你们而言显得不真实。即使你们在最为生动的梦中冒险中捕捉到自己，或发现自己在做梦时灵魂出体旅行，你们仍不会给予这类体验与清醒体验同等的真实性。

quotes.text: Science, however, seeing the body as a mechanism, has promoted the idea that consciousness is trapped within a mechanical model, that man's suffering is mechanically caused in that regard: You simply give the machine some better parts and all will be well (amused). Science also operates as magic, of course, so on some occasions the belief in science itself will seemingly work miracles: The new heart will give a man new heart, for example.
quotes.translation: 然而，科学将身体视为一种机械装置，它宣扬这样的观念：意识被困在机械模型之中，人类的痛苦在机械层面由此产生：你只需给这台机器换上更好的零件，一切就会好转（带着笑意）。当然，科学本身也如同魔法般运作，因此在某些情况下，对科学的信念本身似乎能创造奇迹：例如，新的心脏会赋予一个人全新的心境。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: His core belief in himself as a writer, he saw, was really highly constrictive. He had not realized that before. At the same time he had consciously known it, but allowed it to remain invisible. He realized that the writing and psychic aspects each did want to write, and this was the bridge belief.
quotes.translation: 他发现自己作为作家的核心信念实际上极具限制性。他以前从未意识到这一点。同时他虽在意识层面知晓，却任其保持隐形。他意识到写作与通灵两方面确实都想写作，而这正是桥梁信念。

quotes.text: 5. By now, a number of the world's leading scientists in the physical disciplines have publicly stated their beliefs that basically consciousness plays the primary role in our world and/or universe. For reasons too complicated to go into here, this attitude prevails even with some mathematicians who seek to penetrate to the core of our reality as they understand it.
quotes.translation: 5. 时至今日，许多世界顶尖的物理学科科学家已公开声明他们的信念：基本上意识在我们的世界和/或宇宙中扮演着主要角色。由于原因过于复杂无法在此详述，这种态度甚至盛行于某些试图深入探究他们所理解的现实核心的数学家之中。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: The child is not born a sponge, however, empty but ready to soak up knowledge. It is already soaked in knowledge. Some will come to the surface, so to speak, and be used consciously. Some will not. I am saying here that to some extent the child in the womb is aware of the mother's beliefs and information, and that to some extent (underlined) it is "programmed" to behave in a certain fashion, or to grow in a certain fashion as a result.
quotes.translation: 然而，孩子并非生来就是一块海绵——空无一物却准备吸收知识。它早已浸润于知识之中。可以说，有些知识会浮出水面并被有意识地运用，有些则不会。我在此指出：在某种程度上，子宫中的胎儿能感知母亲的信念与信息；并且在某种程度上（强调），它被“预设程序”以某种方式行事，或因此以某种方式成长。

quotes.text: (10:00.) Usually when you look into your conscious mind you do so for a particular reason, to find some information. But if you have schooled yourself to believe that such data is not consciously available, then it will not occur to you to find it in your conscious mind. If furthermore your conscious data is strongly organized about a core belief, then this will automatically make you blind to experience that is not connected with it.
quotes.translation: （十点整。）通常当你审视自己的意识心时，你是出于某个特定理由，想寻找某些信息。但如果你已训练自己相信这类资料无法有意识地取得，那么你就不会想到要在意识心中寻找它。此外，如果你的意识资料强烈地围绕某个核心信念组织起来，那么这会自动使你对其无关的经验视而不见。

quotes.text: Many people write requesting that I outline the proper methods for achieving astral projection, for example, or psychic advancement, or spiritual understanding. In its fashion, however, this book is geared to bring about the development of such abilities, for it is not a lack of methods that inhibits such activities. Instead, "psychic progress" is hampered by those very negative beliefs that we have tried to bring to your attention.
quotes.translation: 许多人写信要求我概述达成出体经验、心灵进展或灵性了悟的适当方法。然而，这本书以其独特的方式旨在促成这类能力的发展，因为阻碍这类活动的并非缺乏方法。相反，"心灵进展"恰恰受到我们试图引起你注意的那些负面信念的阻碍。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: In this case also your reality colors your beliefs, and your experience is a direct result of your conscious attitudes. By such attitudes as these just mentioned you put clamps upon your inner self, purposely hamper your experience, and reinforce beliefs in the negative aspects of your being.
quotes.translation: 在这种情况下，你的实相同样会染上你的信念色彩，而你的经验是你有意识态度的直接结果。通过刚才提到的这类态度，你给自己的内在自我套上枷锁，刻意阻碍你的经验，并强化你对自己存在负面面向的信念。

quotes.text: No apple tree tries to grow violets. Quite automatically it knows what it is, and the framework of its own identity and existence. (Pause.) You have a conscious mind, but this is only the "topmost" portion of your mind. Much more of "it" is available to you. Much more of your knowledge can be conscious, therefore; but a false belief, a limiting one, is as ambiguous to your nature as any apple tree's idea that it was a violet plant.
quotes.translation: 没有苹果树会试图长出紫罗兰。它自动知晓自己的本质，以及自身身份与存在的框架。（停顿）你拥有意识心，但这只是你心智的"最上层"部分。更多"它"的部分可供你取用。因此，更多你的知识可以成为有意识的；但一个虚假的信念，一个限制性的信念，对你的本性而言，就像任何苹果树认为自己是一株紫罗兰的想法一样荒谬。

quotes.text: All of this happens because the inner portions of your being operate spontaneously, joyfully, freely; all of this occurs because your inner self believes in you, often even while you do not believe in it. These unconscious portions of your being operate amazingly well, frequently despite the greatest misunderstanding on your part of their nature and function, and in the face of strong interference from you because of your beliefs.
quotes.translation: 这一切之所以发生，是因为你存在的内在部分自发、喜悦、自由地运作着；这一切之所以发生，是因为你的内在自我信任你，即便在你常常不信任它的时候。你存在的这些无意识部分运作得惊人地好，尽管你常常对其本质与功能存在极大误解，并且因你的信念而对其施加强烈干扰。

quotes.text: The naming and labelling of "diseases" is a harmful practice that to a large extent denies the innate mobility and ever-changing quality of the psyche as expressed in flesh. You are told that you have "something." Out of the blue "it" has attacked you, and your most intimate organs, perhaps. You are usually told that your emotions or beliefs or system of values have nothing to do with the unfortunate circumstances that beset you.
quotes.translation: 为"疾病"命名和贴标签是有害的做法，这在很大程度上否定了心灵在肉身中表达时固有的流动性与不断变化的特质。你被告知患上了"某种东西"，它突然"袭击"了你，甚至可能波及你最私密的器官。通常你还会被告知，你的情绪、信念或价值体系与你所遭遇的不幸处境毫无关联。

quotes.text: It was only because the childlike characteristics of Augustus Two finally appeared so blatantly that Augustus Two had to be dispensed with. Augustus's wife made the difference, for it was obvious that she did not have the same opinion of this "friend" that her husband had. Her beliefs then became the new foundation, the one point of change that allowed Augustus to view this alternate self-image with any kind of detachment at all.
quotes.translation: 正是因为奥古斯都二世孩童般的特质最终暴露得过于明显，这个身份才不得不被舍弃。奥古斯都的妻子起到了关键作用——显然她对这位"朋友"的看法与丈夫截然不同。她的信念由此成为新的基石，成为让奥古斯都得以超然审视这个替代自我形象的唯一转折点。

quotes.text: Your experience of history, of the days of your life, is invisibly formed by those ideas that exist in the imagination only, and then are projected upon the physical world. This applies to your individual beliefs about yourself and the way you see yourself in your imagination. You are having wars between the Jews and the Arabs and the Christians once again, because emphasis is put upon literal interpretations of spiritual truths.
quotes.translation: 你对历史、对生命岁月的体验，无形中是由那些仅存于想象中、而后投射到物质世界的观念所塑造的。这适用于你对自己持有的个人信念，以及你在想象中看待自己的方式。犹太人、阿拉伯人和基督徒之间再次爆发战争，正是因为人们过分强调对灵性真理的字面解释。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Dictation: As you examine the contents of your conscious mind, it may seem to you that you hold so many different beliefs at different times that you cannot correlate them. They will, however, form into clear patterns. You will find a grouping of core beliefs about which the others gather.
quotes.translation: 口述：当你检视意识心所包含的内容时，可能会觉得在不同时刻持有如此众多不同的信念，以至于无法将它们关联起来。然而，它们终将形成清晰的模式。你会发现一组核心信念，其他信念都围绕其聚集。

quotes.text: Your beliefs, thoughts and feelings are instantly materialized physically. Their earthly reality occurs simultaneously with their inception, but in the world of time, lapses between appear to occur. So I say one causes the other, and I use those terms to help you understand, but all are at once. So are your multiple lives occurring as the immediate realization of your being in the natural extension of its many-faceted abilities.
quotes.translation: 你的信念、思想与感受会立即在物质层面具体化。它们的地球实相与它们的诞生同时发生，但在时间世界里，两者之间似乎存在着时间差。因此我说一个引发另一个，我使用这些术语是为了帮助你理解，但所有这一切都是同时发生的。你的多重生命也是如此，它们是你存在本质在其多面能力的自然延伸中即刻实现的结果。

quotes.text: Use the knowledge as a bridge. Let whatever emotions are involved happen. If you do this honestly, feelings of self-worthlessness or despondency will go through and vanish, changing of their own accord. You may even find yourself impatient with the feelings themselves, or even bored, and hence dismiss them. Do not tell yourself automatically that they are wrong, however, and then try to apply a "positive" belief like a bandaid.
quotes.translation: 将知识作为桥梁，让任何涉及的情绪自然发生。若你诚实地这样做，自我无价值感或沮丧感将会穿透并消散，自行改变。你甚至可能发现自己对这些情绪感到不耐烦，甚至厌倦，从而摒弃它们。但不要自动告诉自己它们是错的，然后试图像贴创可贴一样套用"正面"信念。

quotes.text: The second health area I want to touch upon concerns the elderly. Ideas of retirement fall generally into the same pattern, for hidden within them is the belief that at one time or another, at a specific age, your powers will begin to fail. These ideas are usually accepted by young and old alike. In believing them, the young automatically begin the gradual conditioning of their own bodies and minds. The results will be reaped.
quotes.translation: 我想谈的第二个健康领域涉及老年人。退休观念通常遵循相同模式，因为隐藏其中的是这样一个信念：在某个特定年龄，你的能力将开始衰退。这些观念通常被年轻人和老年人同样接受。相信它们时，年轻人会自动开始对自己身心的渐进式制约。其后果终将显现。

quotes.text: Despite all appearances, conditions of an exterior nature do not cause wars, or poverty, or disease, or any of the unfortunate circumstances apparent in the world. Your beliefs form your reality. Your thoughts generate practical experience. When these change, conditions will change. To add your own energy, focus, and concentration to dire circumstances in other portions of the world does not help, but adds to, such situations.
quotes.translation: 尽管表象如此，外在环境状况并不会导致战争、贫穷、疾病或世上任何明显的不幸境遇。你的信念形成你的实相。你的思想产生实际经验。当这些改变时，状况就会改变。将你自己的能量、专注和聚焦投向世界其他地区的可怕境况，非但无益，反而会助长此类情况。

quotes.text: There is light then that you do not see with physical eyes, as there is sound that you do not hear with your ears. These combine to mentally form the physical image that you know, so you must work from the inside out. Your beliefs are your palette, using the analogy of a painting again.
quotes.translation: 因此存在你们肉眼无法看见的光，正如存在你们耳朵无法听见的声音。这些元素在心灵层面共同构成了你们所认知的物质影像，所以你们必须由内而外地运作。再次借用绘画的比喻，你们的信念就是调色盘。

quotes.text: (10:19.) You will do this by concentrating upon what you want, but feeling no conflict between that and what you have, because one will not contradict the other; each will be seen as a reflection of belief in daily life. As it took some time to build up your present image with its unhealthy aspects, so it may take time to change that picture. But concentration upon the present unhealthy situation will only prolong it. Period.
quotes.translation: （10点19分）你们将专注于自己渴望的事物，同时不使这与现有处境产生冲突，因为两者本不相悖；每个现实都将被视为信念在日常生活中的映照。正如你们耗费时日构筑出带有不健康面向的当前影像，改变这幅图景同样需要时间。但若执着于当前不健康的状况，只会使其延续。句号。

quotes.text: Ruburt's Cézanne material therefore comes very quickly, taking a bare portion of the day. Yet its quality is such that professional art critics could learn from it, though some of their productions might take much longer periods of time, and result from an extensive conscious knowledge of art, which Ruburt almost entirely lacks. The productions of the psyche by their nature, therefore, burst aside many most cherished beliefs.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏的塞尚资料因此来得极为迅捷，仅占用一天中极短的时间。然而其品质之高，足以让专业艺术评论家从中获益——尽管他们某些作品的创作可能需要更长时间，且源于对艺术广泛的有意识认知，而鲁柏几乎完全缺乏这些。心灵的作品因其本质，往往能冲破许多人们最珍视的信念。

quotes.text: This session will serve as a reminder, however, to both of you, and will help brush away the remainder of any old lingering beliefs. Earlier, Ruburt was afraid to trust his body, afraid to let his body heal him, for fear he would be attacked by others. You both also believed that you must protect your special abilities with all of your might -- but you can see the road of contradictions that is constructed in such a fashion.
quotes.translation: 然而，这一节将作为对你们双方的提醒，并有助于扫除任何残留的旧有信念。早些时候，鲁柏不敢信任自己的身体，不敢让身体自我疗愈，生怕遭到他人攻击。你们也曾相信必须竭尽全力保护自己的特殊能力——但你们可以看到，以这种方式构建出的矛盾之路。

quotes.text: A war is one kind of natural event brought about as feelings and beliefs interact on one level. A natural catastrophe represents the same kind of phenomenon at a different level. Your part in these feelings and beliefs will place you in your own "natural" position within such events.
quotes.translation: 战争是一种自然事件，由情感和信念在某一层面相互作用而产生。自然灾害则代表同一类现象在不同层面的显现。你在这类情感和信念中的参与，将使你在这些事件中处于自己"自然"的位置。

quotes.text: Ruburt's perception is highly altered this evening, and this is an example of certain kinds of both affirmation and denial. He has always emphasized his own unique creative and intuitive processes. In so doing, he denied many of the concepts believed in by others. He accepted the belief that any consciousness could be in some kind of direct intimate contact with experiences and realities usually not perceived, but ignored.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏今晚的感知高度改变，这是某种肯定与否定并存的例子。他一向强调自己独特的创造性与直觉过程。如此一来，他否定了许多他人所相信的概念。他接受这样的信念：任何意识都能与通常不被感知、却被忽视的经验与实相，建立某种直接而亲密的接触。

quotes.text: It carries also, however, the imprint of your fears, for the tales say that Atlantis was destroyed. You place it in your past while it exists in your future. Not the destruction alone, but the entire pattern seen through the framework of your beliefs. Beside this, however, many civilizations have come and gone in somewhat the same manner, and the "myth" [of Atlantis] is based somewhat then on physical fact in your terms.10
quotes.translation: 然而，它也承载着你们恐惧的印记，因为传说中亚特兰蒂斯被毁灭了。你们将它置于过去，而它却存在于你们的未来。不仅是毁灭本身，而是透过你们信念框架所见的整个模式。除此之外，许多文明也以类似的方式兴起与消亡，而（亚特兰蒂斯的）"神话"在你们的说法中，多少基于物质事实。

quotes.text: Many people, caught between such conflicting beliefs, fall prey to physical ills during the Christmas season particularly. The churches and the hospitals are often the largest buildings in any town, and the only ones open on Sunday without recourse to city ordinances. You cannot divorce your private value systems from your health, and the hospitals often profit from the guilt that religions have instilled in their people.
quotes.translation: 许多人夹在如此冲突的信念之间，尤其在圣诞季节容易成为身体疾病的猎物。教堂和医院通常是城镇里最大的建筑，也是唯一无需城市法令允许就在周日开放的场所。你们无法将私人价值体系与健康分离，而医院往往从宗教灌输给人们的罪恶感中获利。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: For all of this, however, inner portions of each individual's being are not touched by those beliefs. The ideas will be reflected in their daily experience, certainly, and seem to be justified. Yet beneath, the inner self is quite aware of the great thrusting creativity that occurs in dreams, and realizes that the source of individual energy has nothing to do with such superficial concepts as the nature of good and evil.
quotes.translation: 尽管如此，每个人存在的内在部分并不受这些信念触及。这些想法当然会反映在日常经验中，且看似合理。然而在表层之下，内在自我完全知晓梦境中发生的巨大创造力，并明白个体能量的来源与善恶本质这类肤浅概念毫无关联。

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... In a way you are all your own mutants, creatively altering cellular formations. Period. When your fate seems dependent upon heredity, for example, then the transmission of ideas and beliefs operates; these give signals to the chromosomes. They cause miniature self-images, so to speak, that are mirrored in the cells. In many cases these images can be altered, but not with the technology that you have.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……从某种角度来看，你们都是自己的突变体，创造性地改变着细胞结构。句号。例如，当你们的命运似乎取决于遗传时，观念和信念的传递便开始运作；这些传递向染色体发出信号。可以说，它们引发了微小的自我形象，这些形象在细胞中得以映照。在许多情况下，这些形象可以被改变，但并非通过你们现有的技术。

quotes.text: When people using such methods are told that their writing comes through from a demon or the devil, or an evil spirit, then those invisible beliefs are shoved farther away. Any search into the mind becomes frightening and dangerous, since it might lead to further such "invasion."
quotes.translation: 当使用这类方法的人被告知，他们的书写内容来自恶魔、魔鬼或邪灵时，那些无形的信念便被推得更远。任何对心灵的探索都变得令人恐惧且危险，因为这可能导致进一步的此类"入侵"。

quotes.text: There are many distortions in the universal mind book that Ruburt reads, and yet overall the ideas presented are highly advantageous. I cannot put this emphatically enough: your beliefs form a psychic climate that in itself attracts and forms the materialization of those beliefs.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏所读的宇宙心灵之书里有许多扭曲，但整体而言，其中提出的观念非常有益。我再怎么强调也不为过：你的信念会形成一种心灵气候，这种气候本身会吸引并形成那些信念的物质化。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: Those memories will be used to reach any conclusion, as statistics can be used, for example. Along the way you may settle for a given remembered event or two, and assign to them the reasons for your present behavior. If so, you are already prepared to change your current beliefs and mode of action, and simply use the occurrences or habits of the past as a stimulus or motivation. (See the 616th session in Chapter Two.)
quotes.translation: 那些记忆会被用来得出任何结论，就像统计数据可以被利用一样。在此过程中，你可能会选定一两个特定的记忆事件，并将它们视为你当前行为的原因。如果是这样，你其实已经准备好改变你当前的信念和行为模式，只是将过去的经历或习惯用作刺激或动机。（参见第二章第616节。）

quotes.text: The church's view of reality was the accepted one. I cannot stress too thoroughly the fact that the beliefs of those times structured individual human living, so that the most private events of personal lives were interpreted to mean thus and so, as were of course the events of nations, plants, and animals. The world's view was a religious one, specified by the church, and its word was truth and fact at the same time.
quotes.translation: 当时教会对实相的看法是公认的。我再怎么强调也不为过：那个时代的信念构建了个人的生活方式，以至于个人生活中最私密的事件都被解释为具有特定含义，国家、植物和动物的事件当然也是如此。当时的世界观是宗教性的，由教会具体规定，其话语同时就是真理和事实。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: (10:13.) Many false beliefs therefore are indiscriminately accepted because you have not examined them. You have given the inner self a faulty picture of reality. Since it is the function of the conscious mind to assess physical experience, it [the inner self] hasn't been able to do its job properly. If the inner portions of the self were supposed to have that responsibility, then you would not need a conscious mind.
quotes.translation: （10点13分）因此，许多虚假信念未经审视便被全盘接受。你向内在自己呈现了扭曲的现实图像。由于意识心智的职责是评估物质经验，它（内在自己）便无法妥善履行其职能。倘若内在自我本应承担此责，你便无需意识心智了。

quotes.text: Now: Man has a far greater leeway. He forms his reality according to his conscious beliefs, even while its basis lies in the deep unconscious nature of the earth in corporeal terms. Man's "I am," [seemingly] apart from nature -- a characteristic necessary for the development of his kind of consciousness -- led him into value judgments, and also necessitated some break with the deep inner certainties of other species.
quotes.translation: 现在：人类拥有远为广阔的自由度。他依据自身有意识的信念构建现实，即便其根基深植于地球物质层面的无意识本质之中。人类那看似超脱自然的"我是"——这种对其意识发展不可或缺的特性——既引导他做出价值判断，也必然导致与其他物种深层内在确定性的某种断裂。

quotes.text: Basically you create your experience through your beliefs about yourself and the nature of reality. Another way to understand this is to realize that you create your experiences through your expectations. Your feeling-tones are your emotional attitudes toward yourself and life in general, and these generally govern the large areas of experience.
quotes.translation: 根本上，你通过关于自我及现实本质的信念创造自身经验。理解此点的另一途径是认识到：你通过期待创造经验。你的感受基调是你对自我及生命整体的情感态度，这些基调通常主导着经验的主要领域。

quotes.text: We will resume dictation ... You will react, therefore, to all the information that you receive according to your conscious beliefs concerning the nature of reality. The deeper portions of the self do not have to take the ego's idea of time into consideration, so these portions of the self also deal with data that would ordinarily escape the ego's perception, perhaps until a certain "point" of ego time was reached.
quotes.translation: 我们将继续口述……因此，你会根据自己对实相本质的有意识信念，对你接收到的所有信息做出反应。自我的更深层部分不必考虑自我对时间的观念，因此自我的这些部分也处理那些通常逃过自我感知的数据，也许直到自我时间的某个特定"点"被达到为止。

quotes.text: To be truly reckless in the sense of Seth's definition -- how daring! I'd say that attaining such a state represents quite an achievement. For most of us, including myself, it means shedding many encrusted and limiting personal beliefs. I do get glimpses of that condition of inner and outer freedom; just enough to understand some of the many practical benefits that can flow from it. I can't think of a better goal.
quotes.translation: 按照赛斯的定义，真正地"不顾一切"——多么大胆！我得说达到这种状态本身就是一项了不起的成就。对我们大多数人（包括我自己）来说，这意味着要摆脱许多根深蒂固且限制性的个人信念。我确实偶尔瞥见那种内在与外在自由的状态；这足以让我理解它能带来的诸多实际益处。我想不出更好的目标了。

quotes.text: It is easy enough to look at those belief structures and shrug your shoulders, wondering at man's distorted views of reality. The entire scientific view of illness, however, is quite as distorted (with amused emphasis). It is as laboriously conceived and interwound with "nonsense." It is about as factual as the "fact" that God sends illness as punishment, or that illness is the unwanted gift of mischievous demons.
quotes.translation: 审视那些信念结构并耸耸肩，对人们扭曲的现实观感到困惑，这很容易。然而，整个科学的疾病观同样扭曲（带着戏谑的强调）。它同样煞费苦心地构想，并与"胡言乱语"交织在一起。它和"上帝以疾病作为惩罚"或"疾病是恶作剧恶魔不想要的礼物"这类"事实"一样缺乏真实性。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: I am speaking generally here, for remember that your individual beliefs, thoughts, and emotions cause your reality, so no person dies ahead of his or her time. The individual chooses the time of death. It is true, however, that many cancers and conditions such as AIDS result because the immunity system has been so tampered with that the body has not been allowed to follow through with its own balancing procedures.
quotes.translation: 我这里说的是普遍情况，因为请记住，你个人的信念、思想和情绪造就了你的实相，所以没有人会提前死亡。个体选择死亡的时间。然而，许多癌症和艾滋病等状况确实是由于免疫系统被严重干扰，导致身体无法完成其自身的平衡程序所致。

quotes.text: In the medical field, as in no other, you are faced directly with the full impact of your beliefs, for doctors are not the healthiest, but the least healthy. They fall prey to the beliefs to which they so heartily subscribe. Their concentration is upon disease, not health.
quotes.translation: 在医学领域，你们比其他任何领域都更直接面对信念的全面冲击——因为医生并非最健康的人群，而是最不健康的人群。他们沦为自身热切信奉的信念的牺牲品，其注意力始终聚焦于疾病而非健康。

quotes.text: (9:25.) The beliefs of both of you find justification in physical life, and only reinforce themselves. The fear of robbers attracts robbers. If you think that men are evil, however, you are often not able to examine that as a belief, but take it as a condition of reality.
quotes.translation: （九点二十五分）你们双方的信念都在物质生活中找到佐证，并不断自我强化。对强盗的恐惧反而会招致强盗。然而当你们认为人性本恶时，往往无法将其视为一种信念来审视，而是将其当作现实的固有属性。

quotes.text: There are purposes not nearly as easy to describe, however, intents of a psychological nature, yearnings toward satisfactions not so easily categorized. Man experiences ambitions, desires, likes and dislikes of a highly emotional nature -- and at the same time he has intellectual beliefs about himself, his feelings, and the world. These are the result of training, for you use your mind as you have been taught.
quotes.translation: 然而，有些目的并不那么容易描述，它们是心理性质的意图，对不易归类满足感的渴望。人类体验着高度情绪化的野心、欲望、好恶——同时，他对自己、自己的感受及世界持有知性信念。这些信念是训练的结果，因为你以被教导的方式运用心智。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: I have often stated that the mind-body relationship is one system. The thoughts are as necessary to the whole system as the body's cells are. Ruburt correctly interpreted an analogy I gave him in which I compared thoughts to individual cells, and belief systems to the physical organs, which are composed of cells. The organs obviously are stationary in the body, though the cells within them die and are reborn.
quotes.translation: 我常言身心关系是一个系统。思想对整个系统而言，与身体细胞同样不可或缺。鲁柏正确诠释了我给他的一个比喻：我将思想比作个体细胞，将信念系统比作由细胞组成的身体器官。器官显然在体内固定不动，尽管其内部的细胞不断死亡与重生。

quotes.text: The freedom will also show in our sessions, and rather obviously, within a very brief time. It is already apparent. To become aware of other individuals who are not within your system will be good for him. It will also help him to understand and appreciate my own unique reality, and this now-growing belief should result in a greater familiarity. He should be able to perceive me to some degree before too long.
quotes.translation: 这种自由也会在我们的课程中显现，而且相当明显，在很短的时间内就会如此。它已经显而易见了。让他意识到你们系统之外的其他个体，对他会有好处。这也会帮助他理解并欣赏我独特的实相，而这一正在成长的信念应能带来更深的熟悉感。他应该能在不久之后，在一定程度上感知到我。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: The experiment that would transform your world would operate upon the basic idea that you create your own reality according to the nature of your beliefs, and that all existence was blessed, and that evil did not exist in it. If these ideas were followed individually and collectively, then the evidence of your physical senses would find no contradiction. They would perceive the world and existence as good.
quotes.translation: 能够改变你世界的实验，将基于这样一个基本理念：你根据自己信念的性质创造自己的实相，一切存在皆受祝福，其中并无邪恶存在。若个人与群体皆遵循这些理念，那么你物质感官的证据将找不到矛盾之处。它们会视世界与存在为善。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: A problem caused by guilt, for example, physically materialized as a malady, is meant to lead you to face and conquer the idea of guilt, the belief in it that you hold in your conscious mind. The body itself is always in a state of becoming. You think of it as reaching a certain peak and then deteriorating, or becoming less. That is because you do not understand it as the expression of your being in flesh.
quotes.translation: 举例来说，由罪恶感引发的问题，在物质层面显化为疾病，其目的是引导你面对并克服罪恶感——即你有意识心智中持有的关于罪恶的信念。身体本身始终处于"形成"的状态。你总认为它会达到某个巅峰后衰退，或逐渐衰败。这是因为你不理解它是你存有在血肉中的表达。

quotes.text: There is another consideration involving medicine; though as I mentioned earlier (in the 624th session from Chapter Five), if you accept Western medical beliefs I am not suggesting that you suddenly forsake all doctors. But naturally and left alone, any chemical upsets in the body will right themselves after the inner problems causing them are worked out through any of a variety of innate healing methods.
quotes.translation: 还有另一个涉及医学的考量——虽然如我先前所述（第五章第624节），若你接受西方医学信念，我并非建议你突然放弃所有医生。但若顺其自然，身体内的任何化学失调，在引发失调的内在问题通过任何天生疗愈方法得到解决后，都会自行恢复平衡。

quotes.text: In your society age has almost been considered a dishonorable state. Beliefs about the dishonor of age often cause people to make the decision--sometimes quite consciously--to bring their own lives to an end before the so-called threshold is reached. Whenever, however, the species needs the accumulated experience of its own older members, that situation is almost instantly reversed and people live longer.
quotes.translation: 在你们的社会里，年龄几乎被视为一种不光彩的状态。关于年龄不光彩的信念，常常导致人们——有时相当有意识地——在所谓的门槛到来之前就决定结束自己的生命。然而，每当物种需要其年长成员积累的经验时，这种情况几乎会立即逆转，人们会活得更久。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: If you suspect that abundance is somehow spiritually dangerous,4 then the king might be captured and punished. All kinds of other events might be involved: groups of people, for example, representing bands of "rampaging" desires. The entire drama would involve the "evolution" of an emotion or belief. In the dream state you set it free and see what will happen to it, how it will develop, where it will go.
quotes.translation: 如果你怀疑丰盛在某种程度上具有灵性危险，那么国王可能会被捕获并受到惩罚。各种其他事件也可能卷入其中：例如，群体可能代表"横冲直撞"的欲望。整出戏剧将涉及某个情感或信念的"演化"。在梦境状态中，你释放它，看看它会如何发展、走向何方。

quotes.text: You will be afraid of any powerful emotion, therefore; frightened of the dimensions of your own actuality, and to a large extent be led to run away from an acceptance of the power and energy of your own being. You will be forced to dilute your own experience. Such beliefs have a strong depressing characteristic that can lead you to shut down powerful feelings by immediately considering them negative.
quotes.translation: 因此你会畏惧任何强烈的情感，害怕自身实相的真实维度，并在很大程度上逃避接纳自己存有的力量与能量。你将被迫稀释自己的体验。这类信念具有强烈的压抑特质，会使你立即将强烈感受判定为负面而加以封存。

quotes.text: Your beliefs about dreams color your memory and interpretation of them, so that at the point of waking, with magnificent psychological duplicity, you often make last-minute adjustments that bring your dreams more in line with your conscious expectations. The sexual symbols usually attached to dream images are highly simplistic, for example. They program you to interpret your dreams in a given manner.
quotes.translation: 你对梦的信念会扭曲对梦的记忆与诠释，以致在苏醒之际，你会以惊人的心理双重性进行最后时刻的调整，使梦境更符合意识层面的期待。例如，通常附加在梦境意象上的性象征符号其实极为简化。它们会引导你以特定方式解读梦境。

quotes.text: There is a belief in illness as being morally wrong, and a countering belief in it as being ennobling, uplifting and spiritually good. These value judgments are extremely important, for they will be reflected in your own experience with any illness or disease.
quotes.translation: 有一种信念认为疾病在道德上是错误的，而另一种相反的信念则认为疾病是高尚的、令人升华的、在灵性上是有益的。这些价值判断极为重要，因为它们会反映在你对任何疾病的个人体验中。

quotes.text: Sometimes such cases are handled within another framework, in which Augustus would be considered possessed by an independent "evil" entity whenever Augustus Two took over. Now again, if Augustus somehow changed his beliefs it is possible that even within that framework some kind of cure would be effected. But at the same time the dangers and difficulties would make such a cure relatively impossible.
quotes.translation: 有时这类案例会在另一种框架下处理——每当奥古斯都二号接管时，奥古斯都就会被视为被某个独立的"邪恶"实体附身。现在再说一次，如果奥古斯都设法改变了他的信念，那么即使在这个框架内，某种疗愈也有可能实现。但同时，其中的危险和困难会使这种疗愈相对不可能。

quotes.text: Now we can do no more with our friend this evening. It is all right. He is all right. One small note. The episode last evening was aggressively disrupting to some extent. Mainly because of the inner attitudes of those involved. Symbolically, you see, and beneath it all, both men showed characteristics that under different circumstances could have led them into violence for the sake of their beliefs.
quotes.translation: 今晚我们与这位朋友能做的就到此为止。没关系。他没事。有一点小提示：昨晚的事件在某种程度上具有攻击性的干扰。主要是因为相关人士的内在态度。你看，在象征意义上，在这一切表象之下，两人都展现出在其它情况下可能为了自己的信念而诉诸暴力的特质。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: This does not mean that those individuals might not come down with another disease instead, but it does mean that the belief in disease is patterned and focused to particular symptoms by such methods. No wonder you need health insurance! Illness is not a foreign agency thrust upon you, but as long as you believe that it is, then you will accept it as such. You will also feel powerless to combat it.
quotes.translation: 这并不意味着这些人不会患上另一种疾病，但它确实意味着，对疾病的信念会通过此类方法被模式化并聚焦于特定症状。难怪你需要健康保险！疾病并非强加于你的外来力量，但只要你相信它是，你就会如此接受它。你也会感到无力与之对抗。

quotes.text: It does no good in such a case to bemoan the situation and say, "I want to understand myself but I'm frightened that I will not like what I find." You yourself must change your beliefs. You must stop believing that the inner self is a dungeon of unsavory repressed emotion. It does contain some repressed emotion. It also contains great intuition, knowledge, and the answers to all of your questions.
quotes.translation: 在这种情况下，哀叹处境并说“我想了解自己，但我害怕发现不喜欢的东西”是毫无益处的。你必须自己改变信念。你必须停止相信内在自我是一个充满令人不快的压抑情绪的地牢。它确实包含一些压抑的情绪，但它也包含伟大的直觉、知识，以及你所有问题的答案。

quotes.text: Your systems of belief will of course attract certain kinds of thoughts, with their trails of emotional experience. A steady barrage of hateful, revengeful thoughts should actually lead you to look for the beliefs from which they are gaining their strength.
quotes.translation: 你的信念体系自然会吸引特定种类的思想，并伴随其情感经验的轨迹。持续不断的憎恨与报复念头，实际上应引导你去寻找赋予它们力量的信念根源。

quotes.text: Therefore they are accepted without question too often. They are not recognized as beliefs about reality, but are instead considered characteristics of reality itself. (Pause.) Frequently such ideas appear indisputable, so a part of you that it does not occur to you to speculate about their validity. They become invisible assumptions, but they nevertheless color and form your personal experience.
quotes.translation: 因此它们往往被不加质疑地接受。这些信念不被视为对实相的看法，反而被当作实相本身的特质。（停顿）这类想法常显得无可辩驳，以至于你根本不会去思考其有效性。它们成为隐形的假设，却依然着色并塑造你的个人经验。

quotes.text: Usually they are not analyzed according to your [own] current beliefs. You have been taught to interpret them along the lines of very ritualized procedures. You are told, for instance, that certain objects or images in your dreams have a definite meaning -- not necessarily your own, but following whatever psychological, mystical or religious school of thought in which you happen to be interested.
quotes.translation: 通常它们不会根据你[自己]当前的信念被分析。你被教导要按照高度仪式化的程序来解读它们。例如，你被告知梦中某些物体或意象具有特定含义——未必是你自己的含义，而是遵循你恰好感兴趣的任一心理学、神秘学或宗教思想体系。

quotes.text: In your present system of beliefs, and with the dubious light in which the unconscious is considered, a fear of the emotions is often generated. Not only are they often hindered in waking life, then, but censored as much as possible in dreams. Their expression becomes very difficult; great blockages of energy occur, which in your terms can result in neurotic or even stronger, psychotic, behavior.
quotes.translation: 在你们当前的信念体系里，加上对无意识所持的暧昧看法，往往会产生对情绪的恐惧。因此情绪不仅在清醒生活中常受阻碍，在梦境中也尽可能被审查。它们的表达变得极为困难，导致大量能量阻塞，用你们的话说，可能引发神经症甚至更严重的精神病态行为。

quotes.text: (11:06.) They may still be perceiving reality in terms of their old beliefs. Almost all communications come from this level, particularly when there is a bond of relationship in an immediately previous life. Even at this level, however, such messages serve a purpose. The communicators can inform living relatives that existence continues and they can do it in terms that the living can understand.
quotes.translation: （11:06。）他们可能仍以旧有信念来感知实相。几乎所有沟通都来自这个层面，尤其当存在紧接前世的关联纽带时。然而即使在这个层面，这类讯息仍有其目的。沟通者能告知在世亲属存在仍在延续，并且能用在世者能理解的方式传达。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: From all the available physical data of newspapers, television, letters and private communication, he or she will concentrate only upon those issues that "prove" that point. Suspicion of others will grow, to say nothing about the individual's personal distrust. The belief will reach into the most intimate areas of his or her life, and finally no evidence will seem to be available to disprove it.
quotes.translation: 从所有可得的报纸、电视、信件及私人通讯等物质资料中，他或她会只专注于那些能"证明"该观点的议题。对他人的怀疑会与日俱增，更遑论个人对自身的不信任。这种信念将渗透到他或她生命中最私密的领域，最终似乎再也找不到任何证据能够推翻它。

quotes.text: They can relate to the living, since often their beliefs are still the same; in fortunate circumstances they can communicate their knowledge as they learn. Gradually, however, their own interests change. They take up relationships in their new existence.
quotes.translation: 他们能与生者建立联系，因为他们的信念往往仍然相同；在幸运的情况下，他们能在学习过程中传递知识。然而，他们的兴趣会逐渐改变。他们在新的存在状态中建立关系。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: If you use your conscious mind properly, then, you examine those beliefs that come to you. You do not accept them willy-nilly. If you use your conscious mind properly, you are also aware of intuitive ideas that come to you from within. You are only half conscious when you do not examine the information that comes to you from without, and when you ignore the data that comes to you from within.
quotes.translation: 如果你正确运用意识心，就会审视那些来到你面前的信念。你不会不加分辨地全盘接受。如果你正确运用意识心，也会觉察到来自内在的直觉想法。当你不审视来自外在的信息，又忽视来自内在的资料时，你只处于半清醒状态。

quotes.text: Clear understanding or effective exploration of the unknown reality can be achieved only when you are able to leave behind you many "facts" that you have accepted as criteria of experience. "Unknown" Reality is also written in such a way that it will, I hope, bring many of your cherished beliefs about existence into question. Then you will be able to look even at this existence with new eyes.
quotes.translation: 只有当你能够放下许多你视为经验标准的"事实"时，才能清晰理解或有效探索未知实相。"未知"实相之所以如此书写，也是希望它能让你质疑许多珍视的存在信念。届时你便能以全新眼光审视这存在本身。

quotes.text: The framework of sex is another natural therapeutic system if you have not already hampered its effectiveness by contrary beliefs. Natural "mystical" experience, unclothed in dogma, is the original religious therapy that is so often distorted in ecclesiastical organizations, but it represents man's innate recognition of his oneness with the source of his own being, and of his own experience.
quotes.translation: 性别的框架是另一种天然疗愈系统——前提是你尚未用相悖的信念阻碍其效力。剥离教条外衣的天然"神秘"体验，是原始宗教疗法，虽常在教会组织中被扭曲，却代表着人类与自身存在本源及经验本源合一的天生认知。

quotes.text: Your beliefs automatically attract the appropriate emotions. They reinforce themselves through imagination; and at the risk of repeating myself, because this is so important: Imagination and feeling follow your beliefs. It is not the other way around.
quotes.translation: 你的信念会自动吸引相应的情绪。它们通过想象力强化自身——容我重复这个至关重要的观点：想象力与感受追随你的信念，而非相反。

quotes.text: The unharmonious sounds have become a part of that portion of the body as a result of the inner sound of your own thought-beliefs. That is why it is vital that you not reinforce these inner sounds through repeating the same negative suggestions to yourself. Verbal suggestions are translated into inner sound. This passes through your body in somewhat the same way that some kinds of light do.
quotes.translation: 不和谐的声音已成为身体那部分的一部分，源于你自己思想信念的内在声音。这就是为什么你绝不能通过对自己重复同样的负面暗示来强化这些内在声音，这一点至关重要。言语暗示被转化为内在声音。它通过你的身体，其方式与某些光线通过的方式大致相同。

quotes.text: All of this is also connected with your beliefs about the waking and dreaming states, white being acquainted with the day, and black with the dreaming condition. Here again is the old connection between the God of Light and the Prince of Darkness, or Satan -- all distinctions made at various levels of development, and having to do with the nature of the origin of the present consciousness.
quotes.translation: 所有这些也与你对清醒和做梦状态的信念有关，白色与白天相联，黑色与做梦状态相联。这里再次出现了光之神与黑暗王子或撒旦之间的古老联系——所有这些都是在不同发展层次上作出的区分，并且与当前意识的起源本质有关。

quotes.text: (11:10.) You are doing the same thing now constantly and automatically with whatever beliefs you have, and they are being as constantly and automatically translated. It is the separation of self from beliefs that is so important initially, however.
quotes.translation: （11:10。）你现在正持续且自动地对所持有的任何信念做着同样的事情，而这些信念也同样持续且自动地被转化。然而，最初将自我与信念分离开来是至关重要的。

quotes.text: (11:05.) Give us a moment ... (Pause.) You build smooth structures of beliefs, then look at reality using the beliefs like glasses -- tinted ones. Period. Opposing information will literally be invisible to you.12 It will be ignored or cast aside.
quotes.translation: （11:05）稍等片刻……（停顿）你们构建起光滑的信念结构，然后像戴上有色眼镜般透过这些信念审视现实。句号。相反的信息对你们而言将完全隐形，会被忽视或丢弃。

quotes.text: You will not usually form a creation of it of which you are proud. If you believe firmly in poor health you may use this repressed energy to attack a physical organ -- a gall bladder may become "bad." According to your own belief system, you may trust the integrity of your body and instead project this guilt out upon others -- onto a personal enemy, or a particular race, creed or color.
quotes.translation: 你们通常不会创造出令自己自豪的实相。若你坚信健康不佳，便会用这股被压抑的能量攻击某个身体器官——胆囊可能变得"不好"。根据你自己的信念系统，你或许信任身体的完整性，反而将这份罪疚投射到他人身上——某个私人仇敌，或特定的种族、教条或肤色。

quotes.text: Music is an exterior representation, and an excellent one, of the life-giving inner sounds that act therapeutically within your body all the time. (See Chapter Five.) The music is a conscious reminder of those deeper inner rhythms, both of sound and of motion. Listening to music that you like will often bring images into your mind that show you your conscious beliefs in different form.
quotes.translation: 音乐是内在声音的外在表现，且是极佳的表现形式，这些赋予生命的内在声音始终在你们体内发挥治疗作用（见第五章）。音乐是有意识地提醒那些更深层的内在节奏，包括声音和运动的节奏。聆听你喜欢的音乐，常常会将意象带入你的脑海，这些意象以不同形式向你展示你有意识的信念。

quotes.text: (11:44.) The features in a portrait are painted on canvas or board, but your soul is not painted on your body. It enters into and becomes part of it. Physically, you cannot contain all of your identity, and that "free" portion unconsciously creates the flesh, in your terms. Again, you direct its form through your beliefs, but the unconscious part of you does the "work" of producing it.
quotes.translation: （11:44分）肖像画的特征被描绘在画布或木板上，但你的灵魂并非被描绘在身体上。它进入身体并成为身体的一部分。从物质层面来说，你无法容纳你的全部身份，而那个"自由"的部分，用你们的话说，无意识地创造了肉体。再次强调，你通过信念引导肉体的形态，但你无意识的部分完成了产生它的"工作"。

quotes.text: The old beliefs, of course, and the rational approach, are everywhere reinforced, and so it does indeed have a great weight. The magical approach has far greater weight, if you use it and allow yourselves to operate in that fashion, for it has the weight of your basic natural orientation. The rational approach is the superimposed one. I think that you are both ready to understand that.
quotes.translation: 旧有的信念与理性取向自然处处受到强化，因此确实具有极大的分量。但若你运用魔法取向并允许自己以那种方式运作，它便拥有远为强大的力量，因为它承载着你基本自然倾向的分量。理性取向则是后天叠加的。我认为你们俩都已准备好理解这一点。

quotes.text: The people who died were idealists -- perfectionists of exaggerated quality, whose very desire for the good was tainted and distorted by those beliefs just mentioned. For those beliefs must gradually shut out perception of good from experience.3
quotes.translation: 那些死去的人是理想主义者——具有夸张特质的完美主义者，他们对善的渴望恰恰被上述信念所玷污和扭曲。因为这些信念必然逐渐将善的感知从经验中排除。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Dictation: Natural hypnosis is the acquiescence of the unconscious to conscious belief. In periods of concentrated focus, with all distractions cut out, the desired ideas are then implanted (in formal hypnosis). The same processes occur in normal life, however; areas of primary concentration then regulate your experience both biologically and mentally, and generate similar conditions.
quotes.translation: 口授：自然催眠是无意识对意识信念的默许。在高度专注的状态下，排除所有干扰后，期望的意念便被植入（在正式催眠中）。然而，同样的过程也发生在日常生活中；主要的专注领域会在生物层面与心智层面调节你的经验，并产生类似的条件。

quotes.text: (9:45.) It is far simpler to recognize your own beliefs in regard to religion, politics or similar subjects, than it is to pinpoint your deepest beliefs about yourself and who and what you are -- particularly in relationship with your own life.
quotes.translation: （9:45）要辨认自己在宗教、政治或类似议题上的信念，远比精准定位那些关于自身本质与存在的最深层信念来得简单——尤其是当这些信念与你的人生息息相关时。

quotes.text: (9:32.) Each cell (pause) believes in a better tomorrow (quietly, with amusement). I am, I admit, personifying our cell here, but the statement has a firm truth. Furthermore, each cell contains within itself a belief and an understanding of its own inevitability. It knows it lives beyond its death, in other words.
quotes.translation: （9:32）每个细胞（停顿）都相信更美好的明天（轻声带笑意）。我承认，这里是在拟人化描述细胞，但这句话蕴含着坚实的真理。此外，每个细胞内在都存有对自身必然性的信念与认知——换言之，它知晓自己超越死亡而存在。

quotes.text: If this information becomes available in the dream state you may then say, "I am frightened of dreams. My bad dreams so often come true." So you try to inhibit memory of your dreams. Instead you should examine your conscious beliefs, for they are so strong that they are causing you not only to focus upon calamity in the physical world, but to use your inner abilities to the same end.
quotes.translation: 若这些信息在梦境中显现，你可能会说："我害怕做梦。我的噩梦总是成真。"于是你试图抑制对梦境的记忆。但你应该检视自己的意识信念，因为它们如此强大，不仅让你聚焦于物质世界的灾祸，更驱使你运用内在能力达成同样的结局。

quotes.text: You perform habitually in certain manners as a result of your beliefs. Now if you willfully change some of those habits then you are also getting the message across. The initiative must come from you, and in the present. In a very real manner of speaking, this means changing your viewpoint, that particular perspective with which you view your past and present and imagine your future.
quotes.translation: 你因应自己的信念而习惯性地以特定方式行事。现在若你刻意改变其中某些习惯，你同样在传递这个讯息。主动权必须来自你，且就在当下。从非常实际的角度来说，这意味着改变你的观点——那个你用来审视过去与现在、并想象未来的特定视角。

quotes.text: In all cases, little effort is made to understand the basic problems beneath, and the social segregations merely build up the pressure, so to speak, so that those with like beliefs are kept in situations that only perpetuate the basic causes.
quotes.translation: 在所有情况下，人们几乎不费心去理解底层的根本问题，而社会隔离反而加剧了压力，可以说，这使得持有相似信念的人被困在只会延续根本原因的情境中。

quotes.text: (9:35.) However, there is no great reason for the vast separation that now exists between your waking and sleeping lives. As I mentioned earlier (in the 652nd session in Chapter Thirteen, for instance), the division is largely the result of your mass and private beliefs in the nature of reality, and in the habits the race has acquired of separating "objective" data from subjective.
quotes.translation: （9点35分）然而，你们清醒与睡眠生活之间目前存在的巨大分裂并无充分理由。正如我先前（例如第十三章第652节）提到的，这种分裂主要源于你们对实相本质的大众与私人信念，以及人类种族习于将"客观"数据与主观数据分离的习惯。

quotes.text: I speak to you from the known and unknown desire that gives you your own birth, and that speaks to you from the tiniest, least-acknowledged thought that flies like a pigeon within your skull ... And in this moment of your reality, and in the desire of your being, do you even create All That Is. Bow down before no man, no woman, and no belief -- but know you are indeed the creators.
quotes.translation: 我从那赋予你自身存在的已知与未知渴望中对你说话，从你颅骨内如鸽子般飞掠的最微小、最不被承认的思想中对你说话……而在此刻的实相中，在你存在的渴望中，你甚至创造了万有。不要向任何男人、女人或信念低头——但要明白你确实是创造者。

quotes.text: All of your physical, mental and spiritual abilities are focused together, then, in the brilliant concentration of "present" experience. You are not at the mercy of the past, or of previous convictions, unless you believe that you are. If you fully comprehend your power in the present, you will realize that action at that point also alters the past, its beliefs and your reactions.
quotes.translation: 你所有身体、心智与灵性能力，都聚焦于"当下"经验的璀璨凝聚之中。你并非受制于过去或先前的信念，除非你相信自己受制于它们。若你全然领悟自己在当下的力量，便会明白在那个节点的行动同样能改变过去、其信念及你的反应。

quotes.text: The basic beliefs however were always in your conscious mind, and the reasons for your behavior. You simply had not examined its contents with the realization that your beliefs were not necessarily reality, but often your conceptions of it.
quotes.translation: 然而，基本信念始终存在于你的意识心智中，也是你行为的缘由。你只是未曾审视其内容，并认识到你的信念未必是实相，而往往只是你对实相的概念。

quotes.text: It is extremely difficult to work with yourself in the natural manner when you are surrounded everywhere by the belief that certain drugs, or foods, or doctors will provide the answers. So, in the barrage of mass ideas to the opposite, those who try to allow themselves the benefit of their own innate healing must usually face the stress of wondering whether or not they are right.
quotes.translation: 当你周遭充斥着某种药物、食物或医生能提供答案的信念时，以自然的方式与自己合作变得极为困难。因此，在铺天盖地的相反大众观念中，那些试图让自己受益于内在疗愈能力的人，通常必须面对质疑自己是否正确所带来的压力。

quotes.text: Certain beliefs then will structure your lives, often for given periods. You will grow out of many of them. When you do, the inner structuring will change, but you must not cravenly acquiesce to "leftover" beliefs once you recognize them.
quotes.translation: 某些信念随后会构建你的生活，往往持续特定时期。你会逐渐超越其中许多信念。当你做到时，内在结构便会改变，但一旦你识别出"残余"信念，就绝不能怯懦地默许它们。

quotes.text: In the larger context you realize that the doctor can at best give you temporary relief, yet you may not be completely convinced as yet of your own ability to change your thoughts; or you may be so cowed by their effectiveness that you are frightened. So there is a period of stress in between beliefs, so to speak, while you dispense with one set and are learning to use another.
quotes.translation: 在更大的脉络中，你明白医生最多只能给你暂时的缓解，然而你可能尚未完全确信自己有能力改变思想；或者你被其效力震慑到心生恐惧。因此，可以说在信念转换的过渡期存在一段压力时期——当你摒弃旧有信念，同时学习运用新信念时。

quotes.text: This in many instances will be distasteful to many, but all physical constructions are transformations of energy made manifest as idea, and then constructed into physical reality. Without the idea you have no physical reality, and without belief that our aims are possible, there will be no achievement. The achievement gained, however, will then provide its own physical proofs.
quotes.translation: 这对许多人而言可能难以接受，但所有物质建构都是能量的转化——先显化为理念，再构筑成物质实相。没有理念就没有物质实相，若不相信我们的目标可能实现，便不会有成就。然而，当成就达成时，它自会提供其物质性的证明。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: To be healthy you must believe in health. A good physician is a changer of beliefs. He will replace an idea of illness with one of health. Whatever methods or drugs he uses will not be effective unless this change of belief takes place.
quotes.translation: 要健康，你必须相信健康。优秀的医师是信念的转换者。他会用健康的观念取代疾病的观念。除非这种信念转换发生，否则无论他使用何种方法或药物都不会有效。

quotes.text: As a physical being, your beliefs and concepts form your reality. The psyche from which your identity springs is free of the picture of reality that you have chosen. Period. You choose, in other words, to accept a given picture of the world, and you use that picture as a frame within which you form a life.
quotes.translation: 作为物质体，你的信念与概念形成了你的实相。你身份所源出的心灵，并不受你所选择的实相画面所束缚。句号。换言之，你选择接受某个特定的世界画面，并用那画面作为框架，在其中塑造人生。

quotes.text: (10:31.) Now here is another example. Your conscious thoughts regulate your health. The persistent idea of illness will make you ill. While you believe that you become ill because of viruses, infections or accidents, then you must go to doctors who operate within that system of belief. And because you believe in their cures, hopefully you will be relieved of your difficulty.
quotes.translation: （10点31分。）现在再举一个例子。你的有意识念头调节着你的健康。持续抱持疾病的念头会让你生病。当你相信你是因病毒、感染或意外而生病时，你就必须去找那些在该信念体系内行医的医生。而因为你相信他们的疗法，但愿你能摆脱困境。

quotes.text: Quite without any inductions, you have "hypnotized" yourself into all the beliefs that you have. This simply means that you have consciously accepted them, focused upon them, excluded data to the contrary, narrowed your interests to those specific points, and accordingly activated the unconscious mechanisms that then materialize those convictions through physical experience.
quotes.translation: 完全无需任何诱导，你已将自己"催眠"进你所持有的所有信念。这仅仅意味着你有意识地接受了它们，聚焦于它们，排除了相反的信息，将兴趣缩小到那些特定点上，并因此激活了那些随后通过物质经验将那些信念具体化的无意识机制。

quotes.text: You think of viruses as physical, and of thoughts as mental. You should know that thoughts also have their physical aspects in the body, and that viruses have their mental aspects in the body. At times you have both asked why an ailing body does not simply assert itself and use its healing abilities, throwing off the negative influence of a given set of beliefs and thoughts.
quotes.translation: 你认为病毒是物质的，而念头是精神的。你应该知道，念头在身体中也有其物质面向，而病毒在身体中也有其精神面向。有时你们两人都曾问过，为什么一个生病的身体不干脆坚持己见，运用其疗愈能力，摆脱特定一组信念和念头的负面影响。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Your world and everything in it exists first in the imagination, then. You have been taught to focus all of your attention upon physical events, so that they carry the authenticity of reality for you. Thoughts, feelings, or beliefs appear to be secondary, subjective -- or somehow not real -- and they seem to rise in response to an already established field of physical data.
quotes.translation: 你的世界及其中的一切，首先存在于想象之中。你被教导要将全部注意力聚焦于物质事件，以至于它们对你而言承载着现实的真实性。思想、感受或信念显得次要、主观——或某种程度上的不真实——它们似乎是对已确立的物质事实场域的反应。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: The ego and the outer senses reinforce the belief in a closed system, and therefore close it. The inner senses, when the physical body is relaxed, will carry you through the imaginary boundaries, but a conscious focus upon the boundaries to be passed through will tend to reinforce them. Concentrate upon the goal rather than the means of attaining it, and you will attain it.
quotes.translation: 自我与外在感官强化了封闭系统的信念，从而将其封闭。当身体放松时，内在感官会带你穿越那些想象的边界，但若意识聚焦于需要穿越的边界，反而会强化它们。专注于目标而非达成目标的方法，你便能抵达。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: Obviously there are many ramifications here, and in your society your own belief systems must also be taken into consideration. If you do not believe in the natural healing processes you will simply block them. Your fear of not seeing a doctor then will only cause more damage. On the other hand, if you have faith in medical help, this alone will bring therapeutic benefit.
quotes.translation: 显然这里有许多衍生情况，而在你们的社会中，你们自身的信念体系也必须纳入考量。若你不相信自然疗愈过程，就会直接阻碍它们。你对不看医生的恐惧只会造成更多伤害。另一方面，若你对医疗帮助怀有信心，仅此一点就能带来治疗效果。

quotes.text: Once you understand this you have only to learn to examine the nature of your beliefs, for these will automatically cause you to feel and think in certain fashions. Your emotions follow your beliefs. It is not the other way around.
quotes.translation: 一旦你理解这一点，只需学会检视你信念的本质，因为这些信念会自动导致你以特定方式感受和思考。你的情绪追随你的信念，而非相反。

quotes.text: The present medical profession is sadly hampered because of its own beliefs. Often it operates as a framework in which poor health and disease are not only accepted as normal, but the concepts behind them strengthened. Here you have again, as in psychoanalysis, a hide-and-seek arrangement in which both doctor and patient take part. (See the 616th session in Chapter Two.)
quotes.translation: 可悲的是，当代医学界因其自身信念而备受束缚。它常运作于一个框架中，在此框架内不仅将健康不佳与疾病视为常态，更强化了其背后的概念。如同精神分析学一样，这里再次出现医生与病患共同参与的捉迷藏游戏。（见第二章第616节）

quotes.text: Your beliefs about sexuality, and hence your experience with it, makes you consider it in a very limiting light. The psyche's own knowledge, of course, is far more expansive. Alterations of consciousness, or attempts on the part of the individual to explore the inner self, may then easily display glimpses of a kind of sexuality that can appear to be deviant or unnatural.
quotes.translation: 你们对性的信念——以及由此产生的性体验——使你们以极其局限的视角看待它。而心灵自身的认知显然要广阔得多。当个体尝试探索内在自我或改变意识状态时，很容易瞥见某种看似异常或非自然的性特质。

quotes.text: This happens very frequently when you consciously set the problem before yourself, state it clearly, and then drift into sleep. The same thing happens, however, even without such a conscious set. Dreams give you all kinds of information concerning the state of your body, the world at large, and the probable exterior conditions that your present beliefs will bring about.
quotes.translation: 当你清醒地将问题置于面前、清晰陈述后沉入睡眠时，这种情况经常发生。但即便没有这种有意识的设定，同样的事情也会发生。梦境会提供关于你身体状态、宏观世界，以及当前信念将引发的可能外在状况等各类信息。

quotes.text: Because you accept the rain as a present reality does not mean, either, that you must believe that all days are stormy, and make that obvious misconception a part of your beliefs about reality. So you do not have to pretend that a "dark" thought doesn't exist. You do not have to take it as fact that all of your thoughts would be murky, left alone, and try to hide them.
quotes.translation: 你接受雨是当下的实相，并不意味着你必须相信所有日子都是暴风雨的，并将那个明显的误解纳入你对实相的信念中。所以你不必假装一个"黑暗"的想法不存在。你不必认为你所有的思想若不加干预都会变得阴郁，并试图隐藏它们。

quotes.text: You may believe that wealth is a result of a moral virtue, and comes from "God's" direct benevolence. As a result, poverty becomes evidence of a lack of morality. "God" made so many people poor that obviously no man should dare try to change the situation -- that rationale is often used. The poor, then, following these beliefs, are looked down upon as are the diseased.
quotes.translation: 你可能相信财富是道德美德的结果，来自"上帝"的直接恩惠。结果，贫穷就成了缺乏道德的证明。"上帝"让这么多人贫穷，显然没有人应该试图改变这种状况——这种理由常被使用。于是，遵循这些信念，穷人和病人一样被瞧不起。

quotes.text: Cellular memory can be changed at any point. Present beliefs can insert into the past new memory, both psychologically and physically. The future is in no way predetermined on basic levels. This does not mean that the future cannot be predicted sometimes, for in practical terms you will often continue with certain lines of probability which can be seen "ahead of time."
quotes.translation: 细胞记忆可在任何时刻改变。当下的信念能插入过去的新记忆，无论是心理层面还是生理层面。未来在基本层面上绝非预先注定。这并非意味着未来有时无法预测，因为在实际层面，你常会延续某些可被"提前"看见的或然性路线。

quotes.text: I speak to you of other theoretical realities. I challenge you now to be as creative in another reality as you are in this one. And if it seems to you, because of your beliefs, that you are limited here, then I joyfully challenge each of you to create a city, an environment, and perhaps a world, in which no such limitations occur. What kind of world would you create?
quotes.translation: 我跟你们谈到其他理论性的实相。现在我挑战你们，在另一个实相中像在这个实相中一样具有创造力。如果因为你们的信念，你们觉得自己在此受限，那么我欣然挑战你们每个人，去创造一个城市、一个环境，或许一个世界，在那里没有这样的限制。你们会创造什么样的世界呢？

quotes.text: The kinds of diseases change through historical periods. Some become fashionable, others go out of style. All epidemics, however, are mass statements both biologically and psychically. They point to mass beliefs that have brought about certain physical conditions that are abhorrent at all levels. They often go hand-in-hand with war, and represent biological protests.
quotes.translation: 疾病的种类会随着历史时期而改变。有些变得流行，有些则过时了。然而，所有流行病在生物和心灵层面都是集体性的声明。它们指向那些带来了在各方面都令人厌恶的特定身体状态的集体信念。这些流行病常常与战争相伴，代表着生物性的抗议。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: As the mind developed, the species could hand down to its offspring the wisdom and law of the elders. This is still being done in modern society, of course, when each child inherits the beliefs of its parents about the nature of reality. Apart from all other considerations, this is also a characteristic of creaturehood. Only the means are different with the animals.
quotes.translation: 随着心智的发展，这个物种能够将长者的智慧和法则传给后代。当然，在现代社会中，当每个孩子继承父母关于实相本质的信念时，这种情况仍在发生。撇开所有其他考虑不谈，这也是生物性的一个特征。只是动物使用的方法不同。

quotes.text: Your environment is the physical picture of your thoughts, emotions and beliefs made visible. Since your thoughts, emotions and beliefs move through space and time, you therefore affect physical conditions separate from you.
quotes.translation: 你的环境是你思想、情感和信念的物质化画面。由于你的思想、情感和信念穿越时空，因此你会影响与你分离的物质状况。

quotes.text: Right now I'd bet that man will most certainly try with all of his might every technique he can devise in order to prolong physical life as long as possible -- so great is his conscious fear of death as the consummate extinction for all time to come. Through all of his recorded history, man has created that fear, that belief, with the greatest tenacity imaginable.
quotes.translation: 此刻我敢打赌，这个人必定会竭尽全力尝试他能想出的每一种技巧，尽可能延长肉体生命——因为他有意识地恐惧死亡，视其为永恒的彻底灭绝。纵观人类有记载的历史，人类以所能想象的最大韧性创造了这种恐惧、这种信念。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: The physician is also caught between his religious beliefs and his scientific beliefs. Sometimes these conflict, and sometimes they only serve to deepen his feelings that the body, left alone, will get any disease possible.
quotes.translation: 医生同样被困于其宗教信仰与科学信念之间。有时两者相互冲突，有时它们只会加深他的感受——认为身体若任其自便，便会染上任何可能的疾病。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: In his normal state he accepted only the beliefs he considered were expected of him. As mentioned (in the 628th session in Chapter Six), there was a time before his condition developed when his "good-self thoughts" and his "bad-self thoughts" vied for his attention, and the body tried desperately to react to constant, alternating and often contradictory concepts.
quotes.translation: 在正常状态下，他只接受那些他认为别人期待他持有的信念。如（第六章第628节）所述，在他病情发展之前曾有一段时期，他的"善我之念"与"恶我之念"竞相争夺他的注意力，而身体则拼命应对持续交替、往往相互矛盾的概念。

quotes.text: You are paying in advance for illness that you are certain will come your way. You are making all preparations in the present for a future of illness. You are betting upon disease and not health. This is the worst kind of natural hypnosis, and yet within your system insurance is indeed a necessity, because the belief in illness so pervades your mental atmosphere.
quotes.translation: 你正在为确信会降临的疾病预先付出代价。你在当下为未来的疾病做着一切准备。你赌的是疾病而非健康。这是最糟糕的自然催眠，然而在你的系统内，保险确实是必要的，因为对疾病的信念如此弥漫于你的心灵氛围中。

quotes.text: (11:32.) Illnesses usually represent unfaced problems, in your terms, and these dilemmas embody challenges meant to lead you to greater achievement and fulfillment. Because body and mind operate so well together, one will attempt to cure the other, and will often succeed if left alone. The organism has its own beliefs in health that are unconscious on your part.
quotes.translation: （11:32）疾病通常代表着未面对的问题，用你们的话说，这些困境蕴含着挑战，旨在引导你走向更大的成就与圆满。由于身体和心灵配合得如此完美，一方会试图疗愈另一方，若不加干预往往能成功。有机体自身拥有你们意识层面下对健康的信念。

quotes.text: I will suggest many exercises throughout this book. Some of them will necessitate variations of normal consciousness. I may ask you to forget physical stimuli, or suggest that you amplify them, but I am nowhere stating that your mode of consciousness is wrong. It is limited, not by nature, but by your own beliefs and practice. You have not carried it far enough.
quotes.translation: 我将在本书中建议许多练习。其中一些需要改变正常意识状态。我可能会要求你忘记物理刺激，或建议你放大它们，但我从未说过你的意识模式是错误的。它受到限制，并非源于本性，而是源于你自己的信念与练习。你尚未将其发挥到足够深远。

quotes.text: The truth is then that you form your reality directly. You react consciously and unconsciously to your beliefs. You collect from the physical universe, and the interior one, data that seems to correlate with your beliefs.
quotes.translation: 事实是，你直接形成了你的实相。你有意识或无意识地对你所相信的信念做出反应。你从物质宇宙和内在宇宙中收集那些似乎与你的信念相符的数据。

quotes.text: While your present conscious beliefs dictate your current experience, and while your physical body wears its solidity only in present time to your senses, beneath this both the ever-changing elements of your body and your consciousness are relatively free in time. They exist in a multidimensionality with which rational consciousness is not yet equipped to deal.
quotes.translation: 虽然你当前有意识的信念支配着你当下的经验，虽然你的肉体只在当下时刻对你的感官显现其坚实性，但在这一切之下，你身体不断变化的元素和你的意识在时间中相对自由。它们存在于一种理性意识尚未能处理的多次元性之中。

quotes.text: Vitamins, better food, medical attention, may temporarily rejuvenate the body, but unless you change your beliefs it will quickly become swamped again by your feelings of depression. In such a case you must realize that you make your own loneliness, and resolve to change through both thought and action. Action is thought in physical motion, outwardly perceived.
quotes.translation: 维生素、更好的食物、医疗照顾，或许能暂时恢复身体的活力，但除非你改变信念，否则它很快又会再度被你的沮丧感淹没。在这种情况下，你必须明白是你自己制造了孤独，并决心透过思想与行动来改变。行动是思想在物质中的运动，是能被外在感知的。

quotes.text: In any case, in your private life you may hardly ever encounter your belief in your own unworth, or evil. You will not realize that you actually consider yourself the enemy. You will be so convinced that your projection (onto others) is the enemy that there will be no slack to take up, for all of your feelings of self-hate or self-fear will be directed outward.
quotes.translation: 无论如何，在你的私人生活中，你几乎永远不会遇到自己无价值或邪恶的信念。你不会意识到你实际上把自己视为敌人。你会如此确信你的投射（到他人身上）才是敌人，以至于没有任何余地可以调整，因为你所有的自我憎恨或自我恐惧感都被导向了外在。

quotes.text: A core belief is invisible only when you think of it as a fact of life, and not as a belief about life; only when you identify with it so completely that you automatically focus your perceptions along that specific line.
quotes.translation: 核心信念之所以隐形，只因你视其为生命的事实，而非关于生命的信念；只因你与之完全认同，以致自动将感知聚焦于那条特定路径。

quotes.text: Because like ideas do attract like, both electromagnetically and emotionally, the conscious mind found itself with two complete contradictory systems of belief, and two self-images. (Pause.) To protect the integrity of the physical structure, Augustus's conscious mind neatly divided itself up. No longer were the minute-to-minute messages to the body scrambled.
quotes.translation: 由于相似理念会相互吸引——无论电磁层面还是情感层面——意识心智发现自己同时拥有两套完全矛盾的信念体系与两种自我形象。（停顿）为维护肉体结构的完整性，奥古斯都的意识心智巧妙地将自身一分为二。从此，分秒间传递给身体的讯息不再混乱。

quotes.text: You with your conscious mind are to discriminate among those thoughts as to which ones you want to form into your system of beliefs (intently), but in so doing you are not to pretend blindness. You may at times wish that a rainy day were a sunny one, but you do not stand at the window and deny that the rain is falling, or that the air is cold and the sky dark.
quotes.translation: 你需运用意识心智，在诸多思绪中辨别哪些该纳入你的信念体系（专注地），但在此过程中不可佯装盲目。你或许偶尔希望雨天放晴，却不会站在窗前否认雨正落下、空气寒冷、天色阴沉。

quotes.text: To some degree, such inner data will be colored by the current beliefs of that part of the self most directly confronting the physical world. Those beliefs, however, are also constantly being examined by the inner self.
quotes.translation: 在某种程度上，这类内在资料会受到直接面对物质世界的那部分自我当前信念的染色。然而，这些信念也持续受到内在自我的检视。

quotes.text: Yet at other levels this seeming solidity of events also breaks down. Which you? Which world? A sudden contemporary belief in illness will actually reach back into the past, affecting the organism at that level, and inserting into the past experience of the cells the initiation of those biological events that will then seem to give birth to a present disease.
quotes.translation: 但在其他层面，事件这种看似坚固的特性也会瓦解。哪个你？哪个世界？一个突然出现的当代疾病信念，实际上会回溯到过去，在那个层面影响有机体，并将那些生物事件的起始点植入细胞的过往经验中——这些事件随后看似催生了当下的疾病。

quotes.text: In some cases, the idea of illness is so strong that they have built their earthly years about this psychological center. They project ill conditions upon the new body as they did upon the old one. They are given various kinds of treatment of a psychic nature, and told that the condition of that body is being brought about by the nature of their own beliefs.
quotes.translation: 在某些案例中，疾病的观念如此强烈，以至于他们围绕这个心理中心构建了尘世岁月。他们将病态状况投射到新身体上，如同过去投射到旧身体上一样。他们接受各种灵性本质的治疗，并被告知该身体的状态是由他们自身信念的本质所导致的。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: You are growing in consciousness; therefore using it expands its capabilities. It is not a thing, but an attribute and characteristic. That is why your understanding and desire are so important. The processes initiated are beyond your normal awareness. They occur automatically with your intent if you do not block them through fear, doubt or opposing beliefs.
quotes.translation: 你正在意识中成长；因此运用意识能扩展其能力。意识并非一个物件，而是一种属性与特质。这就是为何你的理解与渴望如此重要。所启动的过程超越你平常的觉知。若你不以恐惧、怀疑或对立信念加以阻碍，这些过程便会随你的意图自动发生。

quotes.text: Your ideas about sexuality and your beliefs about the nature of the psyche often paint a picture of very contradictory elements. The psyche and its relationship to sexuality affects your ideas of health and illness, creativity, and all of the ordinary areas of individual life. In this chapter, therefore, we will consider some of the implications that result.
quotes.translation: 你对性的观念，以及你对心灵本质的信念，往往描绘出一幅充满矛盾元素的图景。心灵及其与性的关系，会影响你对健康与疾病、创造力，以及个人生活所有寻常领域的看法。因此在本章中，我们将探讨由此产生的若干意涵。

quotes.text: You cannot separate your beliefs about reality from the reality that you experience. That is, your beliefs about reality form it. Your ideas about what is possible and what is not possible are reflected in all areas.
quotes.translation: 你无法将你对实相的看法与你所体验的实相分开。也就是说，你对实相的看法塑造了实相。你对什么是可能的、什么是不可能的想法，会反映在所有领域。

quotes.text: I am myself quite happy, and somewhat amused by our relationship, my dear doctor, for in many areas we are indeed very much alike. I know and I appreciate the fact that you are not a young man. I know indeed that you lean toward a belief in immortality, while at the same time you cannot entirely accept the possibility without some sort of scientific proof.
quotes.translation: 亲爱的医生，我对自己相当满意，甚至对我们之间的关系感到有些好笑，因为在许多方面我们确实非常相似。我知道并感激您已不再年轻。我确实知道您倾向于相信永生，但同时您又无法完全接受这种可能性，除非有某种科学证据。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: There are various kinds and stages in such cases. Each individual is unique. Sometimes the framework includes another method of cure, in which portions of each conflicting side of the personality break off to form a clearer psychological structure which can communicate with the other two, act as a referee, and reconcile the opposing beliefs held by each.
