quotes.translation: 这类情况有各种不同的种类和阶段。每个个体都是独一无二的。有时这个架构包含另一种疗愈方法，其中人格中相互冲突的每一方都会分裂出部分片段，形成更清晰的心理结构，这个结构能与另外两方沟通，充当仲裁者，并调和双方所持的相反信念。

quotes.text: In each person's experience, there are areas with which he or she is pleased. When you find yourself dissatisfied, however, question the orders you are giving in that particular arena of experience. The results do not seem, now, to follow your conscious desires. But you will find that they do follow your conscious beliefs, which may be quite different.
quotes.translation: 在每个人的经验中，都有令其满意的领域。然而，当你感到不满时，请质疑你在那个特定经验领域所下达的指令。现在，结果似乎并未遵循你的有意识欲望。但你会发现，它们确实遵循了你的有意识信念，而这些信念可能大不相同。

quotes.text: The formation of events is initially an emotional, psychic, or psychological function. Events are physical interpretations, conventionalized versions of inner perceptive experiences that are then "coalesced" in space and time. Events are organized according to laws that involve love, belief, intent, and the intensities with which these are entertained.
quotes.translation: 事件的成形最初是一种情感、心灵或心理功能。事件是物理性的诠释，是内在感知经验经过"凝聚"于时空后的传统化版本。事件按照涉及爱、信念、意图及其投入强度的法则来组织。

quotes.text: You rule your experiences from the focal point of your present, where your beliefs directly intercept with the body and the physical world on the one hand, and the invisible world from which you draw your energy and strength on the other. This applies to individuals, societies, races and nations, and to sociological, biological and psychic activities.
quotes.translation: 你从当下的焦点主宰你的经验，在此处你的信念一方面直接与身体及物质世界交汇，另一方面则与汲取能量与力量的隐形世界相连。这适用于个人、社会、种族与国家，也适用于社会学、生物学与心灵活动。

quotes.text: Because you do not understand that your thoughts create illness you will continue to undergo it, however, and new symptoms will appear. You will again return to the doctor. When you are in the process of changing beliefs -- when you are beginning to realize that your thoughts and feelings cause illness -- then for a while you may not know what to do.
quotes.translation: 然而，由于你不明白你的思想创造疾病，你会继续经历它，新的症状也会出现。你将再次回到医生那里。当你正在改变信念的过程中——当你开始意识到你的思想和感受导致疾病时——那么有一段时间你可能不知道该怎么办。

quotes.text: "The scientist carries the burden of this alienation, and in his heart he must hope that his mission fails -- for if it succeeds he will have effectively separated man from man's nature in the world of beliefs, philosophically casting man adrift as meaningless psychological debris. Therefore, the scientific community often sabotages its own efforts."
quotes.translation: 科学家背负着这种疏离的重担，内心深处他必须希望自己的使命失败——因为若成功，他将在信念的世界里将人与人的本质彻底分离，从哲学上把人放逐为无意义的心理碎片。因此，科学界常常自我破坏其努力成果。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: All of the available data coming into the organism would be sifted, weighed and valued in a precise search for the material that would give physical emphasis to those beliefs. Information or events running counter would be ignored to a large degree, or distorted in such a fashion that they would be made to fit in with what the mind said was reality.
quotes.translation: 进入有机体的所有可用数据都会被筛选、权衡和评估，精确寻找能为那些信念提供物质佐证的材料。相反的信息或事件将在很大程度上被忽略，或被扭曲成符合心智所认定的现实形态。

quotes.text: Ruburt directed his will in certain areas. Your will is your intent. All of the power of your being is mobilized by your will, which makes its deductions according to your beliefs about reality. Each of you use your will in your own way. Each of you have your own way of dealing with challenges. Ruburt used his will to solve [a series of] challenges.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏在特定领域运用了他的意志。你的意志就是你的意图。你存在的全部力量都由意志调动，而意志根据你对现实的信念做出判断。你们每个人都以自己的方式运用意志，各有应对挑战的独特方法。鲁柏运用他的意志解决了一系列挑战。

quotes.text: In the first stage, Ruburt projected through physical reality and saw personalities who had very recently died in physical terms. Now, it is very difficult to tell you simply what happens at physical death, for the conditions vary considerably, according to the individual; his abilities and beliefs will largely determine what happens in those terms.
quotes.translation: 在第一阶段，鲁柏透过物质实相投射，并看见了在物质层面刚刚去世的人格。现在，要简单地告诉你物质死亡时发生的情况非常困难，因为状况因人而异，差异极大；他的能力与信念将在很大程度上决定那些层面的情况。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: The ego is your idea of your physical image in relation to the world. Your self image is not unconscious, then. You are quite aware of it, though often you reject certain thoughts about it in favor of others. False beliefs can result in a rigid ego that insists upon using the conscious mind in one direction only, further distorting its perceptions.
quotes.translation: 自我是你对自己与世间相关的物质形象的概念。因此，你的自我形象并非无意识的。你相当清楚它，尽管你常常为了偏袒某些想法而拒绝关于它的其他想法。虚假的信念可能导致僵化的自我，坚持只在一个方向上运用意识心，进一步扭曲其感知。

quotes.text: There are built-in biological structures that are activated for the reception of such messages, and they have always been a part of your physical nature as a species. They will not be triggered on a personal basis until your own beliefs allow you to perceive the multidimensional layers of your own experience or at least to accept the possibilities.
quotes.translation: 存在内置的生物结构，用于接收此类讯息，它们一直是你作为物种的物质本性的一部分。它们不会在个人基础上被触发，直到你自己的信念允许你感知自身经验的多维层次，或至少接受其可能性。

quotes.text: Energy, by nature, replaces itself and thrives with use. A false conception of energy however results in a psychic refusal to transform completely available energy into physical terms, merely because of the subconscious belief that such a transformation is basically impossible.
quotes.translation: 能量就其本质而言会自行更新，并因使用而茁壮。然而，对能量的错误认知会导致心灵拒绝将完全可用的能量转化为物质形式，仅仅因为潜意识相信这种转化根本不可能。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: In his normal condition Augustus thought of his own powerlessness -- for he had denied himself normal aggressive action -- and felt this weak. The beliefs activated the body's cellular memory, weakening the body and impeding its function. Yet for a time, while performance was dulled it was steady. A balance was maintained that suited his purposes.
quotes.translation: 在正常状态下，奥古斯都认为自己无能为力——因为他否定了自己正常的进取行动——并因此感到虚弱。这些信念激活了身体的细胞记忆，削弱了身体并阻碍其功能。然而有一段时间，虽然表现变得迟钝，却保持稳定。一种符合他目的的平衡得以维持。

quotes.text: Because you have free will you have the responsibility and the gift, the joy and the necessity, of working with your beliefs and of choosing your personal reality as you desire. I told you earlier (in the 636th session in Chapter Nine) that you cannot fall out of a state of grace. Each of you must intellectually and emotionally accept it, however.
quotes.translation: 因为你们拥有自由意志，所以你们既有责任与天赋，也有喜悦与必要，去与你们的信念合作，并按照你们的渴望选择个人实相。我先前告诉过你们（在第九章第636节中），你们无法从恩宠状态中坠落。然而，你们每个人都必须在理智与情感上接受这一点。

quotes.text: Your beliefs have generated feelings of unworth. Having artificially separated yourselves from nature, you do not trust it, but often experience it as an adversary. Your religions granted man a soul, while denying any to other species. Your bodies then were relegated to nature and your souls to God, who stood immaculately apart from His creations.
quotes.translation: 你们的信念已产生无价值感。由于人为地与自然分离，你们不信任自然，反而常视其为敌手。你们的宗教赋予人类灵魂，却否认其他物种拥有灵魂。于是你们的身体被归为自然，而灵魂归于上帝——那位与自身造物完美分离的神祇。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: This flexibility allows the species great variation overall in its psychological and cultural and political and religious activities. (Long pause.) When any system of reasoning becomes too rigid, however, there are always adjustments made that will allow other information to intrude -- otherwise, of course, your belief systems would never change.
quotes.translation: 这种灵活性使该物种在心理、文化、政治及宗教活动上拥有极大的整体变异性。（长停顿）然而，当任何推理体系变得过于僵化时，总会产生调整，让其他信息得以介入——否则，你们的信念体系将永远无法改变。

quotes.text: (9:40.) Those mythological images and their belief system were shared by all--peasants and the wealthy--to a large degree. They were, then, highly charged emotionally. Whether an artist painted saints or apostles as heroic figures, as ideas embodied in flesh, or as natural men, he commented on the relationship between the natural and the divine.
quotes.translation: （晚上9点40分）那些神话意象及其信仰体系在很大程度上为所有人——无论农民还是富人——所共享。因此，它们充满了强烈的情感能量。无论艺术家是将圣徒或使徒描绘成英雄人物、理念的肉身化现，还是自然的人，他都在评论自然与神性之间的关系。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: Physically, it is true, but again generally speaking, that your body needs certain nourishments. But within that pattern there is great leeway, and the organism itself has the amazing capacity to make use of substitutes and alternates. The best diet in the world, by anyone's standards, will not keep you healthy if you have a belief in illness.
quotes.translation: 从生理层面来说，确实如此，但再泛泛而言，你的身体需要某些营养。然而在这一模式之内存在极大的弹性空间，而机体本身具有惊人的能力去利用替代品和变通之物。若你持有疾病的信念，那么按任何标准衡量世上最好的饮食也无法让你保持健康。

quotes.text: That is simply because everything is now scheduled in a fashion, with the time available. That material, however, is "excellent medicine," and can indeed be life-saving (intently). It is indeed unfortunate that those beliefs that show themselves so simply and effectively in nature seem so mysterious to the usual line of official consciousness.
quotes.translation: 这仅仅是因为现在一切事物都按照可用的时间以某种方式被安排。然而，那份材料是“极好的药方”，确实能救命（专注地）。那些在自然中如此简单而有效地展现自身的信念，对于通常的官方意识来说却显得如此神秘，这确实是不幸的。

quotes.text: The ideas that you have involving the nature of reality will strongly color your experiences, for you will interpret them in the light of your beliefs, even as now you interpret daily life according to your ideas of what is possible or not possible. Your consciousness may withdraw from your body slowly or quickly, according to many variables.
quotes.translation: 你关于实相本质的想法会强烈地影响你的经历，因为你会根据你的信念来诠释它们，就像现在你根据自己对可能或不可能的想法来诠释日常生活一样。根据许多变量，你的意识可能会缓慢或快速地脱离你的身体。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: If you are feeling poorly and happen to read an advertisement for vitamins, or a book about them, and are impressed, you will indeed benefit -- at least for a while. Your belief will make them work for you, but if your insistence upon poor health persists, then the counter suggestion represented by the vitamins will not be effective for long.
quotes.translation: 如果你感觉不适，碰巧读到一则维生素广告或一本关于维生素的书，并深受触动，你确实会受益——至少暂时如此。你的信念会让它们为你发挥作用，但如果你坚持认为自己身体欠佳，那么维生素所代表的相反暗示就不会长久有效。

quotes.text: I also want to emphasize that your present beliefs limit the full and free operation of your intellects, as far as your established fields of knowledge are concerned, for science has placed so many taboos, limiting the areas of free intellectual inquiry. I am not, however, promoting dependence upon feelings above the intellect, or vice versa.
quotes.translation: 我也想强调，就你们既定的知识领域而言，你们当前的信念限制了智力的完全自由运作，因为科学设置了如此多的禁忌，限制了自由智力探究的领域。然而，我并非提倡依赖情感高于智力，或反之亦然。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: To understand yourself and what you are, you can learn to experience yourself directly apart from your beliefs about yourself. What I would like each reader to do is to sit quietly. Close your eyes. Try to sense within yourself the deep feeling-tones that I mentioned earlier (in the 613th session in Chapter One). This is not difficult to do.
quotes.translation: 要了解你自己及你的本质，你可以学习直接体验自己，而不受你对自己信念的干扰。我希望每位读者做的是：安静地坐下，闭上眼睛，试着在内在感受我之前（第一章第613节）提到的深层情感基调。这并不难做到。

quotes.text: In your society particularly, given over so thoroughly to the pursuit of money, such beliefs bring about the most humiliating situations, especially for the male, who has often been told to equate his virility with his earning power. It is easy then to understand that when his capacity to earn is taken away he feels castrated. Period, break.
quotes.translation: 在你们的社会里，尤其因过度追求金钱，这类信念会带来最屈辱的处境，特别是对男性而言——他们常被告知要将男子气概等同于赚钱能力。因此不难理解，当他的赚钱能力被剥夺时，他会感到被阉割。句号，换行。

quotes.text: In regard to your dream: The shakiness represented values and ideas from your mother, primarily about work -- beliefs you always felt were shaky. In the dream you manage to circumnavigate their effects. In daily waking life the shakiness still represents leftover uncertainty, and it is more noticeable whenever any strong interruptions occur.
quotes.translation: 关于你的梦境：摇晃感代表了你母亲关于工作的价值观与观念——那些你始终觉得摇摇欲坠的信念。在梦中你设法绕过了它们的影响。在清醒的日常生活中，这种摇晃感仍代表着残留的不确定性，每当出现强烈干扰时便格外明显。

quotes.text: A friend and I, with some others, staged the ceremony, and from opposite clouds in the sky Allah and I shouted out our claims upon his soul -- while he, poor man, cowered on the ground between us. Now while I tell this story humorously, you must understand that the man's belief brought it about, and so to set him free, we worked it through.
quotes.translation: 我与一位朋友及其他人共同主持了这场仪式，安拉与我分别从天空两端的云层中呼喊，争夺他的灵魂——而那个可怜人蜷缩在我们之间的地面上。当我以幽默口吻讲述这个故事时，你必须明白是那个人的信念导致了这一切，因此为了让他解脱，我们共同完成了这场仪式。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Upon the patient a doctor often assigns and projects his own feelings of helplessness against which he combats. The interactions continue with the patient trying to please the doctor, and at best merely changing from one group of symptoms to another. Far too often the doctor shares the patient's unshakable belief in poor health and disease.
quotes.translation: 医生常常将自己的无助感投射到病人身上，并与之对抗。医患互动中，病人试图取悦医生，结果最多只是从一组症状转换到另一组症状。更常见的是，医生与病人共同持有对健康不佳和疾病的顽固信念。

quotes.text: Fear, faced and felt with its bodily sensations and the thoughts that go along with it, will automatically bring about its own state of resolution. The conscious system of beliefs behind the impediment will be illuminated, and you will realize that you feel a certain way because you believe an idea that causes and justifies such a reaction.
quotes.translation: 恐惧，当被面对并感受其身体感觉及伴随的念头时，会自动引发其自身的化解状态。障碍背后的意识信念系统将被照亮，你将意识到自己之所以有某种感受，是因为你相信某个观念，这个观念引发并合理化这样的反应。

quotes.text: "The end justifies the means." This is another belief, most damaging. Religious wars always have paranoiac tendencies, for the fanatic always fears conflicting beliefs, and systems that embrace them.
quotes.translation: "为达目的不择手段。"这是另一个最具破坏性的信念。宗教战争总是带有偏执倾向，因为狂热者总是恐惧相互冲突的信念，以及包容这些信念的体系。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: If this strikes you as too intellectual a method, then you can also work backward from your emotions to your beliefs. In any case, regardless of which method you choose, one will lead you to the other. Both approaches require honesty with yourself, and a firm encounter with the mental, psychic and emotional aspects of your current reality.
quotes.translation: 如果这让你觉得过于理智化，你也可以从情绪反向追溯至信念。无论选择何种方法，两者终将相互指引。这两种方式都需要你对自己诚实，并直面当前实相中精神、心灵与情感层面的真实状态。

quotes.text: You can stub your toe as easily on a misplaced idea as you can upon an old chair. It will help you, in fact, if you think of your own beliefs as furniture that can be rearranged, changed, renewed, completely discarded or replaced. Your ideas are yours. They should not control you. It is up to you to accept those that you choose to accept.
quotes.translation: 你绊倒在一个错置的念头上，就像绊倒在一把旧椅子上一样容易。事实上，如果你把自己的信念视为可以重新排列、改变、更新、完全丢弃或替换的家具，这对你会有帮助。你的想法属于你，它们不应控制你。接受哪些想法，完全由你自己决定。

quotes.text: Here then is a case where directly opposing beliefs dominated the conscious mind at various times, each operating the body in its own manner. Physically the body has the same capacity for strength regardless of which group of ideas were dominant; but practically speaking, Augustus One was incapable of performing the feats of Augustus Two.
quotes.translation: 这里有一个案例：直接对立的信念在不同时期主宰了意识心智，各自以自身方式操控身体。从身体层面看，无论哪组观念占主导，身体都具备相同的体力潜能；但实际而言，奥古斯都一号无法完成奥古斯都二号的壮举。

quotes.text: If you are religious-minded and fundamental in your beliefs, you may blame a devil who causes you to behave in such and such a manner. As the body creates antibodies* to regulate itself, so you will set up mental and emotional "antibodies," certain thoughts that are "good," to protect you from the fantasies or ideas that you consider bad.
quotes.translation: 如果你有宗教倾向且信仰基要派，你可能会归咎于某个魔鬼，认为是它导致你以某种方式行事。正如身体会产生抗体来自我调节，你也会建立心理和情感的"抗体"——某些你认为"好"的想法——来保护你免受自认为不良的幻想或观念的侵扰。

quotes.text: Later we will have some comments regarding hypnotism. Here let me mention that in those terms you hypnotize yourself constantly with your own conscious thoughts and suggestions. The term hypnosis merely applies to a quite normal state in which you concentrate your attention, narrowing your focus to a particular area of thought or belief.
quotes.translation: 稍后我们将讨论催眠术。在此容我提及：就这些术语而言，你不断用自己有意识的念头与暗示对自己进行催眠。催眠一词仅适用于一种相当正常的状态——你集中注意力，将焦点收窄至某个特定思想或信念领域。

quotes.text: This further unites all species in a cooperative venture that has remained largely invisible because of beliefs projected outward upon the world by both your sciences and religions, generally speaking. All of your grandest civilizations have existed first in the world of dreams. You might say that the universe dreamed itself into being.
quotes.translation: 这进一步将所有物种团结在一个合作的事业中，这个事业在很大程度上一直不可见，因为一般来说，你们的科学和宗教将信念投射到外在世界上。你们所有最伟大的文明最初都存在于梦境世界中。你可以说宇宙通过梦想自己而存在。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: It has to do with the mass beliefs that people chose at various times, and the different roads that were taken in your reality. Any road taken in your reality should -- but does not -- tell you one thing: By the very fact that you have chosen a particular road, you can be sure that other roads, entirely different, have also been taken.
quotes.translation: 这与人们在不同时期选择的大众信念有关，也与你们实相中采取的不同道路有关。你们实相中采取的任何道路都应该——但并没有——告诉你一件事：正是因为你选择了一条特定的道路，你可以确定其他完全不同的道路也被采取了。

quotes.text: The Hebrews conceived of an overseer god, an angry and just and sometimes cruel god; and many sects denied, then, the idea that other living beings beside man possessed inner spirits. The earlier beliefs represented a far better representation of inner reality, in which man, observing nature, let nature speak and reveal its secrets.
quotes.translation: 希伯来人构想了一位监督之神，一位愤怒、公正、有时残忍的神；而许多教派当时否认人类之外的其他生灵拥有内在灵魂。早期的信仰更能代表内在实相——那时人类观察自然，让自然开口说话，揭示其奥秘。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Elements, chemicals, cells, atoms and molecules -- these partially compose your living sculpture, but you are the one who directs their activity through your conscious beliefs, which then initiate all of those great creative powers that give your body its life, and insure its constant reflection of the self that you believe you are.
quotes.translation: 元素、化学物质、细胞、原子与分子——这些部分构成了你活生生的雕塑，但通过你有意识的信念，你才是那个指挥它们活动的人。这些信念继而启动所有那些伟大的创造力，赋予你的身体生命，并确保它持续反映你所相信的那个自我。

quotes.text: (10:00.) Beneath the dreams that you recall are experiences of consciousness that appear only now and then, and in distorted form. These express in nonphysical terms your relationship with your own being. Here you are regenerated, and you are quite free of any conscious beliefs. From this level individual and mass ideals are formed.
quotes.translation: （十点整。）在你记得的梦境之下，存在着意识的体验，它们只是偶尔以扭曲的形式显现。这些体验以非物质的方式表达你与自身存有的关系。在此处你获得重生，完全不受任何有意识信念的束缚。从这一层面，个体与群体的理想得以形成。

quotes.text: Ruburt wanted to go in one particular direction, but with no clear-cut known ways of getting there. He wanted to pursue a course that was unconventional. He felt he needed protection while he learned, and until he attained enough wisdom.14 The search itself would lead to a completely different set of values and a new belief system.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏想朝某个特定方向前进，但没有明确的已知途径可抵达。他想走一条非传统的道路。他觉得自己在学习过程中需要保护，直到获得足够的智慧。14 这场探索本身将导向一套完全不同的价值观和新的信念体系。

quotes.text: They constantly surround themselves with negative suggestions. When disease is seen as an invader, forced upon the integrity of the self for no reason, then the individual seems powerless and the conscious mind an adjunct. The patient is sometimes compelled to sacrifice one organ after another to his beliefs, and to the doctor's.
quotes.translation: 他们不断用负面暗示包围自己。当疾病被视为一种入侵者，无缘无故强加于自我的完整性时，个体便显得无能为力，意识心成了附属品。患者有时被迫将自己的器官一个接一个地牺牲给信念，以及医生的信念。

quotes.text: There are gullies, hills, mountains, valleys, large continents, small islands upon the earth, and the falling rain fits itself to those contours. Your own thoughts, dreams, intents, emotions, beliefs -- these are the natural features of your mind, so that information, impinging upon your mental world, also follows those contours.
quotes.translation: 地球上有沟壑、丘陵、山脉、山谷、大陆与岛屿，雨水会顺应这些地形轮廓。你自己的思想、梦想、意图、情感、信念——这些是你心灵的自然地貌，因此信息冲击你的精神世界时，也会遵循那些轮廓。

quotes.text: The variations are endless. On the whole, in the vast scheme of reincarnational reality, a belief in life's meaning is by far the rule, and other excursions are indeed eccentric variations. Specifically, however, such life episodes will of course involve their "moments" of after-death realization--dismay, shock, or what have you.
quotes.translation: 其变化无穷无尽。整体而言，在轮回实相的宏大架构中，相信生命具有意义是绝对的主流，而其他偏离确实属于罕见的变奏。但具体而言，这类生命插曲当然会包含其死后认知的"瞬间"——惊愕、震撼或诸如此类的体验。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: Two men for example, of precisely the same physical age, of precisely the same physical condition, will be in completely different states of mind, of competence, of effectiveness and of strength, as a direct result of their inner beliefs as to their relative freedom within the framework of the physical system in which they exist.
quotes.translation: 举例来说，两位生理年龄完全相同、身体状况完全相同的男性，会处于截然不同的心智状态、能力水平、效能表现与力量强度——这直接源于他们对自己在所处物质系统框架内相对自由度的内在信念。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)

quotes.text: Your own thoughts and beliefs, having the same kind of inner reality, also transform the interior environments of others. The near-accident mentioned was a physical event but it was initially a mental one. It existed in this nontemporal reality then before, in your terms, it was physically materialized, perceived and reacted to.
quotes.translation: 你自身的思想与信念具有相同的内在实相，同样会转化他人的内在环境。前文提及的"准意外"是物质事件，但最初却是心智事件。用你们的时间术语来说，它在物质化、被感知及被反应之前，早已存在于这种非时间性的实相中。

quotes.text: The old medicine men often dealt far more directly with the patient himself, and understood the nature of beliefs and the prime importance of suggestion. Many of their techniques were adopted for their psychological shock value, in which the patient was quite effectively "brainwashed" out of the disease he believed that he had.
quotes.translation: 古老的巫医往往更直接地处理患者本身，他们深谙信念的本质与暗示的首要重要性。许多疗法之所以被采用，正是因其心理冲击价值——患者会被相当有效地"洗脑"，从而摆脱他们自认为患有的疾病。

quotes.text: She had carried those beliefs of course throughout that period of time, and they were expressed unconsciously through her body -- through gestures, expressions, tones of voice. The whole physical self expected rebuffs. The events of those days, whatever they were, would be interpreted in the light of that mental set (intently).
quotes.translation: 当然，她在整个时期都携带着那些信念，并通过身体无意识地表达出来——通过手势、表情、语调。整个物质自我都在期待被拒绝。无论那些日子里发生什么事件，都会被置于那种心理定势（专注地）下加以诠释。

quotes.text: The Individual and the Nature of Mass Events. Seth explores the connection between personal beliefs and world events, how our realities merge and combine "to form mass reactions such as the overthrow of governments, the birth of a new religion, wars, epidemics, earthquakes, and new periods of art, architecture, and technology."
quotes.translation: 《个人与群体事件的本质》。赛斯探讨了个人信念与世界事件之间的联系，我们的实相如何融合并组合，"形成群体反应，例如政府的更迭、新宗教的诞生、战争、流行病、地震，以及艺术、建筑和科技的新时期。"
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: Your reincarnational selves have as many probable lives as you do. Your beliefs and actions in your present alter "their" experiences, as each of them, in their presents, change yours. If you see these reincarnational selves as one entity, then this becomes quite natural. The whole self is changed by all of its comprehensions.
quotes.translation: 你的转世自我拥有和你一样多的可能人生。你现在的信念和行动改变了"他们的"经历，正如他们每个人在各自的现在改变了你的经历一样。如果你将这些转世自我视为一个整体，那么这就变得非常自然。整个自我因其所有的领悟而改变。

quotes.text: These are quite practical statements. Your body has an overall body consciousness filled with energy and vitality. It automatically rights any imbalances, but your conscious beliefs also affect this body consciousness. Your muscles believe what you tell them about themselves. So does every other portion of your physical body.
quotes.translation: 这些都是非常实际的陈述。你的身体拥有一个充满能量与活力的整体身体意识，它会自动修正任何失衡，但你有意识的信念也会影响这个身体意识。你的肌肉相信你告诉它们的一切，你身体的其他部分也是如此。

quotes.text: Now: Natural disasters are brought about more at an emotional level than at a belief level, though beliefs have an important part to play, for they generate the emotions to begin with.
quotes.translation: 现在：自然灾害更多是在情绪层面而非信念层面引发的，尽管信念扮演着重要角色，因为信念最初催生了情绪。

quotes.text: So the belief in man's sinful nature persisted in my mind, a constant reminder of man's ignorance of his own nature. How could I be sure that our sight wasn't also distorted; that our "sin" was in not accepting sin as a value? Perhaps sin itself contained some value that escaped beyond our calculations, still undiscovered.
quotes.translation: 因此人类原罪的信念始终盘踞我心，不断提醒着人类对自身本性的无知。我如何能确信我们的视野未被扭曲？如何能确定我们的"罪"恰在于拒绝将罪视为价值？或许罪本身蕴含着超越我们算计的、尚未被发现的价值。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: The structuring of beliefs is done in a highly characteristic yet individual manner, so you will find patterns that exist between various groupings, and one can lead you to another.
quotes.translation: 信念的架构方式既高度独特又因人而异，因此你会发现不同信念群组之间存在模式，一个信念能引导你找到另一个。

quotes.text: It will manage to work around the blind spots in the reasoning mind, for example. Often it will sift out from the barrage of conflicting beliefs the particular set that is the most life-giving, and send these forth in what then appears as a burst of revelation. Such revelations result in new patterns that change behavior.
quotes.translation: 举例来说，它会设法绕过理性思维中的盲点。它常常会从相互冲突的信念洪流中筛选出最具生命力的特定组合，并以看似顿悟的形式释放出来。这类顿悟会催生改变行为的新模式。

quotes.text: You may begin to perceive all physical data through the eyes of that core belief alone. You will not look out upon physical reality with the wonder of a child any more, or with the unstructured curiosity of an individual, but always through parental eyes. Thus you will close yourself off from much of physical experience.
quotes.translation: 你可能开始仅透过那个核心信念的眼睛来感知所有物质资料。你不再以孩童般的好奇心或未经结构化的个人好奇心来观察物质实相，而是始终透过父母般的眼光。如此一来，你便将自己隔绝于许多物质体验之外。

quotes.text: It was propelled from inner reality to outward reality through belief, emotion and imagination. Because you cannot see them, it may seem to you that these qualities are not as real, say, as an object. Physically you can only see the results of an emotion, for instance. You cannot hold it in your hands as you can a stone.
quotes.translation: 它经由信念、情感与想象力，从内在实相被推向外在实相。由于你无法看见它们，这些特质对你而言或许不如一个物体那般真实。例如，你只能看见情感的结果，却无法像握住石头那样将它握在手中。

quotes.text: In quite real personal and racial terms, the past is still happening. You create it from your present according to your beliefs. A removed appendix will not reappear physically. There are certain frameworks that are accepted, built into your creaturehood. There is far greater freedom, however, even on the cellular level.
quotes.translation: 在非常真实的个人与种族层面，过去仍在发生。你根据信念从当下创造过去。被切除的阑尾不会在身体上重新出现。某些框架是被接受的，并已融入你的生物本性之中。然而，即使在细胞层面，也存在着远为更大的自由。

quotes.text: When you believe that you are good, your body functions well. I am sure that many of you will say, "I try constantly to be good, yet I am in miserable health, so how can that be?" If you examine your own beliefs the answer will be apparent: You try to be so good precisely because you believe you are so bad and unworthy.
quotes.translation: 当你相信自己善良时，你的身体运作良好。我相信你们许多人会说："我不断努力做个好人，却饱受病痛折磨，这怎么可能？" 若你检视自己的信念，答案便会显而易见：你之所以如此努力做个好人，恰恰是因为你相信自己如此恶劣且毫无价值。

quotes.text: This is the reason for the results of some experiments being conducted abroad, in which accelerated learning takes place, when under hypnosis or otherwise a present individual is convinced that he or she is, for example, a great painter, or a linguist. The present belief activates "latent" abilities within each person.*
quotes.translation: 这就是国外某些实验产生结果的原因——在催眠或其他状态下，当一个人确信自己例如是位伟大的画家或语言学家时，加速学习便发生了。当下的信念激活了每个人内在的"潜在"能力。

quotes.text: (10:28.) The simple fact is that as long as you believe in the concept of evil, it is a reality in your system, and you will always find it manifested. Your belief in it will, therefore, seem highly justified. If you carry this concept through succeeding generations, through reincarnations, then you add to its reality.
quotes.translation: （十点二十八分。）简单的事实是：只要你相信邪恶的概念，它在你的系统中就是真实的，你总会发现它被具体显现。因此，你对它的信念会显得高度合理。如果你将这个观念通过转世世代代传递下去，那么你就增强了它的真实性。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: This path exists on psychological levels, and triggers your perceptive mechanisms, which then of course react and dutifully perceive. Your intent or purpose or belief is one of the main attractions. These serve as beams searching the universe, but the conditions of manifestation also exist. There must be a proper fit.
quotes.translation: 这条路径存在于心理层面，并触发你的感知机制，这些机制随后自然会反应并尽职地感知。你的意图、目的或信念是主要的吸引因素之一。它们如同搜寻宇宙的光束，但显化的条件同样存在。必须存在适当的契合。

quotes.text: It is to his advantage that the earlier publisher did not take the book. It is the subject matter of the book that intrigued both publishers, plus Ruburt's belief in the book. But it was also the subject matter, to some degree, that made Mr. Fell cautious, and that finally caused the earlier publisher to turn it down.
quotes.translation: 早先的出版商没有出版这本书，这对他而言是有利的。这本书的主题吸引了两位出版商，再加上鲁柏对这本书的信念。但在某种程度上，也正是这个主题让费尔先生变得谨慎，并最终导致早先的出版商拒绝了它。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: A person's private experience happens in the context of his psychological and biological status, and basically cannot be separated from his religious and philosophical beliefs and sentiments, and his cultural environment and political framework --
quotes.translation: 一个人的个人体验发生在其心理与生理状态的背景中，并且基本上无法与他的宗教与哲学信念及情感、他的文化环境与政治框架相分离。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: This is done many times without the main personality realizing what is really going on. On occasion automatic writing is utilized, or the Ouija board. Both are methods to uncover invisible conscious beliefs -- that are accepted by you consciously at any given time, say, and deliberately ignored at another given time.
quotes.translation: 这种情况多次发生，而主要人格并未意识到真正发生了什么。偶尔会借助自动书写或通灵板，这两种方法都能揭露那些隐形的有意识信念——这些信念在某个时刻被你清醒地接受，却在另一时刻被刻意忽视。

quotes.text: The imagination always seeks expression. It is always creative, and underneath the frameworks of society it provides fresh incentives and new avenues for fulfillment, that can become harnessed through fanatical belief. When this happens your institutions become more repressive, and violence often emerges as a result.
quotes.translation: 想象力始终寻求表达，它永远具有创造力。在社会框架之下，它提供新的激励与实现途径，但这些可能被狂热信念所束缚。当这种情况发生时，你的制度会变得更加压抑，暴力往往由此滋生。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: But--it now becomes evident--I was myself tinged not by sin in a metaphysical sense (as I thought I might be), but with a belief in sin (itself) that I had not dismissed. Therefore the disclaimer was necessary to protect myself and others from any fatal flaw in our work--a flaw that sin's blindness made invisible....
quotes.translation: 但——现在已显而易见——我自身沾染的并非形而上学意义上的罪（如我曾以为的那样），而是我尚未摒弃的"对罪的信念"本身。因此，这份免责声明对于保护我们自身及他人免受工作中任何致命缺陷的伤害实属必要——这种缺陷因罪的盲目性而变得不可见……
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: In other words I am telling you that your present beliefs, in a manner of speaking, are like the directions given to the entire personality, simultaneously organizing and reorganizing past experience according to your current concepts of reality.
quotes.translation: 换言之，我是在告诉你，你目前的信念，从某种角度来说，就像是对整个人格下达的指令，它会根据你当前对实相的概念，同时组织并重组过去的经验。

quotes.text: Naturally your physical experience reinforces this belief. You structure your perception, therefore, in terms of the lapses that seem to happen between events. This in itself forces you to concentrate your attention in one direction only, and discourages you from perceiving the events in your life in other fashions.
quotes.translation: 自然，你的物质经验会强化这一信念。因此，你依据事件之间看似存在的间隙来构建你的感知。这本身便迫使你将注意力只集中在一个方向上，并阻碍你以其他方式感知生活中的事件。

quotes.text: This is done by combining belief, emotion and imagination, and forming them into a mental picture of the desired physical result. Of course, the wanted result is not yet physical or you would not need to create it, so it does no good to say that your physical experience seems to contradict what you are trying to do.
quotes.translation: 这是通过结合信念、情感与想象力，并将它们塑造成渴望的物质结果的心灵画面来实现的。当然，渴望的结果尚未物质化，否则你便无需创造它，因此，声称你的物质经验似乎与你试图达成的目标相矛盾，是毫无益处的。

quotes.text: Now this is a split of the conscious mind. It does not originate within the inner self. When Augustus Two takes over he is quite conscious. He simply views physical reality through an undeviating system of beliefs. The messages sent to the body are not in the least contradictory. The body is under excellent control.
quotes.translation: 这是意识心的一种分裂。它并非源自内在自己。当奥古斯都二号接管时，他完全清醒。他只是透过一套坚定不移的信念系统来看待物质实相。传送给身体的讯息毫无矛盾之处。身体处于极佳的控制之下。

quotes.text: You cannot do this by ignoring the validity of the thoughts as your experience, however (very intensely), by trying to shove them under the rug of a superficial optimism. Such habitual, unhappy thoughts will bring about the same kind of physical experience, but it is your own system of beliefs that you must examine.
quotes.translation: 然而，你不能通过忽视这些思想作为你经验的有效性来做到这一点（非常强烈地），试图将它们扫入肤浅乐观主义的地毯下。这种习惯性的、不快乐的思想会带来同样类型的物质经验，但你必须审视的是你自己的信念系统。

quotes.text: Paranoia is extremely interesting because it shows the ways in which private beliefs can distort events that connect the individual with other people. The events are "distorted," yet while the paranoid is convinced that those events are valid, this does not change other people's perception of the same happenings....
quotes.translation: 偏执狂极为有趣，因为它展示了私人信念如何扭曲连接个人与他人的事件。这些事件被“扭曲”，然而当偏执狂确信这些事件有效时，这并不会改变其他人对同一事件的感知……
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Within you is the ability to change your ideas about reality and about yourself, to create a personal living experience that is fulfilling to yourself and others. I would like you to write down your beliefs about yourself as you become aware of them. Later you can use this list in a way that you do not now suspect.
quotes.translation: 你内在拥有改变对实相及自身看法的能力，能创造令自己与他人皆感充实的个人生活体验。我希望你逐步觉察自身信念并记录下来，日后你将能以此刻无法想象的方式运用这份清单。

quotes.text: With the initiation of psychic experience, he found himself wanting to write about what happened to him, and to use the material creatively. The previous beliefs in himself as a writer, however, clashed with these new urges because he did not consider anything but fiction as the work of a writer, except for poetry.
quotes.translation: 随着灵性体验的开启，他发现自己渴望书写这些经历，并创造性地运用这些素材。然而先前"身为作家"的自我认知与这些新冲动产生了冲突——因为他始终认为除了诗歌之外，唯有虚构文学才算作家的创作。

quotes.text: To a medical man all of this will seem like the sheerest heresy, because disease will always be seen as an objective thing in the body, to be objectively treated and removed. But a man who feels "that he has no heart" will not be saved by the most sophisticated heart transplant, unless first that belief is changed.
quotes.translation: 在医学人士眼中，这一切纯属异端邪说，因为疾病始终被视为身体中的客观存在，需以客观手段治疗与移除。但一个深信"自己心脏已死"的人，即便接受最尖端的心脏移植手术也无法得救——除非先改变这个信念。

quotes.text: Within the basic framework of the body chosen before physical birth (for reasons that will be discussed later), the individual has full freedom to create a perfectly healthy functioning form. The form is, however, a mirror of beliefs, and will accurately materialize in flesh those ideas held by the conscious mind.
quotes.translation: 在出生前选择的身体基本架构内（原因将在后文讨论），个体拥有完全的自由去创造完美健康且功能健全的形体。然而，形体是信念的镜子，会精确地将意识心所持的观念物质化于血肉之躯中。

quotes.text: He will be at all times a prisoner of clock time and of aging, for he will consider these the primary conditions under which he must operate. And the action, or actions involved in this belief will therefore act upon the physical cells of his body with vengeful force, because he has himself directed them to do so.
quotes.translation: 他将始终是钟表时间与衰老的囚徒，因为他会将这些视为自身运作的首要条件。而此信念所涉及的行动或行为，将以报复性的力量作用于他身体的细胞——这正是他亲自引导它们如此运作的结果。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)

quotes.text: First of all, the unconscious is not a sponge, indiscriminately accepting material regardless of the considerations of your conscious self. All beliefs or suggestions are first sifted through your conscious mind, and only those that you accept are then permitted their penetration into the other areas of the self.
quotes.translation: 首先，无意识并非一块海绵，会不顾你意识心的考量而全盘接收素材。所有信念或暗示都先经由你的意识心筛选，只有那些被你接纳的，才被允许渗透进入自我的其他领域。

quotes.text: Earlier I used, "I am a dependable parent," as an example of a belief. (See the 618th session in Chapter Three.) If to you this means, "I give great attention to seeing that my children brush their teeth, eat enough, and perform properly," then you will interpret the "better and better" suggestion in that light.
quotes.translation: 之前我曾以"我是个可靠的父母"作为信念的例子（见第三章第618节）。如果对你而言这意味着"我十分关注确保孩子刷牙、吃饱、行为得体"，那么你就会以这个角度来诠释"越来越好"的暗示。

quotes.text: Almost everyone in this society is acquainted with the old suggestion, "Every day, in every way, I am getting better and better."* Now that is an excellent suggestion, given by the conscious self to other portions of your being. The results of such a suggestion would also follow your conscious beliefs, however.
quotes.translation: 这个社会里几乎人人都熟悉那句古老的暗示："每一天，在每一方面，我都越来越好。"* 这确实是个绝佳的暗示，由意识自我给予你存在的其他部分。然而，这类暗示的结果仍会遵循你的意识信念。

quotes.text: I mention this here simply because in the overall development of an individual, an illness may also be used as a method to achieve another, constructive, end. In such a case belief would also be involved: Such a person would have to believe that an unhealthy condition was the best way to serve another purpose.
quotes.translation: 我在此提及这点，仅因在个体的整体发展中，疾病也可能被用作达成另一建设性目的的方法。在此情况下，信念同样牵涉其中：此人必须相信不健康的状态是服务另一目的的最佳途径。

quotes.text: The soul is not only dressed in chemical clothes, but wears the apparel woven from all of the elements of the earth. As physical creatures you will be partially changed by any chemical or element, or food or drug that becomes part of your living system, but those effects will follow the nature of your beliefs.
quotes.translation: 灵魂不仅披着化学的衣裳，更穿着由大地所有元素编织的袍服。作为物质生物，任何成为你生命系统的化学元素、食物或药物都会部分改变你，但这些影响将遵循你信念的本质。

quotes.text: Functions in this particular regard are habits. You simply forget how to hear properly, following your belief. All of the minute manipulations necessary to hearing are unconsciously repressed. The actual physical deterioration then does indeed follow. The deterioration however does not occur first, but after.
quotes.translation: 在此特定层面的运作机制即是习惯。你只是遵循信念而遗忘了如何正确聆听。所有听觉所需的细微调控都被无意识地压抑了。随后确实会发生实际的生理退化，但退化并非先发生，而是后发生。

quotes.text: Many individuals are completely blind to their own beliefs about themselves, and the nature of reality. Your own conscious thoughts will give you excellent clues. Often you will find yourself refusing to accept certain thoughts that come to your mind because they conflict with other usually accepted ideas.
quotes.translation: 许多人对自己持有的信念以及现实的本质完全视而不见。您有意识的思维会提供绝佳的线索。您常会发现，自己拒绝接受某些浮现的念头，只因它们与通常被接纳的观念相冲突。

quotes.text: Experiencing your emotions as such is not the same as accepting them as statements of fact about your own existence. Andrea is then supposed to ask, "Why do I feel so inferior?" If you deny the validity of the emotion itself and pretend it away, then you will never be led to question the beliefs behind it.
quotes.translation: 体验情绪本身，与将情绪视为关于自身存在的真实陈述，是截然不同的两回事。安德莉亚应当自问："为何我感到如此自卑？"若你否认情绪本身的真实性，假装它不存在，便永远无法探究其背后的信念。

quotes.text: Now, in the normal course of events you attract experience in the same fashion. You anticipate events. You are aware of them before they happen, whether or not you ever succeed in conscious predictions. You form your life, however, through the intimate interworkings of your own conscious goals and beliefs.
quotes.translation: 在常规事件进程中，你以相同方式吸引经历。无论你是否能成功进行有意识的预测，你都能在事件发生前感知到它们。然而，你正是通过自身意识目标与信念的精密交织，塑造了你的人生。

quotes.text: Events are attracted or repelled by you according to your loves, beliefs, intents, and purposes. Your world provides a theater in which certain events can or cannot occur. Wars, violence, disasters -- these are obviously shared by many, and are a part of your shared psychological and physical environment.
quotes.translation: 事件因你的爱、信念、意图与目的而被你吸引或排斥。你的世界提供了一个舞台，某些事件可能或不可能在其中发生。战争、暴力、灾难——这些显然为许多人所共享，并且是你共享的心理与物理环境的一部分。

quotes.text: Examine what you have written. Realize that a set of beliefs is involved. There is a difference between believing that you are unworthy and being unworthy in fact.
quotes.translation: 检视你所写下的内容。要明白其中涉及一套信念。相信自己毫无价值与事实上毫无价值之间是有区别的。

quotes.text: Seth often uses episodes from our lives as specific examples of larger issues, and our experiences with the flood served as a starting point for his discussion of personal beliefs and disasters. In several other instances he also used our life situation as his source material -- an intriguing turnabout.
quotes.translation: 赛斯常以我们生活中的事件作为更大议题的具体范例，而那次洪水的经历正是他探讨个人信念与灾难的起点。在其他几个场合，他也曾以我们的生活处境为素材——这真是一种有趣的逆转。

quotes.text: (10:08.) The patient, therefore, often feels relatively powerless and at the mercy of any stray virus that might come along. The facts are that you choose even the kind of illness that you have according to the nature of your beliefs. You are immune from ill health as long as you believe that you are.
quotes.translation: (10:08.) 因此，病人常常感到相对无力，只能任凭任何偶然出现的病毒摆布。事实是，你甚至根据自己信念的性质选择所患疾病的种类。只要你相信自己不会生病，你就不会生病。

quotes.text: The imaginative events generate appropriate emotions, which automatically bring about hormonal* changes in your body or affect your behavior with others, or cause you to interpret events always in the light of your beliefs. And so daily experience will seem to justify what you believe more and more.
quotes.translation: 想象性事件会引发相应的情绪，这些情绪会自动导致你体内的激素变化，或影响你与他人的行为，或使你始终根据信念来解读事件。于是日常经验会越来越显得证实你所相信的一切。

quotes.text: When you move from one area of the country to another, it is because you have changed, and so you are drawn to others with the same kind of beliefs and needs, attracted therefore by entirely different natural situations. You will then help perpetuate the "characteristic" climate to which you travel.
quotes.translation: 当你从国家的一个地区迁移到另一个地区，是因为你已经改变了，因此你被吸引到拥有相同信念和需求的人群中，从而被完全不同的自然环境所吸引。随后你将协助延续你所前往之地的“典型”气候。

quotes.text: Tell yourself this often. Create your own life now, using your beliefs as an artist uses color. There is no condition that you cannot change, except one indisputably physically accepted at birth within the realms of creaturehood, such as a liability in terms of a missing organ, or a functional lack.
quotes.translation: 经常这样告诉自己。现在就创造你自己的生命，像艺术家运用色彩那样运用你的信念。没有任何状况是你无法改变的，除了那些在生物性范畴内出生时即被无可争议地生理接受的条件，例如器官缺失或功能缺陷这类先天限制。

quotes.text: Instead you are presented, of course, with a picture of man's body as it reflects, and is affected by, man's beliefs. Doctors expect vision to [begin to] fail, for example, after the age of 30, and there are countless patient records that "prove" that such disintegration is indeed a biological fact.
quotes.translation: 相反，你当然会看到人类身体反映并受其信念影响的图景。例如，医生预期视力在30岁后开始衰退，而无数病历记录“证明”这种退化确实是生物学事实。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: In those terms overpopulation is a violation. In the cases of both war and of overgrowth, the species has ignored its natural guilt. When a man kills another, regardless of his other beliefs a certain portion of his conscious mind is always aware of the violation involved, justify it though he may.
quotes.translation: 从这个角度来看，人口过剩是一种侵犯。无论是战争还是过度繁衍，人类都忽视了其与生俱来的罪恶感。当一个人杀害他人时，无论他持有何种其他信念，其意识中总有某个部分始终清楚这其中的侵犯行为——尽管他可能会为自己的行为辩护。

quotes.text: Such critical-uniting episodes usually do not involve long sickness, though they may, but instead events such as bad accidents. The difficulty may be exteriorized as a broken limb, for example, instead of a broken self, and as the body is repaired the necessary assimilation of belief takes place.
quotes.translation: 这类批判性整合事件通常不会涉及长期疾病，虽然也有可能，但更常表现为严重意外等事件。例如，困境可能外化为断肢而非破碎的自我，随着身体被修复，必要的信念同化过程也随之发生。

quotes.text: "Ruburt's creative nature early began to perceive at least that man's existence contained other realities that were deeper. Some of this is difficult to separate. To leave the church, say, meant to carry still some of the old beliefs, but without the bandaids that earlier offered some protection.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏的创造性本质很早就开始觉察到，至少人类的存在包含了更深层的其他实相。其中有些部分难以区分。比如说，离开教会意味着仍带着某些旧信念，却失去了先前提供些许保护的创可贴。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: You make your own reality. I cannot say this too often. There will be periods where all of your beliefs are at an even par, so to speak. They will agree.
quotes.translation: 你创造你自己的实相。我再怎么强调也不为过。可以说，会有那么一段时期，你所有的信念都处于平衡状态，彼此协调一致。

quotes.text: There may be areas in which he realizes he is not adequate, yet because of his belief in his basic worth he will be able to accept these lacks as a part of himself without feeling threatened by them. He will be able to try to improve his condition without knocking himself down at the same time.
quotes.translation: 他或许会意识到自身在某些领域有所不足，但因深信自身基本价值，便能接纳这些缺失作为自我的一部分而不受其威胁。他能在不自我贬抑的同时，努力改善自身处境。

quotes.text: "Once those old beliefs are understood, they will no longer be considered shameful in themselves, nor humiliating, or as attitudes to be accused of...."
quotes.translation: 一旦那些旧信念被理解，它们本身将不再被视为可耻、羞辱或应受指责的态度。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: If you completely understand what I am saying, however, your new beliefs will -- and quickly -- begin to show themselves in your experience. But you must not be concerned for their emergence, for this brings up the fear that the new ideas will not materialize, and so this negates your purpose.
quotes.translation: 然而，若你完全理解我所说的，你的新信念将——且迅速地——开始在你的经验中显现。但你切不可为它们的出现而担忧，因为这会引发新想法无法实现的恐惧，从而否定你的目的。

quotes.text: This is on the most surface level, however. A true healing, or health profession, would deal intimately with the powers of the psyche in healing the body, and with the interrelationship among the desires, beliefs, and activities of the conscious mind and its effects upon the cellular behavior.
quotes.translation: 但这仅是最表层的层面。真正的疗愈或健康专业，会深入处理心灵在疗愈身体方面的力量，以及意识心的欲望、信念与活动之间的相互关系，及其对细胞行为的影响。

quotes.text: Only a few days before it took place, Ruburt was offered a television engagement in Baltimore, and refused it.* Their car was submerged. Income from Ruburt's classes was lost, yet these side effects were chosen quite in line with Ruburt's and Joseph's conscious beliefs, habits, and practices.
quotes.translation: 就在此事发生前数日，鲁柏曾拒绝巴尔的摩电视台的邀约*。他们的汽车被水淹没，鲁柏授课的收入也付诸东流——但这些副作用恰恰符合鲁柏与约瑟有意识持有的信念、习惯及行为模式。

quotes.text: (I might add that Seth has briefly discussed reincarnation in connection with probabilities in the 631st session in Chapter Seven; in connection with the moment of reflection in the 636th session in Chapter Nine; and in connection with present beliefs in the 657th session in Chapter Fifteen.)
quotes.translation: （我可以补充一点，赛斯在第631节第七章中简要讨论了转世与或然性的关联；在第636节第九章中讨论了转世与反省时刻的关联；在第657节第十五章中讨论了转世与当前信念的关联。）

quotes.text: Dictation: You have yourselves painted a pretty enough picture of what you think of as your own reality, as individuals and as a species. All of your institutions, beliefs, and activities seem to justify your picture, because everything within the overall "frame" will of course seem to agree.
quotes.translation: 口授：你们为自己描绘了一幅相当美好的画面，关于你们所认为的自身实相，无论作为个体还是作为物种。你们所有的机构、信念和活动似乎都印证了这幅画面，因为整个"框架"内的一切当然都会显得一致。

quotes.text: It is more important than you know. Subconsciously like many others you believe that once energy is spent it is gone, and also that you have only some arbitrary amount of energy to be used, after which you will become bankrupt. This is not only a false belief but a dangerous and limiting one.
quotes.translation: 这比你所知的更重要。和许多人一样，你潜意识里相信能量一旦被使用就会消失，并且你只有某个固定数量的能量可用，之后就会破产。这不仅是一个错误的信念，而且是一个危险且限制性的信念。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: Nevertheless, in dreams you are intimately connected with the processes by which physical events are formed. Events, again, gain their characteristics from those significances that you place upon the universe as your own being impresses it with your beliefs, desires, and individual nature.
quotes.translation: 然而，在梦中你与物质事件形成的过程紧密相连。事件再次从你赋予宇宙的意义中获得特性，因为你的存在以你的信念、欲望和个体本质烙印其上。

quotes.text: Certainly for more than the hundredth time I say: "Your beliefs form your reality," and this means that your beliefs structure the events you know.
quotes.translation: 我再说一遍，这绝对不止第一百次了：“你的信念形成你的实相”，这意味着你的信念构建了你所认知的事件。

quotes.text: Now, I can be playful because I am not as serious and mystical as the rest of you. I am myself, and if you were yourselves,28 you would not be so self-consciously profound about your beliefs and the nature of your reality. (Humorously, to a certain student:) You would trust your mustache!
quotes.translation: 现在，我可以轻松些，因为我不像你们其他人那样严肃和神秘。我就是我自己，如果你们也能做自己，就不会对自己的信念和实相的本质如此故作深沉了。（幽默地对某位学生说：）你会信任你的胡子！

quotes.text: Its beliefs about the nature of reality are then given to inner portions of the self. These rely mainly upon the conscious mind's interpretation of temporal reality. The conscious mind sets the goals and the inner self brings them about, using all its facilities and inexhaustible energy.
quotes.translation: 它对实相本质的信念随后被赋予自我的内在部分。这些部分主要依赖意识心对时间性实相的解释。意识心设定目标，内在自我则运用其所有能力与无穷能量来实现这些目标。

quotes.text: Such self knowledge is in itself highly advantageous, and in one way is its own reward. It is impossible, however, to look inward with any clearness if you are unwilling to change your attitudes, beliefs, or behavior, or examine those characteristics that you consider uniquely your own.
quotes.translation: 这种自我认知本身就极具益处，从某种角度而言，它本身就是回报。但若不愿改变自己的态度、信念或行为，或不愿审视那些你认为独一无二的特质，便无法清晰地进行内省。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: He was a study, a living example, of the effects of conflicting unexamined beliefs, a fierce and yet agonized personification of what can happen when an individual allows his conscious mind to deny its responsibilities -- i.e., when an individual becomes afraid of his own consciousness.
quotes.translation: 他是未经检视的冲突信念所造成影响的活生生案例——当个体允许意识心否认其责任时，即当个体开始恐惧自身意识时，所可能发生的激烈而痛苦的具象化呈现。

quotes.text: In a manner of speaking, each belief can be seen as a powerful station, pulling to it from fields of probabilities only those signals to which it is attuned, and blocking out all others. When you set up a new station there may be some static or bleed-through from an old one for a while.
quotes.translation: 可以说，每个信念都可被视为一个强大的电台，从概率场中只吸引那些与其调谐的信号，而屏蔽所有其他信号。当你建立新电台时，旧电台的静电干扰或信号串扰可能会持续一段时间。

quotes.text: The end does not justify the means. If you learn that lesson, then your good intent will allow you to act effectively and creatively in your private experience, and in your relationships with others. Your changed beliefs will affect the mental atmosphere of your nation and of the world.
quotes.translation: 目的不能合理化手段。如果你学会这一课，那么你的善意将让你在个人经验及与他人的关系中有效且创造性地行动。你改变的信念将影响你的国家乃至全世界的心理氛围。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Before you embark upon other journeys of consciousness, understand that your beliefs will follow you and form your experience there as they do here. If you believe in demons you will meet them -- in this life as enemies, and in other realms of consciousness as devils or "evil spirits."
quotes.translation: 在踏上其他意识旅程之前，要明白你的信念会如影随形，在那里如同在此处一样塑造你的经验。若你相信恶魔，你便会遇见它们——此生以敌人的形式，在其他意识领域则以魔鬼或"邪灵"的形式出现。

quotes.text: As you become more proficient at using your conscious mind, then of course you examine the beliefs that surround you, even as you question and often move out of your native environment. You may migrate to a climate in which the prevailing ideas suit you better, as well as the weather.
quotes.translation: 当你越来越熟练地运用意识心时，你自然会检视环绕你的信念，甚至质疑并常常脱离你原本的环境。你可能会迁移到主流思想与气候都更契合你的地方。

quotes.text: Not only this, but even if the race as you know it distorts itself beyond belief, or even destroys itself, the many will not forget. The knowledge, hard won, would be as instinct when the race began again. The losers then would become stern teachers, having learned through experience.
quotes.translation: 不仅如此，即使你所知的种族扭曲到难以置信的地步，甚至自我毁灭，多数人也不会遗忘。那些来之不易的知识，在种族重新开始时将如同本能。而失败者将因亲身经历成为严厉的导师。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: The process of clearing the body out began when Ruburt started his Day 1, but with the great frequency of negative hospital suggestions, and general false beliefs connected with fever, the beneficial aspects indeed had to be taken on faith as largely (underlined) they were (louder).
quotes.translation: 当鲁柏开始他的第一天疗程时，身体清理的过程便已启动，但由于医院大量负面暗示，以及普遍与发烧相关的错误信念，这些有益效果确实在很大程度上（下划线强调）必须依靠信念来接受（音量提高）。

quotes.text: As you come to understand the nature of your own beliefs, you can learn to use the dream state more effectively for your conscious purposes. It is one of the most efficient natural therapies, and the inner framework in which much of your physical body building actually takes place.
quotes.translation: 当你逐渐理解自身信念的本质时，就能学会更有效地运用梦境状态来实现有意识的目的。这是最有效的自然疗法之一，也是你大部分身体构建实际发生的内部框架。

quotes.text: ("Whatever thoughts, beliefs, opinions, theories, or dogmas you write, engrave, or impress on your subconscious mind... You will experience them as the objective manifestation of circumstances, conditions, and events; what you write on the inside, you experience on the outside."
quotes.translation: （"无论你在潜意识中写下、铭刻或烙印何种思想、信念、观点、理论或教条……你都将经历它们，成为环境、状况与事件的客观显化；你在内在写下的，必在外在经历。"
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: Religious beliefs will be examined as they have not been before, and their connections and political affiliations. The Arab world still needs the West, and again, it is better that those issues come to light now, while they must to some extent consider the rest of the world.
quotes.translation: 宗教信仰及其与政治势力的关联，将以前所未有的方式受到检视。阿拉伯世界仍需要西方，而这些问题最好趁现在浮上台面——毕竟他们多少还得顾及世界其他地区的反应。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Such conditions, however, are the results of beliefs, which are mental, and so the most vital work must always be done in that area.
quotes.translation: 然而，这类境况实为信念的产物——信念本质上是心智层面的，因此最关键的功课永远必须在此领域着手。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: This, again, deepened their intimate sense of relationship with nature, and encouraged their tendency to go along with it, to survive within its context rather than combat it. With this set of beliefs, attitudes and background, their decision to stay was highly predictable.
quotes.translation: 这再度深化了他们与自然之间的亲密关联感，助长了顺应自然而非对抗自然的生存倾向。基于这套信念、态度与背景，他们选择留下的决定几乎是可以预见的。

quotes.text: The physically alive body, its activities and condition, are directed through the beliefs of the conscious mind. The body, as explained in this chapter, also has "invisible" counterparts composed of the electromagnetic properties and the interior sound and light qualities.
quotes.translation: 物质活着的身体及其活动与状态，皆由意识心之信念所引导。如本章所述，身体亦拥有由电磁特性与内在声光特质构成的"无形"对应体。

quotes.text: There are many other conditions, though, all having to do with your conscious beliefs. You may think it is better to die quickly of a heart attack, for example. Your individual purposes are not the same so you will manage your body experiences in a great variety of ways.
quotes.translation: 然而还有许多其他状况，皆与你们有意识的信念相关。例如，你可能认为死于突发心脏病更为干脆。由于每个人的个体目的各不相同，你们将以千差万别的方式管理自身的身体体验。

quotes.text: He knew there were many different ways of experiencing even the physical world, and so he rejected all concepts that told him otherwise. The very belief allowed him to use those abilities, and as muscles became more resilient with use, so do psychic and intuitive powers.
quotes.translation: 他知道体验物质世界的方式有无数种，因此他摒弃了所有与此相悖的观念。正是这种信念让他得以运用那些能力——正如肌肉越用越强韧，心灵与直觉的力量也会在运用中日益精进。

quotes.text: There may be, for example, complications arising from a person's intents, loves, and desires that cause the individual to seek certain events that his or her very beliefs make impossible. Current experience will provide a dilemma in which a desired goal seems impossible.
quotes.translation: 举例而言，当一个人的意图、挚爱与渴望交织成复杂脉络时，可能促使他追寻某些事件，而这些事件恰恰被其自身信念所阻碍。当下的经验将呈现两难困境：渴望达成的目标似乎遥不可及。

quotes.text: His rage turns outward as aggression. It is the highest idiocy, however, to project that artificial aggression outward upon the animal kingdom in general. Such beliefs invisibly affect all of your studies -- and worse, they help you misread the activity in nature itself.
quotes.translation: 他的愤怒向外投射为攻击性。然而，将这种人为制造的攻击性泛化到整个动物王国，实属最愚蠢的行径。此类信念无形中渗透你们所有的研究——更糟的是，它们导致你们误读自然本身的运作模式。

quotes.text: They might have felt that they had lost direction at times. For certain periods they might have had problems in which they forgot their aims momentarily, and yet their beliefs in themselves individually and together were strong enough to give them their present reality.
quotes.translation: 他们有时可能感到迷失方向。在某些时期，他们或许曾暂时忘记目标而陷入困境，然而他们对自己及彼此间的信念足够强大，最终成就了当下的实相。

quotes.text: The Church, however, never really found a suitable method of dealing with its women, or with the intuitive elements of its own beliefs. Its fear of a goddess emerging was renewed each time another apparition of the Virgin appeared in one corner or another of the world.
quotes.translation: 然而教会始终未能找到妥善处理女性问题的方法，也未能妥善处理其信仰中的直觉元素。每当世界某个角落再次出现圣母显灵时，他们对女神崛起的恐惧便会重新浮现。

quotes.text: When Augustus Two was in control of the body the chemical makeup varied considerably. It showed significant differences over Augustus's usual hormonal status. The chemical changes were caused by the transition in beliefs that operated, and not the other way around.
quotes.translation: 当奥古斯都二世控制身体时，其化学组成变化显著，与奥古斯都通常的荷尔蒙状态存在明显差异。这些化学变化是由运作中的信念转变所引发，而非相反。

quotes.text: You simply are not able to understand the nature of such consciousnesses, and so you interpret their behavior according to your beliefs. This would be sad enough if you did not often use such distorted data to further define the nature of male and female behavior.
quotes.translation: 你根本无法理解这类意识的本质，因此只能根据自身信念解读其行为。倘若你不常利用这种扭曲的数据来进一步定义男女行为的本质，这倒还算可悲。

quotes.text: Let us make a clear distinction here: Your conscious beliefs direct the flow of unconscious processes which bring your ideas into physical reality, so while your thoughts cause your experience, you are not consciously aware of how this takes place (forcefully).
quotes.translation: 在此我们需明确区分：你有意识的信念引导着无意识过程的流向，这些过程将你的想法转化为物质实相，因此尽管你的思想造就了你的经验，你并未有意识地觉察到这一过程是如何发生的（强烈地）。

quotes.text: Now those who have such beliefs actually lack a necessary deep trust in the nature of consciousness, of the soul, and of All That Is. They concentrate upon not what they think of as the power of good, but fearfully upon what they think of as the power of evil.
quotes.translation: 那些持有此类信念的人，实际上缺乏对意识、灵魂及一切万有本质的必要深层信任。他们并非专注于自己所认为的善的力量，而是恐惧地聚焦于自己所认为的恶的力量。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: You get what you concentrate upon, and your beliefs are largely responsible for those areas in which you concentrate.
quotes.translation: 你专注什么就得到什么，而你的信念在很大程度上决定了你专注的领域。

quotes.text: In the same way, if your core belief stresses your spirituality to such an extent that it cuts off needed sensual expression, then it has become restrictive and will end up strangling, even, that spiritual experience that it was originally meant to express.
quotes.translation: 同样地，如果你的核心信念过度强调灵性，以至于切断了必要的感官表达，那么它就会变得具有限制性，最终甚至会扼杀它原本要表达的那种灵性体验。

quotes.text: Dictation. (A one-minute pause.) In certain terms, "psychedelic experience" cannot be explained within your limited frameworks of reference -- not because such illuminations are beyond explanation but because your present systems of belief are too limiting.
quotes.translation: 口述。（停顿一分钟。）从某种角度来看，“迷幻体验”无法在你有限的参考架构内被解释——并非因为这类启示无法解释，而是因为你目前的信念体系过于局限。

quotes.text: (9:37.) You use your beliefs like searchlights in the dream state, looking for other events that fit in with your ideas about reality. Your convictions help you sift out probable actions appearing as dreams, of course -- from others that do not concern you.
quotes.translation: （9点37分）你在梦境状态中像使用探照灯一样运用你的信念，寻找其他符合你对现实认知的事件。你的信念当然会帮助你筛选出那些以梦境形式出现的可能行动——从其他与你无关的选项中分离出来。

quotes.text: The paranoid organizes the psychological world about his obsession, for such it is, and he cuts everything out that does not apply, until all conforms to his beliefs. An examination of unprejudiced sense data at any point would at any time bring him relief.
quotes.translation: 偏执狂围绕其执念组织心理世界——事实正是如此——他剔除所有不相关的事物，直到一切皆符合其信念。在任何时刻对无偏见感官数据的检验，都能为他带来解脱。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: New paragraph: In somewhat the same manner, your physical brain is a doorway that triggers activity in your mind. Your beliefs then are largely responsible for the areas of the brain that you activate, and for the resulting nonphysical action of the mind.
quotes.translation: 新段落：以类似的方式，你的物质大脑是触发心灵活动的门户。你的信念在很大程度上决定了你激活的大脑区域，以及由此产生的心灵非物质活动。

quotes.text: In all cases, however, such situations instantly bring to mind questions of man's own reality and source, his connections with God, his planet, and the universe. He interprets those questions according to his own beliefs. Let us then look at some of them.
quotes.translation: 然而，在所有这类情况下，这些情境会立刻引发人们对自身实相与源头的疑问，以及他与神、地球和宇宙的关联。他根据自身的信念来诠释这些疑问。那么，让我们来看看其中一些信念。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: History happened in certain definite forms because of a belief in events that did not, in your world of facts, occur. The main, brilliant thrust of those inner events, therefore, splashed out upon the human landscape, propelling peoples and civilizations.
quotes.translation: 历史之所以以某些特定形式发生，是因为对某些事件的信念——这些事件在你们的事实世界中并未实际发生。那些内在事件的主要、辉煌的冲力，因此迸发出来，洒落在人类景观上，推动着民族与文明。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: To become acquainted with your own ideas and beliefs you must walk among them, symbolically speaking, without blinders. You must look through the structures that you have yourself created, the organized ideas upon which you have grouped your experience.
quotes.translation: 要熟悉你自己的观念和信念，你必须象征性地在它们之间行走，不带眼罩。你必须审视你自己所创造的结构，以及那些你据以组织经验的有组织的观念。

quotes.text: Augustus had been taught to fear his own thoughts, to avoid self-examination. Beliefs or ideas that frightened him were not faced, therefore, but initially shoved into corners of the conscious mind, where they lay relatively harmlessly in the beginning.
quotes.translation: 奥古斯都曾被教导要畏惧自己的思想，避免自我审视。因此，那些令他恐惧的信念或想法并未被直面，而是被最初推入意识心灵的角落，在那里它们起初相对无害地潜伏着。

quotes.text: When you are trying to alter your beliefs, look through your past with the new conceptions in mind. If you are ill, remember when you were not. Search your life for proofs of your health. Your very life itself is hard evidence that health is within you!
quotes.translation: 当你试图改变自己的信念时，请带着新的观念回顾你的过去。如果你正患病，请回忆你健康时的模样。在你的人生中寻找健康的证据——你的生命本身，就是健康存于你体内的铁证！

quotes.text: Now I have often said that suffering of itself is not "good for the soul." It is not a virtue, yet certainly many individuals seem to seek suffering. Suffering cannot be dismissed from human experience as a freak matter of distorted emotions or beliefs.
quotes.translation: 我常言痛苦本身并非"灵魂的良药"。它并非美德，然而确实有许多人似乎在追寻痛苦。痛苦无法被当作扭曲情感或信念的异常现象而从人类经验中剔除。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: Dictation: Chapter Six: "The Body of Your Beliefs, and the Power Structures of Beliefs." This is the heading.
quotes.translation: 口授：第六章："你信念的身体，与信念的权力结构"。此为章节标题。

quotes.text: Other means would seem closed to him because of various personal beliefs that would form a vacuum in his experience -- that is, he would see no other way, perhaps, to achieve the same end. This will be discussed much more thoroughly later in the book.
quotes.translation: 由于各种个人信念会在他的经验中形成真空——也就是说，他或许会看不到其他达成同样目的的方法——其他途径对他而言似乎封闭了。这一点将在本书后面更详尽地讨论。

quotes.text: In health terms, it involves conducting yourself once a day as though you were not sick in whatever way given you. But the belief in the present, reinforced for five minutes, plus such a physical action, will sometimes bring literally awesome results.
quotes.translation: 就健康而言，这包括每天一次，以你被赋予的任何方式，表现得仿佛你并未生病。但对当下的信念，经过五分钟的强化，再加上这样的实际行动，有时会带来真正惊人的结果。

quotes.text: In the case of criminals and their belief systems, aggression has a positive value. It becomes a condition for survival. Many other characteristics that might mitigate such behavior are minimized, and can be seen as dangerous by them. They believe --
quotes.translation: 在罪犯及其信念体系的情况下，攻击性具有正面价值。它成为生存的条件。许多可能缓和此类行为的其他特质被最小化，并被他们视为危险。他们相信——

quotes.text: Here the polarity between Augustus One and Two had dissolved, so that the two opposing systems of belief operated alongside each other. Still Augustus would not examine his own words, his own thoughts, or see the contradictions so obvious to others.
quotes.translation: 此处奥古斯都一号与二号之间的极性已经消解，因此两个对立的信念体系并行运作。然而奥古斯都仍不愿审视自己的话语、自己的思想，或看到对他人而言如此明显的矛盾。

quotes.text: I close by saying, as I have said before: You are given the gift of the gods; you create your reality according to your beliefs; yours is the creative energy that makes your world; there are no limitations to the self except those you believe in.
quotes.translation: 最后容我重申：你们被赋予了众神的礼物；你们根据自己的信念创造实相；你们拥有创造自己世界的能量；自我除了你们所相信的限制之外，没有任何限制。

quotes.text: Dictation: The simple diagrams merely represent some general belief systems from the standpoint of "moral values." Your ideas of good and evil affect not only your behavior with others, but your activity in a community and in the world at large.
quotes.translation: 口授：这些简单的图表仅从“道德价值”的角度代表了一些普遍的信念系统。你对善恶的观念不仅影响你与他人的行为，也影响你在社区乃至整个世界中的活动。

quotes.text: You must, of course, be able to distinguish between the world of imagination and the physical world of fact in order to be able to manipulate effectively. But physical reality springs from the imagination, which follows the path of your beliefs.
quotes.translation: 你必须能够区分想象世界与物质事实世界，才能有效运作。但物质实相源自想象，而想象遵循着你的信念轨迹。

quotes.text: （Bill states it is his belief that the "two houses nearby" do refer to the two cottages mentioned above, one shared by Gary and Larry O'Toole, with the font room across from the beach, since they are not far down the street from the A-House bar.
quotes.translation: （比尔表示他相信"附近的两栋房子"确实指上文提到的两间小屋，其中一间由加里和拉里·奥图尔合住，前厅正对海滩，因为它们与A-House酒吧在同一条街上相距不远。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: To be human necessitates fine discrimination in the use of such consciousness. Many people are afraid of their own thoughts. They do not examine them. They accept the beliefs of others. Such actions distort data from both within and without.
quotes.translation: 作为人类，必须在使用这类意识时具备精微的辨别力。许多人畏惧自己的思想，不愿审视它们，反而接受他人的信念。这种行为会扭曲来自内在与外在的信息。

quotes.text: Dictation: Today Ruburt received a call from a young woman I will name Andrea. She is a lovely young blonde. I would like to use this instance as an excellent example of the ways in which conscious beliefs affect your feelings and behavior.
quotes.translation: 口述：今天鲁柏接到一位我称为安德莉亚的年轻女性来电。她是一位可爱的金发女郎。我想用这个案例作为绝佳范例，说明有意识的信念如何影响你的感受与行为。

quotes.text: This is the only basis for any belief, regardless of rationalization. Intellectual acceptance of me as a complete survival personality will follow the intuitive knowledge. The intellect will never, alone, convince the Emotions of any fact.
quotes.translation: 这是任何信念的唯一基础，无论其合理化如何。对我作为完整存续人格的理智接纳，将跟随直觉认知而来。理智独自永远无法说服情感接受任何事实。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: New paragraph: While your beliefs do structure much of your dream activity, other issues are also involved simply because the focus of your awareness is not acutely directed toward physical reality, but is only opaquely concerned with it.
quotes.translation: 虽然你的信念确实构建了大部分梦境活动，但其他因素也参与其中，仅仅是因为你的意识焦点并未敏锐地指向物质实相，而是仅模糊地与之相关。

quotes.text: He fully believes he will become ill if he eats the forbidden foods, and so he does. It never occurs to him to dispense with the belief -- to realize that it alone sets up the conditioning process through the operation of self-hypnotism.
quotes.translation: 他完全相信自己若吃了禁忌食物就会生病，于是果真如此。他从未想过要摒弃这个信念——去认识到正是这个信念通过自我催眠的运作，单独建立了那个制约过程。

quotes.text: Listen to your own conversation as you speak with friends, and to theirs. See how you reinforce each other's beliefs. See how your imaginations often follow the same lines. All of this is quite out in the open if you realize that it is.
quotes.translation: 倾听你与朋友交谈时的对话，也倾听他们的对话。观察你们如何相互强化彼此的信念，观察你们的想象力如何常循着相似的轨迹。若你意识到这一点，这一切便昭然若揭。

quotes.text: Generally speaking, you are here to expand your consciousness, to learn the ways of creativity as directed through conscious thought. The aware mind can change its beliefs, and so to a large extent it can alter its bodily experience ...
quotes.translation: 一般而言，你在此是为了扩展意识，学习经由意识思维引导的创造之道。有觉知的心灵能够改变其信念，因此也能在很大程度上改变其身体经验……

quotes.text: Within that vast form is your own, which is briefer, yet is not lost, not limited and not predetermined. You form your corner of the universe, which is itself a part of another one. Within this the actions and beliefs of one affect all.
quotes.translation: 在那浩瀚的形态中存有你的形态，它虽短暂，却不会消失、不受限制、也非预先注定。你塑造了自己宇宙的角落，而这角落本身又是另一个宇宙的一部分。在此之中，个体的行为与信念影响着整体。

quotes.text: What should you do, then? First of all, you must realize that you are the hypnotist. You must seize the initiative here as you have in other positive aspects of your life. Whatever the superficial reasons for your beliefs, you must say:
quotes.translation: 那么你该怎么办？首先，你必须认识到自己就是那个催眠师。你必须像在生活的其他积极方面一样，在此处掌握主动权。无论你信念的表面理由为何，你必须说：

quotes.text: When someone who has been ill starts on the road to recovery through changing his beliefs, he may be quite surprised to find even his dearest allies suddenly upset, reminding him of the "reality" of his dire state for the same reasons.
quotes.translation: 当某个生病的人通过改变信念开始踏上康复之路时，他可能会惊讶地发现，连最亲密的盟友都会突然感到不安，用同样的理由提醒他自身危境的"现实"。

quotes.text: In somewhat the same way, a strong belief in a particular ability generated in the present will reach into the past and effect whatever changes would have had to occur there (with gestures) in order to now make the ability apparent.
quotes.translation: 以类似的方式，当下对某项特定能力的强烈信念将回溯过去，并促成过去必须发生的任何改变（伴随手势），以便现在让这项能力显现出来。

quotes.text: You can ignore it. You can hold beliefs that blind you to its existence. You will still be graced but unable to perceive your own uniqueness and integrity, and blind also to other attributes with which you are automatically gifted.
quotes.translation: 你可以忽视它。你可以持有蒙蔽你双眼的信念，让你看不见它的存在。你仍将蒙受恩宠，却无法感知自身的独特性与完整性，也看不见你与生俱来的其他天赋特质。

quotes.text: I will deal with some such exercises later on in the book. All such methods, however, are useless if your beliefs hold you back, and so the main thrust of all of my books is to increase your own areas of thought and speculation.
quotes.translation: 我将在本书稍后部分探讨某些这类练习。然而，若你的信念阻碍了你，所有这类方法都将徒劳无功，因此我所有著作的主旨都在于拓展你自身的思考与思辨领域。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: The same procedures as just given for those who are overweight should be used. In each case body conditioning is set up through natural hypnosis. Daily behavior and chemical functioning smoothly follow according to the belief.
quotes.translation: 适用于超重者的相同程序也应采用。在每种情况下，身体调节通过自然催眠建立。日常行为与化学功能会依据信念顺畅运作。

quotes.text: 10. Much of Personal Reality contains material on beliefs, of course; see chapters 14 and 15, among others. Seth's information on "the point of power," given in the 657th session in Chapter 15, is especially appropriate here.
quotes.translation: 10.《个人实相的本质》中自然包含大量关于信念的内容，可参见第14章与第15章等章节。赛斯在第15章第657节中提出的"力量之点"信息，在此处尤为贴切。

related_concepts.name_en: The point of power is in the present

quotes.text: The mind does not hold just active beliefs. It contains many others in a passive state. These lie latent, ready to be focused upon and used; any of them can be brought to the fore when a conscious thought acts as a stimulus.
quotes.translation: 心灵并非仅持有活跃信念，它还以被动状态容纳许多其他信念。这些信念潜伏着，随时可供聚焦运用；当有意识的思想作为刺激时，任何信念都能被带到前台。

quotes.text: Now let us return to Augustus; for here we find again in one individual an excellent example of the way in which seemingly nonphysical thoughts and beliefs can affect and alter the corporeal image. And you may take a break.
quotes.translation: 现在让我们回到奥古斯都；因为我们再次在此人身上看到一个绝佳范例，说明看似非实质的思想和信念如何能影响并改变肉体形象。你们可以休息一下。

quotes.text: (Deliberately at 10:42:) When you are working with your beliefs, find out what you really think about the dream condition, for if you trust it it can become an even more important ally because of your conscious cooperation.
quotes.translation: （刻意地在10:42）当你在处理自己的信念时，要找出你对梦境状态的真实想法，因为如果你信任它，由于你有意识的合作，它甚至可以成为更重要的盟友。

quotes.text: Hypnosis clearly shows in concentrated form the way in which your beliefs affect your behavior in normal life. The various methods simply focus all of your concentration upon a specific area, shutting out any distractions.
quotes.translation: 催眠以浓缩的形式清楚显示，你的信念如何在日常生活中影响你的行为。各种方法只是将你所有的专注力集中于特定领域，排除任何干扰。

quotes.text: The relationship with Frederick Fell will be a good one, although the present contract reflects the publisher's caution, as well as the money already received reflects an impulsive belief in Ruburt on the publisher's part.
quotes.translation: 与弗雷德里克·费尔的关系将会良好，尽管目前的合约反映出出版商的谨慎，而已收到的款项则反映出出版商对鲁柏的冲动性信任。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: The nature and importance of belief appeared so eloquently that Ruburt was astounded, and found himself forced into some complicated psychological footwork. The two "personalities" were no longer separate, but merging.
quotes.translation: 信念的本质与重要性如此清晰地展现出来，以至于鲁柏大为震惊，发现自己被迫进行了一些复杂的心理调整。这两个"人格"不再分离，而是正在融合。

quotes.text: (10:45.) It is for this reason that "past-life information" is so often used to reinforce current personal social situations -- because, like the past in this life, such memories are constructed through present belief.
quotes.translation: (十点四十五分) 正因如此，"前世信息"才经常被用来强化当前个人的社会处境——因为就像这一生中的过去一样，这类记忆是通过当下的信念构建而成的。

quotes.text: You shut them off any time they do not conform to current beliefs about the nature of the self, or about reality in general. The deepest meanings of probabilities lie, however, precisely in their psychological import.
quotes.translation: 每当它们不符合当前关于自我本质或现实整体的信念时，你就将它们关闭。然而，可能性的最深层的意义恰恰在于它们的心理意涵。

quotes.text: The picture of man, animals, and nature depicted in the movie of which you were speaking is the only possible portrayal of reality that could be logically shown, considering the beliefs upon which the premise rests.
quotes.translation: 你们所谈论的那部电影中描绘的人类、动物与自然的图景，若考量其前提所依据的信念，实为唯一能逻辑呈现的现实写照。

quotes.text: Your expectations alone will change your material circumstances. The belief that you can afford the kind of car you require will in itself form the circumstances that will make such an automobile literally possible.
quotes.translation: 仅凭你的期待就能改变你的物质实相。相信你能负担得起所需车辆的信念，本身就会形成让这辆车真正成为可能的情境。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: Now. Individuals within your system have difficulty relating to those with whom they do not have some fairly strong bond, either of common experience, general beliefs, cultural similarities--the list is endless.
quotes.translation: 现在。你们体系中的个体很难与那些没有相当强烈连结的人建立关系——无论是共同经验、普遍信念、文化相似性——这样的清单不胜枚举。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: The conscious mind is meant to make clear judgments about your position in physical reality. Often false beliefs will prevent it from making these, for the egotistically held ideas will cloud its clear vision.
quotes.translation: 意识心本应清晰判断你在物质实相中的位置。但错误的信念常会阻碍它做出这些判断，因为自我执着的观念会遮蔽其清明视野。

quotes.text: I quite realize that many of my statements will contradict the beliefs of those of you who accept the idea that the conscious mind is relatively powerless, and that the answers to problems lie hidden beneath.
quotes.translation: 我深知许多说法会与诸位中那些认为意识心相对无力、问题答案潜藏于下层的信念相悖。

quotes.text: Such experience then convinces you more thoroughly of the reality you perceive until a vicious circle is formed, in which all events mirror beliefs so perfectly that no leeway seems to appear between the two.
quotes.translation: 此类经验随后更彻底地让你确信所感知的实相，直至形成恶性循环——所有事件如此完美地映照信念，以致两者间似乎毫无转圜余地。

quotes.text: You have been trying to squeeze the soul into tight concepts of the nature of existence, making it follow your limited beliefs. The door to the soul is open, and it leads to all the dimensions of experience.
quotes.translation: 你们一直试图将灵魂塞进关于存在本质的狭隘概念里，迫使它遵循你们有限的信念。灵魂之门是敞开的，它通向所有经验维度。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Usually exaggerated opposing emotions will also be apparent. Once you understand this it is not difficult to look at your beliefs to identify these, and to find a bridge to unite the seeming contradictions.
quotes.translation: 通常被夸大的对立情绪也会显现。一旦你明白这点，就不难审视自己的信念以识别这些矛盾，并找到一座桥梁来联结这些看似对立的事物。

quotes.text: When [most of] you think of physical symptoms, of course, you regard your body with a deadly seriousness that to some extent impedes inner spontaneity. You lay your limiting beliefs upon the natural person.
quotes.translation: 当你们[大多数人]想到身体症状时，自然以极度严肃的态度看待身体，这在一定程度上阻碍了内在的自发性。你们将限制性信念强加于自然人格之上。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: The ego therefore is a very necessary force within your field. It is also, regardless of current psychological beliefs, the basis for aggression, in general. All aggression is not detrimental, for example.
quotes.translation: 因此，自我在你们的场域中是非常必要的力量。不论当前心理学界的信念如何，它大体上也是攻击性的基础。例如，并非所有攻击性都是有害的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: First of all, you must realize that no one can change your beliefs for you, nor can they be forced upon you from without. You can indeed change them for yourself, however, with knowledge and application.
quotes.translation: 首先，你必须明白，没有人能替你改变信念，也没有人能强迫你接受外在的信念。然而，凭借知识与实践，你确实可以自行改变它们。

quotes.text: You are not aware of how the body performs its many involuntary functions. The conscious mind could not handle all that data, but those functions perfectly mirror your consciously held ideas and beliefs.
quotes.translation: 你并不清楚身体如何执行众多非自主功能。意识心无法处理所有那些数据，但这些功能完美地映照出你有意识地持有的观念与信念。

quotes.text: Your experience will follow your concentration and belief and expectation. The mind is a great discriminator. It can use its reasoning to bring about almost any possible experience within your framework.
quotes.translation: 你的经验将追随你的专注、信念与期待。心智是伟大的分辨者，它能运用推理在你的框架内引发几乎任何可能的经验。

quotes.text: The thoughts of children give excellent clues as to mankind's nature, but many adults do not remember any childhood thoughts except those that fit, or seem to fit, in with their beliefs about childhood.
quotes.translation: 孩童的思想为人类的本性提供了绝佳线索，但许多成年人除了那些符合或看似符合他们对童年信念的想法之外，并不记得任何童年时期的思绪。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: "When you are working with your beliefs, find out what you really think about the dream condition, for if you trust it, it can become an even more important ally because of your conscious cooperation."
quotes.translation: "当你处理自己的信念时，请找出你对梦境状态的真实想法，因为若你信任它，由于你有意识的合作，它将成为更重要的盟友。"

quotes.text: The solutions lie in the conscious mind -- I cannot emphasize this too strongly -- and in those beliefs that you accept about the nature of reality and, specifically, about the nature of your being.
quotes.translation: 解决之道在于意识心——这点我再强调也不为过——以及在于你所接受的关于实相本质的信念，特别是关于你自身存有的本质。

quotes.text: To me, beside whatever relationship it might have with counterpart reality, the soul-mate belief embodies strongly distorted versions of the ideas contained in the two Seth passages quoted above.
